mock01.word: The thick smoke _____ out of the landfill site is making the city's polluted air even more toxic.
mock01.explanation: "Billowing" (लहराते हुए) means moving outward with an undulating motion — perfectly describing smoke rising from a landfill. Other options do not fit the context.

mock01.word: In the sentence identify the segment which contains the grammatical error. The blue lotus is one of the most beautiful flowers that grows in her garden.
options: is one of the most beautiful flowers
mock01.explanation: The error is "grows" — it should be "grow." Rule: "One of the + plural noun + that/who + plural verb." In this sentence, the relative pronoun "that" refers to "flowers" (plural noun), not to "one" (singular). Therefore, the verb after "that" must be plural: "grow." Correct sentence: "The blue lotus is one of the most beautiful flowers that grow in her garden." This is one of the most commonly tested grammar rules in SSC exams. Common student mistake: Matching the verb with "one" (singular = grows) instead of with "flowers" (plural = grow).

mock01.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. MANDIBLE
mock01.explanation: Mandible (जबड़ा) means the lower jaw bone. Jaw is the direct synonym matching this anatomical term.

mock01.word: The students have performed a new version of Shakespeare's 'Macbeth.'
options: Shakespeare's 'Macbeth' has been performed by the new version of the students.
A new version of Shakespeare's 'Macbeth' have been performed by the students.
A new version of Shakespeare's 'Macbeth' has been performed by the students.
Shakespeare's 'Macbeth' have been performed by the new version of the students.
mock01.explanation: Active → Passive in Present Perfect: Subject+have/has+V3 becomes Object+has/have+been+V3+by Subject. "A new version" is singular, so "has been performed" is correct.

mock01.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice: "Let the fence be repaired by him."
options: Tell him to repair the fence.
mock01.explanation: For imperative passive sentences using "Let + object + be + V3," the active form becomes "Let + subject + verb," giving "Let him repair the fence."

mock01.word: Not get into the car; it has not been cleaned inside.
options: it has not been cleaned inside
mock01.explanation: "Not get" गलत है। "Don't get" (do not का negative form) होना चाहिए जो command देने के लिए प्रयुक्त होता है। Correct Sentence: Don't get into the car; it has not been cleaned inside.

mock01.word: Have you _________ with the difficulties you might have to face?
mock01.explanation: "Reckoned with" (ध्यान में रखना) is the correct phrasal verb meaning to take something into account. None of the other options form a meaningful phrasal verb with "with" here.

mock01.word: The following sentence has been divided into parts. Pompeii was a tragedy / that cost the lives / of millions of people. Select the part with the error, or 'No error'.
mock01.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) that cost the lives. This is a non-restrictive clause providing extra, non-essential information (अतिरिक्त जानकारी). It is more grammatically precise to use "which" instead of "that."

mock01.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the following idiom. To the nines
mock01.explanation: "To the nines" (पूर्णता तक) means to perfection or to the highest degree, often used to describe being perfectly dressed up. For example, "Everyone was dressed to the nines."

mock01.word: Select the correct spelling to fill in the blank: "Subir's long absence is likely to have ___ for employees in the office."
mock01.explanation: The correct spelling is "repercussions" (परिणाम), meaning unintended consequences following an event.

mock01.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom: PEDAL TO THE METAL
options: to enjoy yourself by going out
to go full speed, especially while driving a vehicle
avoid responsibility by giving it to someone else
mock01.explanation: "Pedal to the metal" (पूरी रफ्तार से) — इसका अर्थ है to go at full speed, especially while driving। "Very excessive" (बहुत अधिक), "To enjoy yourself" (मस्ती करना), और "Avoid responsibility" (जिम्मेदारी से बचना) गलत हैं।

mock01.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice: "People say that she is a kind lady."
options: It is claimed people that she is a kind lady.
It was said that she is a kind lady.
It is said by people that she is a kind lady.
People is said that she is a kind lady.
mock01.explanation: In passive voice, the impersonal subject "it" is used with "by people" added at the end, retaining the present tense of "say" as "is said."

mock01.word: Sentences of a paragraph are given below. While the first and the last sentences (S1 and S6) are in the correct order, the sentences in between are jumbled up and named P, Q, R, S. Arrange the sentences in the correct order. (S1) The poster ranks high as a medium of publicity. (P) Advertisement can be displayed before a much larger number of persons for a longer time by means of a poster... (Q) Nor must it be forgotten that a newspaper is bought by the reader... (R) It has, by reason of its size and the facilities which it affords for the use of colour, a very powerful effect on the public mind. (S) It must, however, be remembered that the attention received by a newspaper is much closer... (S6) If more is desired than the instantaneous impact on the mind, the advertising value of the poster falls sharply.
mock01.explanation: S1 talks about the poster's high rank. R explains why (its size and colour effect). P elaborates on the poster's advertising advantage over press. S introduces a contrast — newspaper gets closer attention than poster. Q continues S by explaining why newspapers are special. S6 concludes with the limitation of posters. Correct order: S1, R, P, S, Q, S6.
"Since" indicates a point in time from the past until now. "Was born" (जन्मा था) is past tense — required here. "Am born" (present), "will be born" (future), and "I born" (no verb) are all incorrect.

The original sentence is grammatically correct - this tests inversion. Grammar Rule - Inversion: When a negative adverb (never, seldom, rarely, scarcely, hardly, barely) is placed at the beginning of a sentence for emphasis, the subject and auxiliary verb are inverted. Normal order: We seldom find so many birds...; Inverted: Seldom do we find so many birds... - this is correct (Seldom + auxiliary 'do' + subject 'we' + main verb 'find'). Option B uses 'does' which requires third-person singular, but 'we' requires 'do.' Option C has no inversion. Option D has the wrong tense ('found' is past participle, cannot follow 'did'). Inversion with Negative Adverbs: Never have I seen such beauty; Rarely does he complain; Hardly had she arrived when it started raining.

mock01.word: Select the sentence that does NOT have a spelling error.
options: The whether forecast predicted rain throughout the week.
She is very particular about pronounciation in her classes.
The successful launch took place in an urban area.
There wedding was during the summer, which is why we didn't attend it.
mock01.explanation: Option C is correctly spelt and uses "successful" (सफल, meaning achieving desired results) and "urban" (शहरी) correctly. Option A wrongly uses "whether" instead of "weather"; Option B wrongly spells "pronounciation" instead of "pronunciation" (उच्चारण); Option D wrongly uses "There" instead of the possessive "Their."

mock01.word: Is this the same book like our teacher recommended? Select the option that will improve the underlined part.
mock01.explanation: "Like" is used to compare appearance or behaviour, but here "as" is needed because the book acts as a substitute — "as" means "in the role of."

mock01.word: Choose the word that is opposite in meaning to the given word. Anomalous
mock01.explanation: Anomalous (असामान्य) means unusual or not expected. Customary (प्रथागत/सामान्य) means in accordance with custom or usual practice — the direct opposite. Endemic means native to a place. Outlandish means bizarre. Alien means foreign or unfamiliar.

mock01.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Divine
mock01.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) Celestial. "Divine" (दिव्य) is related to God or the heavens. "Celestial" (स्वर्गीय) carries a similar heavenly meaning.

mock01.word: Find the correctly spelt word.
mock01.explanation: "Temperament" (स्वभाव) is the correct spelling. It refers to a person's nature as it permanently affects their behaviour. All other options are misspelt.

mock01.word: Atlas did not want to ______ a job as arduous as holding up the sky.
mock01.explanation: "Take back" (फिर से ले लेना) means to resume responsibility for something previously given to someone else, fitting the context that Atlas, seeing how exhausting the task was, did not want to resume holding the sky himself. "Take out," "take away," and "take in" do not fit the meaning of reclaiming a burden.

mock01.word: A. The genuine beliefs, though not unconsciously acquired by most children. B. Something of them remains deeply implanted, ready to emerge in a time of stress or crisis. C. The importance of education in forming character and opinion is very great. D. Education is, as a rule, the strongest force on the side of what exists.
mock01.explanation: C — education का importance। A — beliefs कैसे बनती हैं। B — beliefs कैसे रहती हैं। D — education का conservative role।

mock01.word: If no substitution is required, select No improvement. Some agitating miners allegation that there is no emergency measures inside the mines.
mock01.explanation: The noun "allegation" must be changed to the verb "alleged," since the following clause functions as the content of what was alleged, and the tense/number should agree, giving "miners alleged that there were."

mock01.word: 'As far as he is ____, he will love to attend the party.'
mock01.explanation: This tests the fixed phrase 'as far as ... is concerned,' meaning 'in that person's opinion' or 'from that person's point of view.' Confined means restricted/limited - makes no sense. Interested is grammatically acceptable but 'as far as he is interested' is not idiomatic. Anxious is ungrammatical in this fixed phrase. Concerned is the correct word for this fixed expression. Fixed Expression: 'As far as [subject] is concerned' = from the point of view of [subject] - a MUST-KNOW phrase for SSC exams. Correct Sentence: As far as he is concerned, he will love to attend the party.

mock01.word: Don't make him ______ such a naughty thing.
mock01.explanation: After "make" (causative verb), the bare infinitive (base form) of the verb is used, so "do" is correct, not "to do" or "doing."

mock02.word: 'Everyone wants to be happy. I also want to be happy (1)____, not from outside.' Select the most appropriate option for blank No. 1:
mock02.explanation: The phrase 'not from outside' provides a contrast, and the blank introduces the reason/condition of happiness coming from within (happiness comes from within, not from outside). Grammar Rule: Conjunctions must logically connect the clauses - 'because' introduces a reason, 'unless' introduces a condition, and 'nevertheless' introduces a contrast. Always read the full passage to determine the logical relationship the blank represents.

mock02.word: Fountains can ______ also be musical instruments played by obstruction of water jets.
mock02.explanation: "Fountains" plural noun है, इसलिए "themselves" (स्वयं) सही reflexive pronoun है।

mock02.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Speak of the devil
options: The person we were just talking about showed up
The person we were talking about was guilty
The person we were talking about never appeared
The person we were talking about was a devil in disguise
mock02.explanation: This idiom is said when the person being discussed appears unexpectedly.

mock02.word: Choose the word that means the same as the given word. Abandonment
mock02.explanation: Abandonment (परित्याग) means the act of leaving or deserting something. Dereliction (उपेक्षा/त्याग) also means neglecting or abandoning — a direct synonym. Profanity means offensive language. Sanctity means holiness. Commencement means beginning.

mock02.word: and ______ a much broader spectrum than the societal institute of higher education that we frequently communicate ….
mock02.explanation: "Encompasses" (शामिल करता है) means to include or cover something broader, fitting the context of education covering a wide spectrum.

mock02.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute. Poor physical or mental condition.
mock02.explanation: "Ill health" का अर्थ है a condition of poor physical or mental well-being। Example: After months of battling ill health, she finally began to feel stronger.

mock02.word: ___ yourself with the new perfume.
mock02.explanation: "Pamper" (आराम और सुख देना) means to indulge oneself with comfort and enjoyment, fitting the sense of treating oneself to a new perfume. "Prosper" means to succeed; "Percolate" means to filter gradually; "Purify" means to remove impurities — a perfume does not literally purify a person.

mock02.word: In the sentence identify the segment which contains the grammatical error. All employees of the National Sugar Factory differ from their boss on the medical and pension policy.
options: on the medical and pension policy.
mock02.explanation: When disagreeing on views or opinions, the correct preposition to use is "with," not "from." The corrected sentence reads "...differ with their boss on the medical and pension policy."

mock02.word: The playwright borrowed the (crux) of plot from Shakespeare. Select the synonym of the bracketed word.
mock02.explanation: Crux (सार/मुख्य भाग) means the most essential part of something. Essence, meaning the intrinsic nature of something, captures this meaning precisely.

mock02.word: It's running a two-week campaign calling on people to keep their eyes ______ for text in English, Japanese, and Chinese.
mock02.explanation: "There's a particular focus" means a specific, singled-out emphasis, fitting the context that the campaign especially targets public transport signs, maps, and historic site information. "Important," "Signifying," and "Meticulous" do not fit the sense of singling out specific types of signs for attention.

mock02.word: She was laughing from the ___ exuberance of the performance.
mock02.explanation: "Sheer" (बिल्कुल/पूर्ण) means not mixed with anything else; used to create emphasis on completeness. "Utter" (चरम) and "Total" (सम्पूर्ण) also mean extreme but are less idiomatic in this context. "Perfect" (परिपूर्ण) doesn't fit naturally here. Correct sentence: She was laughing from the sheer exuberance of the performance.

mock02.word: Due to the pandemic, / the prices of / medicines increased / every day since / last December.
mock02.explanation: "since last December" के साथ present perfect tense आता है। "increased" की जगह "have increased" होना चाहिए। Correct Sentence: ...the prices of medicines have increased every day since last December.

mock02.word: Ignoring the red flag / warning in the beach / is no joke. Select the segment with the error, or 'No Error'.
mock02.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) warning in the beach. A beach is considered a surface location. The correct preposition to use for a surface is "on," giving "on the beach."

mock02.word: Which one of the following words is correctly spelt?
mock02.explanation: Assassination (हत्या) is correctly spelled with double 's' in both the initial and middle positions, followed by the suffix "-ation." It means the killing of a prominent person for political or religious reasons.

mock02.word: Identify the segment containing a grammatical error: "By the time she retires, / she has been teaching / at the university for 30 years."
mock02.explanation: For a future point in time, the future perfect continuous tense "will have been teaching" should be used instead of "has been teaching."

mock02.word: Parts of a sentence about parental benefit are given in jumbled order. Arrange them correctly. O: also benefit from knowing P: there are several reasons Q: why even parents would R: more about how and why S: children do the things that they do
mock02.explanation: P introduces the existence of reasons; Q identifies who benefits; O states the nature of the benefit; R specifies what is learned; S completes the sentence with children's behaviour.

mock02.word: Select the wrongly spelt word.
mock02.explanation: The correct spelling is Ingenuous (भोला/सरल), meaning showing innocent or childlike simplicity. Innocuous (हानिरहित) means not harmful. Inimical (शत्रुतापूर्ण) means hostile. Invulnerable (अजेय) means impossible to harm.
"Hard to stomach" — यह fixed phrase है जिसका अर्थ है difficult to accept or tolerate। "Find it hard to stomach" में "to" infinitive marker है। "For," "With," और "At" इस phrase में नहीं आते।

options: lest he would not be weak
mock02.explanation: The structure "lest + subject + should" expresses precaution against an undesired outcome; "should be weak" correctly fits this construction.

mock02.word: Identify the segment containing the grammatical error: "He was formerly / a doctor of / the corporate hospital."
mock02.explanation: The preposition "at" should be used instead of "of" when referring to the place of employment, giving "a doctor at the corporate hospital."

mock02.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom: 'After the government interventions, the enrolment and retention of female students have increased by leaps and bounds.' (Duplicate of Question 42 in the source)
mock02.explanation: This question is a duplicate of Question 42 in the source material. See Question 42 for the full explanation: 'By leaps and bounds' means making very rapid progress; among the options, 'swiftly' best captures this.

mock02.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom: DO A GOOD TURN
options: take a turn in the right way
mock02.explanation: "Do a good turn" (किसी की सेवा करना) — इसका अर्थ है to perform a helpful service to someone। Example: "If I can't do a good turn for anybody, I would not do them a bad turn." "Make a profit" (मुनाफा कमाना) और "Return a gift" (तोहफा वापस करना) गलत हैं।

mock02.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word. He was the most honest and good-hearted man that she had ever met.
mock02.explanation: "Honest" means free of deceit; truthful and sincere. The best synonym is Sincere = free from pretence or deceit; proceeding from genuine feelings. (Hindi: ईमानदार / सच्चा) Example: She offered her sincere apologies for the mistake. Trap options: Deceptive (भ्रामक) = misleading; Biased (पक्षपाती) = unfairly prejudiced; Fake (नकली) = counterfeit — all carry negative or opposite meanings.

mock02.word: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. The original inhabitants of a country
mock02.explanation: Aborigines refers to the original or earliest known inhabitants of a region, existing there before colonisation or the arrival of outsiders. (Hindi: मूल निवासी / आदिवासी) Etymology: Latin "ab origine" literally means "from the origin/beginning." Key distinctions: Immigrants = people who come from another country to live permanently (OPPOSITE meaning — they ARRIVE later); Residents = people who live somewhere on a long-term basis (not necessarily original); Slaves = persons who are the legal property of another and forced to obey them (completely unrelated).

mock02.word: Identify the segment which contains a grammatical error: "I can explain you the reason for the delay in executing your order."
mock02.explanation: "Explain" के साथ indirect + direct object construction नहीं होती। "Explain you" गलत है; सही रूप "explain to you" होगा। Correct sentence: "I can explain to you the reason for the delay in executing your order."

mock03.word: Select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the Idiom/Phrase. Penny wise and pound foolish
options: Careless with large amounts, but not small
An intelligent person who behaves foolishly
Dissipating money without caring about consequences
mock03.explanation: "Penny wise and pound foolish" (छोटे में सावधान, बड़े में लापरवाह) means being careful about small amounts of money while carelessly wasting large amounts. It reflects poor financial judgment about the bigger picture.

mock03.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Alacrity
mock03.explanation: Alacrity (उत्साह) means quick and cheerful readiness to do something. Willingness, meaning being happy to do something, captures this meaning best.

mock03.word: The husks of corn or other grains separated by winnowing or threshing.
mock03.explanation: Chaff (भूसा) refers to the husks of corn or other grains separated by winnowing or threshing — exact match. Peel means the outer skin of a fruit or vegetable. Hay means grass that has been mown and dried. Fodder means food, especially dried hay or feed, for cattle.

mock03.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Coincide
mock03.explanation: Coincide (मेल खाना) means to happen at the same time or to match. Differ, meaning to be unlike, is the direct opposite.

mock03.word: ___ to the hope that he's still alive.
mock03.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) cling. "Cling to the hope" is an established idiomatic expression meaning to hold tightly onto a belief or expectation (उम्मीद पर टिके रहना).

mock03.word: Select the correctly spelt word.
mock03.explanation: "Reimbursement" (प्रतिपूर्ति) is correctly spelt, meaning paying back money spent by someone. The correct spellings of the others are "Bureaucracy" (a system of officials running an organisation) and "Surveillance" (the careful watching of a person or place); "Recruitment" is also correctly spelt but is not the intended answer here since the question asks for the one matching the given meaning.

mock03.word: His wealth ______ only by Jeff Bezos
mock03.explanation: The sentence requires passive voice in present tense. "Is surpassed" (पीछे छोड़ा जाता है) correctly shows that Gates's wealth is surpassed only by Jeff Bezos.

mock03.word: The ______ QR codes will help you access the relevant e-resources.
mock03.explanation: "Subsequent" (बाद के) means following in order, correctly referring to QR codes after the first one.

mock03.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word. DISCREET
mock03.explanation: "Discreet" (विवेकपूर्ण) means being careful and prudent in one's speech or actions. "Careful" is its closest synonym. "Rash" (लापरवाह) is its antonym.

mock03.word: If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution required.' When challenged, the man had no answer for his actions.
mock03.explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct. "When" is used as a conjunction meaning "at the time that," and the clause functions correctly as written.

mock03.word: Select the sentence that contains a spelling error.
options: Fermented apple juice is touted to have many health benefits
Intriguing as it is, the house was never sold because it was thought to be haunted
It is important for scientists to acknowledge their humanity
...based on believes about the supernatural... (beliefs — गलत)
mock03.explanation: "believes" verb है जबकि यहाँ noun "beliefs" (विश्वास) होना चाहिए।

mock03.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. PERSECUTION
mock03.explanation: Persecution (अत्याचार) means unfair, cruel treatment over time. Oppression, meaning being governed unfairly and cruelly, is the closest match.

mock03.word: The athlete's obsequious manners impressed the coach. Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word.
mock03.explanation: Obsequious (चापलूस/आज्ञाकारी) means excessively obedient or attentive. Law-abiding, meaning following rules, is the closest match among the given options.

mock03.word: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange them in the correct order. A. Omnivores get energy and nutrients from plant and animal materials. B. The many diverse animals categorised as omnivores may be divided into sub-categories. C. An omnivore is an animal that can feed and thrive on both plant and animal substances. D. They also have the capacity to integrate food sources like algae, fungus and bacteria into their diet.
mock03.explanation: C — omnivore की definition। A — कैसे energy लेते हैं। D — और क्या खाते हैं। B — sub-categories। यह logical flow है।

mock03.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of highlighted word: EXERTION
mock03.explanation: "Exertion" (परिश्रम/प्रयास) का अर्थ है using your body with effort। इसका antonym है "Inaction" (निष्क्रियता) — doing nothing, lack of action। "Suffocation" (दम घुटना), "Traction" (खिंचाव), और "Reaction" (प्रतिक्रिया) असंबंधित हैं।

mock03.word: She raced _____ towards the train.
mock03.explanation: "Frantically" (बेचैनी से) means in a hurried and disorderly manner. The context of racing toward a train implies urgency and panic, making "frantically" the best fit.

mock03.word: Modern ______ practices and medicine have greatly subdued the impact...
mock03.explanation: "Sanitary" (स्वच्छता संबंधी) — hygiene और health conditions से related practices। Modern sanitation ने plague के impact को कम किया। "Unhygienic" (अस्वच्छ — opposite), "Ongoing" (जारी), और "Modern" (आधुनिक — redundant यहाँ) गलत हैं।

mock03.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined segment. The company was known for its deserving respect or admiration business practices and honest and fair ethical standards.
mock03.explanation: Honourable (सम्माननीय) means deserving of respect or admiration — the most precise match. Decent means conforming to accepted standards but is weaker. Respectable implies social acceptance, not necessarily ethical standards. Trustworthy relates to trust, not the full sense of being admirable.

mock03.word: Select the wrongly spelt word:
mock03.explanation: 'Menifestation' is incorrectly spelt. The correct spelling is MANIFESTATION, meaning the action or fact of showing something clearly, a sign or evidence of something. Eradication, Manipulation, and Regularization are all correctly spelt. Spelling Trick for Manifestation: Man-i-fest-a-tion - remember 'manifest' (to show clearly) + '-ation' (noun suffix).

mock03.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Banish
mock03.explanation: Correct Answer: (C) Deport. "Banish" (निष्कासित करना) means to officially send someone away from a country or place. "Deport" is the precise synonym.

mock03.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Pick the option that gives their correct order. P. The earliest years of a child's life are critical. Q. This is why an establishment has been created for the protection of children in these crucial years, known as ECCE. R. It is during the early years that children develop the cognitive, physical, social and emotional skills that they need to succeed in life. S. These years determine child's survival and thriving in life, and lay the foundations for her/his learning and holistic development.
mock03.explanation: P introduces the topic (critical early years). S extends the idea explaining why they are crucial. R provides specific developmental details. Q concludes by explaining the creation of ECCE based on the above importance. Order: P → S → R → Q.

mock03.word: In the following question, out of the given four alternatives, select the one which best expresses the meaning of the given word. QUIESCENT
mock03.explanation: Quiescent means in a state or period of inactivity or dormancy; quiet and still; not active. (Hindi: निष्क्रिय / शांत / सुप्त अवस्था में) The closest synonym is Dull = lacking interest or excitement; inactive; not lively. Example: The volcano was quiescent for several years before its next eruption. Latin root: "quiescere" = to rest. Other options: Questionable (संदिग्ध) = doubtful; Alive (जीवित/सक्रिय) = alert and active (ANTONYM of quiescent); Bright (उज्ज्वल/चतुर) = intelligent and quick-witted (also an ANTONYM).

mock03.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Soft, inner part of a nut or fruit-stone
mock03.explanation: "Kernel" (गुठली) refers specifically to the inner softer part of a seed, fruit stone, or nut. "Pulp" is the soft juicy edible flesh of a fruit, "Caramel" is burnt sugar, and "Bean" is a seed/pod of climbing plants — these are different concepts from the inner part of a nut/stone.

mock03.word: Select the option that is the incorrectly spelt word among the underlined word in the sentence: He has to be conscious of his public manners as he is a decendent of a reputed family in this region.
mock03.explanation: "Descendent/decendent" in the sentence should be corrected to "Descendant" (वंशज), meaning a person descended from a particular ancestor. The other options (A, C, D) are simply incorrect variant spellings with no standard meaning.

mock03.word: Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. "Since 1988, / the people of Rajasthan / have been searching / for 25-year-old man."
mock03.explanation: A singular countable noun requires an article. The correct phrase is "for a 25-year-old man."

mock04.word: Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it, if any. Workers and companies in all sectors can contribute their skills to meet society new needs.
mock04.explanation: The error is in "to meet society new needs." The correct phrase should be "to meet society's new needs" using the possessive form.

mock04.word: The workers ______ against the new labour laws.
mock04.explanation: "Remonstrated against" (विरोध करना/शिकायत करना) — to protest against something। "Remonstrate against" एक fixed phrase है। "Opposed" (विरोध किया), "Implicated" (फँसाया), और "Dissented" (असहमत हुए) — "remonstrate against" जितने specific नहीं हैं।

mock04.word: Identify the segment that contains an error: "They had been dancing / for an hour / when our dance teacher / had arrived."
mock04.explanation: "Had arrived" should be in simple past tense "arrived," since it marks the specific point that interrupted the ongoing action of dancing.

mock04.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: CHASTISE
mock04.explanation: "Chastise" (दंड देना/सुधारना) का अर्थ है to punish somebody physically। इसका synonym है "Discipline" (अनुशासित करना) — to train by using punishment to correct disobedience। "Order" (आदेश), "Sever ties" (संबंध तोड़ना), और "Chase" (पीछा करना) असंबंधित हैं।

mock04.word: So, clearly, death was something that the surviving Europeans had on their mind, and it is not surprising that they ______ an image to represent it.
mock04.explanation: "Conjured" (कल्पना से प्रकट करना) means to bring to mind or create through imagination, fitting the idea of inventing an image (the Grim Reaper) to represent death. "Desired" means wanted; "Forced" and "Compelled" mean to make someone do something against their will — none capture the act of imaginatively creating a symbol.

mock04.word: The ______ of his bed was suddenly cool.
mock04.explanation: Dampness (नमी/सीलन) describes moisture from rain seeping into the wall, causing the bed area to feel cool.

mock04.word: Choose the correctly spelt word:
mock04.explanation: "DODGE" (बचना/टालना) — correctly spelled। "BRILIANT" → correct: BRILLIANT (दो 'l')। "SIEZE" → correct: SEIZE (ei नहीं, ei के बाद c आती है)। "RECEPROCATE" → correct: RECIPROCATE। इसलिए DODGE सही है।

mock04.word: Identify the segment which contains the grammatical error. Electric heaters were providing to all the officers at the guest house.
mock04.explanation: This sentence is in simple past passive tense. The correct format is: Object + was/were + V3 (past participle). "Providing" must be replaced by "provided." Correct sentence: Electric heaters were provided to all the officers at the guest house.

mock04.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Last straw
options: The only option left after unsuccessful attempts
The final problem in a series of problems that makes the situation unbearable
mock04.explanation: "Last straw" (आखिरी सीमा) का अर्थ है the final problem that causes one to finally lose patience। Example: He told me that was the last straw and broke up with me.

mock04.word: ...they are subjected to harsh practices such as ______ and beating.
mock04.explanation: Poisoning (विषाक्तीकरण) refers to injury caused by toxic substances, matching the cruel practices mentioned alongside "beating."

mock04.word: Direction: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock04.explanation: "Pronounciation" is incorrectly spelt; the correct spelling is "Pronunciation" (उच्चारण), referring to the way a word is said. "Mischievous" means fond of causing trouble playfully, "Coincidence" means a remarkable concurrence of events, and "Homogeneous" means of the same kind — all correctly spelt.

दो errors हैं: (1) "But" गलत है — smiling और looking pleased contrast नहीं हैं, इसलिए "and" होना चाहिए। (2) "Looking" (present participle) past tense sentence में "looked" होना चाहिए। Correct sentence: "She was smiling and looked pleased about something."

mock04.word: Barriers ______ communication hinder the communication process.
mock04.explanation: "Barriers to" (के लिए बाधाएं) is the correct fixed collocation, meaning obstacles preventing communication from happening smoothly. "From" indicates origin; "Against" indicates opposition; "In" does not fit grammatically with "barriers" here.

mock04.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock04.explanation: The correct spelling is "Abstain" (परहेज करना), meaning to choose not to do or have something.

mock04.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word. I still cherish those little pranks and naughty tricks that we did during our school days.
mock04.explanation: Correct Answer: (C) impish. "Naughty" means playfully mischievous. "Impish" (शरारती) shares the exact same meaning.

mock04.word: Select the option that contains an error: "The news is very good to be true."
mock04.explanation: The correct phrase should be "too good to be true," indicating scepticism or doubt. "Very good" implies positive and believable, contradicting the intended meaning.

mock04.word: Portia claims that even if she … to live as long as Sibylla, she would die as … as Diana because she can only be claimed ______ the manner specified by her father's will.
mock04.explanation: The preposition "in" sets the precise condition under which Portia can be claimed, matching the legal context of being bound "in the manner specified by her father's will." "From" indicates a starting point; "Through" indicates means; "At" indicates a specific time — none fit describing a binding condition or manner.

mock04.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Pull yourself together
options: Move in a specified direction together
mock04.explanation: "Pull yourself together" means to maintain or regain your composure (संयम बनाए रखना), not to physically move together as the wording might suggest.

mock04.word: To shake off
mock04.explanation: "To shake off" means to free oneself from something unwanted — like shaking off a cold or a bad habit. It means to rid oneself of something (पीछा छुड़ाना).

mock04.word: 'I am sorry to ____ on you at this late hour.'
mock04.explanation: The context involves someone entering a space/calling on someone at an inconvenient hour, apologising for it. Interrupt means to stop something momentarily - used with events/speeches, not personal time. Instruct means to give formal directions - out of context. Interfere means to involve oneself unhelpfully - does not collocate with 'on'. Intrude on (phrasal verb) means to go into a place/situation where one is not expected - fits the meaning and collocates with 'on'. Key Rule: Always check for preposition collocations; 'intrude on' is a fixed phrase. Correct Sentence: I am sorry to intrude on you at this late hour.

mock04.word: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. The current state of the literature / on the efficacy under cognitive-behavioural therapy / for individuals with generalised anxiety disorder / suggests that it is a promising treatment option.
options: The current state of the literature
on the efficacy under cognitive-behavioural therapy
for individuals with generalised anxiety disorder
suggests that it is a promising treatment option.
mock04.explanation: The preposition "under" is incorrect here; the correct preposition is "of," since "of" commonly introduces prepositional phrases that complement nouns (noun + of + noun, e.g., "the capital of Peru"). The phrase should read "on the efficacy of cognitive-behavioural therapy."

mock04.word: He often / persists to ask / awkward questions / at the board meetings. Select the segment that has the error.
mock04.explanation: The preposition "in" is used after the verb "persists" — "persists in." If a verb follows a preposition, it must be in the "-ing" form: "persists in asking."

mock04.word: The tribals of India are ______ still a neglected lot.
mock04.explanation: "By and large" (सामान्यतः) means on the whole, considering everything. It fits since the sentence implies most tribals remain neglected.

mock04.word: The firelight danced on the walls and ______ strange shadows around the room.
mock04.explanation: "Cast shadows" (परछाइयाँ डालना) — यह fixed phrase है। "Firelight cast strange shadows" — standard literary expression। "Made," "Threw," और "Created" — सभी possible हैं पर "cast shadows" idiomatic है।

mock04.word: Identify the segment which contains a grammatical error: "She is not as smart as I think at first."
mock04.explanation: "I think" गलत है। "At first" past time को refer करता है, इसलिए "I thought" होना चाहिए। Correct sentence: "She is not as smart as I thought at first."

mock05.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Demerit
mock05.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) Virtue. "Demerit" (दोष) is a fault or flaw. "Virtue" (सद्गुण) is an advantageous quality and serves as its opposite here.

"Prohibited to carry" गलत है। सही phrase "prohibited from carrying" है — "prohibited from" + gerund का प्रयोग होता है। Correct sentence: "Passengers traveling by air are prohibited from carrying sharp objects in cabin baggage."

mock05.word: In fact, the deputy mayor's father ______ was sweeping the piazza in front of the church in preparation for market day.
mock05.explanation: "Worker" fits the context since the passage explicitly discusses the absence of manual workers and mentions that "the last worker retired six months ago." "Councillor," "member," and "mayor" refer to officials, not the manual labour role being discussed.

mock05.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice: "As was customary, the young prince was brought to the podium in a diamond studded carriage for the coronation ceremony."
options: As was customary, the young prince is bought to the podium...
As was customary, the young prince brought in a diamond studded carriage to the podium...
As was customary, they brought the young prince to the podium in a diamond studded carriage for the coronation ceremony.
As was customary, the palace staff brought the young prince to the podium in a diamond studded carriage for the coronation ceremony.
mock05.explanation: Passive voice में agent "palace staff" implied था। Active में: Subject (palace staff) + V2 (brought) + Object (young prince) + rest। Simple Past Passive → Simple Past Active। "Palace staff brought the young prince..."

mock05.word: The effects of human-caused global warming are ______ for people alive today.
mock05.explanation: "Irreversible" का अर्थ है cannot be changed or undone। Global warming के effects अपरिवर्तनीय हैं।

mock05.word: ...but further out he could see a darker belt of ______ water.
mock05.explanation: "Open water" (खुला पानी) — ice के बाहर unfrozen, खुला पानी। Ice harbor के अंदर था, बाहर open water था। "Closed" (बंद — opposite), "Dried" (सूखा), और "Wet" (गीला — redundant for water) गलत हैं।

mock05.word: The scientist says that while the complete core meltdown at Chernobyl was a major disaster, it had fall far short of catastrophe many nuclear power critics had feared.
options: it fell much short of the catastrophe
it fell far away of the catastrophe
it fell far short of the catastrophe
it fell far shorter of the catastrophe
mock05.explanation: Correct Answer: (C) it fell far short of the catastrophe. "Fell far short of" is the correct past-tense idiomatic phrase, meaning it failed to meet a certain level or expectation.

mock05.word: In question, four alternatives are given for the idiom underlined in the sentence. Mr. Avinash is very nice and friendly, but his brother is very toffee-nosed.
options: A person who thinks he is intelligent and perfect
A person who never trusts anyone
A person who thinks he is of a high social class and looks down on people of lower class
A person who is always angry
mock05.explanation: Correct Answer: (C) A person who thinks he is of a high social class and looks down on people of lower class. "Toffee-nosed" (घमंडी) is an informal British idiom used to describe someone who is snobbish and believes they are socially superior to others.

mock05.word: We had to undergo a great many difficulties.
mock05.explanation: The sentence is correct. "A great many + plural noun" is a standard English expression meaning "a large number of." No change is needed.

mock05.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: BOLSTER
mock05.explanation: Bolster means to support or improve something, or make it stronger (e.g. She tried to bolster my confidence). Strengthen means to make or become stronger - the most direct synonym. Embolden means to make someone brave/confident - close, but bolster is broader (can strengthen any system, argument, or position, not just confidence). Weaken and Discourage are antonyms of bolster. Synonyms for Bolster: strengthen, support, reinforce, boost, uphold, buttress. Antonyms: weaken, undermine, discourage, obstruct.

mock05.word: Parts of a sentence about post-WWII Europe are given in jumbled order. Arrange them correctly. O: that the Soviet army had liberated P: from the fascist forces came under Q: the east European countries R: the control of the USSR S: after the Second World War
mock05.explanation: S sets the timeframe; Q introduces the subject countries; O describes their liberation; P explains what happened next; R concludes with control by the USSR.

mock05.word: In 1927 he got his wish, ______ a deal with Universal to produce a new series based on the adventures of a character called Oswald the Lucky Rabbit.
mock05.explanation: "Striking a deal" (समझौता करना) is a common fixed phrase meaning to reach an agreement, fitting the context of Disney entering a business partnership with Universal. "Crossing" refers to going across; "Bewitching" means enchanting; "Possessing" means owning — none fit the act of finalizing a business agreement.

mock05.word: complete free, equitable and quality primary and secondary education ______ to relevant and effective learning outcomes.
mock05.explanation: "Leading to" is a participial phrase modifying "education." It correctly shows that the education should lead to relevant learning outcomes without changing the sentence's tense.

mock05.word: Select the correct direct form: "The ticket collector asked the passenger why he had not purchased a full ticket for his daughter."
options: "Why haven't you purchase a full ticket for your daughter?"
"Why didn't you purchase a full ticket for your daughter?"
"Why haven't you purchased a full ticket for his daughter?"
"Why he had not purchased a full ticket for his daughter?"
mock05.explanation: Indirect → Direct: (1) "asked" → "said to" + question marks। (2) "had not purchased" → "didn't purchase" (past perfect → simple past में)। (3) "his" → "your"। (4) Direct question word order। Correct: "Why didn't you purchase a full ticket for your daughter?"

mock05.word: Identify the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Fathomable
mock05.explanation: "Fathomable" (समझने योग्य) means something that can be understood. Its antonym is "Incoherent" (असंगत) — meaning unclear and difficult to understand. "Inauspicious" (अशुभ) means not promising.

mock05.word: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange them correctly. (a) Freedom of thought and expression are fundamental human rights. (b) There are two important human rights. (c) Through good deeds, one can gain dignity. (d) Everyone has the inherent right to be treated with dignity and courtesy at all times.
mock05.explanation: (d) introduces dignity → (c) expands on earning it → (b) transitions to human rights generally → (a) specifies those rights.

mock05.word: Select the option that will improve the underlined part. His mouth left open as he realise the truth.
options: was felt opened as he realise
mock05.explanation: "left open" गलत है — "fell open" (खुल गया) सही idiom है। "realise" की जगह "perceived" (past tense) होना चाहिए। Correct Sentence: His mouth fell open as he perceived the truth.

mock05.word: Proboscis helps ______ the juices of the flowers.
mock05.explanation: "To drink" (पीना) correctly describes the action of consuming liquid through the proboscis; gulp, eat, and gobble are less precise for sucking liquid.

mock05.word: In order to attract more tourists, ______ resorts are built which take neither the local architecture … the ecology into consideration.
mock05.explanation: "Sprawling" (फैला हुआ) means spreading out untidily over a large area, fitting the description of resorts built carelessly across the landscape. "Scheming" relates to secret planning; "Stretching" relates to elasticity — neither fits describing built structures, while "sprawling resorts" is a standard, natural collocation.

mock05.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. SHOVE
mock05.explanation: Shove (धक्का देना) means to push someone or something with force. Poke (ठोकना/धक्का) means a quick, sharp push or prod — same meaning. Halt means to bring to an abrupt stop. Pull means to exert force toward oneself. Shout means to utter a loud cry.

mock05.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences about the God of Food. Select the correct order. A. It is also very sad that ill-treatment is meted out to one of your gifts. B. "I can't take it anymore," he complained. C. An ancient story tells us that the God of Food came before the Divine Presence, battered, exhausted, and downcast. D. The Lord smiled and said to him, "Those who abuse you and indulge in excess are destroying themselves."
mock05.explanation: C introduces the God of Food's arrival; B explains his complaint; A elaborates on the ill-treatment; D concludes with the Lord's response.

mock05.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of highlighted word: UTILITARIAN
mock05.explanation: "Utilitarian" (व्यावहारिक) का अर्थ है designed to be useful or practical rather than attractive। इसका synonym है "Functional" (प्रायोगिक) — having a special activity or purpose। "Fanciful" (कल्पनाशील), "Distasteful" (अरुचिकर), और "Necessary" (आवश्यक) भिन्न अर्थ रखते हैं।

mock05.word: If substitution is not required select 'No improvement.' To fight on the battlefield for the sake of one's country needs a great strongness.
mock05.explanation: "A great strongness" is not a standard English phrase; the correct word is "courage" (साहस), meaning the ability to do something that frightens one, which fits fighting for one's country far better than physical "strength."

mock05.word: Only an informed and educated citizen can make ______ choices and undertake research and development.
mock05.explanation: "Intelligent" (बुद्धिमान) is the only grammatically and contextually fitting adjective describing the type of choices an educated citizen makes.

mock05.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Loose cannon
options: Someone who is enthusiastic and eager
Someone who is unpredictable and liable to cause problems
mock05.explanation: "Loose cannon" (अनियंत्रित व्यक्ति) describes someone unpredictable who is likely to cause unintended problems or damage.

mock06.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech. Mr. Collin said, "Let me deliver the letters to the postman."
options: Mr. Collin said that let he be allowed to deliver the letters to the postman.
Mr. Collin requested that he might be allowed to deliver the letters to the postman.
Mr. Collin suggested with joy that he wished to deliver the letters to the postman.
Mr. Collin asked if he was allowed to deliver the letters to the postman.
mock06.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) Mr. Collin requested that he might be allowed to deliver the letters to the postman. When direct speech starts with "Let me," expressing a request, the standard indirect speech conversion uses "requested that he might be allowed to."

mock06.word: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error. I had to hold on to the wall to steady itself or I could have fallen.
options: I had to hold on to the wall
mock06.explanation: Reflexive pronouns must match the subject. The subject is "I," so the correct reflexive pronoun is "myself," not "itself."

mock06.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom in the given sentence. On the face of it, nothing seems to be fishy about the matter.
mock06.explanation: "On the face of it" (सतही तौर पर) means seemingly, based on available evidence, or what appears on the surface, without deeper investigation.

mock06.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment which contains a grammatical error. "Do you want to join us for lunch?" / "I'm sorry" / "my physics class will start at 1 p.m." / "and don't finish till 3 p.m."
options: Do you want to join us for lunch?
my physics class will start at 1 p.m
mock06.explanation: This is a subject-verb agreement error. The subject of this segment is "my physics class," which is singular (it refers to one specific class). However, the verb used is "don't," which is the contraction of "do not" — a plural form. A singular subject requires a singular verb, so the correct contraction here is "doesn't" (does not). The corrected sentence reads: "I'm sorry, my physics class will start at 1 p.m. and doesn't finish till 3 p.m." Remember: in English, the rule is that singular subjects pair with singular verbs and plural subjects pair with plural verbs — always check the actual grammatical subject of the clause, not just the noun that happens to sit closest to the verb.

mock06.word: Sentences of a paragraph — S1: He sat in his seat. A. hardly showing any signs of hearing. B. beside the bed, C. gazing sternly. D. At the patient's face. S6: what they were saying to him.
mock06.explanation: B — location (bed के पास)। C — action (sternly देखना)। D — target (patient का face)। A — non-response।

mock06.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. "Amrit felt a chill run down his spine as Mrityu gazed at him mid-conversation."
mock06.explanation: "Gazed" (टकटकी से देखा) means to look steadily and intently for a long time. The antonym is "Glanced" (एक नज़र डाला) — meaning to take a brief or hurried look.

mock06.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute. By killing a large number of people, they thought they would get more benefits.
mock06.explanation: "Genocide" का अर्थ है deliberate killing of a large group of people। Example: He may be charged with genocide.

mock06.word: He would often lock himself ______ in his room.
mock06.explanation: "Lock himself up" is the correct idiomatic phrase meaning to confine oneself, matching his isolated state.

mock06.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock06.explanation: "Negociate" is incorrectly spelt; the correct spelling is "Negotiate" (बातचीत करना). "Nuisance" means an annoyance, "Negligible" (नगण्य) means insignificant, and "Necessary" means essential — all correctly spelt.

mock06.word: ...British people can collect in crowds ______ do not turn into mobs.
mock06.explanation: "Which" — relative pronoun जो animals और things को refer करता है। यहाँ "crowds" (noun) को refer किया जा रहा है, people को नहीं। "Who" और "Whom" persons के लिए हैं। "That" भी हो सकता था लेकिन crowds जैसी collective noun के लिए "which" अधिक appropriate है।

mock06.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word: DEVOUT
mock06.explanation: "Devout" (धर्मनिष्ठ) का अर्थ है deep religious commitment रखने वाला। इसका antonym है "Passive" (निष्क्रिय) — accepting things without active response. "Proficient" (कुशल), "Profound" (गहरा), और "Pure" (शुद्ध) इससे सम्बंधित नहीं हैं।

mock06.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Out of the given options pick the one that gives their correct order. A. Customs officials in Thailand say it's the biggest seizure in the country's history. B. The elephant tusks were tracked from the Democratic Republic of Congo for two months. C. Four tonnes of ivory, with a market value of $6 million - it was an impressive haul. D. Officials say they were being transported to Laos, from where they believed the ivory would be sold to customers across Asia.
mock06.explanation: C introduces the topic independently (the haul of ivory), B follows by describing how Thai customs assessed its significance, A continues with details of how the tusks were tracked, and D concludes by explaining where the ivory was being transported and why, forming a logical news narrative.

mock06.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom: NECK OF THE WOODS
mock06.explanation: "Neck of the woods" (आस-पड़ोस) — इसका अर्थ है a specific area or neighbourhood। "Barren land" (बंजर ज़मीन), "Valley" (घाटी), और "Highways" (राजमार्ग) गलत हैं।

mock06.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Silver bullet
options: A very expensive solution used only as the last measure
A simple and seemingly magical solution to a complicated problem
A solution which is worse than the problem
A thing of beauty but which is actually deadly
mock06.explanation: "Silver bullet" (जादुई समाधान) का अर्थ है an easy solution to a difficult problem। Example: There was no silver bullet that could have prevented the atrocities.

mock06.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word with reference to its usage in the sentence. The scientist's hypothesis was substantiated by extensive research and experimentation.
mock06.explanation: The target word "Pervasive" means widespread or existing everywhere. "Extensive" (विस्तृत) means covering a large area or being comprehensive, which is the closest match. Substantiated means proven by evidence; Experimentation refers to testing; Hypothesis is a proposed explanation — none mean widespread.

mock06.word: Select the word that is opposite in meaning (ANTONYM) to the word given below. Polarize
mock06.explanation: Polarize (ध्रुवीकरण करना) means to divide or cause to divide into two sharply contrasting groups or sets of opinions. Combine (मिलाना) means to unite or join together — exact opposite of dividing. Orchestrate means to arrange elements to produce a desired effect. Conjoin also means to join but is closer in meaning. Segregate is closer in meaning to polarize, not opposite.

mock06.word: ...the learning that a person ______ is incomplete.
mock06.explanation: "The learning that a person gains" is the natural collocation (शब्द-युग्म) describing acquired knowledge.

mock06.word: ...rich and poor, ______ shoulders with each other.
mock06.explanation: "Rub shoulders with" (मिलना-जुलना) — इस idiom का अर्थ है to meet and talk with someone in a friendly way। Example: "The awards dinner gave me the opportunity to rub shoulders with some great poets." "Join," "Pat," और "Hit" इस idiom में नहीं आते।

mock06.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word. The clairvoyant witches told Macbeth his future.
mock06.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) Prophetic. "Clairvoyant" (भविष्य देखने वाला) refers to having the ability to perceive events in the future. "Prophetic" is the closest synonym.

mock06.word: The strong Indian batting line-up will _____ a big worry for the visiting cricket team.
mock06.explanation: "Pose" (प्रस्तुत करना) is used in fixed phrases like "pose a threat/worry/risk." It means to cause or present a problem. No other option fits this idiomatic usage.

mock06.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute. One who is extravagantly romantic, chivalrous and impractical.
mock06.explanation: Quixotic (काल्पनिक/अव्यावहारिक) describes someone who is impractically idealistic and romantic, derived from the character Don Quixote.

mock06.word: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence: "Unless you carry a Covid-19 negative report, you can travel by air."
mock06.explanation: "Unless" (जब तक नहीं) यहाँ grammatically सही है — "Unless you carry a negative report" का अर्थ है "if you do not carry a negative report." Sentence correct है।

mock06.word: The biggest ______ canyon is over 350 km long.
mock06.explanation: "Such" (इस प्रकार का) — previously mentioned type को refer करता है। Context में deep crevices वाले canyons का example दिया जा रहा है। "One" (एक), "Like" (जैसा), और "So" (इतना) यहाँ fit नहीं होते।

mock06.word: Choose the correctly spelt word.
mock06.explanation: "VACILLATE" (हिचकिचाना) means to waver or hesitate, and is correctly spelt. The correct spellings of the others are "STILETTOS" (a type of high-heeled shoe), "OBSEQUIOUS" (obedient or submissive), and "MAINTENANCE" (the act of preserving something).

mock06.word: Select the option that will improve the underlined part. I knew very little Spanish when I came here, but I have been learning and can now make myself understood.
options: as soon as I came here, but
mock06.explanation: वाक्य व्याकरणिक रूप से सही है। "when I came here" — simple past tense का सही प्रयोग है।

mock07.word: She knew that to apologize would be ______ to admitting she had failed.
mock07.explanation: "Tantamount" (के बराबर) — having the same effect as something। "Tantamount to admitting failure" — apologizing = failure admit करना। "Tandem" (जोड़ी में), "Tangible" (ठोस/मूर्त), और "Tantalizing" (ललचाने वाला) गलत हैं।

mock07.word: Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. "Though the / global financial crisis, / China's economy continues / to grow at an impressive rate."
options: To grow at an impressive rate
mock07.explanation: "Though" must be followed by a complete clause (subject + verb). The correct preposition here is "Despite" (के बावजूद), which can directly precede a noun phrase.

mock07.word: 'A stitch in time saves nine!' This wise saying suggests that an (1) action taken on time to rectify an error rules ______ the possibility of accumulation of such errors and future ….
mock07.explanation: "Rule out" (बाहर रखना/हटा देना) is a phrasal verb meaning to exclude or eliminate, fitting the context of timely action eliminating the possibility of accumulated errors and damage. The other prepositions do not form a valid phrasal verb with "rule" here.

mock07.word: Select the meaningful and correctly spelt word to fill in the blank: "The colourful procession was the last item to be performed in the ___."
mock07.explanation: Pageantry (भव्य प्रदर्शन) means a spectacular display, especially in a public ceremony, fitting the description of the colourful procession.
"No fewer than" (कम से कम) is the correct phrase for countable nouns like "pedestrians," meaning at least that number.

mock07.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word: Affliction
mock07.explanation: Affliction means a cause of pain, distress, or suffering (e.g. Poverty is one of the greatest afflictions of our time). Consolation means something/someone that makes you feel better when you are sad or disappointed - the direct opposite. Predicament means a difficult/unpleasant situation - similar to affliction, not its antonym. Mortgage is a financial term - unrelated. Jeopardy means danger/risk of harm - similar to affliction in effect. Antonym Pair: Affliction ↔ Consolation / Relief / Comfort / Solace.

mock07.word: Select the most appropriate synonym. COVE
mock07.explanation: "Cove" means a small sheltered bay. "Bay" (खाड़ी) means a broad inlet of the sea — both refer to a body of water enclosed by land, making them synonyms.

mock07.word: Fill in the blank with the most suitable word. ___ on the silver tray like a white snake, the wedding garland was as thick as my forearm.
mock07.explanation: "Coiled" (कुंडलित) means wound or twisted in a spiral — exactly like a snake would rest on a tray. "Repeated" (दोहराया) means done again. "Circulated" (प्रसारित) means moved through a system. "Bartered" (वस्तु-विनिमय) means exchanged without money. Correct sentence: Coiled on the silver tray like a white snake, the wedding garland was as thick as my forearm.

mock07.word: The knowledge and abilities that students are expected to master as they progress through the four years of high school are referred to as the high school curriculum.
mock07.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) advance through. "Advance through" accurately describes forward movement and progression through an academic curriculum.

mock07.word: Select the antonym of the given word: TACITURN
mock07.explanation: "Taciturn" (अल्पभाषी) का अर्थ है कम बोलने वाला, reserved व्यक्ति। इसका antonym है "Loquacious" (बातूनी) — a person who talks a great deal. "Laconic" (संक्षिप्त) और "Reticent" (मितभाषी) taciturn के synonyms हैं। "Withdrawn" (अंतर्मुखी) भी similar meaning रखता है।

mock07.word: Select the wrongly spelt word among the given options related to "Satellite".
mock07.explanation: "Satellite" (उपग्रह) is the correct spelling. If it appeared misspelt in the original, it is the wrongly spelt word. "Session," "Satiate," and "Settle" are correctly spelt.

mock07.word: In the sentence identify the segment which contains the grammatical error. Deepa walked down the road slowly without looking anybody.
mock07.explanation: The verb "look" requires the preposition "at" when referring to directing one's gaze toward someone, so the phrase should be "looking at anybody," not just "looking anybody."

mock07.word: kick at their ______ with their clawed hind feet.
mock07.explanation: "Adversary" (शत्रु) means an opponent or attacker, fitting the context of a predator being kicked away.

mock07.word: Drop me a message when the food will be ready. Select the correct substitution.
mock07.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) the food is ready. In time clauses starting with "when," "until," or "as soon as," the present simple tense is used to refer to future events. Therefore, "the food is ready" is correct.

mock07.word: Sentences of a paragraph about a stick are given in jumbled order. Arrange them correctly. A. I stood upon the hearth-rug and picked up the stick which our visitor had left behind him. B. Moreover, it was just such a stick as Mortimer, the old-fashioned family lawyer used to carry. C. It was a fine, thick piece of wood, bulbous-headed, of the sort which is known as a "Penang lawyer." D. "Tell me, Watson, what do you make of our visitor's stick?" I asked.
mock07.explanation: A introduces picking up the stick; C describes its physical appearance; B adds further description using "moreover"; D concludes with someone asking about the stick.

mock07.word: A. | once met a gentleman who told me about a game he plays with his grandchildren. B. He shares a titbit or two and it starts off a conversation. C. He then asks his teenage grandchildren if they know who those people are. D. He picks some names of politicians, rock stars or even criminals from his generation.
mock07.explanation: A — gentleman का introduction। D — game क्या है। C — grandchildren से पूछना। B — conversation शुरू होना।

mock07.word: To my utter ______, the little dark boy was striding up and down the lawn.
mock07.explanation: "Astonishment" (आश्चर्य) fits "to my utter astonishment," expressing great surprise at the scene witnessed.

mock07.word: The ______ of the knife in his hand made her sick.
mock07.explanation: "Sight" का अर्थ है a view या glimpse। यहाँ knife को देखना context है। Correct Sentence: The sight of the knife in his hand made her sick.

mock07.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Paragon
mock07.explanation: Paragon (परम उदाहरण) means a perfect example of something or someone. Epitome (प्रतीक/सर्वोत्तम उदाहरण) means a perfect example of a particular quality or type — same meaning. Transient (क्षणिक) means temporary. Associate (सहयोगी) means to connect or link. Grumpy (चिड़चिड़ा) means bad-tempered.

mock07.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Not informed about or aware of something.
mock07.explanation: Oblivious (अनजान/बेखबर) correctly means not aware or informed about something happening around you. Uneducated refers to lack of formal schooling. Absent means not present. Inconscient means not awake or aware of one's environment — slightly different context.

mock07.word: Women must be ______ equal rights.
mock07.explanation: "Accorded" (प्रदान किया गया) means to be given or granted something officially. "Women must be accorded equal rights" is the most formal and appropriate phrasing here.

mock07.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Sumptuous
mock07.explanation: Sumptuous (शानदार, महंगा) means luxurious or splendid, often implying expense. "Inexpensive" (सस्ता) means low in cost, the clear opposite. Unremitting means continuous; Lucrative means profitable; Delectable (स्वादिष्ट) means highly enjoyable — none oppose "luxurious."

mock07.word: Identify the segment with the grammatical error: "Notwithstanding we were / all busy that weekend / we had to / cancel the outing."
mock07.explanation: "Notwithstanding" is incorrect here; "as" should be used to express reason, giving "As we were all busy."

mock07.word: Choose the incorrectly spelt word.
mock07.explanation: "PERCIEVE" is incorrectly spelt; the correct spelling is "PERCEIVE," meaning to become aware of something through the senses. "VISCOUS" means thick and sticky, "BENEVOLENT" (दयालु) means well-meaning, and "BOISTEROUS" means energetic and noisy — all correctly spelt.

mock07.word: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the parts in the correct order to form a meaningful sentence. (A) between the two countries (B) will inevitably count (C) the recent political incident (D) against the peace process
mock07.explanation: Part C introduces the main subject (the political incident), Part A adds context about the incident's location, Part B introduces the verb phrase about its impact, and Part D completes the sentence by specifying the nature of that impact, giving: "The recent political incident between the two countries will inevitably count against the peace process."

mock08.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words: 'In a way that is done with great energy and enthusiasm' (heartily)
mock08.explanation: Heartily means with great energy, enthusiasm, and sincerity - the correct answer. Feelingly means with deep and sincere emotion - more solemn/emotional than enthusiastic. Earnestly means in a serious and determined way, often without humour - too serious, lacks enthusiasm. Encouragingly means in a way that gives confidence or hope - a different meaning. Context: The phrase 'great energy and enthusiasm' is the key clue - only heartily captures both energy and enthusiasm together.

mock08.word: Select the grammatically correct sentence among the options describing a discussion:
options: The open discussion with the media yesterday on the new policies of the government was an excellent one.
An open discussion on the new policies of a government with a media yesterday was an excellent one.
A open discussion on the new policies of the government with the media yesterday was a excellent one.
An open discussion on the new policies of a new government with media yesterday was an excellent one.
mock08.explanation: Correct Answer: (A). This option correctly uses the definite article "the" for the specific discussion and accurately pairs the vowel sound of "excellent" with the article "an."

mock08.word: Once, Hercules ______ to hold it for Atlas while the latter went looking for golden apples.
mock08.explanation: "Offered" (पेशकश की) means to express willingness to do something for someone, fitting the context that Hercules volunteered to hold the sky for Atlas. "Ordered" implies giving a command to someone else; "Afforded" relates to being able to bear a cost; "Extended" does not pair correctly with "to" in this construction.

mock08.word: Sentences of a paragraph (1 and 6 fixed), arrange A-D: (1) During the colonial rule in India, Robert Bruce was the first who noticed tea plants growing near Rangpur in 1823. A. The species was found to be similar to that grown in Chinese tea gardens. B. By 1862, the Assam tea industry comprised around 160 gardens. C. The leaves of the plants were examined and the consequence was something fruitful. D. Mr. Maniram Dewan was the first Indian tea planter... (6) Today the region has grown to become one of the world's largest tea-producing belts.
mock08.explanation: 1 → C (leaves examined) → A (species identified similar to Chinese tea) → D (Maniram Dewan established commercial plantation) → B (160 gardens by 1862) → 6. Chronological and logical flow. Correct order: CADB

mock08.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined group of words: "The fishermen of our village are constantly complaining of the poor number of fishes getting caught these days."
mock08.explanation: "Haul" (बड़ी मात्रा में पकड़ा गया) — a large amount caught, collected, or stolen। "Poor haul" — कम मात्रा में मछलियाँ पकड़ना। "Horde" (बड़ा समूह), "Heap" (ढेर), और "Host" (बड़ा समूह) इस specific fishing context में गलत हैं।

mock08.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Slack off
mock08.explanation: "Slack off" (ढिलाई बरतना) means to reduce or decrease one's effort or activity, often used in the context of work or study, signifying a reduction in workload or pressure. "To make most effort" and "to be very rude" are unrelated, and "to understand a hidden meaning" does not match this idiom.

mock08.word: Posters are ______ to promote a brand or pass important information.
mock08.explanation: "Posters are used to promote" — passive construction में "used" सही है।

mock08.word: He explained that he knew the place well because he used to live there. (Change to Direct Speech)
options: "I know the place well because I use to live here," he explained
"I know the place well because I used to live here," he explained
"I knew the place because I used to live here," he explained
"I have known the place well because I used to live here," he explained
mock08.explanation: Indirect से Direct में बदलते समय "knew" → "know" (simple past → simple present) हो जाता है। "he" → "I" में बदलता है। "there" → "here" होता है।

mock08.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word: RIFE
mock08.explanation: "Rife" (व्यापक/प्रचलित) का अर्थ है widespread, abundant। इसका antonym है "Scarce" (दुर्लभ/अपर्याप्त) — insufficient for the demand। "Ripe" (पका हुआ), "Popular" (लोकप्रिय), और "Serene" (शांत) इससे असंबंधित हैं।

mock08.word: Don't let the failure / demotivate you, as / failures are the / stepping stones to success. Identify the segment with the error.
mock08.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) Don't let the failure. "Failure" is used here in a general, abstract sense (भाववाचक अर्थ). Therefore, the definite article "the" is unnecessary and should be removed.

mock08.word: after thousands of hectares of fish enclosures were made ______, free
mock08.explanation: "Encroachment" (अतिक्रमण) means illegal occupation of land/water. Fish enclosures were removed after encroachment was cleared, giving dolphins unobstructed space.

mock08.word: Select the word that is closest in meaning to the word given below. Whimsical
mock08.explanation: Whimsical (तरंगी, सनकी) means playful, fanciful or quirky. "Funny" conveys a similar sense of amusement and lightheartedness, so it is the closest synonym. Weak means lacking strength; Respectful means showing esteem; Humiliated (अपमानित) means embarrassed — none match the playful sense of "whimsical."

mock08.word: In the given sentence, one word may have been incorrectly spelt. Select the correctly spelt word from the given alternatives. If there is no error, select 'No error.' The staff members were attempting to manoeuvre the board of directors for the abolition of pay disparity.
mock08.explanation: The spelling "manoeuvre" in the original sentence is already correct. It means a movement or series of moves requiring skill and care. (Hindi: चाल / युक्ति) Example: Snowboarders performed daring manoeuvres on precipitous slopes. Note: The American English spelling is "maneuver" but British/SSC standard uses "manoeuvre." All three alternative options given are misspelt.

mock08.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the bracketed word "[deface]."
mock08.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) build. "Deface" (बिगाड़ना / बदसूरत करना) means to spoil the surface or appearance of something. Its opposite is to construct or "build."

mock08.word: Select the misspelt word.
mock08.explanation: The correct spelling is "Exhilarated" (उत्साहित/प्रसन्न), meaning feeling very happy, animated, or elated.

mock08.word: A ______ of small stones is called a mosaic.
mock08.explanation: Pattern (नमूना) means a repeated decorative design, which matches the description of a mosaic made of arranged stones.

mock08.word: The coronavirus pandemic has brought most of the US to ______ standstill.
mock08.explanation: "A" is used before non-specific singular countable nouns like "standstill," making it grammatically correct here.

mock08.word: ...drop ______ in a desk drawer.
mock08.explanation: "Them" — "papers" (plural) को refer करता है। "She took the papers and dropped them in a desk drawer." "It" (singular), "Those" और "These" (demonstrative pronouns) यहाँ less appropriate हैं।

mock08.word: Select the most appropriate idiomatic expression that can substitute the underlined segment. Today's managers' meeting has given me a lot to think about as I decide whether or not to stay with this company.
mock08.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) food for thought. "Food for thought" (विचार करने योग्य बात) means something that warrants serious consideration, matching the underlined meaning exactly.
"Had been doing" (past perfect continuous) correctly shows an ongoing action before Ravindra fell ill, matching the context of returning to previous work.

mock08.word: Fill in the blank with the most suitable word: "One may ______ what he only holds in trust; he shares what is his own."
mock08.explanation: "Allocate" (वितरित करना) — to distribute something that belongs to others। Trust में रखी चीज़ को distribute करना। "Appropriate" (उचित — adjective), "Appellate" (अपील संबंधी), और "Apply" (लागू करना) इस context में गलत हैं।

mock08.word: Parts of the given sentence are given as options. Identify the part that contains a grammatical error: "As long as there was corruption in India, there can be no progress."
mock08.explanation: यह sentence present condition के बारे में बात करती है। "Was" (past tense) गलत है; "is" (present tense) होना चाहिए। Correct sentence: "As long as there is corruption in India, there can be no progress."

mock08.word: Select the word that is OPPOSITE in meaning to the underlined word: People with generous hearts can see the intrinsic value in something which looks worthless to others.
mock08.explanation: Intrinsic (आंतरिक/स्वाभाविक) means belonging to something as part of its nature; inherent. Extraneous (बाहरी/असंबंधित) means of external origin — opposite of intrinsic. Impertinent means not showing respect. Genuine means that can be trusted. Unrelated means not connected in any way.

mock08.word: Select the sentences that contain no spelling errors regarding "eucalyptus."
mock08.explanation: The correct spelling is "eucalyptus" (एक प्रकार का पेड़), referring to a fast-growing evergreen tree introduced widely across regions.

mock08.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. WREAK
mock08.explanation: Wreak (कारण बनना) means to cause a large amount of damage or harm, as in "wreak havoc." Cause closely matches this meaning, while fail, neglect, and forget convey different ideas.

mock09.word: Select the most appropriate 'one word' for the expression given below. Fear of heights.
mock09.explanation: Acrophobia (ऊंचाई का भय) is the specific fear of heights, derived from Greek "akron" (edge/peak) and "phobos" (fear). Agoraphobia is fear of open or crowded spaces. Claustrophobia is fear of enclosed spaces. Homophobia relates to negative attitudes toward homosexuality.

mock09.word: I should have stopped them then and there. But it never even _________ to me that I should.
mock09.explanation: Occurred (दिमाग में आया) means "come into the mind of" — "it never even occurred to me" is a fixed expression.

mock09.word: Fill in the blank with the correct option. While I was in the mall, I ___ a friend whom I hadn't met for five years.
mock09.explanation: "Ran into" (अचानक मिल जाना) means to meet someone by chance, perfectly fitting the context of unexpectedly meeting an old friend. "Ran off" means to escape; "Run down" means to reduce in size; "Ran up" refers to a period preceding an event — none mean "to meet by chance."

mock09.word: Direction: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the given sentence. The ruins of this building do tell us the story of a defunct organisation.
mock09.explanation: Defunct means no longer existing or functioning. "Obsolete" (अप्रचलित, पुराना) carries the same meaning of being no longer in use. Ancient means belonging to the distant past; Contemporary means belonging to the present; Prominent means important or famous — none convey "no longer functioning."

mock09.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Be my guest
mock09.explanation: "Be my guest" (कृपया करें) is a polite way of giving someone permission to do something, or to invite them to do something as if they were a welcome guest.

mock09.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute. A person who likes to argue about anything
mock09.explanation: "Contentious" का अर्थ है a person who is prone to causing disputes or arguments। Example: John is known to be contentious.

mock09.word: The man who / was committed / the theft last night / was caught this evening.
mock09.explanation: "was committed" (passive voice) यहाँ गलत है। सही शब्द "committed" होगा क्योंकि वाक्य में active voice चाहिए। Correct Sentence: The man who committed the theft last night was caught this evening.

mock09.word: The councillor (said that, "We plan to make the state a safer place for) everyone.'
options: said, "We planned to make the state a safe place for
said, "We plan to make the state a safer place for
said that, "We are planning to make the state a safe place for
