mock09.explanation: "That" is never used after "said" in direct speech. So options C and D are eliminated. The sentence implies a comparison between the present and a better future state, so the comparative degree "safer" is correct over "safe."

mock09.word: Identify the segment which contains the grammatical error: 'Not complying by any of the laws can land you into serious trouble.'
mock09.explanation: The error is the use of the preposition 'by' after the verb 'comply.' Grammar Rule: The verb 'comply' is always followed by the preposition 'with,' not 'by.' 'Comply with' means to act in accordance with a rule, law, or wish. 'By' is used for proximity, method, or agent - it cannot follow 'comply' in this context. Fixed Collocation: comply with (not 'by', not 'to', not 'for'). Correct Sentence: Not complying with any of the laws can land you into serious trouble.

mock09.word: an overarching programme has been, therefore, ______.
mock09.explanation: The sentence uses passive voice in present perfect tense: "has been prepared" (तैयार किया गया है). "Prepared" as past participle correctly completes this structure.

mock09.word: Ask or beg for something earnestly (ईमानदारी से) or humbly (विनम्रतापूर्वक)
mock09.explanation: "Supplicate" का अर्थ है to ask for something in a humble way। Example: I knew I could not go on supplicating for more.

mock09.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Jump on the bandwagon
options: To ride in a vehicle with others
Get involved in an activity because it is likely to succeed
mock09.explanation: This idiom means joining a growing movement once it seems likely to become successful (सफल होते दिख रहे काम में शामिल होना).

mock09.word: Select the correct active form of the given sentence. Let the fire be kept burning at night.
options: The fire should be burnt at night.
You should keep the fire burning at night.
Keep the fire burning at night.
The fire was kept burning at night.
mock09.explanation: The passive sentence implies an action that should be actively maintained. The active form is a direct imperative command: "Keep the fire burning at night."

mock09.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Cease
mock09.explanation: Cease (बंद करना) means to stop or come to an end. Continuation, meaning carrying something on over time, is the opposite of stopping.

mock09.word: He was a kind man and wanted to join a ______ circus.
mock09.explanation: "Travelling circus" is the standard, idiomatic phrase for a circus that tours from place to place.

mock09.word: 'Get me a banana!' The little boy ran … the house and ______ shortly.
mock09.explanation: "Reappeared" (पुनः प्रकट हुआ) fits the context of the boy returning after running into the house.

mock09.word: Select the wrongly spelt word among the given options related to "gobble".
mock09.explanation: The correct spelling is "gobble" (जल्दी-जल्दी खाना). If it appeared as "goble" or any misspelt variant, that is the wrongly spelt word. "Glory," "goggles," and "global" are correctly spelt.

mock09.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word. It is irrelevance, at least to me, whether the grant was (improvident — अदूरदर्शी) or not.
mock09.explanation: "Improvident" का अर्थ है lacking foresight, spendthrift। इसका antonym "Thrifty" (careful in managing resources — मितव्ययी) है।

mock09.word: Choose the word that means the same as the given word: VACILLATE
mock09.explanation: "Vacillate" (डांवाडोल होना) का अर्थ है to be unable to decide, often changing mind back and forth। इसका synonym है "Hesitate" (झिझकना) — to pause before doing or saying something due to uncertainty। "Advance" (आगे बढ़ना), "Stir" (हिलाना), और "Plunge" (डुबकी लगाना) असंबंधित हैं।

mock09.word: A word has been written in four different ways. Select the CORRECTLY spelt word.
mock09.explanation: The correct spelling is L-I-A-I-S-O-N. Liaison means communication or cooperation that facilitates a close working relationship between people or organisations. (Hindi: समन्वय / संपर्क) Example: The strong liaison between parents and teachers is based on the students' best interests. Memory tip: Note the double vowel pattern — LI-AI-SON.

options: on if we shall be moving
mock09.explanation: दो errors हैं: (1) "If" की जगह "whether" होना चाहिए — doubt या alternatives express करने के लिए "whether" प्रयुक्त होता है। (2) Past tense sentence में "shall" की जगह "should" होना चाहिए। Correct sentence: "There was an argument about whether we should move to another city."

mock09.word: Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it, if any. A guru is someone who does not charge you a fee because someone who is tied in greed couldn't release you.
options: Charge you a fee because someone
A guru is someone who does not
Who is tied in greed couldn't release you
mock09.explanation: The given sentence is in present simple tense throughout. Therefore "couldn't" (past form) is incorrect; the correct word is "can't" (present form).

mock09.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech: He said,"I shall need a copy of the documents." (repeat check)
options: He said that he will need a copy of the documents.
He asked if I would need a copy of the documents.
He mentioned that he needed a copy of the documents.
He said he would need a copy of the documents.
mock09.explanation: "Shall" in direct speech changes to "would" in indirect speech, and the pronoun "I" changes to "he" to match the reporting subject. (Same as an earlier repeated item in the source document.)

mock09.word: By ______ a good kill on a particular day, the larger raptor can go without hunting two or three weeks.
mock09.explanation: "Making a killing" (बड़ा शिकार करना) — यह idiom है जिसका अर्थ है to earn a lot of resources in a short time। Context में raptor के शिकार की बात हो रही है। "Taking," "Doing," और "Getting" इस idiom में fit नहीं होते।

mock09.word: Aid workers have delivered vast quantities of food on the refugee camps.
mock09.explanation: "on" की जगह "to" होना चाहिए। "delivered to" एक phrasal verb है जिसका अर्थ है किसी जगह सामान पहुँचाना। Correct Sentence: Aid workers have delivered vast quantities of food to the refugee camps.

mock10.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom: 'Newton's work in the field of physics speaks volumes about his genius.'
options: does not give a detailed report
mock10.explanation: 'Speaks volumes' means to convey a great deal of information or to make something very clear, even without words (e.g. Her silence spoke volumes about her disappointment). It is used when something communicates a lot through implication, evidence, or inherent quality. 'Makes a lot of noise' and 'gives many books' are literal misinterpretations. 'Does not give a detailed report' is the exact opposite of the idiom's meaning. Idiom Tip: When an idiom contains 'speaks' or 'says,' it often relates to communication in a non-literal sense.

mock10.word: Select the correct indirect form: 'You said, "Priya kept watching television till her mother exploded at her."'
options: You said that Priya had kept watching television till her mother exploded at her.
You said that Priya kept watching television till her mother had exploded at her.
You said that Priya had kept watching television till her mother had exploded at her.
You said that Priya kept watching television till her mother exploded at her.
mock10.explanation: "Till" time clause को indicate करता है। Time clause में tense same रहती है। "Kept" past simple और "exploded" past simple — दोनों time clause में हैं इसलिए change नहीं होंगे। Correct: "You said that Priya kept watching television till her mother exploded at her."

mock10.word: He lost his mother when he was seven and his father when he was thirteen, ___ which the orphan boy was sent off by guardian uncles to a grammar school at Hawkshead.
mock10.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) upon. "Upon which" is an idiomatic phrase meaning "as a result of which" or "immediately after which" (जिसके परिणामस्वरूप).

mock10.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. A storm in a teacup
options: Lot of fuss that will soon be forgotten
mock10.explanation: "A storm in a teacup" (छोटी बात का बड़ा बखेड़ा) refers to a great deal of anger or worry over something unimportant, that will soon be forgotten. For example, "The whole controversy turned out to be a storm in a teacup."

mock10.word: Select the option that can replace the underlined phrase to make the sentence grammatically correct. "After flip through half dozen profiles with the same name, he chance upon Elina's profile, he succeed in zeroing in on her now recognizable face."
options: Flip through half dozen profile... he chance upon, he succeeded
Flipping through half dozen profile... he chance upon, he succeeded
Flipping through half dozen profiles... he chanced upon, he succeed
Flipping through half a dozen profiles... he chanced upon, he succeeded
mock10.explanation: Correct grammar requires: "flipping" (gerund), "half a dozen", "profiles" (plural), and past tense verbs "chanced" and "succeeded".

mock10.word: It's one of the advantages I get from ______ a bachelor and, according to my nearest and dearest, practically a half-witted bachelor … that.
mock10.explanation: "Being a bachelor" correctly uses the present participle of "to be" to describe a continuous state.

mock10.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice: "Why are you repeatedly called by her?"
options: Why had she called you repeatedly?
Why was she calling you repeatedly?
Why does she call you repeatedly?
mock10.explanation: Since the passive verb is in present simple passive form "are called," the active equivalent uses "does + subject + V1," giving "Why does she call you repeatedly?"

mock10.word: Select the most appropriate idiom to fill in the blank. Rani has ___ about the sanitation in the colony. She can't stop thinking about it.
mock10.explanation: "Have a bee in your bonnet" (किसी विषय को लेकर बहुत व्यस्त रहना) refers to a situation where a person feels very strongly about a subject and keeps thinking or talking about it. "A bone to pick" means wanting to discuss an annoyance; "a bird's eye view" means a view from above; "a bed of thorns" means a difficult situation — none match continuous preoccupation.

mock10.word: Vivek has a strong resemblance _________ his grandfather.
mock10.explanation: The preposition "to" is used with "resemblance" for identifying a particular relationship between one person and another. "Resemblance to" is the correct prepositional phrase.

mock10.word: Choose the word which is most nearly the OPPOSITE in meaning as the word in Bold: Chugging
mock10.explanation: Chugging means consuming a liquid in large, continuous gulps quickly and noisily. Sipping means drinking slowly and in small amounts - a clear opposite (large/fast gulps vs small/slow sips). Gulping and Guzzling are both synonyms of chugging. Greedily is an adverb, not an antonym. Antonym Pair: Chugging / Gulping / Guzzling ↔ Sipping / Savouring.

mock10.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. Do not touch any items displayed on glass shelves.
options: Any items displayed on glass shelves not be touched.
Any items displayed on glass shelves will not be touched.
No items displayed on glass shelves should be touched.
Items displayed on glass shelves are not touched.
mock10.explanation: "Do not" in imperative passive changes to "should not be." "Not" changes to "No" in passive of negative imperative.

mock10.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech: "'How beautiful she is!' said Betty."
options: Betty exclaimed with joy how beautiful she was.
Betty exclaimed with joy how beautiful she is.
Betty exclaimed with joy that she was very beautiful.
Betty said that how beautiful she was.
mock10.explanation: Exclamatory sentence को indirect speech में: (1) "said" → "exclaimed with joy" (joy का भाव है)। (2) Inverted commas → "that"। (3) "is" → "was" (past tense में)। (4) Exclamatory structure → assertive sentence। Correct: "Betty exclaimed with joy that she was very beautiful."

mock10.word: The affection of her husband carried her up in the midst of all her problems.
mock10.explanation: "Bore her up" (सहन करने में सहायता करना) means to help one endure or persevere through emotional distress — perfectly fitting the context of a husband's affection sustaining someone through difficulties. "Tore her up" means caused grief. "Stood her up" means failed to meet. "Drove her up" means made someone irritated.

mock10.word: After travelling for 2 days straight, Reema was too ___.
mock10.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) knackered. "Knackered" (बुरी तरह थका हुआ) is an informal term meaning extremely exhausted, making it the perfect fit after two days of straight travel.

mock10.word: The politician used a/an mild and indirect word to describe the controversial policy, in order to soften the blow.
mock10.explanation: A "mild and indirect word" used to replace something harsh describes a "Euphemism" (शिष्टोक्ति), e.g., "passed away" instead of "died." Oxymoron is a contradictory phrase; Metaphor is a figurative comparison; Hyperbole (अतिकथन) is exaggerated speech — none match the idea of softening harsh language.

mock10.word: Choose the word that is opposite in meaning to the given word. Antsy
mock10.explanation: Antsy (बेचैन/अधीर) means restless or impatient. Collected (शांत/संयमित) means calm and composed — the exact opposite. Perturbed (चिंतित) is nearly synonymous with antsy. Pure (शुद्ध) and Adulation (अत्यधिक प्रशंसा) are unrelated.

mock10.word: Tapan faced a very difficult situation during the whole lockdown period and seeing him go through all this, I forgave his pardonable mistakes.
mock10.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) Venial. "Venial" (क्षमा योग्य) means a slight or forgivable offense, which serves as the exact synonym for "pardonable."

mock10.word: Identify the segment which contains the grammatical error: 'One of the reason behind malnutrition is the improper way of food intake.'
mock10.explanation: This is the same rule as Q45 - the phrase 'One of the' must always be followed by a plural noun. Incorrect: One of the reason. Correct: One of the reasons. Rule Reminder: One of the + Plural Noun - one of the most frequently tested error-spotting rules in SSC CGL and CHSL exams. Correct Sentence: One of the reasons behind malnutrition is the improper way of food intake.

mock10.word: Physical activity has been projected to be helpful on avoidance of depression, by the experts.
options: projected to be helpful about avoidance
projected to be helpful in the avoidance
projected to be helpful for avoidance
projected to be helpful of avoidance
mock10.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) projected to be helpful in the avoidance. When discussing something that aids or assists, the correct preposition is "in" (e.g., "helpful in something").

mock10.word: That is why the newspaper is considered a source of ______.
mock10.explanation: "Power" (शक्ति) fits the newspapers' role in influencing public opinion, as described in the passage.

mock10.word: Which word in the given sentence is the ANTONYM of nibble? "They sprinted across the floor and leaped up to the elevated platforms, poking their soft muzzles into the stanchions to gobble the grain."
mock10.explanation: "Nibble" (छोटे-छोटे टुकड़ों में खाना) का antonym है "Gobble" (जल्दी-जल्दी खाना) — eating food too fast। "Muzzles" (थूथन), "Elevated" (ऊँचा), और "Sprinted" (तेज़ दौड़ना) असंबंधित हैं।

mock10.word: ...mastodons bellowing ______ primaeval swamps.
mock10.explanation: "Bellowing into primaeval swamps" suggests sound spreading throughout the environment, making "into" the best fit.

mock10.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Up in the air
options: Something that is tossed up in the air
Something that is certain and decided
Something that is invisible and unattainable
mock10.explanation: "Up in the air" (अनिश्चित) means something is undecided or unknown, not literally floating in the sky.

mock10.word: The town of Wall stands today as it has ______ for six hundred years...
mock10.explanation: "Stood" (खड़ा रहा) — upright position में exist करना। "The town has stood for six hundred years" — यह persistence और existence को दर्शाता है। "Erected" (खड़ा किया), "Edified" (उन्नत किया), और "Established" (स्थापित किया) past perfect में यहाँ less natural हैं।

mock10.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: BESTIAL
mock10.explanation: "Bestial" (क्रूर/पाशविक) का अर्थ है savagely cruel and depraved। इसका synonym है "Brutish" (क्रूर/अप्रिय) — cruel and unpleasant। "Greedy" (लालची), "High-minded" (उच्च नैतिक), और "Noble" (शालीन) भिन्न अर्थ रखते हैं।

mock11.word: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error. If there is no error, select 'No error.' We talked about writing a will, but it / was one of those thing you never / get around to doing.
options: was one of those thing you never
We talked about writing a will, but it
mock11.explanation: After "one of those," the noun must always be in plural form. "Thing" should be "things." The construction "one of those things" always takes a plural noun. Correct sentence: We talked about writing a will, but it was one of those things you never get around to doing.

mock11.word: Brick ______ tempted him with … of water and he loved the palm pith that was used as … for baking bricks.
mock11.explanation: "Kilns" (भट्टियाँ) refers to furnaces used for baking bricks or pottery, exactly matching the context of brick-making mentioned in the passage. "Jar" and "tub" are containers unrelated to brick-baking, while "factory" is too general compared to the specific term "kilns."

mock11.word: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence. He said, "You were making excuses."
options: He said that I had been making excuses.
He said that you were making excuses.
He said that you had been making excuses.
He said that I was making excuses.
mock11.explanation: In reported speech, Past Continuous (were making) shifts to Past Perfect Continuous (had been making). "You" changes to "I" since the speaker is reporting what was said to them. Correct sentence: He said that I had been making excuses.

mock11.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute. To the utmost or most absolute extent or degree.
mock11.explanation: Quite (पूर्णतः) means to the utmost or most complete degree, while barely means hardly, merely means only, and primarily means mainly.

mock11.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. FOLIAGE
mock11.explanation: Foliage (पत्ते/हरियाली) means plant leaves collectively. Greenery, meaning green vegetation, is the closest synonym.

mock11.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. The doctors claim to discover / a cure for cancer, / and the medicine will be put / to test very soon.
options: and the medicine will be put
mock11.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) The doctors claim to discover. The doctors are claiming an achievement from the past, not a future action. Therefore, the perfect infinitive form "claim to have discovered" is the correct usage.

mock11.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Acclimatise
mock11.explanation: Acclimatise (अनुकूलित होना) means to adjust to a new environment or situation. "Disarrange" (अव्यवस्थित करना) means to disturb the order of something, the closest opposite among the options, since it implies undoing a settled state. Prioritise means to rank by importance; Placate means to calm someone; Decriminalise means to remove legal penalties.

mock11.word: Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. "The compensation that the / Government agreed to provide to / the dependents of the / train accident is fairly less."
mock11.explanation: People cannot be dependents of an "accident". The correct, logical phrase is "the dependents of the train accident victims."

mock11.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error: "Investing on real estate / can earn you quick profit / but it comes with / a certain amount of risk attached to it."
options: a certain amount of risk attached to it
mock11.explanation: "Investing on" गलत है। Real estate में invest करने के लिए preposition "in" प्रयुक्त होती है। Correct sentence: "Investing in real estate can earn you quick profit but it comes with a certain amount of risk attached to it."

mock11.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Acquisitive
mock11.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) Eager. "Acquisitive" (पाने की इच्छा रखने वाला) means having a strong desire to gain or acquire things. "Eager" closely aligns with this strong desire.

mock11.word: ...a book that leaves us to ______ what is unsaid.
mock11.explanation: "Interpret" (व्याख्या करना) — unsaid/unstated चीज़ों को समझना और उनकी व्याख्या करना। Literary works में readers को meaning खुद निकालनी होती है। "Deny" (नकारना), "Accept" (स्वीकार करना), और "What is" — context में गलत हैं।

mock11.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. FASTEN
mock11.explanation: Fasten (जोड़ना/बांधना) means to attach or join securely. Affix (चिपकाना/जोड़ना) means to attach or stick something to something else — same meaning. Allow means to permit. Halt means to stop movement. Hurry means to move with haste.

mock11.word: Question: increasingly______.
mock11.explanation: "Increasingly careless" — adverb "increasingly" के बाद adjective "careless" (लापरवाह) आता है।

mock11.word: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them contains an error. "Let us, at any rate, / give him an outline out the case / and hear what he has to say."
options: Give him an outline out the case
And hear what he has to say
mock11.explanation: "Outline out" uses the wrong preposition. The standard collocation is "outline of" (an outline of the case).

mock11.word: It can / get extreme cold / during / the winters.
mock11.explanation: "Extreme" is an adjective but here it modifies another adjective "cold," so an adverb is needed. The correct word is "extremely cold."

mock11.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. No one hears a happy billionaire.
options: A happy billionaire is never heard of.
A happy billionaire was never heard.
A happy billionaire is not heard.
A happy billionaire has never been heard.
mock11.explanation: Active: No one + hears + a happy billionaire. Passive: "A happy billionaire" becomes subject; "hear" becomes "heard of" (maintaining the phrasal meaning); "never" replaces "no one."

mock11.word: Select the segment in which a word has been INCORRECTLY used. Is the Abominable Snowman a friction of the mountaineers' imagination?
mock11.explanation: "Friction" (घर्षण) means the resistance that one surface encounters when moving over another — it does not fit this context. The correct word is "fiction" (कल्पना).

mock11.word: As the proverb says, 'He who hunts two hares, loses ______.'
mock11.explanation: The proverb conveys that pursuing two goals at once risks losing both hares, making "both" the correct fit.

mock11.word: Identify the segment with grammatical error. Let us work out / the details of / our endeavour, / shouldn't we?
mock11.explanation: The correct tag question for a sentence beginning with "Let us" is "shall we," not "shouldn't we."

mock11.word: Select the wrongly spelt word.
mock11.explanation: The correct spelling is "Besiege" (घेर लेना), meaning to surround a place with an army.

mock11.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock11.explanation: The correct spelling is "Verifiability" (सत्यापन योग्यता), meaning the quality of being able to be checked or proven true.

mock11.word: And, well, ______ of what may or may not be true of my personal scholastic rigour, I suspect the school-stress dream is quite a common one.
mock11.explanation: "Regardless of" (बावजूद इसके कि) means despite or although, used to introduce a contrasting statement, fitting the structure "regardless of what may or may not be true." "Notwithstanding" and "Nevertheless" are similar but don't fit grammatically with "of" in this exact construction the way "regardless of" does; "Inasmuch" means "to the extent that," which doesn't fit the intended contrast.

mock11.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock11.explanation: The correct spelling is Wisdom. It means the ability to make sensible decisions based on knowledge or experience. Freedom, Boredom, and Kingdom are all correctly spelled.

The sentence is in future tense. "Before she received" (past tense) is incorrect here. It should be "before she receives" (present tense), as future sentences use present tense in subordinate clauses.

mock11.word: Competitions can be lucrative for ______ owners, with some prizes amounting to tens of thousands of dollars.
mock11.explanation: Since the following noun "owners" is plural, the modifying noun before it must be in its singular form to function correctly as an adjective phrase, fitting "bird owners." "Pigeons," "animal," and "people" either create grammatical mismatch or are less precise in context.

mock12.word: Corruption is an evil generally more _______ in the underdeveloped and developing countries of the world.
mock12.explanation: Rampant means flourishing or spreading in an uncontrolled way; unchecked and widespread. (Hindi: अनियंत्रित / बेलगाम) Corruption being "rampant" means it is widespread, difficult to control, and present in large amounts. Example: Corruption is rampant in many developing nations. Other options: Reluctant (अनिच्छुक) = unwilling or hesitant — wrong context entirely; Distant (दूर) = far away in space or time — makes no sense here; Extravagant (फिजूलखर्च) = lacking restraint in spending — different meaning and context.

mock12.word: We have already said that inconsistency is not a matter of ______ education or lack of logical ______.
mock12.explanation: "Inadequate" (अपर्याप्त) — insufficient education। "Rigour" (कठोरता/सटीकता) — logical rigour की कमी। "Inconsistency is not a matter of inadequate education or lack of logical rigour" — logically coherent। बाकी options context में गलत हैं।

mock12.word: A soprano voice floated from the open windows, resonating ______ the darkening greenery.
mock12.explanation: "Resonating over the darkening greenery" correctly conveys the sound spreading across the landscape.

mock12.word: Select the meaning of the given idiom. Split one's sides
mock12.explanation: "Split one's sides" (पेट पकड़कर हंसना) means to laugh very hard or to be extremely amused.

mock12.word: Select the correctly spelt word.
mock12.explanation: "Battalion" (सैन्य दल) — सही spelling है। "Batalion" में double 't' और double 'l' दोनों missing हैं। "Batallion" में double 't' missing है। "Battallion" में extra 'l' है।

mock12.word: Sentences of a paragraph are given below. While 1st and 6th sentences are correct, arrange A-D: (1) A vacation is looked forward to as a time to relax. A. But, destiny had other plans for me. B. I was very excited to go on a vacation to Goa. C. My bags were packed and I decided to retire early for the night. D. It is a welcome break from the daily routine which is often monotonous. (6) I fell off the bed and fractured my foot!
mock12.explanation: 1 → D (vacation as break from monotony) → B (excited about Goa) → C (bags packed, going to sleep) → A (destiny had other plans) → 6 (fell off bed). Logical narrative: general idea → specific excitement → preparation → twist → consequence. Correct order: DBCA

mock12.word: Drown the whole thing in an abnormal, ______ atmosphere...
mock12.explanation: "Dreamy" (स्वप्निल) — lyrical, magical farce के context में dreamy atmosphere appropriate है। "Littered" (कूड़े से भरा), "Hygienic" (स्वच्छ), और "Plentiful" (प्रचुर) इस literary/atmospheric context में गलत हैं।

mock12.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Occlude (अवरुद्ध करना)
mock12.explanation: "Occlude" का अर्थ है to close up or block an opening। "Obstruct" भी किसी चीज़ को रोकना है — दोनों समानार्थी हैं।

mock12.word: The same voice that ______ everything suddenly to life.
mock12.explanation: "Brought everything suddenly to life" correctly conveys the voice's awakening effect on the scene.

mock12.word: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence. "I have heard the podcast before," Nita says.
options: Nita says that she is hearing the podcast before.
Nita says that she had heard the podcast before.
Nita says that she has heard the podcast before.
Nita says that she had heard the podcast ago.
mock12.explanation: When the reporting verb is in present tense ("says"), there is no tense change in indirect speech. "Have" changes to "has" since "Nita" is a singular third-person subject. "Before" is correctly retained. "Ago" cannot replace "before" in present perfect context.

mock12.word: Select the option that contains an error: "The politician was accused of accepting bribes and lied about it to the public."
mock12.explanation: The error lies in verb tense consistency. Both actions (accepting bribes and lying) should be in the same form: "accepting bribes and lying about it to the public."

mock12.word: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error. If there is no error, select 'No error.' And it just recently awarded/ a million-dollar contract/ that reeks of corruption.
mock12.explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct. "Just recently" is a valid adverb combination. The subject "it" pairs correctly with the past tense verb "awarded." "That reeks of corruption" is a correct relative clause. No grammatical error exists.

mock12.word: Find the error: "Aviation is important...The success of the aviation industry (2)/ is also integral to national competition, economic developing and social progress. (3)/ No error (4)."
mock12.explanation: "Developing" should be the noun "development," since it is listed parallel to "competition" and "progress."

mock12.word: A major ______ of modern times is that we have come to assume that all creation is ______ to the material interests of us
mock12.explanation: "Fallacy" (भ्रांति/गलत धारणा) — untrue notion। "Subservient" (अधीन) — acting as subordinate। "Symbiotic" (सहजीवी) — mutual dependence। तीनों मिलकर: "A major fallacy...creation is subservient to material interests...cut the symbiotic relationship" — logical।

mock12.word: We rise ______ the Sun on every Sunday.
mock12.explanation: "With" here means "at the same time as," indicating that waking happens simultaneously with sunrise.

mock12.word: Sentences of a paragraph about Don DeLillo are given in jumbled order. Arrange them correctly. A. "A brilliant story about death and the fear of death," said the original jacket blurb on Don DeLillo's 1985 novel, White Noise. B. The writer that inspired Netflix's latest hit, White Noise, is a dazzling chronicler of modern America. C. So this feels like a good time to look again at White Noise's author. D. This month, Noah Baumbach's Netflix film, White Noise, dazzles its way on to our screens.
mock12.explanation: B introduces DeLillo as a legendary novelist; A describes his novel White Noise; D connects it to the recent film adaptation; C concludes by urging readers to revisit DeLillo's work.

mock12.word: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. A. The use of graphics helps in simplifying complicated concepts in subjects such as mathematics, biophysics, molecular biology and quantum mechanics. B. Visual materials can also aid in the comprehension and elaboration of a learning segment. C. Graphic images enhance students' motivation and interest while also speeding up learning. D. The internet provides students with a range of visual elements and does not limit them to a single individual's interpretation.
mock12.explanation: Correct Answer: (C) DCBA. D introduces the concept of visual elements online. C states general benefits, B adds comprehension benefits, and A provides specific subject examples where this is applied.

mock12.word: Weathering occurs as a result of different chemical and physical ______ that occur on the earth
mock12.explanation: Interactions (अंतःक्रिया) best describes the complex chemical and physical processes between rock particles forming soil.

mock12.word: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. A. Pandit Vidyadhar Bhattacharya was consulted by Sawai Jai Singh II of Jaipur when creating these enormous instruments. B. The word Jantar Mantar, which in Sanskrit signifies instruments and formulas, has been corrupted. C. His measurements of the equinoxes' precession and the ecliptic's obliquity are his two most important contributions to astronomy. D. Samrat Yantra, Ram Yantra, and Jai Prakash were his original creations.
mock12.explanation: B introduces the general term "Jantar Mantar." A details the architect behind them. D lists his creations, and C concludes with his specific astronomical (खगोलीय) achievements.

mock12.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word: "We should desecrate our national flag."
mock12.explanation: "Desecrate" (अपवित्र करना) — to treat something sacred with violent disrespect। इसका antonym है "Sanctify" (पवित्र करना) — to make something holy or sacred। "Damage" (नुकसान पहुँचाना), "Violate" (उल्लंघन करना), और "Integrate" (एकीकृत करना) antonym नहीं हैं।

mock12.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. You must / be hurry / otherwise you will / miss the train.
mock12.explanation: "be hurry" गलत है। "be" का प्रयोग "hurry" से पहले नहीं होता। सही वाक्य: "You must hurry, otherwise you will miss the train."

mock12.word: Perhaps India should listen to her ______ sages who taught the importance of balance.
mock12.explanation: "Ancient" (प्राचीन) means belonging to the distant past, fitting the description of sages who lived long ago and taught wisdom. "Initial" means existing at the beginning of something specific; "Antique" describes valuable old objects; "Extinct" means no longer existing — none fit describing wise figures from history.

mock12.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. The child was irritable / because she had not / soundly slept / the previous night.
mock12.explanation: The adverb "soundly" should come after the auxiliary verb "had" and before the main verb "slept," not after it: "had not slept soundly."

mock12.word: Identify how you will ask everyone if the sweets will be delivered by Jack today in active voice.
options: Jack will deliver the sweets today
Will Jack be delivering the sweets today?
Will Jack deliver the sweets today?
Are the sweets to be delivered by Jack today?
mock12.explanation: Active voice में Jack subject है जो action perform करता है। Question form में auxiliary verb "will" पहले आता है। Correct Sentence: Will Jack deliver the sweets today?

mock12.word: Identify the segment which contains the grammatical error. The match is about to begin since the captain as well as the team are on the field.
options: The match is about to begin
mock12.explanation: "As well as" does not combine two subjects the way "and" does. The verb agrees only with the first subject — "captain," which is singular. So "are" must be changed to "is." Correct sentence: The match is about to begin since the captain as well as the team is on the field.

mock13.word: Find out the correctly spelt word.
mock13.explanation: Monstrous (राक्षसी/भयानक) is the only correctly spelled word here. All other options add extra vowels incorrectly — "monsterous," "monstorous," and "monstorus" are all wrong forms.

mock13.word: If there is an error in the given sentence, choose the correct option without changing the tense. I will have been studying English for three years by the end of this course.
options: I have been studying English for three years by the end of this course.
I will have been studying English for three years by the end of this course.
I had been studying English for three years by the end of this course.
I was studying English for three years by the end of this course.
mock13.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) I will have been studying English for three years by the end of this course. The original sentence is completely correct. It uses the future perfect continuous tense to describe an ongoing action that will be completed by a specific time in the future.

mock13.word: The doctor often made his patients _____ for long.
mock13.explanation: "Make" is a causative verb. The structure is: MAKE + PERSON + BASE FORM OF VERB. So "made his patients wait" is correct — not "waiting" or "waited."

mock13.word: Take one's hat off to someone
mock13.explanation: "Take one's hat off to someone" (प्रशंसा करना) means to express deep admiration or applause for someone's achievement. Example: "We take our hats off to the rescue team."

mock13.word: The lights on the ______ of the church went out...
mock13.explanation: "Facade" (अग्रभाग/मुखौटा) — building का main front face जो street की तरफ होता है। Church की lights उसके facade पर थीं। "Tunnel" (सुरंग), "Vent" (वेंट), और "Architecture" (स्थापत्य) context में गलत हैं।

mock13.word: Select the sentence that does NOT have a spelling error.
options: The artist received recognition for her impressive etchings, bucolic (ग्रामीण) landscapes, and recreation of natural scenes around her
The insociance of the rock star... (insouciance — गलत)
The focus of the exibition is... (exhibition — गलत)
mock13.explanation: Option B में सभी words सही हैं — recognition, impressive, etchings, bucolic, landscapes, recreation — सब correctly spelt हैं।

mock13.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the option that gives their correct order. A. Here, the picture perfect French Quarter and heritage laden Tamil streets blend into one another. B. The conflict of coexistence is evident in many metropolitan cities. C. The old and new, like two different worlds, surviving side by side yet never crossing that chasm. D. There is a place though, Puducherry, where contrasts are celebrated like nowhere else.
mock13.explanation: B introduces the topic independently (conflict of coexistence in cities), D follows by introducing the contrasting example of Puducherry, A continues by describing how the French Quarter and Tamil streets blend there, and C concludes by reflecting on the old and new existing side by side without crossing, completing the descriptive passage.

mock13.word: Ranveer never has the habit of making a beeline for his academic activities.
mock13.explanation: "Making a beeline" (सीधे और तेज़ी से जाना) का अर्थ है to go straight towards something quickly and directly।

mock13.word: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. Select the option that arranges them correctly. (P) society benefits from diversity (Q) enriching cultures and (O) by fostering understanding and empathy (R) perspectives from around the world
mock13.explanation: P — subject। O — how society benefits। Q — what gets enriched। R — from where। Correct Sentence: Society benefits from diversity by fostering understanding and empathy, enriching cultures and perspectives from around the world.

mock13.word: High blood sugar is ______ the development of diabetes, obesity, heart disease.
mock13.explanation: "Linked to" (जुड़ा हुआ) indicates a connection or association between two things, fitting the relationship between blood sugar and diseases.

mock13.word: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don't find any error, mark 'No error' as your answer. In course by time, / he was wise enough to realise / the mistakes of his past life. / No error
options: he was wise enough to realise
the mistakes of his past life.
mock13.explanation: "In course by time" is not a standard expression; the correct idiomatic phrase is "in course of time," meaning gradually or over a period of time. The corrected sentence reads "In course of time, he was wise enough to realise the mistakes of his past life."

mock13.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word:
mock13.explanation: 'Lucretive' is incorrectly spelt. The correct spelling is LUCRATIVE, meaning producing a great deal of profit, profitable. Productive, Representative, and Palliative are all correctly spelt. Spelling Tip: Lucr-a-tive - not 'Lucr-e-tive.' Remember: the root is Latin lucrum (profit/money).

mock13.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Coveted
mock13.explanation: Coveted (अभिलषित) means greatly desired or envied. Desired (इच्छित) means wanted or wished for — same meaning. Despised means felt contempt for. Covered means put over something. Renounced means formally declared abandonment of something.

mock13.word: The ______ years of a man's life are spent in the lap of his mother.
mock13.explanation: "Formative" (विकासशील, संरचना-निर्माणक) means relating to a person's development, fitting the context of the years that shape a person's character early in life. "Forthcoming" and "Imminent" mean about to happen; "Anticipated" means expected — none specifically describe a developmental period of life.

mock13.word: Directions: Choose the option that best describes the meaning of the given idiom/phrase. Memory like sieve
mock13.explanation: "Memory like sieve" (कमज़ोर याददाश्त) means having a poor or unreliable memory, often forgetting things easily — just as a sieve cannot hold things passing through it. For example, "After the accident, he developed a memory like a sieve."

mock13.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice: "By whom have the problems been solved?"
mock13.explanation: For "by whom" passive questions, the structure becomes "Who + has/have + V3," giving "Who has solved the problems?" matching the present perfect tense.

mock13.word: You ought to have ___ yourself of this golden opportunity.
mock13.explanation: "Availed" (लाभ उठाया) means to make use of something, especially an opportunity, and is used with a reflexive pronoun ("availed himself/yourself"). "Seized" and "Grabbed" mean to take hold of something but don't typically pair with a reflexive structure here; "Claimed" means to assert something is true.

mock13.word: Identify the word that is misspelt.
mock13.explanation: The correct spelling is "idiosyncrasy." (Duplicate check of an earlier item.)

mock13.word: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. Proceeding in a gradual way and secretly intending to cause harm
mock13.explanation: Insidious (धीरे-धीरे हानिकारक) means spreading gradually or without being noticed, but causing serious harm — exact match. Benevolent means kind, helpful, and generous. Malevolent means having a desire to harm other people. Solvent means a liquid that can dissolve.

mock13.word: Until recently, 3D printing has been sugar-based, but technology is emerging that reliably prints savoury and fresh ingredients. (Antonym of savoury)
mock13.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) Sweet. "Savoury" (नमकीन / मसालेदार) describes food that is salty or spicy rather than sweet. Its direct antonym is "Sweet."

mock13.word: The reduced air pressure on airline flights can lessen the amount of oxygen in passenger's blood for twenty-five percent.
options: in passenger's blood for twenty-five percent
The reduced air pressure on airline flights
can lessen the amount of oxygen
mock13.explanation: "for" की जगह "by" होना चाहिए। "by" percentage के साथ comparison दर्शाने के लिए use होता है। Correct Sentence: ...in the passenger's blood by twenty-five per cent.

mock13.word: Select the correct direct form of the given sentence. I suggested to my brother that we should go to the hills for a change.
options: I said to my brother,"Shall we go to the hills for a change?"
I said to my brother,"We shall go to the hills for a change."
I said to my brother,"Let us go to the hills for a change."
I requested to my brother,"We should go to the hills for a change."
mock13.explanation: "Suggested" in indirect speech converts to "Let us" in direct speech for suggestions. "We should" in indirect corresponds to "Let us" in direct.

mock13.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. PERCEIVE
mock13.explanation: Perceive means to become aware of something through the senses or observation. "Notice" (ध्यान देना) carries the identical meaning of becoming aware of something. Obtain means to get; Continue means to keep doing; Ignore means to pay no attention — none fit.

mock13.word: (Social media passage — Stenographer) The true source of _________ is our confrontational style, not the problem itself.
mock13.explanation: Contestation (विवाद/मतभेद) refers to an action or process of agreeing or disagreeing on something — perfect for describing the source of conflict.

mock13.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom: "The threat of the pandemic is not just a CRY IN THE WILDERNESS."
mock13.explanation: "A cry in the wilderness" (अनसुनी चेतावनी) — इसका अर्थ है someone who points out dangers in a situation but nobody pays attention। Example: "He was a voice crying in the wilderness as he tried to expose the vast corruption." "Mourning" (शोक), "Tears of sorrow" (दुःख के आँसू), और "Spreading rumours" (अफवाह फैलाना) गलत हैं।

mock14.word: There is a road from Wall, a winding track rising sharply up from the forest, where it is ______ with rocks and small stones.
mock14.explanation: "Lined with rocks" (पत्थरों से भरी/सजी) — road के किनारे rocks और stones हैं। "Lined" यह visual description देता है। "Sequenced" (क्रमबद्ध), "Queued" (कतार में), और "Furnished" (सुसज्जित) इस context में गलत हैं।

mock14.word: Direction: Sentences of a paragraph given below in jumbled order. A. Locomotive acts was introduced in 1865. B. It was around 65 cm long for the Chinese emperor. C. Nicolas Joseph Cugnot has the credit for developing the first automobile in 1769. D. Richard Trevithick demonstrated the first steam-powered road vehicle. S1 (fixed). Ferdinand Verbiest designed the first working steam-powered vehicle in China in 1672. S6 (fixed). Pyreolophore is the name of the world's first internal combustion engine.
mock14.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) BCDA. B provides detail regarding Verbiest's vehicle from S1. C and D follow in chronological (कालानुक्रमिक) order (1769 to Trevithick). Finally, A mentions the 1865 acts.

mock14.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Frenzy
mock14.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) Harmony. "Frenzy" (उन्माद) is a state of wild, chaotic excitement. The opposite is a state of peaceful agreement, which is "Harmony" (सामंजस्य).

mock14.word: Select the grammatically correct sentence from the options given.
options: An troll, the boy shared on the social media created the big laughter.
The troll, the boy shared on social media created a big laughter.
A troll, a boy shared in on social media created an big laughter.
The troll, a boy shared on an social media created a big laughter.
mock14.explanation: Option B correctly uses specific articles ("The troll", "the boy"), no article for the mass noun "social media", and the correct article "a" for "big laughter".

mock14.word: Sentences comparing urban populations of India and the US are given in jumbled order. Select the correct sequence. a. This figure is predicted to climb as the percentage of Americans residing in rural areas falls. b. The United Nations reports that India's urban population is growing at 1.1 percent annually. c. While 82% of Americans live in urban settings, only 30% of Indians do so. d. It's still a considerably smaller percentage of the Indian population than in the US that resides in urban areas.
mock14.explanation: b presents India's urban growth data; d compares this to the US percentage; c gives the specific current figures; a concludes with the predicted future trend for the US.

mock14.word: Parts of the given sentence. Identify which contains the error: "You must avoid riding in a crowded bus or travelling in a metro during rush hour as both are quiet unpleasant experiences."
options: as both are quiet unpleasant experiences
you must avoid riding in a crowded bus
mock14.explanation: "Quiet" (शांत — adjective) गलत है। यहाँ adjective "unpleasant" को modify करने के लिए adverb "quite" (काफी/बिल्कुल) होना चाहिए। Correct: "as both are quite unpleasant experiences."

mock14.word: ...it will choose the one that ______ fights off its infection.
mock14.explanation: A superlative adjective is needed since multiple honey types are compared; "best" fits, while "better" only compares two things.

mock14.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM to substitute the underlined word: There was an interminable struggle from his part in his fight against cancer.
mock14.explanation: Interminable (अनंत/अंतहीन) means endless or seemingly without end. Intermittent (रुक-रुककर होने वाला) means not continuous; occurring at intervals — opposite of unceasing. Overwhelming means causing sudden strong emotion. Inappropriate means not suitable. Unsustainable means not able to be maintained.

mock14.word: Treks in wilderness areas are even ______ as children can then engage with all kinds of life-forms.
mock14.explanation: "Even better" (और भी बेहतर) is the correct comparative phrase here; "best" is superlative and grammatically unsuitable in this context.

mock14.word: When we got / to the train station, / the Rajdhani Express / had already been leaving.
mock14.explanation: "Had already been leaving" (past perfect continuous) is incorrect. Since the action was completed before another past action, past perfect "had already left" is required.

mock14.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences about Kerala floods/rainfall. Pick the option that gives their correct order (based on: state pounded by rainfall transforming deficit to surplus; Kerala facing deluge a year after 100-year floods; worst-affected northern districts; copious rainfall being both...).
mock14.explanation: B introduces Kerala facing a deluge again a year after the worst floods. A describes the past week's intense rainfall transforming deficit to surplus. D discusses the copious rainfall's dual nature. C identifies the worst-affected districts where relief work is ongoing. (Note: source document text for this item is truncated/incomplete.)

mock14.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. The recent discoveries have risen hopes about possible applications.
mock14.explanation: "Rise" is an intransitive verb and cannot take an object. "Hopes" is being used as an object here, which is incorrect. "Raise" is the transitive verb that can take an object. Correct sentence: The recent discoveries have raised hopes about possible applications.

mock14.word: Select the most appropriate synonym for the underlined word in the given sentence: 'One of Ram's leisure activities is tennis.'
mock14.explanation: Leisure means free time when one is not working, used for enjoyable activities. Recreation refers to activities done for enjoyment when not working - both describe activities pursued for pleasure during free time. Main means most important - an adjective, doesn't replace a noun here. Lead means to be in charge of - unrelated. Languish means to grow weak or suffer neglect - almost an antonym in spirit. Synonyms for Leisure: recreation, relaxation, free time, pastime, downtime.

mock14.word: Some earthquakes are ______ felt and cause little damage.
mock14.explanation: "Barely" (मुश्किल से) means almost not at all, fitting the context that some earthquakes are barely felt and cause little damage, implying minimal impact. "Easily," "Strongly," and "Weakly" do not correctly convey the sense of an earthquake being almost imperceptible.

mock14.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Desiccate
mock14.explanation: Desiccate (सुखाना) means to remove the moisture from something, typically to preserve it. Hydrate (नम करना/जल मिलाना) means to cause to absorb water or moisture — exact opposite. Honour means to regard with great respect. Ascend means to go up or climb. Venerate means to regard with great respect.

mock14.word: Problems often ______ when you least expect them.
mock14.explanation: "Arise" का अर्थ है to appear या come up। Problems arise = समस्याएं उत्पन्न होती हैं। Correct Sentence: Problems often arise when you least expect them.

mock14.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Ecstasy
mock14.explanation: Ecstasy (परमानंद) means a state of extreme happiness. Bliss (आनंद) means complete happiness — the closest synonym. Mourning (शोक) means grief. Fantasy (कल्पना) is an imagined pleasant situation. Appreciation (प्रशंसा) means recognizing value.

mock14.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word: Naive (From: 'After years of working in politics, she had become naive and jaded, convinced that all politicians were cynical and that the system was rigged.')
mock14.explanation: Naive means lacking experience or wisdom, too ready to believe or trust others without question. Cynical means believing people are motivated purely by self-interest, distrustful of human sincerity. A naive person believes everything without question; a cynical person believes nothing and questions everyone's motives - these are direct opposites. Jaded means tired and bored due to having too much of something - describes fatigue, not the opposite of naivety. Corrupt means dishonest - unrelated. Convinced means completely sure about something - unrelated. Antonym Pair: Naive ↔ Cynical / Shrewd / Worldly / Astute / Sophisticated.

mock14.word: Select the one which best expresses the meaning of the given word. Ennui (倦怠)
mock14.explanation: "Ennui" का अर्थ है lack of excitement; boredom; lethargy (आलस्य)।

mock14.word: Theaters across the US have ceased ______ in an effort to adhere to guidelines...
mock14.explanation: Operations (कार्य/संचालन) refers to the functioning of a business; theatres ceasing operations means they stopped functioning due to the pandemic.

mock14.word: He is nothing else in my house ______ a liability.
options: More than one of the above
mock14.explanation: The adverb "else" is always paired with "but," not "than." The correct sentence reads "nothing else... but a liability."

mock14.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM. EXOTIC
mock14.explanation: "Exotic" (विदेशी/असामान्य) means foreign or excitingly different. Its antonym is "ordinary" (साधारण) — something common and familiar, not foreign or unusual.

mock14.word: ...you have actually participated in the subject area, rather than ______ studied it.
mock14.explanation: "Merely" (केवल/सिर्फ) — "only" या "just" studying का emphasis करता है, actual participation से contrast में। "Purely" (शुद्ध रूप से), "Entirely" (पूर्णतः), और "Hardly" (मुश्किल से) इस intended contrast को नहीं दर्शाते।

mock14.word: Select the most appropriate synonym for the given word: Connote
mock14.explanation: Connote means (of a word) to imply or suggest an idea or feeling in addition to its literal meaning (e.g. chocolate connotes pleasure and indulgence). Convey means to make ideas, thoughts, or feelings known - both share the meaning of communicating or transmitting a meaning or idea. Comment means to say what you think - too narrow. Care means to be worried/interested in - unrelated. Impart means to pass information/knowledge to others - similar but less precise. Vocabulary Note: Connote specifically refers to the associated/secondary meaning of a word, while denote refers to the literal/primary meaning.

mock14.word: Choose the most appropriate option to change the narration (direct/indirect) of the given sentence. Amar urged Amit to stop taking tranquillizers without consulting the doctor.
options: "Amit, please stop taking tranquillizers without consulting the doctor," said Amar.
Amit said to Amar,"Please stop taking tranquillizers without consulting the doctor."
Amar says to Amit,"Please consult the doctor before taking tranquillizers."
"Amit, please stop taking tranquillizers without consulting the doctor," Amar was advising.
mock14.explanation: "Urged" indicates a request, so converting to direct speech uses "please." The subject Amar speaks to Amit, so "Amit" is addressed in direct speech.

mock15.word: " → Improvement:
mock15.explanation: "Too much noisy" गलत है। "Too" adjective/adverb के साथ आता है directly। "Too noisy" — सही idiom है जो excessive noise को indicate करता है। "Much noisy" और "Much too much noisy" grammatically incorrect हैं।

mock15.word: (Amazon passage — CPO) It is _________ the size of the continent of Australia.
mock15.explanation: Roughly (लगभग) means approximately — the passage says the Amazon Rainforest is approximately the size of Australia.

mock15.word: A courage gives / enormous strength to people / to fight for what they want. Identify the segment with the error.
options: to fight for what they want
mock15.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) A courage gives. "Courage" is an uncountable abstract noun and does not take the indefinite article "a." The phrase should simply be "Courage gives."

mock15.word: Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. "If we / were meeting at 6 p.m., / we will / have plenty of time."
mock15.explanation: In conditional sentences (unreal conditions), if the "if" clause uses past tense, the main clause must use "would," not "will." Correct: "...we would have plenty of time."

mock15.word: Choose the correctly spelt word.
mock15.explanation: Malodorous (दुर्गंधयुक्त) means smelling very unpleasant — correctly spelt. HEIRARCHY is misspelt (correct: HIERARCHY). APPAULLING is misspelt (correct: APPALLING). PESTILLENTIAL is misspelt (correct: PESTILENTIAL).

mock15.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. ATTRIBUTE
mock15.explanation: Attribute means a characteristic or quality regarded as inherent to someone or something. "Quality" directly matches this meaning of a distinctive feature. Speech means expression of thoughts; Praise means approval; Respect means admiration — none match.

mock15.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Duty
mock15.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) Exemption. "Duty" (कर्तव्य) is a moral or legal obligation. The opposite is being free from an obligation, which is "Exemption" (छूट).

mock15.word: (Naini Lake passage — CGL Tier II) Uncertainty looms over the revival of Naini Lake due to differences in the opinions of residents and local bodies regarding its desilting and _________.
mock15.explanation: Beautification (सुंदरीकरण) refers to the process of making something more beautiful or attractive — provides a clear counterpart to "desilting."

mock15.word: Rearrange the parts of the sentence in correct order. P. to focus more on edible oils/oilseeds than on wheat Q. the MSP for wheat is up by 2 per cent while R. that of rapeseed-mustard is up by 8.6 per cent, S. perhaps indicating that the government wants
mock15.explanation: Q presents the MSP data for wheat. R continues with the comparison for rapeseed-mustard. S draws the logical inference about government intent. P specifies the government's priority. Order: Q → R → S → P.

mock15.word: She has been writing this novel for a year but she did not finish it yet.
mock15.explanation: "Did not finish" is incorrect with "yet." When "yet" is used, the present perfect tense is required. "Has not finished it yet" is the correct form.

mock15.word: Raghav had a good ______ for stage plays.
mock15.explanation: "Troupe" का अर्थ है a group of actors or performers। "Raghav had a good troupe for stage plays."

mock15.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the correct order: A. I have seen a great many houses in my time...but of one house I have kept a very vivid memory. B. It was, properly speaking, rather a cottage than a house... C. The cottage was lost to sight among the mulberry-trees... D. It had white stucco walls, tiled roof, and a dilapidated chimney.
mock15.explanation: A (introduces the special house) → B (describes it as cottage) → D (details of walls/roof) → C (surroundings). A introduces, B describes general appearance, D gives specific structural details, C describes environment. Correct order: ABDC

mock15.word: Identify the segment which contains the grammatical error: 'Raja Ravi Varma was one of the first artist who tried to create a style that was both modern and traditional.'
options: to create a style that was
mock15.explanation: The error is in 'one of the first artist.' Grammar Rule: The phrase 'one of the' is always followed by a plural noun, since it implies there are multiple entities in the group and the subject is one member of that group. Incorrect: one of the first artist. Correct: one of the first artists. The verb agreement rule follows: 'One of the artists who tried...' - the verb agrees with 'one' (singular), not 'artists' (plural). Formula: One of the + Plural Noun + Singular Verb (e.g. She is one of the students who has won the prize). Correct Sentence: Raja Ravi Varma was one of the first artists who tried to create a style that was both modern and traditional.

mock15.word: Select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the Idiom/Phrase. To sleep like a dog
mock15.explanation: "To sleep like a dog" (गहरी नींद सोना) means to sleep very deeply and peacefully. It is similar to the more common idiom "sleep like a log." Insomniac (नींद न आना) is the opposite.

mock15.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. MISSIVE
mock15.explanation: Missive means a written communication, especially a letter. "Letter" directly matches this meaning of a written or printed message. Energy means power; Rocket means a missile; Objective (वस्तुनिष्ठ) means factual or impartial — none match.

mock15.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Primeval
mock15.explanation: Correct Answer: (C) Recent. "Primeval" (प्राचीन / आदिम) refers to the earliest times in history. "Recent" (हाल ही का) is its exact opposite.

mock15.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word:
mock15.explanation: 'Tolerent' is incorrectly spelt. The correct spelling is TOLERANT, meaning showing willingness to allow the existence of opinions or behaviour one does not necessarily agree with. Business, Shield, and Seldom are all correctly spelt. Spelling Rule: The suffix '-ant' (not '-ent') is used in tolerant, relevant, important, dominant, pleasant. Learn these by grouping: important, tolerant, relevant, dominant, vibrant.

mock15.word: A warning of impending danger
mock15.explanation: "Monition" (चेतावनी) means a warning or information that something dangerous is about to happen. "Naive" means lacking experience, "Daft" means silly.

mock15.word: My sister resembles to my mother a lot in looks and in temperament.
mock15.explanation: The verb "resemble" is not followed by the preposition "to." The correct usage is simply "resembles."

mock15.word: In the following question, out of the given four alternatives, select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the Idiom/Phrase. Pig in a poke
options: A brave man amongst all the cowards
Something that is bought without examining properly
To bring something to an end
mock15.explanation: "Pig in a poke" (बिना जांचे खरीदी गई वस्तु) refers to buying or accepting something without first inspecting it, similar to buying a pig hidden in a bag without seeing it. For example, "He bought the car online and it turned out to be a pig in a poke."

mock15.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word: OBJURGATION
mock15.explanation: "Objurgation" (कड़ी फटकार/कठोर आलोचना) — harsh rebuke or criticism। इसका antonym है "Endorsement" (समर्थन/अनुमोदन) — approval or support। "Hamper" (बाधा डालना), "Reproof" (हल्की फटकार), और "Commination" (दंड की धमकी) — different meanings।

mock15.word: Select the correct direct form of the given sentence. I asked my friend how one could be merry when everything was so messed up.
options: I said to my friend,"How can one be merry when everything is so messed up?"
I asked to my friend,"How can one be merry when everything was so messed up?"
I said to my friend,"How one could be merry when everything was so messed up?"
I said to my friend,"Could one be merry when everything is so messed up?"
mock15.explanation: Converting indirect to direct: past perfect "could" changes back to present "can"; "was so messed up" changes back to "is so messed up." "Asked" (indirect) becomes "said to" (direct).

mock15.word: ...but circumstances forced him to ______ profession.
mock15.explanation: Context में John को financially मजबूर होकर renowned artists के काम को duplicate करने में specialize करना पड़ा। Complete: "circumstances forced him to specialize in duplicating the work of renowned artists."

mock15.word: Identify the error in the following sentence. "Akash would be staying to his uncle's place during the holidays."
mock15.explanation: "Staying to" is incorrect usage. The correct preposition for a location is "at" (staying at his uncle's place).

mock15.word: Select the sentence that best reports the sentence given below. "If you do not return the books, the library will cancel your membership," said the librarian to the boy.
options: The librarian said that he would cancel the membership if the books were not returned.
The librarian said that if the boy did not return the books, the library would cancel his membership.
The librarian told the boy that the library will cancel the membership if he does not return the books.
The librarian told the boy to return the books or else he would cancel the membership.
mock15.explanation: When converting from direct to reported speech, quotation marks are removed, present tense verbs shift to past tense, and pronouns change appropriately ("you" becomes "he," "your" becomes "his"). Option D correctly fulfils all these conditions while capturing the conditional warning naturally.

mock16.word: IAF for its rapid deployment of assets at forward locations ______ response to the border row with China.
mock16.explanation: "In response to" (के जवाब में) एक fixed phrase है। "In response to the border row" सही है।

mock16.word: Sentences of a paragraph are given below. S1 and S6 are fixed; arrange A, B, C, D. S1. A library is a systematic and organised collection of informational resources. A. These resources are made accessible to a defined community for their reference or borrowing. B. It provides physical or digital access to the users. C. They facilitate group study and collaboration. D. A library's collection includes books, periodicals, newspapers, etc. S6. Libraries range in size from few shelves to several items.
mock16.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) ABCD. A details the accessibility of resources, B explains the mode of access, C mentions collaboration (सहयोग), and D specifies the physical collection, building logically toward S6.

mock16.word: Check-up ______ set up in the west of England...
mock16.explanation: The sentence describes a past event, so the past tense "was" is required to match the narrative timeline.

mock16.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. This is / too grave sin / to be / pardoned.
mock16.explanation: "Sin" is a countable noun starting with a consonant. After the structure "too + adjective," the indefinite article "a" must be used before a singular countable noun. Therefore "too grave sin" must be "too grave a sin." Correct sentence: This is too grave a sin to be pardoned.

mock16.word: Select the most appropriate one-word substitution for the given group of words. A person who is experiencing dementia brought about by old age
mock16.explanation: Senile (बुढ़ापे से कमज़ोर) refers to the weakness or mental infirmity of old age, often describing conditions like dementia associated with aging — exact match. Sentinel means a guard who stands and keeps watch. Primal means essential or fundamental. Primitive means relating to an early stage of evolutionary development.

mock16.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Timorous
mock16.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) Brazen. "Timorous" (डरपोक) means showing or suffering from nervousness or a lack of confidence. The opposite is "Brazen" (निर्लज्ज / साहसी), meaning bold and without shame.

mock16.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word: 'The singer complained to the police that she was purportedly attacked backstage immediately after the concert.'
mock16.explanation: Purportedly means claimed or believed to be true but with some doubt or uncertainty (supposedly). The antonym logic: purportedly = something supposedly/possibly true, so its opposite is impossibly = something that could not be true. Maliciously means with intent to harm - unrelated. Dramatically means in a theatrical manner - unrelated. Severely means extremely strictly or harshly - unrelated. Antonym Pair: Purportedly (supposedly) ↔ Impossibly (cannot be so).

mock16.word: She's been off work with ___ stomach.
mock16.explanation: Upset (परेशान/गड़बड़) when used with stomach means having disturbed digestion. Overset (भावनात्मक रूप से परेशान) refers to emotional disturbance. Agitated (बेचैन) and Perturbed (चिंतित) describe mental states, not physical stomach conditions. Correct sentence: She's been off work with an upset stomach.

mock16.word: Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it. If there is no error, choose 'No error.' Our hands have been crucial tools for finding and eat food, and helping us navigate the world.
options: tools for finding and eat food
mock16.explanation: When listing actions, all verbs must be in the same form (parallel structure — समांतर संरचना). "Finding" is a gerund, so "eat" must also be "eating." Correct sentence: Our hands have been crucial tools for finding and eating food, and helping us navigate the world.

mock16.word: Select the most appropriate idiom for the following statement. The manager was punished for his negligence (लापरवाही) in work.
mock16.explanation: "Bring to book" (जवाबदेह ठहराना, दंडित करना) का अर्थ है to punish someone or make them accountable for their actions। Example: The fraudulent investor was finally brought to book.

mock16.word: Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error. Solutions to the country's interior problems are still a long way away.
mock16.explanation: "interior" (भीतरी) गलत है। "internal" (आंतरिक) होना चाहिए। Correct Sentence: Solutions to the country's internal problems are still a long way away.
"Used to" के बाद gerund (V+ing) आता है। "Use to drink" दोनों तरह से गलत है — "used" (past participle form) + "drinking" (gerund) होना चाहिए। Correct sentence: "I am not used to drinking coffee."

mock16.word: it plays a crucial role in the ______ of species over time.
mock16.explanation: "Diversification" (विविधीकरण) describes the evolutionary outcome of adaptation producing varied species.

mock16.word: (Moon passage — CHSL) As it does so, we see different amounts of its side _________ by the Sun.
mock16.explanation: Lit (प्रकाशित) means provided with light or lighting; illuminated — the Sun provides light to one side of the Moon.

mock16.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Procrastination
mock16.explanation: "Procrastination" (टालमटोल) means delaying or postponing something. "Suspension" (स्थगन) refers to the temporary prevention of something from continuing — closely related in meaning to delaying. "Resumption" (पुनः आरम्भ) means restarting.

mock16.word: Complete the sentence with ANTONYM of (sturdy): The old house was so _____ that it could be shattered into pieces with a single blow.
mock16.explanation: "Sturdy" means strong and not easily damaged. Its antonym is "fragile" (नाज़ुक) — easily broken or damaged. The sentence context (shattered with a single blow) confirms this.

mock16.word: Identify the INCORRECTLY spelt word in the given sentence. The most frequent medallion composition on a carpet consists of a more or less elaborate motif superimposed on the centre of a patterned field and often complemented with corner pieces, which are typically quardants of the central medallion.
mock16.explanation: The correct spelling is "Quadrant" (चतुर्थांश), meaning an arc of 90° that is one-quarter of a circle.

mock16.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Cloistered (एकांतवासी)
mock16.explanation: "Cloistered" का अर्थ है seclude करना, एकांत में रखना। "Isolated" का अर्थ है cut off या apart — दोनों समानार्थी हैं।

mock16.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word: MEMORY
mock16.explanation: Memory means a person's ability to remember things; a specific thing remembered. Oblivion means the state of being unaware or unconscious of what is happening, the state of being forgotten - the direct opposite of memory. Suppress means to prevent something from being seen/expressed - related but not an antonym. Retrospection means the action of reviewing past events - actually related to memory, not its opposite. Impart means to pass on knowledge - unrelated. Antonym Pair: Memory ↔ Oblivion / Forgetfulness / Amnesia.

mock16.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. IMPLORE
mock16.explanation: Implore means to beg someone desperately to do something. "Plead" (गुहार लगाना) means to make an emotional appeal, which is essentially the same action. Command means to order; Refuse means to say no; Honour means to fulfil an obligation — none match.

mock16.word: Select the option that contains an error. The company was sued for breach of contract, but it still deny any wrongdoing.
mock16.explanation: The subject "company" is singular and third person, so the verb must also be singular ("denies"), not the plural form "deny." This is a subject-verb agreement error, and the correct sentence reads "but it still denies any wrongdoing."

mock16.word: He was ______ for a while.
mock16.explanation: "Inconsolable" (असांत्वनीय/बेहद दुखी) — बच्चे की बिल्ली खो गई, इसलिए वह बहुत दुखी था। "Petrified" (भयभीत), "Sanguine" (आशावादी), और "Blithesome" (प्रसन्न) context में गलत हैं।

mock16.word: Fill in the blank with the most appropriate word. We must help to ___ the homeless and physically disabled people.
mock16.explanation: "Render" (प्रदान करना) refers to the act of providing something, such as help, which fits the sentence's meaning of giving assistance. "Exert," "Donate," and "Contribute" cannot grammatically take an action like "help" as their object in this construction.

mock16.word: Fill in the blank with an appropriate option. Wash, rinse and drain water from quinoa and ___.
mock16.explanation: "Set aside" (अलग रखना) means to put something to the side for later use — perfectly fitting the cooking instruction context. "Set upon" means to attack. "Set off" means to trigger or start. "Set in" means to become established.

mock16.word: This was a source of great ______ to the boys.
mock16.explanation: "Irritation" (झुंझलाहट) — boys market नहीं जा सकते थे, इससे वे annoyed थे। "Ebullience" (उत्साह), "Hatred" (घृणा), और "Abhorrence" (विद्वेष) इस mild feeling के लिए too strong हैं।

mock17.word: Fill in the blank with an appropriate option. Pandavas returned to Hastinapur after their thirteen years of ___.
mock17.explanation: In the Mahabharata, the Pandavas were banished from Hastinapur for thirteen years. Exile (निर्वासन) means being forced to leave one's home or country as punishment — perfectly fitting the context.

mock17.word: Choose the word that is opposite in meaning to the given word. Nadir
mock17.explanation: Nadir (निम्नतम बिंदु) means the lowest point or bottom of something. Climax (उच्चतम बिंदु/चरम) means the highest point or peak — the direct opposite. Base and Bottom are synonyms of nadir. Docile means submissive and is completely unrelated.

mock17.word: Select the option that will improve the underlined part. Amoeba is a microscopic single-celled organism found in pond water.
mock17.explanation: वाक्य पूर्णतः सही है। "organism found in" — singular noun के साथ past participle सही रूप से प्रयुक्त है।

mock17.word: Once the interview was over I ______ felt light and free.
mock17.explanation: "Suddenly" का अर्थ है quickly and unexpectedly। Interview खत्म होते ही अचानक हल्का महसूस होना context में सही है। Correct Sentence: ...I suddenly felt light and free.

mock17.word: ...bottled water indicates ______ for nature
mock17.explanation: Contempt (तिरस्कार) conveys disregard or disdain, matching the critical tone about humans disrespecting nature's complexity.

mock17.word: A gentle breeze whispered through the ______ trees, rustling their vibrant leaves.
mock17.explanation: "Lustrous" (चमकदार) describes shiny, vibrant trees, matching the imagery of "rustling their vibrant leaves."

mock17.word: ...cloud computing had become a highly demanded services or ______...
mock17.explanation: "Utility" (उपयोगिता/सेवा) — a service used by the public। Cloud computing एक public utility बन गई है। "Authentication" (प्रमाणीकरण), "Performance" (प्रदर्शन), और "Subscription" (सदस्यता) इस context में कम सटीक हैं।

mock17.word: Elders are the … force for any family and hence the love and respect for elders comes from ______ and is not artificial.
mock17.explanation: "Within" (अंदर से) means inside or from inside, fitting the context that the love for elders comes from inner conviction rather than external pressure, and is not artificial. "Surrounding" refers to area around something; "Without" means lacking something; "Outcoming" relates to a result — none fit the meaning of coming from inside oneself.

mock17.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. An extreme form of nationalism characterized by an aggressive foreign policy.
mock17.explanation: Jingoism (अंधराष्ट्रवाद) is extreme nationalism or chauvinism, especially characterized by an aggressive foreign policy. Vandalism is destruction of property. Humanism emphasizes reason and shared humanity. Nationalism is general patriotism — not as extreme as jingoism.

mock17.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Savage
mock17.explanation: Savage (जंगली/असभ्य) means primitive and uncivilized. Civilised (सभ्य) means brought to a stage of social and cultural development — the direct opposite. Brutal (क्रूर) is a near synonym. Adequate (पर्याप्त) and Impartial (निष्पक्ष) are completely unrelated.

mock17.word: In the following question, a word has been written in four different ways. Select the correctly spelt word.
mock17.explanation: सही spelling "Fahrenheit" है — यह temperature scale का नाम है, जो Prussian scientist Daniel Gabriel Fahrenheit के नाम पर है।

mock17.word: Identify the segment that has an error: "The police told / the agitators to back in, / else they would / lathi-charge."
mock17.explanation: The correct phrasal verb is "back off" (पीछे हटना), not "back in," meaning to retreat or withdraw.

mock17.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Plight
mock17.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) Benefit. "Plight" (दुर्दशा / कठिन परिस्थिति) refers to a dangerous, difficult, or unfortunate situation. Its opposite is an advantage or "Benefit" (लाभ).

mock17.word: Select the correct passive form of the given sentence. Pay that bill today.
options: Let that bill to be paid today.
That bill has been paid today.
