That bill should be paid today.
Let that bill be paid today.
mock17.explanation: The sentence is imperative, giving an instruction about necessity. In passive voice, "should be" conveys the necessity: "Pay" becomes "should be paid."

mock17.word: Take possession of the records immediately so that they are not ______ with.
mock17.explanation: "Tampered with" (छेड़छाड़ करना) means to interfere with something in an unauthorized way; this past participle fits the passive structure "are not tampered with."

mock17.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice: "What is written is that understood by you?"
options: Did you understand what was written?
Is the write-up/written matter understood by you?
mock17.explanation: This question retains its passive structure in meaning, with "is" as the helping verb and "understood" as the past participle, correctly forming "Is the write-up understood by you?"

mock17.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock17.explanation: The correct spelling is "Burglar" (सेंधमार) — a person who enters a building illegally to steal. "Bourgeoisie" (बुर्जुआ वर्ग) and "Burlesque" (नाटकीय व्यंग्य) are correctly spelled.

mock17.word: Covid-19 pandemic has resulted in (an slow change in flowing of capital) generated by workers.
options: An deliberate change to flow of capital
A sluggish change in the flow of capital
An abrupt change in the flow of capital
An sudden change to flow of capital
mock17.explanation: Sluggish (धीमा/सुस्त) means slow-moving — a more formal and precise replacement for "slow" in this context, maintaining correct grammatical structure.

mock17.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. I am not sure / if the red car / in the parking slot / is your.
mock17.explanation: "Your" (तुम्हारा — विशेषण) is a possessive adjective used before nouns. Here we need a possessive pronoun "Yours" (तुम्हारा — सर्वनाम) to indicate ownership independently. Correct sentence: I am not sure if the red car in the parking slot is yours.

mock17.word: When she … refuge with him from a bad dream, nothing could lull ______ back to sleep better than Mo
mock17.explanation: The blank refers back to "she" (Meggie), the subject discussed throughout the sentence, so the correct pronoun is "her." "It," "them," and "him" do not match the female subject established earlier in the sentence.

mock17.word: Select the correct direct form of the given sentence. Amanda asked Mohit how she had offended his friend.
options: Amanda said to Mohit,"How did I offended his friend?"
Amanda said to Mohit,"How have I offended your friend?"
Amanda said to Mohit,"How she had offended his friend?"
Amanda said to Mohit,"How have I offend your friend?"
mock17.explanation: Converting indirect to direct: past perfect "had offended" changes to present perfect "have offended." Pronoun "she" reverses to "I." Possessive "his" changes to "your."

mock17.word: He ______ his bike as fast as he could to reach home.
mock17.explanation: "Rode" is the past tense of "ride," meaning to travel by sitting on and controlling a bicycle, horse, or similar vehicle — for example, "He rode his bike as fast as he could to reach home." This is the only word that grammatically and semantically fits the blank, since the sentence describes someone travelling on a bike. The other homophones/near-homophones have entirely different meanings: "road" is a noun referring to a wide way leading from one place to another, with a surface vehicles can use (e.g., "The road was closed for repairs"); "rowed" is the past tense of "row," meaning to propel a boat using oars (e.g., "They rowed across the lake"); "rote" refers to mechanical or habitual repetition of something to be learned (e.g., "Learning by rote is not always effective"). A useful tip for homophone questions is to first identify the part of speech required by the sentence (here, a past-tense verb), which immediately eliminates "road" and "rote."

mock17.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word. The kids have been pestering (परेशान करना) me to buy them new toys.
mock17.explanation: "Pestering" का अर्थ है to annoy somebody repeatedly। इसका antonym "Pacifying" (angry person को शांत करना) है।

mock17.word: Posters are used to promote a brand or pass ______ important information.
mock17.explanation: "Pass along information" (जानकारी आगे देना) — "along" सही preposition है।

mock17.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the option that gives the correct order. A. My mother, Vimala Kulkarni, told me similar words when I got married. B. They were usually like: 'You must adjust to your new house and in-laws...' C. When I was young, before a girl got married, her mother would give her some words of advice. D. But along with this, she said something which helped me immensely in later life.
mock17.explanation: C introduces the general practice of mothers giving advice before marriage. B elaborates on what kind of advice. A transitions to personal experience — the author's mother. D reflects on the impact — the special thing her mother said. Correct sequence: C → B → A → D.

mock18.word: Choose the word that means the same as the given word. Calibrate
mock18.explanation: Calibrate (मापना/समंजित करना) means to measure or adjust the accuracy of an instrument. "Gauge" similarly means to measure or determine the extent of something, making it the correct synonym. Gauze is a thin fabric; Assail (हमला करना) means to attack; Affront (अपमान करना) means to insult.

mock18.word: Identify the part that has an error: "The use of calculators / is prohibited / in / the examination."
mock18.explanation: The sentence is correct; "is" agrees with the singular subject "the use," which is the correct verb form here.

mock18.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word:
mock18.explanation: "Voilence" गलत spelling है। Correct spelling है "Violence" (हिंसा)। "Wildernesses" (जंगल), "Exterminated" (नष्ट किया), और "Unmanageable" (अनियंत्रणीय) — सभी correctly spelt हैं।

mock18.word: Sentences of a paragraph about mountain climbers are given in jumbled order. Arrange them correctly. A. However, physical fitness and a stubborn fearlessness never let a climber fail. B. The equipment can be made so light that no extra energy is spent in carrying it. C. To a large extent, the physical difficulties can be reduced by paying attention to the equipment. D. It is certain that weather plays an important part in the success or failure of mountain climbers.
mock18.explanation: D introduces weather's role; C discusses reducing difficulties through equipment; B elaborates on lightweight equipment; A concludes with the importance of fitness and fearlessness.

mock18.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence. Her behaviour was so ostentatious that it attracted the attention of everyone in the room.
mock18.explanation: Ostentatious (आडंबरपूर्ण) means showy or designed to attract attention. "Restrained" (संयमित) means characterized by reserve and moderation, the direct opposite. Attractive means appealing; Gentle means mild; Repulsive (घृणित) means causing disgust — none oppose showiness.

mock18.word: Select the meaning of the given idiom: LOSE YOUR MARBLES
mock18.explanation: "Lose your marbles" (पागल हो जाना) — इस idiom का अर्थ है to go insane or to lose one's mind। Example: "After working for 20 hours straight, he felt like he was about to lose his marbles." "Lose respect" (सम्मान खोना), "Miss an opportunity" (अवसर गँवाना), और "Be nervous" (घबराना) गलत हैं।

mock18.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the correct order: A. Ben was eating an apple, and seemed to be in high spirits. B. Ben Rogers came into view presently, the very boy whose ridicule he had been dreading. C. Tom went on dipping the brush into the paint and whitewashing the fence, and paid no attention to Ben. D. Tom took up his brush and calmly resumed his work.
mock18.explanation: D (Tom resumed work) → B (Ben Rogers appeared) → A (Ben eating apple, high spirits) → C (Tom ignored Ben). Tom is introduced, Ben appears, Ben's mood described, Tom's reaction shown. Correct order: DBAC

mock18.word: The road has been rough, they abandoned their journey further. Select the correct substitution for the underlined part.
mock18.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) being rough. "Being rough" functions as a participial phrase indicating the condition or reason (स्थिति या कारण) that caused them to abandon their journey.

mock18.word: Find the part with an error: "The information contained in this electronic (1)/ message and any attachments to this message (2)/ is intended for the exclusive use of the recipient. (3)/ No Error (4)."
mock18.explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct; "information" is treated as singular, correctly taking the singular verb "is."

mock18.word: You can have accused / me of being lazy, / but I will not finish / the project right now. Identify the segment with the error.
mock18.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) You can have accused. Modal auxiliary verbs (like can, could) must be followed by the base form of the verb. The correct structure is "You can accuse."

mock18.word: Find the part that has an error: "Neither of her cousins is readily to try their luck in this game."
mock18.explanation: The correct phrase should be "ready to try their," as "ready" is the appropriate adjective, not the adverb "readily."

mock18.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. Sara will go to the seminar first and later attend class. (Select option to substitute the underlined word "later")
mock18.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) No substitution required. The word "later" means afterwards in time (बाद में), which perfectly fits the sequence of events in the sentence. No change is needed.

mock18.word: ...gives meaning to the concept of quantification and ______ value for us humans.
mock18.explanation: "Unequivocal" (निर्विवाद/स्पष्ट) — leaving no doubt, giving clear and definite value। Mathematics unambiguous value provide करती है। "Unapparent" (अदृश्य), "Incomprehensible" (अबोधगम्य), और "Undecipherable" (अपठनीय) negative meaning रखते हैं।

mock18.word: A glib … with tremendous reserves of patience is ______ to be successful.
mock18.explanation: "Certain to be successful" means strongly believed to succeed without doubt, fitting the context of a patient orator's guaranteed success in flattery. "Fixed," "Clear," and "Sure" do not form the precise idiomatic collocation "certain to" expressing confident prediction here.

mock18.word: Improve the underlined part of the sentence. Choose 'No improvement' if grammatically correct. He sharpened his pencil, but the lead broken as soon as he started writing.
mock18.explanation: The sentence is in the past tense throughout. "Broken" is a past participle, not a simple past verb. The correct simple past form of "break" is "broke." Correct sentence: He sharpened his pencil, but the lead broke as soon as he started writing.

mock18.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. RIND
mock18.explanation: Rind (छिलका) refers to the outer covering of a fruit, similar to peel, which means to remove the outer skin.
"Out and out" (पूर्ण रूप से) means thoroughly or completely, matching the intended meaning of "complete" used in the sentence.

mock18.word: We have ______ great spiritual values contrasted with materialistic progress of the West.
mock18.explanation: "Inherited" (विरासत में मिला) correctly conveys that these values were passed down through generations, linking back to "inheritors" mentioned earlier.

mock18.word: Choose the word that is opposite in meaning to the given word: REPROVE
mock18.explanation: "Reprove" (डाँटना/आलोचना करना) का अर्थ है to criticize or correct gently। इसका antonym है "Eulogize" (प्रशंसा करना) — to speak or write in high praise of someone। "Rap" (तीखी आलोचना), "Pummel" (पीटना), और "Keelhaul" (कठोर दंड देना) असंबंधित हैं।

mock18.word: Dr Kurt Lewin found that when subjects made a public … to do something, they were more likely to stay the course than if they ______ their wishes to themselves.
mock18.explanation: "Kept their wishes to themselves" contrasts directly with making a public commitment, matching the sentence's logic.

mock18.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. WRENCH
mock18.explanation: Wrench (अचानक मोड़ना/खींचना) means a sudden violent twist or pull. Wrest (बलपूर्वक छीनना) means to forcibly pull something from a person's grasp — same meaning. Supply means to provide something. Reject means to dismiss. Deter means to discourage someone.

mock18.word: Fill in the blank with the most appropriate word. The committee members were in ___ over the budget for the function.
mock18.explanation: "Dissent" (असहमति) means a strong difference of opinion on a subject, fitting the context of disagreement over budget. "Difference" is too generic; "Deference" (आदर) means respect and politeness; "Descent" means movement downward — none fit a disagreement.

mock18.word: The following sentence has been split into segments. Identify the segment that contains a spelling error: "According to a report / every day / thousands of animals / are slautered."
mock18.explanation: The correct spelling is "slaughtered" (वध किया गया), meaning the killing of animals, especially for food.

mock18.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. PERNICIOUS
mock18.explanation: Pernicious (हानिकारक) means having a harmful effect, especially in a gradual or subtle way. "Spiteful" (द्वेषपूर्ण) is the closest in meaning, conveying a wish to harm or hurt. Delicious means tasty; Harmless and Beneficial are actually opposite in meaning to pernicious.

mock18.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech: He said,"I shall need a copy of the documents."
options: He said that he will need a copy of the documents.
He asked if I would need a copy of the documents.
He mentioned that he needed a copy of the documents.
He said he would need a copy of the documents.
mock18.explanation: "Shall" in direct speech changes to "would" in indirect speech, and the pronoun "I" changes to "he" to match the reporting subject.

mock19.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Pick the option that gives their correct order. P. won the right to host the 2020 Games, explicitly stated that Q. Tokyo 2020 represented a key way to open up Japanese R. culture and people to the watching and visiting world S. Shinzo Abe, who was prime minister at the time Japan
mock19.explanation: S introduces Shinzo Abe and his role as prime minister when Japan won hosting rights. P continues with what he explicitly stated. Q explains what Tokyo 2020 represented. R concludes with opening up culture and people to the world. Order: S → P → Q → R.

mock19.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. DISHEARTENED
mock19.explanation: Disheartened means having lost confidence or determination; dispirited. "Depressed" conveys a similar state of unhappiness or low spirits. Disgraced (अपमानित) means having lost respect publicly; Disliked means felt distaste for; Disgusted means feeling revulsion — none match the sense of lost confidence.

mock19.word: Agra's marble-marvel has such an overwhelming presence … other monuments in the town have been relegated ______ oblivion.
mock19.explanation: The verb "relegate" takes the preposition "to" (relegated to oblivion), meaning to pass into insignificance, which fits the description of lesser monuments fading due to the Taj Mahal's prominence. "With," "on," and "at" do not correctly pair with "relegated" in standard English usage.

mock19.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM. PERPETRATE
mock19.explanation: "Perpetrate" (अपराध करना) means to commit a crime. Its antonym is "prevent" (रोकना) — to stop something from happening. Other options are all synonyms.

mock19.word: Choose the word that means the same as the given word. Hostility
mock19.explanation: Hostility (शत्रुता) means antagonism or opposition toward someone. Enmity (वैर/दुश्मनी) means deep-seated hatred or animosity — a direct synonym. Authorize (अधिकृत करना) means to give permission. Anchor (लंगर) and Organize (व्यवस्थित करना) are completely unrelated.

mock19.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence. They realised that the ghost was imperturbable.
mock19.explanation: Imperturbable (अविचलित) means calm and not easily disturbed. "Excitable" (उत्तेजित होने वाला) means easily agitated, the direct opposite. Exasperating means irritating; Startling means surprising; Contaminating means polluting — none capture the idea of being easily disturbed.

mock19.word: Sentences of a paragraph are given below. S1 and S6 are fixed; arrange A, B, C, D. S1. Generation gap is actually the difference between the ebullience of youth and the caution of old age. A. Or, it is expression of the rivalry between the old and the young. B. The old declare the younger lot to be arrogant fools; the young declare the older people to be senile and insipid. C. The old want to prolong their hold on authority while the young are over-eager to grab it. D. The gulf between the two generations cannot be bridged. S6. However, in modern times, lifestyles are changing so fast that the gap becomes easily perceptible.
mock19.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) ACBD. A continues S1's definition. C details the conflict of authority. B gives specific examples of the mutual insults, and D concludes that this divide cannot be bridged (पाटा नहीं जा सकता).

mock19.word: Are you looking forward ___ Nikhil again?
mock19.explanation: "Looking forward to" is a phrasal verb where "to" is a preposition, not part of an infinitive. After a preposition, the gerund form (V+ing) must be used. Therefore "to seeing" is correct. "To see" and "to be seeing" are infinitive forms, incorrect after a preposition.

mock19.word: Sentences of a paragraph about house sparrows are given in jumbled order. Arrange them correctly. A. However, their population is declining nowadays due to noise pollution. B. It is one of the most common birds along with the other two species. C. Their constant presence outside our homes makes it easy to overlook them. D. House sparrows can be found in most places with and without houses.
mock19.explanation: D introduces house sparrows; B notes their commonality; C discusses being overlooked; A concludes with their declining population.

mock19.word: Find the correctly spelt word (reconciliation of the worksheets).
mock19.explanation: Reconciliation (सुलह/मेल-मिलाप) means the restoration of friendly relations. It is spelled with "recon-cili-ation." All other options have vowel errors making them incorrect. Correct sentence: The reconciliation of the worksheets is not an easy job.

mock19.word: Select the word that is ANTONYM of TIMOROUS: "Timorous"
mock19.explanation: "Timorous" (डरपोक/साहसहीन) — showing lack of confidence, fearful। इसका antonym है "Brazen" (निडर/बेशर्म) — bold, shameless, unapologetic। "Gutless" (कायर), "Scared" (डरा हुआ), और "Reserved" (संयमित) — सभी similar या related हैं।

mock19.word: I don't fancy to go out this evening.
mock19.explanation: "Fancy" (पसंद करना) is followed by a gerund (verb+ing), not an infinitive. So "to go out" must be replaced with "going out."

mock19.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. The immediate context is the Dunkirk evacuation. Select their correct order: R. On May 10 the German blitzkrieg attack on the Netherlands began... P. The Dutch defenders fell back westward, and by noon on May 12 German tanks were on the outskirts of Rotterdam. Q. Queen Wilhelmina and her government left the country for England on May 13... S. The invasion of Belgium also began on May 10... [On May 11 the Belgian front was broken...]
mock19.explanation: R (May 10 — Netherlands attack) → P (Dutch fall back, May 12 tanks at Rotterdam) → Q (May 13 — Queen leaves, surrender) → S (Belgium invasion also on May 10 — parallel event). Chronological order of events. Correct order: RPQS

mock19.word: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. Arrange them correctly. (P) are not (Q) The initial days of my high school (R) to remember (S) very good
mock19.explanation: (Q) introduces the subject → (P) is the verb → (S) completes the clause → (R) finishes the sentence.

mock19.word: Select the antonym of 'loathed' as used in the passage.
mock19.explanation: Loathed (तीव्र घृणा करना) का अर्थ है किसी से बेहद नफरत करना। इसका antonym होना चाहिए एक तीव्र सकारात्मक भावना। "Adored" (अत्यंत प्रेम करना) — deep love and respect — loathed का सटीक विपरीत है। "Cursed" (श्राप देना) और "despised" (घृणा करना) दोनों negative हैं; "shunned" (बचना) भी negative connotation रखता है।

mock19.word: (a) one of the primary obstacles encountered (b) is the risk of sample contamination (c) during the analysis of controlled dangerous substance (d) from improper handling and collecting methodologies (e) proof bloodstain patterns and DNA evidence
mock19.explanation: a — subject। c — context। e — specific evidence। b — obstacle। d — cause। Correct Sentence: One of the primary obstacles encountered during the analysis of CDS proof bloodstain patterns and DNA evidence is the risk of sample contamination from improper handling.

mock19.word: Select the most appropriate segment to substitute the underlined segment of the given sentence. If substitution is not required select 'no improvement.' The animal resembled with a cat.
mock19.explanation: The verb "resemble" does not take any preposition after it in standard English, so "resembled with a" must be replaced simply with "resembled," giving "The animal resembled a cat."

mock19.word: Hitler's concentration camps ___ the bodies of the dead captives subsequently.
mock19.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) mutilated. "Mutilated" (अंग-भंग करना) means to inflict serious damage or disfigurement on a body, fitting the grim historical context.

mock19.word: Delhi University's new institution called School of Public Health will offer a ______ of programs.
mock19.explanation: Plethora (बहुतायत) means a very large amount of something. The sentence indicates the institution offers many programs, so "plethora" fits.

mock19.word: The Grim Reaper seems to have ______ in Europe during the 14th century
mock19.explanation: "Appeared" (प्रकट होना) means to become visible or known, fitting the idea that the symbolic figure of the Grim Reaper became known in Europe during this period. "Found" implies something previously lost being located; "Rested" implies stopping after activity; "Died" implies the subject no longer being alive — none fit the sense of a symbol coming into being.

mock19.word: After one's own heart
options: Sharing or having one's tastes or views
Infatuation with a person which is not reciprocated
Don't have the courage to do something bad for a good person
mock19.explanation: "After one's own heart" means someone who shares your interests, tastes, or values. Example: "He's a man after my own heart" — meaning he thinks/acts just like you.

mock19.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Pick the option that gives their correct order. A. "It's sort of cool to be on a ship but even more amazing is this Earth we live on," Williams remarked... B. Williams, who is set to be one of the first to fly aboard Boeing's Starliner, was introduced at the festival by Mike Massimino. C. On Sept. 22, 2018 NASA astronaut Sunita Williams talked to families and space enthusiasts about space travel. D. She was speaking at the Intrepid Air, Sea & Space Museum during the recent Space & Science Festival.
mock19.explanation: C introduces NASA astronaut Sunita Williams and the event context. D describes where she was speaking. B describes her introduction at the festival. A tells what she said when introduced.

mock19.word: Choose the word that is opposite in meaning to the given word. Sane
mock19.explanation: Sane (स्वस्थमना) means having or showing reason, sound judgment, or good sense; in a healthy mental condition. Paranoid (संदेहग्रस्त/पागलपन) means characterized by delusions of persecution — opposite of sane. Reposeful means calm and peaceful. Stoical means enduring pain without showing feelings. Halcyon means idyllically happy and peaceful.

mock19.word: Find the word that is the ANTONYM OF piquant
mock19.explanation: Piquant (तीखा/रोचक) means having a pleasantly strong or spicy taste. Bland (बेस्वाद) means not having a strong or interesting taste — exact opposite. Pungent means having a strong taste or smell — similar to piquant. Interesting means attracting attention. Peppery means easily annoyed.

mock19.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the correct order: A. A fine carriage with rubber tyres...rolled up to the house of a landowner called Gryabov. B. A drowsy footman met him in the hall. C. "Are the family at home?" asked the Marshal. D. Fyodor Andreitch Otsov, the district Marshal, jumped out of the carriage.
mock19.explanation: A (carriage arrives) → D (Marshal gets out) → B (footman meets him) → C (Marshal asks question). Scene set करो, character introduce करो, action describe करो, dialogue दो। Correct order: ADBC

mock20.word: Select the correct active form of the given sentence: "By whom have these facts been verified?"
mock20.explanation: Passive → Active conversion में: Subject को doer बनाओ, verb को active form में लाओ। "Have been verified" (Present Perfect Passive) → "has verified" (Present Perfect Active)। "By whom" → "Who"। Format: Who + has + verified + these facts? Correct sentence: "Who has verified these facts?"

mock20.word: I settled ______, hoping the kid would calm down.
mock20.explanation: "Settled down" (शांत होकर बैठ जाना) means to sit comfortably and relax. The narrator settled down hoping the child would stop shrieking.

mock20.word: Parts of sentences are given below in jumbled order. Arrange to form a meaningful paragraph. R (fixed). Mahatma Gandhi led a long struggle against untouchability A. Dr B R Ambedkar, the father of the Indian Constitution, belonged to the lower B. Such as entry into temples and public places C. And worked for the welfare of the so called lower castes D. Caste and made many efforts to secure basic rights for them
mock20.explanation: Correct Answer: (C) CADB. C connects directly to Gandhi's struggle. A introduces Dr. Ambedkar and links directly to D ("lower Caste"). B provides examples of the rights mentioned in D.

mock20.word: We were busy at the work and went for lunch only at 2.30 pm.
mock20.explanation: "At the work" is incorrect. The correct fixed phrase is "at work" (without any article). Certain fixed prepositional phrases never take articles.

mock20.word: In mourning, it is an essential part of coming ______ terms with loss.
mock20.explanation: The fixed phrase is "coming to terms with loss," so "to" is the correct preposition.

mock20.word: Direction: Sentences of a paragraph in jumbled order. A. Even getting what they want doesn't satisfy some kids; they only want more. B. Too many parents these days can't say no. C. The struggle to set limits has never been tougher—and the stakes have never been higher. D. As a result, they find themselves raising children who respond greedily to the advertisements aimed right at them.
mock20.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) BDAC. B introduces the main premise (parents can't say no). D provides the immediate result of this. A elaborates on the children's greedy reaction, and C concludes with the broader struggle of parenting.

mock20.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. Mike decided to skip work.
options: Work is being skipped by Mike.
Work is decided to be skipped by Mike.
The work was decided to be skipped by Mike.
mock20.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) The work was decided to be skipped by Mike. This option accurately maintains the meaning of the principal verb ("decided") in the passive structure.

"Heading into" गलत है। "Heading for" का अर्थ है something is likely to happen soon। Example: "It appears that the rebels are heading for victory." Correct sentence: "It was apparent that the girl was heading for a serious disaster."

mock20.word: It is one of the most plentiful and essential ______ of compounds.
mock20.explanation: "Essential of compounds" — यहाँ "of" group membership को indicate करता है। "One of the most plentiful of compounds" — compounds के group में से एक। "For," "In," और "With" grammatically गलत हैं।

mock20.word: Select the correct direct form: "Balaji said that he had already paid the instalment when the notice came to him."
options: Balaji said,"I already paid the instalment when the notice had come to me."
Balaji said,"He has already paid the instalment when the notice came to him."
Balaji said,"I had already paid the instalment when the notice came to me."
Balaji said,"I have already paid the instalment when the notice will come to me."
mock20.explanation: Since the reporting verb has no object,"said" remains unchanged; "he" becomes "I," and the past perfect tense "had already paid" remains unchanged, as past perfect tense does not change in narration transformation.

"There being no porters" — Absolute Phrase structure में present participle "being" प्रयुक्त होता है। यह independent clause का काम करता है। "Going" (जाना), "Been" (past participle — गलत), और "Having" (रखना) इस structure में गलत हैं।

mock20.word: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. Making a show of being morally superior to others
mock20.explanation: Sanctimonious (पाखंडी) means giving the impression that you feel you are better and more moral than other people — exact match. Sanctum means a holy place. Profligate means using money, time, or materials carelessly. Approving means showing that you believe something is good.

mock20.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Cold comfort
mock20.explanation: "Cold comfort" (बहुत कम संतोष) का अर्थ है poor or inadequate consolation। Example: Another drop in the inflation rate was cold comfort for the jobless.

mock20.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Play it by ear
mock20.explanation: "Play it by ear" means to handle a situation in an impromptu or flexible manner without a fixed plan in advance, often used when plans need to change based on circumstances. For example, "Since the meeting schedule is uncertain, let's play it by ear."

mock20.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Judiciously (विवेकपूर्ण तरीके से)
mock20.explanation: "Judiciously" का अर्थ है using good judgment। इसका antonym "Irrational" (not based on reason — तर्कहीन) है।

mock20.word: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the right order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. An old mouse, who was wise enough to keep his distance, whispered to a friend, "Many a bag have I seen in my day, but never one with a cat's head." B. She hoped that the mice would no longer be afraid to come near her. C. A cat, grown feeble with age and no longer able to hunt mice as she once did, thought to herself how she might entice them within reach of her paw. D. Thinking that she might pass herself off for a bag or for a dead cat at least, she suspended herself by the hind legs from a peg.
mock20.explanation: C introduces the main idea (the feeble cat scheming), D follows describing her plan to disguise herself, B shows her hope that the plan would work, and A concludes with the wise old mouse seeing through the trick, completing the fable.

mock20.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word: WAX
mock20.explanation: "Wax" (बढ़ना) का अर्थ है to grow larger or increase। इसका antonym है "Wane" (घटना) — to grow smaller or decrease। "Mourn" (शोक करना), "Error" (गलती), और "Insolent" (ढीठ) असंबंधित हैं।

mock20.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Compunction
mock20.explanation: Compunction (पश्चाताप) means a feeling of guilt that follows wrongdoing. Scruples, meaning a feeling of doubt that prevents wrong action, matches this closely.

mock20.word: The unfortunate results of ______ too far are obvious in the environmental degradation.
mock20.explanation: "Venturing" (जोखिम भरा कार्य करना) means undertaking a risky or daring course of action, fitting the context that taking unbalanced risks led to environmental degradation. "Pioneering" means introducing new methods; "Risking" means exposing to danger but doesn't fit grammatically as "venturing too far" does; "Climbing" means ascending — none fit as precisely.

mock20.word: Sukhen always seeks to capitalise on opportunities. Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word.
mock20.explanation: Capitalise (लाभ उठाना) means to take advantage of something. Forfeit, meaning to lose or give up something, is the opposite of benefiting from an opportunity.

mock20.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word given in the bracket to fill in the blank: "The development of the region depended on the ___ (rapaciousness) of the corporate contribution."
mock20.explanation: "Rapaciousness" (लालच) means aggressively greedy. Its antonym is "Generosity" (उदारता) — the quality of being kind and giving. "Voracity" is a synonym.

mock20.word: Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it. If there is no error, choose 'No error.' People that are lonely have higher levels of the hormone cortisol.
mock20.explanation: "That" is used for things, not people. When referring to human beings, "who" must be used. Correct sentence: People who are lonely have higher levels of the hormone cortisol.

mock20.word: Select the option that will improve the underlined part: "As long as he completes his work on time, I have no cause for worry."
mock20.explanation: "As long as" correctly means "provided that," and the sentence is already grammatically correct as written.

mock20.word: He was never in the best of tempers ______ anyway.
mock20.explanation: "Anyway" (वैसे भी/चाहे जो हो) — यह indicate करता है कि Anders का temper हमेशा ही खराब था, circumstances चाहे जो हों। "Somewhat" (कुछ हद तक), "Always" (हमेशा), और "Whenever" (जब भी) इस nuance को capture नहीं करते।

mock20.word: Brick … tempted him with ______ of water and he loved the palm pith that was used as … for baking bricks.
mock20.explanation: "Plenty of" correctly precedes the uncountable noun "water," meaning an abundant amount, fitting the context that brick kilns offered him generous amounts of water as an attraction. "Many" is used with countable nouns; "Load" requires a different sentence structure; "Much" is more typical in negative or question constructions, not affirmative statements like this one.

mock21.word: It is also rich in fibre and ______ in weight loss as it contains saturated fats.
mock21.explanation: "Aids" (सहायता करती है) — singular verb जो singular subject "it" (Ragi) के साथ agreement रखती है। "Ragi aids in weight loss" — correct usage। "Services" (सेवाएँ), "Benefit" (लाभ), और "Help" (मदद) — subject के साथ verb agreement नहीं बनाते।

mock21.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom: 'After the government interventions, the enrolment and retention of female students have increased by leaps and bounds.'
mock21.explanation: 'By leaps and bounds' means making very rapid progress or growing very quickly, in a sudden and remarkable way. Among the options, swiftly best captures the idea of rapid growth. Note: While 'swiftly' is given as the correct option, 'rapidly' or 'dramatically' would be even more precise synonyms in normal usage. Origin: Believed to come from the sport of long jump, where athletes make a running start and leap as far as possible; first used in the 16th century for physical activity, later used metaphorically for rapid progress.

mock21.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute. A speech by an actor at the end of a play
mock21.explanation: "Epilogue" का अर्थ है the concluding part of a literary work, especially a play। Prologue — beginning part।

mock21.word: If no substitution is required, select 'No improvement.' Your coming home to dinner on time should be a rule rather the exception.
options: should be a rule rather being the exception
shall be a rule rather than a exception
should be the rule rather than the exception
mock21.explanation: The correct idiom is "the rule rather than the exception" — both "rule" and "exception" require the definite article "the." "Rather than" is the correct conjunction here, not just "rather." Options A, B, and D either miss the article or use wrong conjunctions.

mock21.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Decadent
mock21.explanation: Correct Answer: (C) Virtuous. "Decadent" (पतनशील) characterizes a state of moral or cultural decline. "Virtuous" (सदाचारी), meaning having high moral standards, is its opposite.

mock21.word: Select the most appropriate synonym. RESILIENT
mock21.explanation: "Resilient" (लचीला) means able to spring back into shape. "Supple" means moving and bending with ease — both describe flexibility and elasticity.

mock21.word: "Their plan of acquisition was ___ flawed from the very beginning; it was bound to fail."
mock21.explanation: "Fatally" (घातक रूप से) means causing failure or disaster. "Fatally flawed" means so severely flawed that failure was inevitable.

mock21.word: Sentences of a paragraph are given below. S1 and S6 are fixed; arrange A, B, C, D. S1. Under normal conditions A. talk of nothing in particular B. he would welcome us with a smile C. crack a joke or two D. for a couple of minutes S6. and then state the actual business.
mock21.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) BCAD. The sequence starts with greeting (B), followed by cracking a joke (C), making small talk (A), and its duration (D), which flows perfectly into stating the actual business (S6).

mock21.word: Experts said that several steps were being taken to promote foreign trade as it constituted 45% of the country's economy. (Change to Direct Speech)
options: Experts said, "Several steps should be taken..."
Experts said, "Several steps had been taken..."
Experts said, "Several steps are being taken to promote foreign trade as it constitutes 45% of the country's economy."
Experts said, "Several steps were being taken..."
mock21.explanation: In indirect speech, past continuous "were being taken" shifts back from present continuous "are being taken." Similarly, "constituted" (past) shifts back from "constitutes" (present) in direct speech.

mock21.word: Parts of a sentence about Pakistan's constitution are given in jumbled order. Arrange them correctly. O: its first constitution P: and soon got himself elected Q: the administration of the country R: general Ayub Khan took over S: after Pakistan framed
mock21.explanation: S sets the time context; O completes the constitutional reference; R introduces Ayub Khan's takeover; Q specifies what he took over; P concludes with his subsequent election.

mock21.word: Harry was the only person to have ever (survived) an attack by Voldemort — by somehow ______ the latter's 'killing curse'.
mock21.explanation: "Rebounding" का अर्थ है to bounce back from a negative event — Harry ने curse को वापस Voldemort पर ही लौटा दिया।

mock21.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock21.explanation: "Athelete" is incorrectly spelt; the correct spelling is "Athlete" (खिलाड़ी), meaning a person skilled in sports. "Convenience" means ease of doing something, "Dilemma" (दुविधा) means a difficult choice, and "Guarantee" means a formal assurance — all correctly spelt.

mock21.word: The following sentence has been divided into parts. The world will/ not stop for you, / it will keep on going. Select the part with the error, or 'No error'.
mock21.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) No error. The sentence maintains the correct tense and subject-verb agreement throughout.

mock21.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock21.explanation: The correct spelling is Raspberry (रास्पबेरी), a small soft red fruit. "Russberry" has both incorrect vowels and arrangement. Refrigerator, Pistachio, and Treasure are all correctly spelled.

mock21.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: MUNDANE
mock21.explanation: "Mundane" (साधारण/नीरस) का अर्थ है ordinary, lacking excitement — रोजमर्रा की जिंदगी से जुड़ी सामान्य चीज़ें। "Ordinary" (साधारण) इसका सटीक synonym है। "Abnormal" (असामान्य), "Wonderful" (अद्भुत), और "Exciting" (रोमांचक) सभी इसके विपरीत अर्थ रखते हैं।

mock21.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the word 'VAULT':
mock21.explanation: "Vault" (तिजोरी/भूमिगत कमरा) — a secure room for valuables, or an underground chamber। इसका synonym है "Crypt" (भूमिगत कक्ष) — an underground room or vault beneath a church। "Raisins" (किशमिश), "Ration" (राशन), और "Monarch" (सम्राट) असंबंधित हैं।

mock21.word: "Transnational linkages, for example, sever the nation-state's integrity and corrupt state-society relations."
mock21.explanation: The word "cohesion" refers to the action or fact of forming a united whole — for example, "The cohesion among the team members was evident in their smooth coordination during the project." The word "erode" means to gradually wear away or be worn away by natural agents — for example, "The coastline has been eroded by the sea." Together, "cohesion and erode" fit the sentence's meaning about national integrity being worn away. The distractors work as follows: "divergence" means the process of diverging or moving apart; "relativity" refers to the dependence of phenomena on relative motion (most famous as Einstein's theory); "severance" means the action of ending a connection or relationship; "revolution" means a forcible overthrow of a government or social order; "controversy" means prolonged public disagreement; and "diminution" means a reduction in size or importance. None of these pairs match the sense of the original sentence as precisely as "cohesion and erode."

mock21.word: ...zero, the base of the present ______ system.
mock21.explanation: "Numeral system" (अंक प्रणाली) correctly refers to the number system, unlike the unrelated mystical terms.

mock21.word: All the horses, except one, always / behaved in a rough manner when / Robert come to clean the stable.
options: All the horses, except one, always
behaved in a rough manner when
Robert come to clean the stable
mock21.explanation: "come" (present tense) गलत है। "behaved" के साथ tense consistency के लिए "came" (past tense) होना चाहिए। Correct Sentence: ...when Robert came to clean the stable.

mock21.word: The bus from Haridwar to Delhi ______ at 11:15 a.m.
mock21.explanation: The trigger word is "daily" — indicating a habitual, repeated, or scheduled action. For habitual and scheduled actions, we use Simple Present Tense. The subject "bus" is singular and third person, so the verb must take the third-person singular form with -s: "arrives." Rule: For timetables, schedules, and habits — use Simple Present Tense (e.g., "The train leaves at 6 AM every day"). Why other options are wrong: "Arrived" = Simple Past tense (wrong — we need present); "Come/Arrive" = base form without -s (wrong — singular subject needs "arrives"); Also, in the context of transport/schedules, "arrives" is more precise and appropriate than "comes."

mock21.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Pick the option that gives their correct order. A. Now, that the real estate sector has got its own regulator, RERA, from May 1, 2017, things are expected to improve. B. For many of the home buyers, across locations the delay has extended to almost six years or more. C. In the absence of a regulator and with no rules in place, the builder-buyer battle appeared one-sided. D. For the buyers of real estate properties the delay in possession of their homes has been the biggest concern.
mock21.explanation: D introduces the subject — delay in possession of homes. B quantifies the delay extent. C explains why (no regulator). A provides the resolution — RERA introduced. Paired sentences DB (D introduces problem, B quantifies it), then C (reason), then A (solution). Correct sequence: D, B, C, A.

mock21.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A person preoccupied with an unrealistically optimistic approach to life
mock21.explanation: "Quixotic" (अवास्तविक रूप से आदर्शवादी) describes a person who is extremely idealistic, unrealistic, and impractical. Pragmatic and Practical describe someone dealing with problems realistically; Rational describes someone using logical thought — all opposite to the idealistic sense intended here.

mock21.word: Select the word that is similar in meaning (SYNONYM) to the word given below. Glib
mock21.explanation: Glib means fluent and voluble but often shallow or insincere; in this context it aligns with "Easy," meaning achieved without effort. Inarticulate (अस्पष्ट) means unable to express clearly; Implausible (अविश्वसनीय) means not believable; Sincere is actually opposite in meaning to glib, since glib implies insincerity.

mock21.word: Parts of a sentence about driving offences are given in jumbled order. Arrange them to form a meaningful sentence: Did (a)/ offences (b)/ know (c)/ you (d)/ that men (e)/ four times (f)/ commit (g)/ driving (h)/ as many (i)/ women do? (j)/ as (k)
options: a, d, c, e, g, f, i, h, k, j, b
a, d, c, e, f, g, i, h, k, j, b
a, c, d, e, g, f, i, h, k, j, b
d, a, c, e, g, f, i, h, k, j, b
mock21.explanation: The correctly arranged sentence reads,"Did you know that men commit four times as many driving offences as women do?" following logical question structure.

mock21.word: If no substitution is needed, select 'No substitution.': 'There was a bell over the door that jingled whenever a customer entered.'
mock21.explanation: The original sentence is grammatically correct. Relative pronoun 'that' can be used for both people and things - here it refers to 'a bell' (a thing), so 'that' is correct. 'Whenever' means every time, appropriate since the bell rings every time a customer enters. 'Jingled' (past tense) is correct for the past-tense narrative. Option A's 'wherever' means 'in whatever place' - makes no sense here. Option B's 'who' is used for people, not things. Option C's 'jingle' is present tense, inconsistent with the past-tense narrative. Relative Pronoun Rules: Who = for people only; Which = for things and animals (non-restrictive clauses); That = for people OR things (restrictive clauses); Whose = for possession.

mock22.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock22.explanation: The correct spelling is "Plaque" (एक स्मारक पट्टिका), meaning an ornamental tablet fixed to a wall in commemoration of a person or event.

mock22.word: Over generations, genetic ______ can lead to evolutionary change.
mock22.explanation: "Mutation" (उत्परिवर्तन) specifically refers to genetic changes passed down generationally, matching the context.

mock22.word: In the sentence identify the segment which contains the grammatical error. We must get this post send as soon as possible.
options: We must get this post send
mock22.explanation: Since the action of sending has not yet occurred but needs to be completed, the passive participle "sent" should be used instead of the base form "send," giving "We must get this post sent as soon as possible."

mock22.word: Harry and Meghan have decided to work and become financially independent.
options: have decide to work and become
has decided to work and become
have decided to work and became
mock22.explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct. "Harry and Meghan" is plural, so "have decided" is correct. Both "decided" and "become" are correctly parallel in form.

mock22.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given word. Pugnacious
mock22.explanation: "Pugnacious" means eager or quick to argue, quarrel, or fight — for example, "The pugnacious student was always ready to debate with his teachers." "Waspish" describes someone who is readily inclined to express anger or irritation — for example, "Her waspish comments made the discussion very tense and uncomfortable." Both words describe a sharp, combative disposition, making "waspish" the best match. The distractors describe different temperaments: "impatient" means quickly irritated by delay, which is related but not as specifically combative; "good-tempered" means even-tempered and calm, the opposite of pugnacious; "arrogant" means having an exaggerated sense of one's own importance, which is a separate trait from quarrelsomeness.

mock22.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom: TO PUT SOMEONE'S BACK UP
mock22.explanation: "To put someone's back up" (किसी को चिढ़ाना) — इसका अर्थ है to make someone annoyed or angry। Example: "Just ignore him, he's only trying to put your back up." "Help" (मदद करना), "Deceive" (धोखा देना), और "Support" (समर्थन करना) गलत हैं।

mock22.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. They asked me my name.
options: My name is asked by them.
My name was asked by them.
mock22.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) I was asked my name. The sentence is in the simple past tense. The indirect object "me" becomes the subject "I," taking the past passive verb "was asked."

"Looking around into" गलत है — दो prepositions एक साथ नहीं चलते। "Looking into" का अर्थ है to investigate या examine a possibility। Correct sentence: "Vishnu and family are looking into the possibility of moving to Canada."

mock22.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Fundamental
mock22.explanation: Fundamental (मौलिक/आधारभूत) means basic, core, or essential. Neutral (तटस्थ) implies the absence of a defining or foundational position — the closest opposite. Impact means effect. Least refers to quantity. Link means connection — none of these are antonyms.

mock22.word: Choose the correct spelling of the underlined word. If the given spelling is correct, choose 'No correction.' This rule is necesary to maintain discipline.
mock22.explanation: The correct spelling is necessary. It means something that is needed for a purpose or reason. The original word "necesary" is missing the double 's'.

mock22.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Hesitate
mock22.explanation: Hesitate (हिचकिचाना) means to pause in uncertainty. Dither, meaning to be indecisive, closely matches this meaning.

mock22.word: Choose the word that means the same as the given word. Quandary
mock22.explanation: Quandary (दुविधा) means a difficult situation where one is unsure what to do. Impasse (गतिरोध) means a situation where no progress can be made — the closest synonym. Cinch means something easy. Clique is an exclusive small group. Breeze means something effortless.

mock22.word: Gifting is generally taken to be the easiest act to ______, if only you have the heart to part with something in your ______.
mock22.explanation: "Perform" (करना) — an act को perform करना। "Possession" (स्वामित्व/अधिकार) — जो चीज़ आपकी है उसे देना। "To perform an act...in your possession" — logically coherent। बाकी options context में गलत हैं।

mock22.word: Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error: "Jerry driving / home late / when the incident / took place."
mock22.explanation: The auxiliary verb "was" is missing before "driving" to form the past continuous tense; the correct phrase is "Jerry was driving."

mock22.word: Identify the sentence that has no spelling error.
options: She tried to manuvre her trolley around people.
The computer's software detected an anamoly caused by a virus.
A clergyman read the litugry from the prayer-book.
What advice did he give to young entrepreneurs?
mock22.explanation: "Entrepreneurs" (उद्यमी) is spelt correctly here; the other sentences misspell "manoeuvre," "anomaly," and "liturgy" respectively.

mock22.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word. The next move of the opponent (प्रतिद्वंद्वी) was anticipated (पूर्वानुमानित) by the team.
mock22.explanation: "Anticipated" का अर्थ है foresee या expect करना। "Predicted" भी यही अर्थ रखता है — दोनों समानार्थी हैं।

mock22.word: He was enjoying himself sitting alone ______ the fireside.
mock22.explanation: "By the fireside" (आग के पास) — fireplace के बगल में बैठना। "By" यहाँ location को indicate करता है। "In" (अंदर), "Over" (ऊपर), और "Near" (पास) — सभी grammatically possible हों लेकिन "by the fireside" एक fixed expression है।

mock22.word: I am tired ______ this, Chandler.
mock22.explanation: "Tired of" एक fixed collocation है जिसमें "of" preposition का प्रयोग होता है। Correct Sentence: I am tired of this, Chandler.

mock22.word: Sentences of a paragraph given below in jumbled order: A. Targeted advertising is playing an increasingly important role in social media marketing. B. It wasn't until a few years ago that people began to perceive social media as a marketing tool. C. Social media has long been regarded as a means of communicating with people via instant messaging and blogging. D. In marketing, social media is a quick and easy means to share a company's information, such as advertising and products or services available to customers.
mock22.explanation: C explains the original use of social media. B transitions to its modern use as a marketing tool. D explains this marketing role, and A concludes with targeted advertising specifically.

mock22.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Opaque
mock22.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) Intelligible. "Opaque" (अपारदर्शी / अस्पष्ट) abstractly means not able to be seen through or hard to understand. The opposite is "Intelligible" (समझने योग्य).

mock22.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. VITIATE
mock22.explanation: Vitiate (दूषित करना/कमजोर करना) means to impair the quality of something. Affirm (पुष्टि करना) means to state confidently or uphold — the direct opposite. Violate, Annul, and Cancel are all close in meaning to vitiate or further its negative sense.

mock22.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word: MAVERICK
mock22.explanation: "Maverick" (स्वतंत्र विचारक) — a person with independent, unusual opinions। इसका antonym है "Conformist" (परंपरागत) — one who behaves like everyone else and does not want to be different। "Custodian" (रखवाला), "Versatility" (बहुमुखी प्रतिभा), और "Brilliance" (प्रतिभा) असंबंधित हैं।

mock22.word: The average weather in a location over several years is ______ to as the climate.
mock22.explanation: The fixed phrase "referred to as" is the correct collocation (शब्द-युग्म) describing how something is termed.

mock22.word: Select the correct Indirect form of the sentence. Anil said, "I want you to help me with my plants."
options: Anil said that he wanted to help me with my plants.
Anil says that he wants to help me with my plants.
Anil said that I wanted you to help me with my plants.
Anil said that he wanted me to help him with his plants.
mock22.explanation: Since there is no third-person listener mentioned, the main reporting verb remains "said." The first-person pronouns "I," "me," and "my" become "he," "him," and "his," while the second-person "you" (referring to the reader/listener) becomes "me," giving the correct indirect form in option D.

mock22.word: Fill in the blank with an appropriate option. My heart ___ hard, bumping the broken glass, nicked by sharp edges.
mock22.explanation: "Thumped" (जोर से धड़का) conveys a strong, rhythmic pounding — exactly describing a rapidly beating heart. Lifted means raised higher. Inhibited means restrained. Thundered implies a loud external sound, not a heartbeat.

mock23.word: It left a legacy of instant and ______ death.
mock23.explanation: "Deferred" (विलंबित) means delayed or postponed, fitting the context that some deaths from the disaster occurred immediately while others happened after a long delay due to lingering poisoning effects. "Averted" means prevented; "Momentary" means lasting only a moment; "Detained" means held back — none capture the idea of delayed consequence.

mock23.word: Identify the segment which contains a grammatical error: "Two elderly gentlemen told the rather improbable story to mislead the police personnel."
mock23.explanation: यहाँ "the rather improbable" में article का गलत प्रयोग है। Indefinite article 'a' होना चाहिए क्योंकि यह एक विशेष कहानी का पहला उल्लेख है। Correct sentence: "Two elderly gentlemen told a rather improbable story to mislead the police personnel."

mock23.word: Hopefully, things will return to normal in the _____.
mock23.explanation: The auxiliary verb "will" indicates future tense. Only "future" correctly completes the sentence — things returning to normal is a future expectation.

mock23.word: Identify the segment that contains the error: "Among the reasons cited by the employee, for quitting the job, were the pressure of work, the outstation duties and desiring to pursue her hobbies."
options: Among the reasons cited by the employee
and desiring to pursue her hobbies
were the pressure of work, the outstation duties
mock23.explanation: "Desiring" should be in the simple form "desire" to maintain grammatical parallelism with the other listed reasons.

mock23.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined word. "Sarika's husband had been undergoing vicissitudes in his fortune since 2002."
mock23.explanation: "Vicissitudes" (उतार-चढ़ाव) refers to unwelcome changes and fluctuations in life. "Tribulations" (कष्ट) means great troubles or suffering — the closest in meaning. "Hibernation" (शीतनिद्रा) means a state of inactivity in winter; "Consolations" (सांत्वना) means comfort after a loss.

mock23.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Hold your horses
options: To keep the horse chain intact
Holding and keeping things by yourself
Give time to others for some rest
To tell someone to stop and consider carefully
mock23.explanation: "Hold your horses" (रुको और सोचो) is used to tell someone to stop and consider carefully their decision or opinion.

mock23.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word: DOWNCAST
mock23.explanation: "Downcast" (उदास/निराश) का अर्थ है feeling despondent or sad। इसका antonym है "Cheerful" (प्रसन्न) — noticeably happy and optimistic। "Disappointed" (निराश), "Distressed" (कष्टग्रस्त), और "Successful" (सफल) antonym नहीं हैं।

mock23.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. It is suggested that you do your duty to help the poor in your parish.
options: The poor people in your parish are to be helped by you.
You may be allowed to help the poor people in your parish.
Helping poor people is your duty in your parish.
The poor in your parish should help you.
mock23.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) The poor people in your parish are to be helped by you. This correctly changes the active objective ("help the poor") into a passive subject with "are to be helped," reflecting duty.

mock23.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Take away your breath
mock23.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) Astonishing. To "take someone's breath away" means to inspire greatness, awe, or surprise. "Astonishing" (दंग कर देना) fits this perfectly.

mock23.word: We are not likely to abandon the city as a ______ institution...
mock23.explanation: "Cultural institution" (सांस्कृतिक संस्था) — passage में museums, concert halls, sports teams का ज़िक्र है, ये सब cultural activities हैं। "Educational" (शैक्षणिक), "Psychological" (मनोवैज्ञानिक), और "Religious" (धार्मिक) इस broader context को capture नहीं करते।

mock23.word: Select the most appropriate idiom that can substitute the underlined segment. "Unless sales improve next month, the company will be in a critical position in which failure seems imminent."
mock23.explanation: "Up against the wall" (मुश्किल में होना) means being in a desperate situation with limited options. "A damp squib" (निराशाजनक परिणाम) means something that fails to meet expectations.

mock23.word: Select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the Idiom/Phrase. To breast the tape
mock23.explanation: "To breast the tape" (दौड़ जीतना) originates from competitive running where a tape is stretched across the finish line. The first runner to break this tape with their chest wins. It figuratively means to be first to accomplish something.

mock23.word: If no substitution is required, select No improvement. The big farmers with deepest tubewells still have water, but many others face a water crisis.
mock23.explanation: Since a comparison is being made between two groups of farmers (those with better access to water versus others), the comparative degree "deeper" should be used instead of the superlative "deepest."

mock23.word: was soon ______ by the sun.
mock23.explanation: "Lightened" means becoming lighter in colour due to sun exposure, fitting the description of fading clothing.

mock23.word: Select the antonym of the given word. SOBER
mock23.explanation: "Sober" means serious, sensible, and solemn (and, in its other common sense, not affected by alcohol — composed and steady). "Agitated" means feeling or appearing troubled, anxious, or nervous, which is the opposite of being composed and steady. Be careful here: "serious" is actually a near-synonym of "sober," and "calm" describes a state of not showing strong emotion — which is also close in meaning to "sober," not its opposite. "Nervous" (anxious or apprehensive) is closely related to "agitated" but is listed as a distractor to test precision rather than as the correct answer. The key distinction to remember is that sober implies calm self-control, so its antonym must denote a loss of that composure — making "agitated" the best fit.

mock23.word: Select the correct passive form of the given sentence. Bhavna saw him carrying your suitcase.
options: He had been seen by Bhavna carrying your suitcase.
He was seen by Bhavna carrying your suitcase.
Your suitcase was seen by Bhavna carrying him.
Your suitcase carried by him was seen by Bhavna.
mock23.explanation: Active voice: Bhavna (subject) + saw (verb) + him (object). Passive: "him" becomes "he" (subject); "saw" becomes "was seen" (simple past passive); "by Bhavna" is added.

mock23.word: Select the correct active form of the given sentence. It is said that Einstein was a genius.
options: People have said that Einstein was a genius.
People are saying that Einstein was a genius.
People say that Einstein was a genius.
People said that Einstein was a genius.
mock23.explanation: "It is said that" (impersonal passive) translates to active using "People say that." The tense is present simple ("is said"), so the active also uses present simple ("say").

mock23.word: If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution.' You have better taken care of your health.
mock23.explanation: "Had better" (बेहतर है कि) is a fixed phrase used to advise someone, followed by the base form of the verb (infinitive without "to"), so it must be "had better take," not "have better taken."

mock23.word: Schools will restart physical classes partially and states have ______ their own conditions and procedures to ensure the safety of students.
mock23.explanation: Devised (योजना बनाना) means to plan or invent something by careful thought. The sentence discusses preparations made by schools, so "devised" fits best. Insisted means to assert firmly; accepted means to agree; adopted means to take up.

mock23.word: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. A. If you complete one of your assignments early B. do not work on any other assignment C. go back through that assignment only D. if you are found working on another assignment E. this is not permitted F (fixed). Your contract will be terminated.
mock23.explanation: A provides the condition. C gives the instruction. B warns against other assignments. E states it's not permitted, and D sets up the conditional consequence leading to F.

mock23.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word given in bold: SOLICITOUS
mock23.explanation: "Solicitous" (चिंतित/परवाह करने वाला) का अर्थ है showing care and concern। इसका antonym है "Heedless" (लापरवाह) — showing reckless lack of care or attention। "Worried" (चिंतित) तो near synonym है, "Knowledgeable" (ज्ञानी) और "Interesting" (रोचक) असंबंधित हैं।

mock23.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute: "Give off gas bubbles"
mock23.explanation: "Effervescent" (बुलबुले छोड़ने वाला) — giving off bubbles; fizz। "Buoyant" (तैरने वाला), "Flat" (बुलबुले रहित — opposite), और "Effuse" (बहना/रिसना) असंबंधित हैं।

mock23.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Having a chip on one's shoulder
options: Having an unnecessary feeling of guilt
Having an irritated and unfriendly attitude
Having a proud and self-centred arrogance
Showing great resistance to any adversities
mock23.explanation: This idiom describes someone who is easily offended or has a confrontational attitude, often due to feeling wronged in the past.

mock23.word: Select the most appropriate word for the given group of words: "A slowly moving mass or river of ice"
mock23.explanation: "Glacier" (हिमनद) — a slowly moving mass or river of ice formed by accumulation and compaction of snow। "Typhoon" (तूफान), "Avalanche" (हिमस्खलन — snow falling rapidly), और "Blizzard" (भीषण बर्फीला तूफान) — सभी अलग weather phenomena हैं।

mock23.word: When combined with the ______ provided by the public authority, conventional information by local people can prevent animals from succumbing to floods.
mock23.explanation: "Undertaking" (प्रयास / कार्य) used as a noun here refers to the effort or initiative provided by the authorities, fitting the sentence structure.

mock24.word: People from all ______ of life, rich and poor, rub shoulders with each other.
mock24.explanation: "All walks of life" (जीवन के सभी क्षेत्रों से) — यह एक fixed idiom है जिसका अर्थ है people from different backgrounds। "Parts" (हिस्से), "Tours" (यात्राएँ), और "Groups" (समूह) इस idiom में नहीं आते।

mock24.word: ______ these people were not scholars, but looters and grave robbers looking to make money
mock24.explanation: "Often" (अक्सर) is an adverb meaning many times on different occasions, fitting the context that the people digging up monuments were frequently looters and grave robbers. "Regularly" implies fixed intervals; "Seldom" means rarely, which contradicts the meaning; "Commonly" means by most people, which is less precise here than "often."

mock24.word: Given below are six sentences. Sentence A is the beginning and F is the last. The middle four are jumbled. Select their correct order: A. We soon ran into a nomad's tent... B. She quickly made us cups of butter tea... C. This time our hostess was a young woman. D. Only then did we notice what a picturesque figure our young hostess made. E. The warmth ran through our frozen bodies... F. She wore a sheepskin cloak...
mock24.explanation: A (scene set) → C (hostess introduced) → B (tea made) → E (warmth felt) → D (noticed her appearance) → F (her description). C introduces hostess, B her action, E effect of warmth, D noticing her appearance — logical narrative flow. Correct order: CBED

mock24.word: The shopkeeper was obliged to dispense to the service of his salesman.
mock24.explanation: "Dispense with" is the correct phrasal verb meaning to discard or terminate. The preposition "to" is incorrect; "with" must be used, and "services" (plural) is more appropriate.

mock24.word: (Climatic disasters passage) To mitigate the effects of these disasters, it is essential to invest in disaster preparedness and _________.
mock24.explanation: Response (प्रतिक्रिया/राहत कार्य) refers to a reaction or reply to a situation — here it means disaster response measures alongside preparedness.

mock24.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Tell upon
mock24.explanation: "Tell upon" means to have an effect on someone or something, usually in a negative or detrimental way. Example: This over-working of the brain began to tell upon his mental health.

mock24.word: The young king tried to speak, but his tongue seemed to _____ the roof of his mouth.
mock24.explanation: "Cleave to" (चिपक जाना) means to stick or adhere firmly. The phrase "tongue clove to the roof of the mouth" is a well-known expression describing difficulty speaking.

mock24.word: Fill in the blank with an appropriate option. The ___ of revenge demanded a life for a life.
mock24.explanation: Obligations (दायित्व/कर्तव्य) refers to duties one is bound to perform morally or legally. In the context of a revenge code demanding equal retribution, "obligations" perfectly fits the serious tone. Cheering, Moaning, and Crowning are all contextually inappropriate.

mock24.word: ..people who ______ healthy one day might be dead the next."
mock24.explanation: "People who were healthy one day might be dead the next" — past simple tense में condition describe हो रही है। "Seemed," "Looked," और "Appeared" — सभी appearances को indicate करते हैं, actual health status को नहीं।

mock24.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock24.explanation: The correct spelling is "Contemptible" (घृणित), meaning deserving contempt or despicable.

mock24.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Elusive
mock24.explanation: Elusive (मायावी) means difficult to find, catch, or understand. "Definite" (निश्चित) means clearly stated and not vague, making it the opposite. Explosive means prone to explode; Affordable means reasonably priced; Rigid means inflexible — none oppose "hard to grasp."

mock24.word: Choose the correct word for "A period of time in the past that was idyllically happy and peaceful."
mock24.explanation: Halcyon (सुखद और शांतिपूर्ण काल) refers to a period in the past that was idyllically happy and peaceful. Memory trick: During a cyclone there is destruction — Halcyon is the opposite, i.e., calm and joyful. Accommodate means to lodge. Condone means to overlook wrongdoing.

mock24.word: ..he got ______ behind two women whose loud, stupid conversation..."
mock24.explanation: "Got stuck" (फँस गया) — Anders दो women के पीछे queue में फँस गया। "Got stuck behind two women" — most natural expression। "Trapped" (फँसाया गया), "Placed" (रखा गया), और "Held" (रोका गया) less natural हैं।

mock24.word: Select the word that is OPPOSITE in meaning to the underlined word. On several occasions he defended the cause of moderation and justice against the fanatical crusaders.
mock24.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) Liberal. "Fanatical" (कट्टर) implies single-minded, intolerant extremism. Its opposite is "Liberal" (उदार), meaning open to new behavior or opinions.

mock24.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the word given in the brackets to fill in the blank. The university team won the match in the end with a _________ (cunning) move.
mock24.explanation: Cunning (चालाक) means having or showing skill in achieving one's ends by deceit. Slick (चालाकीपूर्ण/कुशल) means clever at persuading people, skillful and effective in a clever but not completely honest manner — same meaning. Brilliant means exceptionally clever or talented. Random means without method or conscious decision. Quick means moving fast.

mock24.word: It is a sin against God and humanity to ______ places of religious worship.
mock24.explanation: Desecrate means to treat a sacred place or thing with violent disrespect; to violate or profane something holy. (Hindi: पवित्र स्थान को अपवित्र करना / अपमान करना) The context gives two powerful clues: "sin against God and humanity" + "places of religious worship" — both point to an act of religious violation, which is desecration. Example: Vandals desecrated the ancient temple. Other options fail grammatically or contextually: Upright (adjective/adverb — wrong part of speech for a verb slot); Disapprove = to have an unfavourable opinion (too mild for "sin against God"); Modest = unassuming (completely unrelated).

mock24.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice: "Historians tell us how this country was divided into three."
options: We are told by historians how this country has been divided into three.
We are told by historians how this country divided into three.
We are told by historians how this country was divided into three.
We have been told by historians how this country was divided into three.
mock24.explanation: The main clause "Historians tell us" converts to "We are told by historians," while the subordinate clause tense ("was divided") remains unchanged.

mock24.word: Markets do not work in a fair manner when producers are few and powerful whereas consumers purchase in small amounts and are ______.
mock24.explanation: "Scattered" (बिखरे हुए) contrasts with "few and powerful" producers, describing widely dispersed consumers.

mock24.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A person as a source of intellectual light or moral inspiration.
mock24.explanation: Luminary (प्रकाशक/प्रेरणास्रोत) means a person who inspires or influences others, especially one prominent in a particular sphere. Lurch (अचानक झटका) means a sudden movement. Luminaire (प्रकाश उपकरण) is a complete electric light unit. Lunette (चाप आकार की खिड़की) is an arched aperture.

mock24.word: Select the idiom that gives the most appropriate meaning of the underlined phrase. "'Honesty is the best policy' does not remain valid in the present times."
mock24.explanation: "Hold good" (वैध रहना) means to remain valid or applicable. So, "does not hold good" matches "does not remain valid".
"For" indicates purpose; the team anticipates a pitch beneficial for their victory, making "pitch for their easy victory" correct.

mock24.word: Ben was always been a good father. Select the correct substitution.
mock24.explanation: Correct Answer: (C) had always been. The sentence indicates consistent past behaviour, requiring the past perfect tense. The adverb "always" is correctly placed between the auxiliary verb "had" and the main verb "been."

mock24.word: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given: 'A written statement made under oath'
mock24.explanation: Affidavit means a written statement confirmed by oath or affirmation, typically used in legal proceedings - the correct answer. Testimony refers to a spoken or written statement given as evidence, generally in court - a broader term. Affirmation is a declaration/confirmation but does not necessarily involve an oath. Statement is a very general term for any declaration, oral or written. Legal Vocabulary: Affidavit = written + under oath; Testimony = spoken + in court; Deposition = sworn testimony given outside court; Affirmation = declaration without religious oath.

mock24.word: If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No improvement.' My shoes were worn out so I had to buy a new one.
mock24.explanation: Since "shoes" is plural, the replacement item should also be referred to in the plural as "new ones," not the singular "a new one," to maintain grammatical agreement.

mock24.word: A wooden frame for holding an artist's work while it is being painted or drawn
mock24.explanation: Easel (चित्रफलक) means a wooden frame for holding an artist's work while it is being painted or drawn — exact match. Lectern is a tall stand with a sloping top. Platform is a raised level surface. Podium is a small platform on which a person may stand.

mock25.word: The following sentence has been divided into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. She will discuss the project / (with her mentor) / when / she would meet him tomorrow.
mock25.explanation: In a time clause (after conjunctions like "when"), we use the present tense, not the future. The correct form should be "she meets him tomorrow."

mock25.word: "Everything in the village was grown organically as the majority of the population indulged in farming for ___."
mock25.explanation: "Sustenance" (जीविका) means the maintaining of someone in life or existence — i.e., earning a living. "Nourishment" means food for health.

mock25.word: She decided to go on a/an ______ vacation.
mock25.explanation: "Recreational" का अर्थ है relating to activity done for enjoyment। Example: She decided to go on a recreational vacation.

mock25.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Commotion
mock25.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) Bustle. "Commotion" (हलचल) is a state of confused and noisy disturbance. "Bustle" represents a similar noisy, energetic activity.

mock25.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word: DESIDERATE
mock25.explanation: "Desiderate" (तीव्र इच्छा करना) का अर्थ है to feel a keen desire for something। इसका antonym है "Disregard" (अनदेखा करना) — to take no notice or treat as unimportant। "Acquire" (प्राप्त करना), "Mandate" (आदेश), और "Quarrel" (झगड़ा) असंबंधित हैं।

mock25.word: Select the option that correctly expresses the given sentence in passive voice: "Why didn't she bring her son to my house?"
options: Why wasn't her son brought by her to my house?
Why isn't her son brought to my house?
Why wasn't her son brought to my house?
Why isn't her son brought by you to my house?
mock25.explanation: The past simple passive structure "wasn't + V3" is used, with "by her" retained to indicate the original subject performing the action.

mock25.word: the urgent need to disguise the ravages of smallpox, ______ left their faces pitted and scarred.
mock25.explanation: "Which" relative pronoun है जो non-essential additional information देता है। "Smallpox, which left their faces pitted" — सही है।

mock25.word: Given below are six sentences, four of which, are jumbled. Pick the option that gives their correct order. Sentence 1. The British love chocolate. P. In fact, they spend more on chocolate than any other European country! Q. There is milk chocolate, dark chocolate and white chocolate. R. If you go to any newsagent's shop in the UK you'll see a huge selection of chocolate bars on sale. S. There are small bars of chocolate, medium-sized bars and massive bars of chocolate. Sentence 6. You can buy mint-flavoured chocolate, chocolate-covered peanuts or raisins, chocolate with nuts, chocolate with toffee... the list is endless!
mock25.explanation: P logically follows Sentence 1 by supporting the claim of Britain's love for chocolate with a spending statistic. R then introduces the wide variety of chocolate bars seen in shops, while Q and S list the different kinds and sizes of chocolate, leading naturally into Sentence 6's extended list of chocolate varieties.

mock25.word: The town of Wall stands today as it has ______ for six hundred years, on a high ______ of granite slab
mock25.explanation: "Jut" (बाहर निकला होना) — granite slab का forest से ऊपर उठना। "A high jut of granite slab" — यह physical description है। "Trench" (खाई), "Furrow" (नाली), और "Line" (रेखा) इस geographical feature को describe नहीं करते।

mock25.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Mechanical (यांत्रिक)
mock25.explanation: "Mechanical" का अर्थ है operated by machine। इसका antonym "Manual" (done by hand or person — हाथ से किया गया) है।

mock25.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock25.explanation: The correct spelling is "Fortuitous" (संयोगवश), meaning happening by chance, especially as a pleasant surprise.

mock25.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Serendipity
