mock25.explanation: Serendipity (सुयोग) means the occurrence of fortunate events by chance. "Misfortune" (दुर्भाग्य) means bad luck or an unfortunate event, making it the direct opposite. Tangibility means being perceptible by touch; Permanency means lasting indefinitely; Tranquillity (शांति) means calmness — none oppose the idea of fortunate chance.

mock25.word: Choose the incorrectly spelt word.
mock25.explanation: The correct spelling is Consent (सहमति), meaning to give permission for something. "Conscent" is not a valid English word. Splendid (शानदार), Meagre (अपर्याप्त), and Marriageable (विवाह योग्य) are all correctly spelled.

"Chanted" (एक साथ गाया/जपा) — a form of singing done together, rhythmically। "Sang together in a concert" का best substitute है "chanted together in a show।" "Enunciated" (स्पष्ट उच्चारण किया), "Enjoined" (आदेश दिया), और "Articulated" (स्पष्ट किया) गलत हैं।

mock25.word: Our host started to read and the ______ stopped.
mock25.explanation: "Chatter" (बकबक/गपशप) — reading शुरू होने पर लोगों की बातचीत रुक गई। Complete: "Our host started to read and the chatter stopped." "Noise" (शोर), "Laughter" (हँसी), और "Music" (संगीत) less specific हैं।

mock25.word: The boy had run away / because he couldn't stand / his father beat him / for one more day.
mock25.explanation: "stand" के बाद gerund (क्रिया का -ing रूप) आता है। "beat" की जगह "beating" होना चाहिए। Correct Sentence: The boy had run away because he couldn't stand his father beating him for one more day.

mock25.word: Direction: Sentences of a paragraph in jumbled order. A. On 13 January 1928, the press was presented with this scientific achievement, which made the front pages of several major national newspapers. B. These were all in research labs. C. On 7 September 1927, Philo Farnsworth broadcasted the first 'television' system. D. Needless to say, a straight line was not mass marketable, therefore there were only a few dozen televisions in the globe by the end of the 1920s.
mock25.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) CADB. C introduces the chronological start (1927). A details the press presentation the following year (1928). D mentions the limited marketability, and B notes that these limited models were in research labs.

mock25.word: the sky transformed into a radiant canvas, painted with ______ of orange, pink, and gold.
mock25.explanation: "Hues" (रंग) means shades of colour, fitting the description of the sky's colours.

mock25.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Sordid
mock25.explanation: Sordid (अनैतिक) means involving dishonourable actions. Honourable, meaning honest and deserving respect, is its direct opposite.

mock25.word: It stirs the mind so ______ that it causes great unrest.
mock25.explanation: "Violently" (हिंसक / ज़ोरदार रूप से) conveys the intense, disruptive force that causes "great unrest," matching the sentence's intensity.

mock25.word: The chef's culinary creations were infused with a distinctive ___ that set them apart from typical restaurant fare.
mock25.explanation: "Flair" (विशेष शैली) means a distinctive talent or stylish characteristic in performing an activity, fitting the context of unique culinary creations. "Flare" means a sudden burst of light; "Fair" means just or impartial; "Flere" means "some or a few" — none fit this context.

mock25.word: Elders are the ______ force for any family and hence the love and respect for elders comes from … and is not artificial.
mock25.explanation: "Driving force" (प्रेरक शक्ति) is a fixed phrase meaning the main motivating power behind something, fitting the description of elders as central to family unity. "Caring" means feeling concern; "Diverting" means causing to change direction; "Deriving" means obtaining from a source — none form a standard phrase with "force" in this context.

mock25.word: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains the grammatical error. The Indian Railways are setting to run special trains for the Ardh Kumbh Mela — the mega holy event that is slated to start soon.
mock25.explanation: The fixed construction "set to + verb" (meaning planned or ready to begin) should be used, not "setting to." The correct sentence reads "The Indian Railways are set to run special trains..."

mock25.word: Out of the four sentences given, three have spelling errors. Identify the sentence that has no spelling error.
options: Meenakshi, a former councillor, is aspiring for a ticket to contest the elections.
Meenakshi, a former councelor, is aspiring for a ticket to contest the elections.
Meenakshi, a former counciler, is aspiring for a ticket to contest the elections.
Meenakshi, a former councilor, is aspiring for a ticket to contest the elections.
mock25.explanation: The correct spelling is "councillor" (पार्षद), meaning a member of a council; the other options contain spelling errors in this word.

mock25.word: ...they release their moisture through ______, which can fall as rain...
mock25.explanation: Precipitation (वर्षण) refers to moisture falling from clouds as rain, snow, etc., matching the described process.

mock26.word: are opting to ______ from Delhi driven by the increasing air pollution.
mock26.explanation: Families are "migrating" (पलायन करना) away from Delhi due to air pollution. The context of moving away from a polluted city strongly suggests "migrate" or "move away."

mock26.word: Inscrutable is an adjective which means impossible to understand. Select the most appropriate antonym.
mock26.explanation: Inscrutable (समझ से परे) means difficult to interpret. Comprehensible, meaning able to be understood, is its direct opposite.

mock26.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute. The soldier struck the enemy with the short sword fixed onto the end of the gun.
mock26.explanation: "Bayonet" का अर्थ है a blade fixed to the muzzle of a rifle used to stab। Example: The soldier fixed his bayonet to the end of his rifle.

mock26.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock26.explanation: "Potraite" is incorrectly spelt; the correct spelling is "Portrait" (चित्र), meaning a painting or drawing of a person. "Pageant" refers to a public entertainment procession, "Pamphlet" is a small informational booklet, and "Persevere" (दृढ़ता से लगे रहना) means to continue despite difficulty — all correctly spelt.

mock26.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom: 'This year's budget is unlikely to pass as law since the president's proposed budgets typically face a jaundiced eye, but this one has gotten even more scepticism.'
mock26.explanation: 'Jaundiced eye' means a view or attitude characterized by cynicism, scepticism, or prejudice; it implies looking at something with distrust and negativity. 'Jaundiced' literally refers to the yellowish tint caused by jaundice, a disease that can cause a distorted view; metaphorically it means a prejudiced or cynical view. Among the options, resentment (anger or displeasure about something unfair) most closely aligns with this cynical, negative attitude. Jealousy means envy - not quite the same. Confidence and Goodwill are opposite in spirit. Medical → Metaphor: Jaundice (medical condition) → Jaundiced eye (idiom for prejudice/cynicism).

mock26.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Wipe the floor with someone
mock26.explanation: "Wipe the floor with someone" (किसी को बुरी तरह हराना) means to inflict a humiliating defeat on someone. Example: They wiped the floor with us in a 3-0 win.

mock26.word: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. Robin has less marbles than George.
mock26.explanation: "Less" is used with uncountable nouns. "Marbles" is a countable noun, so "few" is needed. Since a comparison is being made between two entities, the comparative form "fewer" (कम — गणनीय संज्ञाओं के लिए) is correct. Correct sentence: Robin has fewer marbles than George.

mock26.word: Select the most appropriate idiom (in the context) to fill in the sentence. Factory owners are protesting because they think that the government has _________ of plastic manufacturing companies by closing their factories in big cities.
options: Separated the wheat from the chaff
Been barking up the wrong tree
mock26.explanation: "Upset the apple cart" (योजना बिगाड़ना) means to ruin plans or arrangements; to spoil something. Factory owners feel the government has spoiled their plans — perfect fit.

mock26.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Made his mark
mock26.explanation: "Made his mark" means to achieve something notable. "To distinguish himself" (खुद को अलग करना / पहचान बनाना) captures this meaning perfectly.

mock26.word: It's running … two-week campaign calling on Koreans and foreigners ______ to keep their eyes peeled for … in English, Japanese and Chinese text.
mock26.explanation: "Calling on Koreans and foreigners alike" is a fixed phrase construction meaning an appeal made equally to both groups, fitting the context of the inclusive campaign. "Similarly," "same," and "like" do not form this standard idiomatic phrase with "alike" at the end.

mock26.word: If there was such a thing as perfect food, eggs would be a ______.
mock26.explanation: Contender (दावेदार) is a noun describing something that competes for a title, fitting the metaphor of eggs being a "perfect food" candidate.

mock26.word: Find the correctly spelt word.
mock26.explanation: "Stridency" (कर्कशता) is the correct spelling, meaning harshness of tone or manner. Example: "Many employees were alarmed by the director's new stridency."

mock26.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. The sewage water smells foul.
options: The sewage water has a foul smell
The sewage water is foul when it is smelt
mock26.explanation: When a sentence uses a linking/sensory verb + adjective, the passive voice follows this structure: Subject + verb (be, according to tense and number) + Adjective + when + pronoun (according to number) + helping verb + 3rd form of the main verb. Example pattern: Active: "Rose smells sweet." → Passive: "Rose is sweet when it is smelt." Applying the same pattern: Active: "The sewage water smells foul." → Passive: "The sewage water is foul when it is smelt." Important note: The official SSC answer key for this question marked Option A ("Passive construction is not possible") as correct. However, the grammatical passive construction DOES exist for sensory verbs as demonstrated. Testbook's solution also supports Option C as the correct passive construction.

mock26.word: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. Solicit votes from electors or members.
mock26.explanation: Canvass (मत माँगना) means to try to get political support or votes by visiting people in an area. Resident means one who lives in a place. Cellar is a room below the ground floor. Biopsy is a medical tissue examination.

mock26.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock26.explanation: "Exhilerate" गलत spelling है। सही spelling "Exhilarate" (उत्साहित करना) है।

mock26.word: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. Select the option that arranges the parts in the correct sequence to form a meaningful sentence. (P) dreams provide an escape (Q) where reality is suspended (R) into a world (O) of endless possibilities
mock26.explanation: The sentence begins with part (P), "dreams provide an escape," which introduces the subject. Part (R), "into a world," logically follows, describing where this escape leads. Part (O), "of endless possibilities," continues the thought by describing the nature of that world. The sentence then ends with part (Q), "where reality is suspended," which finalizes the description of this imagined world. Reading it together: "Dreams provide an escape into a world of endless possibilities where reality is suspended." Option A (OPQR) breaks the flow by placing "into a world" prematurely; Option C (OQRP) places "where reality is suspended" too early, disrupting the natural progression; Option D (RPOQ) starts with "into a world," which lacks context since nothing has been introduced yet.

mock26.word: He named it ______ his friend and general Champa...
mock26.explanation: "Named after" (के नाम पर) is a fixed phrase meaning to give someone's name to something, fitting the naming of the city.

Reading शुरू होने से पहले लोग बात कर रहे थे। Host के पढ़ना शुरू करते ही "chatter" (गपशप/बातचीत) रुक गई। "Silence" (शांति — already present होती), "Reading" (पढ़ना), और "Noise" (शोर — less specific) गलत हैं।

mock26.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. The woodcutter felled / some trees / with hardly / many effort at all.
mock26.explanation: "Hardly any" (मुश्किल से कोई) is the correct fixed phrase meaning "almost no," so "many" must be replaced with "any" to make the sentence correct: "The woodcutter felled some trees with hardly any effort at all."

दो errors हैं: (1) Preposition "on" गलत — "turned a deaf ear" के बाद "to" आता है। (2) "Advices" गलत — "Advice" uncountable noun है, इसका plural नहीं होता। Correct sentence: "He turned a deaf ear to the advice of his parents."

mock26.word: We should never ___ with the rules of driving.
mock26.explanation: "Tamper" (हेरफेर करना) means to interfere with something improperly, fitting the warning against meddling with driving rules. "Reckon" means to consider; "Trifle" means to treat without seriousness; "Temper" relates to mood — none fit the sense of interference here.

mock26.word: Fill in the blank with the correct word: '____ he was late, he came home by nine.'
mock26.explanation: The sentence presents a contrast: he was late, yet he managed to come home by nine - best expressed by the conjunction 'although,' meaning despite the fact that. 'Even' alone is not a conjunction - 'even though' or 'even if' would work, but not 'even' by itself. 'Because' introduces a reason, but there is no cause-effect relationship here, only contrast. 'Despite' requires being followed by a noun/gerund, not a clause. Contrast Conjunctions: Although, though, even though, while, whereas, however, but, yet, nevertheless, nonetheless. Correct Sentence: Although he was late, he came home by nine.

mock26.word: Select the option that will improve the underlined part: "Megha's habit of procrastination puts her colleagues on lot of trouble."
mock26.explanation: "Put to a lot of trouble" एक phrasal verb है जिसका अर्थ है to cause problems for someone। "On lot of trouble" गलत है — "to a lot of trouble" सही है। Correct sentence: "Megha's habit of procrastination puts her colleagues to a lot of trouble."

mock26.word: The finest example is the ______ of bottled water...
mock26.explanation: "Sale" directly relates to the commercialisation theme of the passage, referring to bottled water being marketed and sold for profit.

mock26.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock26.explanation: "Acquarium" गलत spelling है। Correct spelling है "Aquarium" (मछलीघर) — 'c' यहाँ नहीं आता। "Pledge" (वचन), "Bouquet" (गुलदस्ता), और "Abridge" (संक्षिप्त करना) correctly spelt हैं।

mock27.word: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. The act of looking back on past time.
mock27.explanation: Retrospection (अतीत की समीक्षा) means the act of looking back on or reviewing past events or situations. Introspection means examining one's own mental processes. Circumspection means cautiousness. Inspection means close examination.

mock27.word: Find the part of the given sentence that has an error: "Atmospheric rivers are long, narrow bands of moisture in the atmosphere that extends from the tropics to higher latitudes."
options: atmospheric rivers are long, narrow bands
of moisture in the atmosphere that extends
from the tropics to higher latitudes
mock27.explanation: Subject "bands" plural है इसलिए verb भी plural होनी चाहिए। "Extends" की जगह "extend" होना चाहिए — subject-verb agreement का नियम। Correct sentence: "Atmospheric rivers are long, narrow bands of moisture in the atmosphere that extend from the tropics to higher latitudes."

mock27.word: Spreading awareness about the rights of the consumers and the relief open to them in case they did not get their money's worth of goods and services has rightly been identified as the first priority.
options: do not get their money's worth
did not getting their money's worth
will not get their money's worth
mock27.explanation: Main clause present perfect tense में है, इसलिए subordinate clause में present tense "do not get" होना चाहिए। Correct Sentence: ...in case they do not get their money's worth.

mock27.word: Identify the segment which contains the grammatical error: 'She'd rather (1)/ play the drums (2)/ than sing. (3)/ No Error (4)'
mock27.explanation: The sentence 'She'd rather play the drums than sing' is grammatically correct. Grammar Rule: After 'would rather' (or 'd rather'), we use the base form (infinitive without 'to') of the verb, and both actions in the comparison must follow this pattern. Both 'play the drums' and 'sing' are correctly in base form. Structure: Subject + would rather + [Base Verb] + than + [Base Verb] (e.g. I would rather walk than drive.)

mock27.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Pick the option that gives its correct order. A. There he brought them up as his own children. B. He took them away to his hut. C. He found the two children and the basket. D. One day an old man came to the jungle.
mock27.explanation: D introduces the old man independently. C tells what he found. B tells what he did with the children. A concludes how he raised them. Order: D → C → B → A.

mock27.word: ...she had totally lost ______ at the party.
mock27.explanation: Context में Gabriella party में नहीं थी, घर पर थी। "Lost herself" (खो गई/मग्न हो गई) — किताब में इतनी खोई हुई थी। Complete: "she had totally lost herself at the party" — यानी party में रहते हुए भी किताब में डूबी थी।

mock27.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word: IGNOMINY
mock27.explanation: "Ignominy" (अपमान/बदनामी) का अर्थ है public shame and loss of honour। इसका antonym है "Glory" (गौरव) — fame or honour achieved for something। "Restorative" (पुनर्स्थापक), "Pejorative" (अपमानजनक), और "Beneficial" (लाभकारी) असंबंधित हैं।

mock27.word: This is because ______ species disperse seeds only through elephants.
mock27.explanation: "Certain" (कुछ निश्चित) means some specific ones, but not all, fitting the context that only some plant species rely exclusively on elephants for seed dispersal. "No," "all," and "every" are too absolute and don't logically fit the idea that only some species depend solely on elephants.

mock27.word: Sentences of a paragraph given below in jumbled order. A. This means adverse health effects on the people of our country B. Such as high blood pressure, high cholesterol, diabetes, heart diseases leading to premature death. C. It is assuming alarming proportions in our country. D. To avoid this, lifestyle needs to be changed. S1 (fixed). Obesity is a condition where the body accumulates excess fat, leading to a considerable increase in body weight. S6 (fixed). Lack of sports and sedentary lifestyle also contribute to higher obesity level.
mock27.explanation: C highlights the rise of obesity defined in S1. A mentions the health effects. B lists those specific effects. D provides a solution before linking to the lifestyle factors in S6.

mock27.word: Select the most appropriate 'one word' for the expression given below. Fear of heights
mock27.explanation: Acrophobia (ऊंचाई का डर) is the fear of heights — derived from Greek "akron" (edge/peak) and "phobos" (fear). Agoraphobia is fear of open/crowded spaces. Claustrophobia is fear of enclosed spaces. Homophobia is negative attitude toward homosexuality.

mock27.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom: 'Eat humble pie'
options: To live a life of humility
To enjoy life to the fullest
mock27.explanation: 'Eat humble pie' means to admit one's fault, mistake, or inferiority in a humble and contrite manner; to apologise under embarrassing circumstances. Historical Origin: The idiom comes from Medieval England where 'umble pie' (from 'umbles' - the offal/internal organs of a deer) was served to lower-class people while the wealthy ate prime meat. To eat humble/umble pie was to accept a lower social position; over time the phrase evolved to mean admitting one's error or inferiority.

mock27.word: Ram placed her in one of the ______ leather chairs opposite his desk.
mock27.explanation: "Quilted leather chairs" (रजाई जैसी बनावट वाली चमड़े की कुर्सियाँ) — two layers of fabric with padding, stitched in patterns। Furniture को describe करने का appropriate term। "Marinated" (मैरीनेट किया हुआ), "Brewed" (पीसा हुआ), और "Stewed" (उबाला हुआ) — food preparation terms हैं।

mock27.word: date back about 1,500 years ago, and ______ the epitome of Indian rock-cut architecture.
mock27.explanation: The sentence uses past tense context ("date back"). "Were" (थे) correctly shows that the caves were considered the epitome of Indian rock-cut architecture historically.

mock27.word: A vertical passageway into a mine
mock27.explanation: "Shaft" (सुरंग/खान-मार्ग) means a long, narrow, typically vertical hole that gives access to a mine. Other options refer to passages or waterways of different kinds.

mock27.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. A GOOD TURN
mock27.explanation: "A good turn" means a kind or helpful act done for someone else without expecting anything in return. (Hindi: भला काम / उपकार) Example 1: He did you a good turn by resigning. Example 2: You did me a good turn warning me that Abigail was going to be there. The phrase emphasises doing something directly beneficial or useful for another person. It is NOT about luck or opportunity — those are external things, whereas a 'good turn' is a deliberate act of kindness.

mock27.word: Direction: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the given sentence. The deteriorated building was declared unsafe for the inmates.
mock27.explanation: Deteriorated means having become worse or damaged. "Dilapidated" (जीर्ण-शीर्ण) means old and broken, especially of buildings, matching the context exactly. Dissipated means scattered or wasted; Meliorated means improved; Reprobated means condemned — none fit a damaged building.

mock27.word: In fact, … was sweeping the piazza in front of the … church in preparation for market day, ______ the deputy mayor
mock27.explanation: "Local" (स्थानीय) is the correct adjective describing a church belonging to the town, fitting grammatically before the noun "church." "Next," "neighbour," and "near" are either adverbs or nouns that don't fit grammatically as adjectives modifying "church" here.

mock27.word: It is ______ the characteristic of the British culture...
mock27.explanation: "Perhaps" (शायद) — uncertainty या possibility express करता है। Author यहाँ British crowd की discipline के बारे में uncertain tone में बात कर रहा है। "Moreover" (इसके अलावा), "Only" (केवल), और "Therefore" (इसलिए) इस context में fit नहीं होते।

mock27.word: Select the correctly spelt word.
mock27.explanation: "Chauvinist" सही spelling है। Immigrant, Contemporary, Cosmopolitan बाकी सही spellings हैं — Imigrant और Cosmopoliton गलत हैं।

mock27.word: Despite being his competitor, John was generous and forgiving in defeat and congratulated his opponent on his well-deserved victory.
mock27.explanation: The phrase "generous and forgiving" describes noble, large-hearted behaviour, especially in victory or defeat. "Magnanimous" (महानुदार) exactly captures this quality. Altruistic (निःस्वार्थ) means selflessly concerned for others; Fainthearted (डरपोक) means lacking courage; Venomous (विषैला) means full of malice — none fit the context.

mock27.word: Select the most appropriate opposite word to substitute the underlined segment: 'The cynical investors believed in every word the analysts said.'
mock27.explanation: Cynical means believing people are motivated purely by self-interest, distrustful of human sincerity. Credulous means having too great a readiness to believe things, easily fooled - the direct opposite of cynical. The sentence paradoxically says the cynical investors 'believed every word,' so the question asks for the antonym of cynical, which is credulous. Distrustful is actually a synonym of cynical, not an antonym. Persuasive and credible are unrelated/apply to different things. Antonym Pair (High-Frequency SSC): Cynical ↔ Credulous / Naïve / Trusting / Gullible.

mock27.word: India is already full of ______ pegs in round holes, who are unfit for their roles and are doing more harm than good.
mock27.explanation: "Square pegs in round holes" (अनुपयुक्त व्यक्ति) is a fixed idiomatic expression meaning people placed in roles unsuitable for them, exactly matching the context describing people unfit for their professional roles. "Large," "small," and "circular" do not complete this fixed idiom.

mock27.word: was set up in 1964 and ______ in the year 1965
mock27.explanation: "Authorised" (अधिकृत किया गया) सबसे उपयुक्त है — Indrajal Comics को 1965 में characters के लिए authorise किया गया था।

mock27.word: A group of fish
mock27.explanation: मछलियों के समूह को "School" या "Shoal" कहते हैं। Troop — soldiers का समूह। Bunch — things का समूह।

mock27.word: Identify the segment which contains a grammatical error: "The two buses moving side by side gave the illusion of been stationary."
mock27.explanation: "Been stationary" गलत है। यहाँ preposition 'of' के बाद present participle चाहिए। "Been" past participle है जो यहाँ अनुचित है। सही रूप "being stationary" होगा। Correct sentence: "The two buses moving side by side gave the illusion of being stationary."

mock28.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Latent
mock28.explanation: "Latent" (गुप्त / अव्यक्त) means existing but not yet developed or manifest; hidden. Its antonym is "Manifest" (स्पष्ट) — meaning clear or obvious.

mock28.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM for 'apprehension' in the given sentence. Shradha was never in such a situation before; her excitement was understandable for her peers.
mock28.explanation: Apprehension (भय, चिंता) means fear or anxiety about something uncertain. "Excitement" (उत्साह) means a state of being thrilled, which is opposite to the nervous feeling of apprehension. Before, Peers, and Situation are unrelated words that merely appear elsewhere in the sentence.

mock28.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom: IN YOUR BIRTHDAY SUIT
options: a suit which covers one's entire body
a suit which is designed for only birthday functions
mock28.explanation: "In your birthday suit" (बिना कपड़ों के) — इसका अर्थ है not wearing any clothes। Example: "They were sunbathing in their birthday suits." बाकी तीनों options literal अर्थ लेते हैं जो गलत है।

mock28.word: Select the wrongly spelt word.
mock28.explanation: "Dissiminiate" गलत spelling है। Correct spelling है "Disseminate" (प्रसारित करना)। "Nascent" (उभरता हुआ), "Malleable" (लचीला), और "Obsequious" (चापलूस) correctly spelt हैं।

mock28.word: ...some may be taken up by plants and released back into the atmosphere through a process called ______.
mock28.explanation: Transpiration (वाष्पोत्सर्जन) is the process by which plants release absorbed water as vapour, matching the context exactly.

mock28.word: Hurry up! There's still _________ work left to do.
mock28.explanation: "Much" is used with uncountable nouns to indicate a large quantity. "Work" is an uncountable noun.

mock28.word: Select the most appropriate idiom (in the context) to fill in the sentence. He tries to perform well but good projects done by him are _________ He needs to put in extra effort.
mock28.explanation: "Few and far between" (कभी-कभार) means not happening often; rare. The sentence implies he does very few good projects, making this idiom perfect.

mock28.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. PONDER
mock28.explanation: Ponder means to think about something carefully before deciding. "Meditate" (विचार करना) similarly means to think deeply about something, making it the correct synonym. Terminate means to end; Agitate means to trouble or disturb; Celebrate means to mark an occasion with joy — none match.

mock28.word: Select the correct option to substitute the given set of words. To skip about playfully (repeat)
mock28.explanation: Gambol (खेलते हुए उछलना) means to run or jump about playfully. (Duplicate of an earlier item in the source document.)

mock28.word: She is suffering from ____.'
mock28.explanation: Acrophobia means an extreme or irrational fear of heights - the correct answer. Heliophobia means an extreme/irrational fear of sunlight. Sociophobia means an extreme/irrational fear of social situations or interactions. Claustrophobia means an extreme/irrational fear of confined or enclosed spaces. Phobia Vocabulary (SSC Favourite Topic): Acrophobia = heights; Claustrophobia = confined spaces; Agoraphobia = open/crowded spaces; Hydrophobia = water; Xenophobia = foreigners/strangers; Heliophobia = sunlight; Sociophobia = social situations.

mock28.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Fastidious
mock28.explanation: Fastidious (अतिसूक्ष्मदर्शी/सावधान) means very attentive to and concerned about accuracy and detail. Indifferent (उदासीन) means having no particular interest; unconcerned — opposite of fastidious. Difficult means needing much effort or skill. Fussy means hard to please. Critical means expressing adverse comments.

mock28.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. One who is well-versed in the knowledge of plants.
mock28.explanation: Botanist (वनस्पति शास्त्री) is an expert in the scientific study of plants. Florist (फूलों का व्यापारी) sells flowers. Gardener (माली) tends a garden. Vegetarian (शाकाहारी) avoids meat — none of these involve scientific plant knowledge.

mock28.word: Select the option that will improve the underlined part: "The walls of the temple caved out due to weak construction."
mock28.explanation: "Caved in" (धराशायी होना) means to collapse inward, the opposite of "caved out," which is grammatically incorrect for describing collapse.

mock28.word: Identify the segment which contains a grammatical error: "Mona took a little time to thought before she gave her consent to the proposal."
mock28.explanation: "To thought" गलत है। Infinitive 'to' के बाद हमेशा V1 (base form of verb) आती है। इसलिए "thought" की जगह "think" होना चाहिए। Correct sentence: "Mona took a little time to think before she gave her consent to the proposal."

mock28.word: The following sentence has been divided into parts. The team dwelt with / their performance / in the last quarter. Select the part with the error.
mock28.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) The team dwelt with. The correct phrasal verb is "dwelt on," which means to think or speak about a subject at length (विस्तार से सोचना या बात करना). "Dwelt with" is incorrect.

mock28.word: Courts are ______ of the Constitution as they are expected to see that it is always respected.
mock28.explanation: The sentence describes courts as entities that watch over and protect the Constitution. 'Sentinels' means people or groups that watch over someone or something, guards, watchers - fits perfectly. 'Masters' implies authority/control over the Constitution, not protection of it. 'Dictators' means people who rule with absolute power - negative connotation, doesn't fit. 'Leaders' means people in charge - too generic, doesn't convey the protective/guardianship role. Vocabulary: Sentinel comes from the Italian sentinella (guard); it connotes vigilance and protection - perfect for describing the judiciary's role. Correct Sentence: Courts are sentinels of the Constitution as they are expected to see that it is always respected.

mock28.word: ...you will make it ______ a place called success!
mock28.explanation: "To" indicates reaching a destination, fitting the metaphor of arriving at success.

mock28.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word: 'Social economics is a meta-discipline in which economics is entrenched in social, political and cultural behaviours.'
mock28.explanation: Entrenched means firmly established and difficult to change. Transient means lasting only for a short time, temporary - the direct opposite of entrenched (deeply fixed, permanent). Averted means prevented or avoided - unrelated. Startling means causing surprise or shock - unrelated. Entrusted means given responsibility to someone - unrelated. Antonym Pair: Entrenched (permanent, deep-rooted) ↔ Transient (temporary, fleeting, short-lived).

mock28.word: He turned ______ the road and along the sound below the church.
mock28.explanation: "Turned off the road" (सड़क से मुड़ना) — main road से अलग दिशा में जाना। "Off" यहाँ direction change को indicate करता है। "Inside" (अंदर), "Over" (ऊपर), और "Under" (नीचे) context में गलत हैं।

options: for peace and as an admired critic by
for peace and as an admired critic on
about peace and as an admired critic about
in peace and as an admired critic about
mock28.explanation: "On" correctly connects with "critic" to indicate the subjects being critiqued, maintaining proper parallel structure in the sentence.

mock28.word: One of the options may contain a grammatical error. Select the option that has the error, if any. Are you yet afraid of going out in the dark?
mock28.explanation: The word "yet" is typically used in negative sentences and questions. The correct phrase should be "Are you still."

mock28.word: ...she began to ______ around the house impatiently.
mock28.explanation: "Prowl" (इधर-उधर घूमना) — बिल्ली का restlessly और stealthily घूमना। यह cats के natural behavior को describe करता है। "March" (कदम ताल), "Trot" (तेज़ चलना), और "Sleep" (सोना) इस context में गलत हैं।

mock28.word: Consistency can never be attained in a sporadic or ______ manner.
mock28.explanation: The blank requires a verb connecting 'being constant' with the activity of 'repeating a task' - the intended meaning is that consistency involves/means repeating a task. Involves means to include or contain as a necessary part - grammatically clean and contextually perfect. Envelops means to surround/cover completely - wrong context. Initiates means to begin something - doesn't convey the ongoing nature of consistency. Key Verb: 'Involves' is a high-frequency word especially when defining or describing what something entails.

mock28.word: Select the sentence that is expressed in passive voice.
options: Children are participating in sand-art competition.
mock28.explanation: Passive Voice Structure: The object of the active sentence becomes the subject, and the action is done to it, not by it. Option D: 'The consignment was delivered yesterday' - the subject (the consignment) receives the action (was delivered); the doer is not mentioned - classic passive voice (Past Simple Passive: was + past participle). Options A, B, and C all have subjects performing the action - active voice. Quick Passive Check: Ask - Is the subject doing the action or receiving the action? If receiving → Passive.

mock28.word: Select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the Idiom/Phrase. Dead ringer
mock28.explanation: "Dead ringer" (हूबहू नकल) means a person or thing that looks exactly like another — so similar they could be mistaken for each other. Example: Her identical twin was a dead ringer for her.

mock29.word: Charles Darwin is renowned for his book on evolution, 'On the Origin of Species', which laid down a treasure of substantiation that evolution occurs.
options: laid about a treasure of substantiation
laid away a treasure of substantiation
laid out a treasure of substantiation
mock29.explanation: "laid out" (स्पष्ट रूप से प्रस्तुत करना) सही phrasal verb है। इसका अर्थ है किसी चीज़ को clearly explain करना। Correct Sentence: ...which laid out a treasure of substantiation that evolution occurs.

mock29.word: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. Select the option that arranges the parts in the correct sequence. (P) the paintings of Sayed Haider Raza blended (Q) considered as one of the (R) foremost pioneers of Indian modern art (S) Parisian modernism with the colours of Indian Rajput art
mock29.explanation: (P) and (S) form the complete main clause. (Q) and (R) form the descriptive tag attached at the end.

"Turn up one's nose" (नाक-भौं चढ़ाना) — इसका अर्थ है to refuse or look down upon something as unworthy। "Turn up their face" idiom के रूप में मौजूद नहीं है। Correct sentence: "Some politicians turn up their nose at the hard life of downtrodden people."

mock29.word: The flames of the fire leap into the air.
mock29.explanation: The context refers to a past incident. When talking about past incidents, we use the past (V2) form. "Leapt" is the past form of "leap."

mock29.word: Select the sentence that best expresses the active form of the sentence given below. He is said to be one of the finest pianists in the world.
options: People said him to be one of the finest pianists in the world.
People say he is one of the finest pianists in the world.
People said he was one of the finest pianists in the world.
People say that he is one of the finest pianists in the world.
mock29.explanation: Since the given passive sentence has no explicit subject, a dummy subject "People" is used in the active version. The tense in both voices must remain the same (simple present), so the active form correctly retains the present tense: "People say that he is one of the finest pianists in the world."

mock29.word: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the highlighted word: "The government's utilitarian stance was appreciated by all."
mock29.explanation: "Utilitarian" (व्यावहारिक) — designed to be useful or practical। Synonym है "Functional" (प्रायोगिक/व्यावहारिक)। "Fanciful" (काल्पनिक), "Distasteful" (अरुचिकर), और "Necessary" (आवश्यक) अलग meaning रखते हैं।

mock29.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: POIGNANT
mock29.explanation: "Poignant" (हृदयस्पर्शी) का अर्थ है arousing feelings of sympathy deeply। इसका synonym है "Touching" (भावुक करने वाला) — that touches your emotions deeply। "Quiet" (शांत), "Beautiful" (सुंदर), और "Happy" (खुश) भिन्न अर्थ रखते हैं।

mock29.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. Tell her to finish the work.
options: To finish the work be told to her.
She be told to finish the work.
She should be told to finish the work.
Let her be told to finish the work.
mock29.explanation: Imperative sentences in passive voice always begin with "Let." The structure is: Let + object + be + past participle. So "Tell her to finish the work" becomes "Let her be told to finish the work."

mock29.word: ...A ______ range of items are on display.
mock29.explanation: "Wide range" (विस्तृत श्रृंखला) — marketplace में items की variety के लिए यह standard phrase है। "Vast" (विशाल), "Narrow" (संकीर्ण — opposite), और "Limited" (सीमित — opposite) इस context में कम सटीक हैं।

mock29.word: (Sportsmanship passage) Sportsmanship can be thought of as a sustaining and reasonably stable attribute or possession that _________ individuals on the basis of how they are expected to behave in sporting circumstances.
mock29.explanation: Identifies (पहचानता है) means to recognize or tell who or about what a person is — synonymous with recognizing individuals on the basis of expected behavior.

mock29.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word: VOCIFEROUS
mock29.explanation: "Vociferous" (ज़ोरदार/मुखर) का अर्थ है expressing opinions loudly and forcefully। इसका antonym है "Mild" (सौम्य) — gentle and not severe or strong। "Assertive" (दृढ़), "Political" (राजनीतिक), और "Biased" (पक्षपाती) असंबंधित हैं।

mock29.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word: CUPIDITY
mock29.explanation: "Cupidity" (लालच) का अर्थ है excessive desire for wealth। इसका antonym है "Largesse" (उदारता) — generosity or liberal giving of gifts or money। "Avarice" (लोभ) और "Greed" (लालच) synonyms हैं; "Hatred" (घृणा) असंबंधित है।

mock29.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Reluctantly
mock29.explanation: Correct Answer: (C) Zealously. "Reluctantly" (अनिच्छा से) means doing something without eagerness. The opposite is doing something with great energy and enthusiasm, which is "Zealously" (उत्साहपूर्वक).

mock29.word: Select the most appropriate option: "A detailed list of things in a place"
mock29.explanation: "Inventory" (सूची/माल-सूची) — a complete list of items in a place। "Directory" (निर्देशिका — names/addresses), "Glossary" (शब्दावली — word meanings), और "Itinerary" (यात्रा-कार्यक्रम) — सभी अलग lists हैं।

mock29.word: Due to this, it would ______ easily.
mock29.explanation: The passage discusses fabric fading and wearing out from sun and rain, so "rip and tear" logically describes the fabric's deterioration.

mock29.word: You may try to ______ insults at him but he will remain polite even in the face of grave provocation.
mock29.explanation: "Hurl insults" (अपमान करना/गालियां फेंकना) is a fixed phrasal collocation meaning to shout offensive words at someone, fitting the context of trying to provoke a polite flatterer. "Haul" means to pull or transport; "Haze" refers to obscured atmosphere; "Hurt" means to cause pain — none fit this standard collocation with "insults."

mock29.word: ...while the white youngster walked ______ behind him.
mock29.explanation: "Abjectly" (दीनतापूर्वक) expresses a degraded, submissive manner, contrasting with the dark boy's commanding stride.

mock29.word: Select the most appropriate option for the underlined segment in the given sentence: 'Most hotels/ offer for their guests/ complimentary breakfast/ during their stay there.'
mock29.explanation: The verb 'offer' is a transitive verb - it takes a direct object without requiring a preposition. Incorrect: 'offer for their guests' - the preposition 'for' is unnecessary and ungrammatical. Correct: 'offer their guests' - 'guests' is the indirect object, 'complimentary breakfast' is the direct object. Grammar Rule - Transitive vs Intransitive Verbs: Transitive verbs take a direct object without a preposition (offer, give, send, bring, show) - e.g. She offered him tea (correct), not 'She offered for him tea.' Intransitive verbs cannot take a direct object (laugh, arrive, sleep, go). Correct Sentence: Most hotels offer their guests complimentary breakfast during their stay there.

mock29.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word: CONTEMPT
mock29.explanation: "Contempt" (तिरस्कार/अवमानना) का अर्थ है disdain or scorn towards someone। इसका antonym है "Approbation" (स्वीकृति/प्रशंसा) — approval, praise, or favourable opinion। "Odium" (घृणा), "Opprobrium" (निन्दा), और "Obloquy" (आलोचना) synonyms हैं।

mock29.word: Shekhar Gupta is one of the ___ journalists of India.
mock29.explanation: Eminent (प्रतिष्ठित) means famous, important, and well-respected in one's profession. Imminent (आसन्न) means something unpleasant about to happen soon — wrong context. Exponent means one who advocates an idea. Pertinent means closely connected to the subject.

mock29.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock29.explanation: The correct spelling is "Dilemma" — not "Dillemma." The word has only ONE "l" and ONE "m" — D-I-L-E-M-M-A. A dilemma is a situation in which a difficult choice must be made between two or more alternatives, especially equally undesirable ones. (Hindi: दुविधा / कठिन परिस्थिति) Example: He wants to make money, but he also disapproves of it — that is Den's dilemma in a nutshell. All other options — Professional, Perpetuate, Electronics — are correctly spelt.

mock29.word: Select the wrongly spelt word.
mock29.explanation: The word "bristel" does not exist in standard English; the intended word is "bristle," meaning a short, stiff hair, typically one of many growing on an animal's skin or on a brush — for example, "The artist used a brush with stiff bristles to create texture in the painting." The remaining three words are spelled correctly: "brittle" means easily broken when subjected to pressure, fragile (e.g., "The brittle glass shattered on impact"); "brisk" means quick and energetic (e.g., "She went for a brisk walk in the fresh morning air"); "bridle" is a headgear used to control a horse, consisting of buckled straps to which a bit and reins are attached (e.g., "He carefully fitted the bridle onto the horse"). In spelling questions, watch especially for words that closely resemble a correctly spelled relative — "bristel" is a near-miss of "bristle," a classic examiner trick.

mock29.word: the ______ of many feudal privileges.
mock29.explanation: "Elimination" (उन्मूलन) means complete removal, fitting the abolition of feudal privileges.

mock29.word: (NEP passage) The National Educational Policy discusses the _________ and scope of vocational training.
mock29.explanation: Outline (रूपरेखा) means to give importance to or present key facts and information about something — suits discussing the scope and nature of vocational training.

mock29.word: We use turmeric on wounds, also to dye ______ and it is added extensively in our cooking.
mock29.explanation: "Locally" (स्थानीय रूप से) means with reference to a particular nearby area, fitting the context of farm produce coming from the local area. "Closely" refers to a close relationship; "Nearby" is an adjective, not fitting the adverbial position needed here; "Nearly" means almost — "locally" correctly conveys the idea of area-specific production.

mock30.word: a fine ______ of humour and pathos (करुणा).
mock30.explanation: "A fine blend of humour and pathos" — humour (हास्य) और pathos (करुणा) का सुंदर मिश्रण।

mock30.word: The committee reached ___ decision regarding the appointment of the chairman.
mock30.explanation: Unanimous (सर्वसम्मत) means fully in agreement — all members agreed on one decision. Exemplary means serving as an example. Compatible means able to exist together. Agreeable means pleasurable. Only "unanimous" fits the context of a committee decision.

mock30.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock30.explanation: The correct spelling is "Diligence" (परिश्रम), meaning careful and persistent work or effort. "Dilligence" has an extra 'l.'

mock30.word: Sedimentation ______ the plains of silt, a natural fertilizer.
mock30.explanation: "Deprived" (वंचित किया) means to take something away from someone or something, fitting the context that sedimentation prevented the plains from receiving the fertile silt they needed. "Destroyed" means demolished; "Distributed" means given out; "Disabled" means impaired — none precisely capture taking away a resource.

mock30.word: Given below are six sentences. A is beginning, F is last. Arrange middle four: A. When I studied Engineering thirty-five years back, I was the only girl in the course. B. People often ask me how I managed. C. I could only see boys and more boys everywhere. D. But when I look back I feel it was not very difficult. E. Having a girl in the class was unusual for the boys, and initially I was the target for teasing. F. But over a period of time they became my best friends.
mock30.explanation: A (introduction) → C (describes situation) → B (people's question) → D (reflection) → E (initial teasing) → F (friendship). C describes what she saw, B what people ask, D her current reflection, E the initial experience. Correct order: CBDE

mock30.word: We are not oblivious ______ the dangers that lie in our path.
mock30.explanation: "Oblivious to" (अनजान) means unaware of something. This fixed preposition pairing is grammatically correct here.

mock30.word: India comes second in the list of countries ______ most English speakers, albeit not as the first language.
mock30.explanation: "With" is used to include something within a group, fitting the context that India ranks second among countries "with" the most English speakers. "At" indicates a particular time; "By" indicates a method or agent; "From" indicates origin — none fit the meaning of inclusion in a ranked group.

"Set up" का अर्थ है किसी चीज़ को स्थापित करना — journey के लिए गलत है। "Set off" (यात्रा शुरू करना) — to begin a journey — यहाँ सटीक है। Correct sentence: "We set off on the journey early in the morning."

mock30.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech: Raman said,"Let us enjoy some snacks."
options: Raman said that they will enjoy some snacks.
Raman suggested that let they be enjoyed some snacks.
Raman proposed that they should enjoy some snacks.
Raman said that let us enjoy some snacks.
mock30.explanation: "Said" changes to "proposed" to convey suggestion; "us" changes to "they," and the modal "should" with the base verb "enjoy" correctly forms the indirect suggestion.

mock30.word: Science, according to popular ______...
mock30.explanation: "Belief" (विश्वास/धारणा) — singular uncountable noun जो "popular opinion" को indicate करती है। "Believes" (verb), "Beliefs" (plural, यहाँ singular चाहिए), और "Believer" (noun, wrong meaning) — सभी गलत हैं।

mock30.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Capacious (विशाल, प्रशस्त)
mock30.explanation: "Capacious" का अर्थ है having a lot of space; roomy। इसका antonym "Cramped" (छोटा और restricted) है।

mock30.word: Arrange the sentences in the right order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. No version of the origin of California's name has been fully accepted, but there is wide support for the contention that it derived from an early 16th-century Spanish novel. B. California is a constituent state of the United States of America. C. It was admitted as the 31st state of the union on 9 September 1850 and by the early 1960s it was the most populous U.S. state. D. The influence of the Spanish settlers of the 18th and 19th centuries is evident in California's architecture and place-names.
mock30.explanation: B comes first: it introduces the subject - California is a state of the USA - the broad, general opening statement. A comes next: it introduces an interesting detail about the origin of California's name, expanding on the topic. C comes third: gives historical facts about California's admission to the union and population, continuing the historical information. D comes last: discusses Spanish influence on California's architecture and place names, connecting back to A's mention of the Spanish novel origin and providing a concluding thought. Para Jumble Strategy: 1) Find the opening sentence - usually the most general, introductory statement. 2) Find connectors - pronouns, articles, transition words that link sentences. 3) Find the closing sentence - usually a consequence, conclusion, or summary.

mock30.word: Adhil called back but he wasn't able to find the scooter part he needed.'
options: called off but he wasn't able to
called up to but he wasn't able to
called around but he wasn't able to
called down but he wasn't able to
mock30.explanation: The sentence describes Mr. Adhil making multiple phone calls to different places to find a specific scooter part. 'Called around' means to make a series of phone calls to different places/people to find or obtain something - perfectly fits the context. 'Called off' means to cancel - doesn't fit. 'Called up to' is not a standard idiomatic expression. 'Called down' means to scold or reprimand - doesn't fit. 'Called back' implies returning one call, not making multiple calls to find something. Phrasal Verbs with 'call': Call off = cancel; Call up = telephone/recruit; Call around = phone multiple places; Call back = return a phone call; Call out = shout/expose someone.

mock30.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Pick the option that gives their correct order. A. It took us a long time to make the ladder but we finished it before nightfall. B. We had to fix the ladder to the lower branches of our tree. C. The ladder was then pulled up and fixed in place. D. We did this by shooting an arrow, with a rope attached to it, over the branch.
mock30.explanation: B introduces the topic of fixing the ladder, D explains the method used (shooting an arrow with a rope), C describes the result of that method (the ladder being pulled up), and A concludes the narrative by stating the task was completed before nightfall.
"Fell behind" (पिछड़ जाना) — to fail to keep up with something, especially studies। "Fell over" (गिर जाना), "Fell out" (झगड़ना), और "Fell for" (प्यार में पड़ना) — इस context में गलत हैं।

mock30.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a spelling error. Majority of the people / do not comprehend / the vissicitudes / of regular life.

mock30.word: Some patches of the ___ garden have a rich cover of grass now.
mock30.explanation: "Sprawling" (फैला हुआ) means built or spread over a wide area in an irregular way, fitting the description of a garden. "Stroking" means moving a hand gently over something; "Stretching" means pulling to make wider; "Slouching" (झुकना) means having a drooping posture — none describe a garden's extent.

mock30.word: They had to wait for the luggages (a) to be put into the cab (b) before they themselves got into it (c). No error (d).
mock30.explanation: "Luggages" is incorrect. "Luggage" (सामान) is an uncountable noun and has no plural form. It should remain "luggage" without any 's'.

mock30.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Steer clear of
options: Avoid someone or something because it is dangerous for you
mock30.explanation: "Steer clear of" (किसी खतरनाक चीज़ से बचना) — इसकी उत्पत्ति nautical terminology से हुई है। Example: She decided to steer clear of any company with a similar work culture.

The word 'great' is incorrectly used here; the sentence means Satya shows appreciation to his friends. 'Grateful' means showing appreciation for something done or received, thankful - the correct word. 'Great' means large in size, ability, or importance - doesn't describe a person's attitude of thankfulness. 'Greatful' (Option A) is a misspelling of grateful, not a real word. 'Thanked' is a past tense verb - grammatically incorrect in this context. Common Spelling Error: Many students misspell grateful as greatful - remember it comes from 'gratitude,' so it's grat-e-ful, not great-ful. Correct Sentence: Satya is always grateful to his friends who helped him when he was in trouble.

mock30.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock30.explanation: The correct spelling is "Vacant" (खाली), meaning not filled or occupied.

mock30.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Deplore

mock30.word: The following sentence has been divided into parts. The team worked / very hard / on the project. Select the part with the error, or 'No error'.
mock30.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) No error. The sentence is conceptually and grammatically correct.

mock30.word: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences. (P) Applied arts require an object to be functional, as well as beautiful. (Q) They recognised that their work belonged to the higher social classes... (R) Fine art is categorised as something which only has an aesthetic or conceptual function. (S) There are a number of different categories of objects and processes under the umbrella term of art. (T) In the twentieth century, artists began to challenge the established notion of art. (U) Art is typically divided into two areas: fine art and the applied arts.
options: H, P, S, T, U, Q
S, U, P, Q, R, T
S, U, R, P, T, Q
H, P, S, T, Q, U
mock30.explanation: (S) introduces art broadly → (U) divides it into two areas → (R) defines fine art → (P) defines applied art → (T) mentions artists challenging this → (Q) explains why they challenged it.

mock30.word: was unveiled ______ the Global Wildlife Programme conference.
mock30.explanation: "At the conference" (conference में) — location दर्शाने के लिए "at" सही preposition है।

mock31.word: Select the option that contains an error: "Children should watch TV from distance as it will affect their eyesight."
mock31.explanation: The article "a" is missing before "distance"; the correct phrase should be "TV from a distance."

mock31.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Through the grapevine

"Pulled over" गलत है। "Pulled through" — to survive or overcome a dangerous/difficult situation — यहाँ सटीक है। Correct sentence: "He is a weak student and somehow pulled through the exams."

mock31.word: We hardly knew nothing / about the manager / who had recently joined.
mock31.explanation: "hardly knew nothing" double negative है जो गलत है। "hardly" पहले से negative sense देता है, इसलिए "nothing" की जगह "anything" होगा। Correct Sentence: We hardly knew anything about the manager who had recently joined.

mock31.word: Direction: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word. The teacher was accused of brazen favouritism in assigning the grades.
mock31.explanation: Brazen (निर्लज्ज) means bold and shameless. "Unashamed" carries the same sense of shamelessness, making it correct. Modest means humble; Shy means timid; Ashamed (शर्मिंदा) means feeling guilt — these are nearly opposite in meaning to "brazen."

mock31.word: I was carried by her rapturous song.
mock31.explanation: "Carried away" (भावनाओं में बह जाना) means to be aroused to a high degree of emotion or enthusiasm. "Carried off" means to succeed in something difficult. "Carried of" and "Carried to" are not standard phrasal verbs. Correct sentence: I was carried away by her rapturous song.

mock31.word: Snow fluttered in Josie's window as she got ready for work. Select the correct substitution.
mock31.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) outside Josie's window. Logically, snow falls outdoors, so it would be observed fluttering "outside" the window, not inside.

mock31.word: The chair I have bought recently is costly than what I bought last year.
mock31.explanation: When comparing exactly TWO things using the word "than," we must use the Comparative Degree of the adjective — not the Superlative. Comparative of "costly" = "costlier" (or "more costly"). Superlative = "costliest" — used when comparing THREE or more items; a superlative is NEVER followed by "than." Tense note: The sentence uses present tense ("I have bought recently") so the verb must also be present — "was costly" (past tense) and "will be costly" (future tense) are both wrong. Correct sentence: "The chair I have bought recently is costlier than what I bought last year." Grammar Rule: Adjective + -er + than = Comparative; The + Adjective + -est = Superlative (no "than" follows).

mock31.word: ...can lead to build-up of a ______ toxic chemical called glutamate.
mock31.explanation: "Potentially" (संभावित रूप से) — toxicity की possibility को indicate करता है, certainty को नहीं। Context में glutamate की nature describe हो रही है। "Utterly" (पूर्णतः), "Perpetually" (लगातार), और "Grandly" (शानदार तरीके से) इस context में अनुचित हैं।

mock31.word: In fact, … was sweeping the piazza in front of the … church in preparation for market day, ______ the deputy mayor

"Also" means in addition; "Along" means in a line with something long; "Above" means at a higher position — none fit this collaborative physical positioning.

mock31.word: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. The river appears to have / got its name / from the town nearby. / No error

mock31.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock31.explanation: The correct spelling is "Practitioner" (व्यवसायी), meaning someone actively engaged in a profession such as medicine or law.

mock31.word: Then he heard them drip and ______ against the wall under the windows.
mock31.explanation: "Puddle" (छोटा जल-संचय) means to form a small pool of liquid, matching the description of raindrops collecting against the wall.

mock31.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the correct order: A. Whistling and prancing he moved with a carefree gait. B. His bugle, bow and arrow hung loosely on his shoulders. C. On a fine morning Robin Hood woke early and headed in the direction of Nottingham. D. When he was passing a shady path, he ran into a tinker coming along, singing merrily.
mock31.explanation: C (Robin Hood woke and set off) → A (his carefree manner of walking) → B (his weapons described) → D (met a tinker). Introduction → mood/manner → physical description → event. Correct order: CABD

mock31.word: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. The beginning of the sentence is "Zombies are not real". Arrange the remaining parts to form a meaningful sentence. (P) sound effects (Q) make it believable (R) but special (S) and visuals
mock31.explanation: (R) continues the base sentence → (P) is the subject of the new clause → (S) adds to the subject → (Q) is the predicate.

mock31.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Suffice
mock31.explanation: Suffice (पर्याप्त होना) means to be enough or adequate. Dissatisfy (असंतुष्ट करना) means to fail to satisfy someone — making it the exact opposite. Excessed (अधिक मात्रा) and Immatured (अपरिपक्व) are unrelated antonyms. Satirize (व्यंग्य करना) means to mock through satire, which is irrelevant here.

mock31.word: We use turmeric on wounds, also to dye ______ and it is added extensively in our cooking.
mock31.explanation: "Sustenance" (जीविका) refers to the means of sustaining life, especially food, fitting the context of people farming for their livelihood. "Conservation" relates to preventing loss; "Provision" means supplying something; "Nutrition" refers to what is eaten to maintain health — "sustenance" best captures farming as a means of livelihood.

mock31.word: Others are disastrous and the damage is ______.
mock31.explanation: "Extensive" (विस्तृत) means great in amount or scope, fitting the description of significant earthquake damage. "Rampant" describes something unchecked and spreading; "Plentiful" and "Profuse" describe abundance but are less fitting for describing the scale of physical damage from a disaster.

mock31.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error: "Going to the weekly market / in every Tuesday / is a ritual that has been followed / by generations in my family."
options: is a ritual that has been followed
mock31.explanation: Days के साथ preposition "on" प्रयुक्त होती है, "in" नहीं। इसलिए "in every Tuesday" की जगह "on every Tuesday" होना चाहिए। Correct sentence: "Going to the weekly market on every Tuesday is a ritual that has been followed by generations in my family."

mock31.word: Some scientists believe that retirement to a ______ lifestyle initiates or aggravates medical problems.
mock31.explanation: "Sedentary lifestyle" (निष्क्रिय जीवनशैली) — जहाँ कोई physical activity न हो, वह medical problems बढ़ाती है।

mock31.word: The plan recognises ______ addresses concerns pertaining to climate changes.
mock31.explanation: दो actions "recognises" और "addresses" को जोड़ने के लिए conjunction "and" सही है।

mock31.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM to replace the italicised word. "I am astonished to see her mercurial demeanour in making any task a success."
mock31.explanation: "Mercurial" (अस्थिर) means subject to sudden, unpredictable changes of mood. Its antonym is "Invariable" (अपरिवर्तनीय) — meaning unchanging and constant. The other options are synonyms.

mock31.word: Arrange the sentences to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. That magic is believing in yourself a hundred per cent. B. There is only one magic in life that can help you turn your dreams into reality. C. You will be able to face life's challenges with courage only if you believe in yourself and in your capacity to meet those challenges. D. Therefore, the most important lesson that a family can teach its members is to have this faith in oneself.
mock31.explanation: B comes first: introduces the theme - 'there is only one magic in life' - the topic sentence. A comes second: 'That magic is believing in yourself' - the pronoun 'that' links directly back to B's 'one magic.' C comes third: expands on the idea of self-belief and its practical application. D comes last: 'Therefore' signals a conclusion, summarising the lesson for families. Clue Words Used: 'That magic' in A refers back to 'one magic' in B (backward reference); 'Therefore' in D is a conclusion marker.

mock32.word: Select the most appropriate idiom for the underlined words in the given sentence. The candidate blamed the persistent traffic jam as reason of his being late and the interviewer believed him though he was not convinced.
options: thought err is human and to forgive is divine
did not judge the book by its cover
mock32.explanation: "Gave the benefit of doubt" (संदेह का लाभ देना) means to accept someone's statement as true despite some uncertainty about its accuracy, trusting their good intentions when there is ambiguity. "Changed his tune" means altering one's opinion; the other options describe forgiveness or not judging by appearance, which don't match this context.

mock32.word: Select the most appropriate synonym. THWART
mock32.explanation: "Thwart" (विफल करना) means to prevent someone from accomplishing something. "Impede" (बाधा डालना) means to obstruct or hinder — making them synonyms.

mock32.word: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. Select the option that arranges the parts in the correct order to form a meaningful sentence. A. The prosperity and business acumen B. Mumbai and Kolkata indicate that in the commercial sector C. Of the great industrial and commercial houses in D. India has a considerable potential for modernisation
mock32.explanation: The sentence begins with the subject "The prosperity and business acumen" (A), followed by the noun-preposition phrase naming where these houses are located (C), then the object describing the commercial sector (B), and finally completing with the remaining conclusion about modernisation (D), giving: "The prosperity and business acumen of the great industrial and commercial houses in Mumbai and Kolkata indicate that in the commercial sector India has a considerable potential for modernisation."

mock32.word: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. The South Indian dish was ___ by the visitors.
mock32.explanation: Accepted (स्वीकृत) means regarded favorably or with approval, which fits the meaning. Gratified (प्रसन्न करना) means to give pleasure. Doomed (बर्बाद) means ill-fated, and Condemned (निंदित) means declared unfit — both are inappropriate here.

mock32.word: "It may seem ___ that the hours of women's chores at home have not declined in spite of the availability of many appliances."
mock32.explanation: "Perplexing" (भ्रमित करने वाला) means completely baffling. It highlights the surprising fact that work hours haven't decreased despite modern appliances.

mock32.word: Sentences of a paragraph are given below. While the first (1) and the last (6) sentences are in correct order, the sentences in between (A–D) are jumbled up. Arrange them. (1) Nicholas Chorier is not a usual photographer. (A) He uses a kite to hoist his camera into the skies and clicks photographs while the camera dangles mid-air. (B) He is a kite aerial photographer. (C) Fascinated, he literally tied his two hobbies, kite flying and photography, together for a living. (D) During a trip to India to make a photo report on kite making, he learnt about this unique style of photography. (6) Today he is one of the most well-known kite aerial photographers in the world.
mock32.explanation: 1 introduces Chorier as unusual. B immediately specifies what kind of photographer he is (kite aerial). A describes his method in detail. D explains how he discovered this style (trip to India). C explains how he combined his hobbies. 6 concludes with his fame. Correct sequence: 1, B, A, D, C, 6.

mock32.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. To keep a good table
options: To offer sumptuous food to one's guests
mock32.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) To offer sumptuous food to one's guests. "To keep a good table" (शानदार भोजन परोसना) is an idiomatic phrase meaning to provide excellent and plentiful food, especially for guests.

mock32.word: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. Rohan got his birthday gift in the twilight.
options: received his birthday present at dusk
received his birthday present in the dawn
received his birthday present in the genesis
received his birthday present in the morning
mock32.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) received his birthday present at dusk. "Twilight" refers to the period just before dark. "At dusk" (गोधूलि बेला / शाम) is the more natural, idiomatic way to express this time, and "received" is a stronger verb than "got."

mock32.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. By leaps and bounds
mock32.explanation: "By leaps and bounds" (तेज़ी से, अत्यंत शीघ्रता से) का अर्थ है to make rapid progress। Example: Since hiring the new CEO, the company has grown by leaps and bounds.

mock32.word: only by Jeff Bezos, ______ has a current net worth of $116 billion.
mock32.explanation: "Who" is used for people as subject of a relative clause. Jeff Bezos is a person and is the subject of "has a net worth" — so "who" is correct.

mock32.word: ...may face pressure from peers, parents and society to ______ to certain expectations.
mock32.explanation: Conform (अनुरूप होना) means to comply with rules or expectations, fitting the social pressure described in the passage.

mock32.word: Identify the segment containing the grammatical error: "Most of voters / in Taiwan seem to have / once again voted for / the democratic party and President Tsai Ing-wen."
options: the democratic party and President Tsai Ing-wen
mock32.explanation: "Most of" should be followed by a definite article before the noun, so the correct form is "Most of the voters" or simply "Most voters," not "Most of voters."

mock32.word: Which one of the following words is correctly spelt?
mock32.explanation: Independence (स्वतंत्रता) is correctly spelled — meaning the state of being independent or self-governing. Options B, C, and D all have vowel errors making them incorrect spellings.

mock32.word: Select the grammatically correct sentence.
options: I was scared out of my wits the first time I got on plane.
I was scared out of my wits the first time I got on a plane.
I was scared out of my wits a first time I got on the plane.
I was scared out of my wits the first time I got on the plane.
mock32.explanation: The sentence requires the definite article "the" before "first time" since a specific past incident is being referenced, and the indefinite article "a" before "plane" since it is being mentioned for the first time, giving "the first time I got on a plane."

mock32.word: Select the sentence that contains no spelling errors.
options: Furrow from the protestors was heard outside the president's house.
Furoroe from the protestors was heard outside the president's house.
Furore from the protestors was heard outside the president's house.
Furorow from the protestors was heard outside the president's house.
mock32.explanation: Furore (हंगामा/उत्तेजना) is correctly spelled and means an outbreak of public anger or excitement. "Furrow" (नाली/खांचा) means a groove in soil. "Furoroe" and "Furorow" are not real English words.

mock32.word: By accelerating digitisation and leveraging next-generation technologies, the life insurance sector can double its _____ to Rupees 70 lakh crores.
mock32.explanation: "Assets" (संपत्ति/संसाधन) means resources with economic value. Doubling assets is the logical outcome of improved technology and digitisation in the insurance sector.

mock32.word: I have been ___ over the two options in my head and I think we should go with the first one.
mock32.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) mulling. "Mull over" (गंभीरता से विचार करना) is a phrasal verb meaning to think deeply and at length about something.

mock32.word: Directions: Select the most appropriate meaning of the following idiom. To pay lip service
mock32.explanation: "To pay lip service" (केवल जबानी समर्थन देना) means to express agreement or support verbally without truly believing in or acting on it. For example, "She claims to favour training but has only paid lip service to the idea."

mock32.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined group of words. Henny Penny is a story that is passed down from generation to generation through spoken word.
mock32.explanation: Folktale (लोककथा) refers to a story that is passed down from generation to generation, usually by word of mouth — exact match. Fable is a short story with animals as characters conveying a moral. Epic is a long narrative poem about a hero. Parable is a simple story used to illustrate a moral or spiritual lesson.

mock32.word: Select the correct passive form of the given sentence. Why do we have to accept these conditions?
options: Why do these conditions have to be accepted?
Why should these conditions be accepted?
These conditions do not have to be accepted.
Why are these conditions needed to be accepted?
mock32.explanation: In passive interrogative voice: Why + be + subject + past participle. The object "these conditions" becomes the subject; "accept" becomes "have to be accepted."

mock32.word: (People have been long known) how important the trees are to them.
mock32.explanation: "Have been long known" is awkward. The correct present perfect form is "have long known" — meaning they have possessed this knowledge for a long time and it remains true today.

mock32.word: He ran _____ the field to catch the ball.
mock32.explanation: "Across" means from one side to the other. Running across a field to catch a ball is the most logical and grammatically correct usage here.

mock32.word: Through the …, lonely years of my childhood, when my father's palace … to tighten its grip … me until I couldn't ______, I would go to my nurse and ask for a story.
mock32.explanation: The sentence describes a difficult childhood situation where the palace's grip made it hard for the narrator to do something basic. "Breathe" is needed in its infinitive form here (not requiring an auxiliary verb), making it the only grammatically correct option. "Breath" is a noun, "breathed" is past participle, and "breathing" is present participle — none fit the infinitive construction required.

mock32.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Tendency
mock32.explanation: "Tendency" means an inclination towards a particular characteristic or type of behaviour — for example, "She has a tendency to talk too much when she is nervous." "Proneness" refers to a natural inclination to suffer from, do, or experience something, often something negative — for example, "Her proneness to anxiety makes her avoid crowded places." This closely matches the sense of an inherent inclination. The distractors all relate to dislike rather than inclination: "distaste" is a strong feeling of dislike; "aversion" is a strong feeling of dislike or opposition; "decoy" is something used to lure or mislead, which is unrelated in meaning altogether.

mock32.word: He was one of the youngest racers to have made it to the F1 ______.
mock32.explanation: "Circuit" (सर्किट) is the technical term for an F1 racetrack.

mock33.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Bide one's time
options: To wait patiently for an opportunity
To join a popular trend or activity
To face a difficult situation with courage and determination
mock33.explanation: "Bide one's time" (सही मौके का इंतज़ार करना) का अर्थ है to wait patiently for an opportunity। Example: She decided to bide her time and wait for the perfect moment to make her move.

mock33.word: Read the sentence to find if there is any grammatical error: "Some witness said the grenade was thrown (1) / from a motorcycle while others said (2) / it was lobbed at the check post from a moving car (3) / No error (4)."
mock33.explanation: "Witness" should be plural ("witnesses") to agree with the plural verb "said," correcting the subject-verb agreement error.

mock33.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom: BANG FOR THE BUCK
mock33.explanation: "Bang for the buck" (पैसे का अधिक मूल्य) — इसका अर्थ है more value for money। Example: "How much bang for the buck did you really think you would get from a twelve-year-old bike?" "Dash against something" (टकराना) और "Less value for money" (कम मूल्य) गलत हैं।

mock33.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock33.explanation: "Sacrilage" is incorrectly spelt. The correct spelling is "Sacrilege" (पवित्र वस्तु का अपमान) — meaning the violation of something sacred. All other words are correctly spelt.

mock33.word: Improve the underlined part of the sentence. Choose 'No improvement' if grammatically correct. I won't bore you with the details, but I finally finish my taxes.
mock33.explanation: The context clearly indicates a completed action in the past. The past tense verb "finished" (पूर्ण किया) is needed instead of present tense "finish." Correct sentence: I won't bore you with the details, but I finally finished my taxes.

mock33.word: When she … refuge with him from a bad dream, nothing could lull ______ back to sleep better than Mo
mock33.explanation: "Inherited" (विरासत में मिला) means to receive traits passed down from a parent, fitting the context that Meggie's love of books came from her father, Mo, who is also a reader. "Gained," "extracted," and "procured" describe acquiring something through effort, not naturally receiving a trait from a parent.

mock33.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock33.explanation: The correct spelling is Acceptable (स्वीकार्य), meaning satisfactory or adequate. "Acceptible" has an incorrect vowel 'i' in place of 'a'. Dearth, Corrupt, and Barely are all correctly spelled.

"Dream of" के बाद gerund (V+ing) आता है। "Of raising" — preposition "of" + gerund "raising"। "To raise" (infinitive — गलत), "To raising" (grammatically incorrect), और "Of raise" (base verb — गलत) सभी wrong हैं।

mock33.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in positive degree of comparison: "Istanbul is a beautiful city."
options: The city of Istanbul is as beautiful as other cities.
No other place I have seen is as beautiful as Istanbul.
mock33.explanation: Using the structure "No other place...as...as" expresses the superlative quality of Istanbul's beauty through the positive degree of comparison.

mock33.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Modest
mock33.explanation: Correct Answer: (C) Demure. "Modest" refers to someone who is unassuming and humble. "Demure" (संकोची / शर्मीला) is its closest synonym.

mock33.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Forthright (सीधा, ईमानदार)
mock33.explanation: "Forthright" का अर्थ है direct and honest। इसका antonym "Devious" (dishonest और underhanded — बेईमान) है।

mock33.word: English is more ______ than any single Indian language!
mock33.explanation: The sentence compares English to individual Indian languages in terms of spread and usage; the blank needs a word meaning 'widely spread or commonly found'. Prevalent means widely existing or commonly occurring in a particular area or time - fits perfectly. Frequented means visited often - applies to places, not languages.
