Extended means made longer or larger, which doesn't make sense comparing languages. Grammar Rule: In comparative sentences using 'more ___ than,' the blank must be an adjective that logically describes the quality being compared.

mock33.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Get the axe
mock33.explanation: "Get the axe" (नौकरी से निकाले जाना) means to be discharged or fired, expelled, or rejected.

mock33.word: Jagdeep, the Bollywood comedian, began his film career at eight as a live childlike actor in the 1950's.
options: began his film career at eight
mock33.explanation: "live" की जगह "lively" (जीवंत, उत्साही) होना चाहिए। "lively" adjective है जो "childlike" को qualify करता है। Correct Sentence: ...as a lively childlike actor in the 1950's.

mock33.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: QUINTESSENTIAL
mock33.explanation: "Quintessential" (सर्वोत्कृष्ट/प्रतिनिधित्वपूर्ण) का अर्थ है representing the most perfect or typical example of a class। इसका synonym है "Typical" (विशिष्ट) — having the distinctive qualities of a particular type। "Fantastic" (अद्भुत), "Initial" (प्रारंभिक), और "Charming" (आकर्षक) भिन्न अर्थ रखते हैं।

Relative clause का subject "taste" singular है, इसलिए verb भी singular होनी चाहिए। "Tingle" की जगह "tingles" होना चाहिए। Correct sentence: "Chillies have a strong, spicy taste that tingles your taste buds."

mock33.word: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase. Grudging
mock33.explanation: Grudging means reluctant; unwilling; given or allowed only with resentment. (Hindi: अनिच्छुक / ईर्ष्यापूर्ण) The closest synonym is Envious = jealous; feeling or showing envy of someone else's possessions or achievements. (Hindi: ईर्ष्यालु / जलनखोर) Example: She won the grudging respect of her boss. Example: I'm very envious of your new coat — it's beautiful. Antonym: Generous, Willing, Enthusiastic. Other options: Favouring = supporting or preferring (opposite of grudging); Generous = kind and giving (antonym); Contented = satisfied and at ease.

mock33.word: An unpardonable ______, but it was now too late to make amends for it.
mock33.explanation: "Lapse" (चूक) means a brief slip or mistake, which fits the context of forgetting an essential item like a rifle. "Yearn" means to long for something; "Reward" means something given for good behaviour; "Crime" is too severe a word for the small forgetfulness described.

mock33.word: One of the options may contain a grammatical error. Rohit Sharma's century was very acclaimed in the media.
mock33.explanation: The phrase "very acclaimed" is incorrect. The correct phrase should be "highly acclaimed."

mock33.word: Select the meaning of the given idiom: BAD BLOOD
mock33.explanation: "Bad blood" (दुश्मनी/वैर) — इस idiom का अर्थ है hostility (दुश्मनी/वैमनस्य) or ill feeling between individuals or groups। Example: "There has been bad blood between the two families for years." "Poor blood circulation" (खराब रक्त परिसंचरण), "Family of ill-repute" (बदनाम परिवार), और "Feeling of brotherhood" (भाईचारे की भावना) गलत हैं।

mock33.word: Health club membership has grown by leaps and bounds after the pandemic. (Meaning of idiom)
mock33.explanation: ऊपर Q.75 में explain किया जा चुका है।

mock33.word: Select the correctly spelt word.
mock33.explanation: "Sovereignty" (सर्वोच्च सत्ता) is correctly spelt, meaning supreme power or authority. The correct spellings of the others are "Grievance" (a real or imagined cause for complaint) and "Itinerary" (a planned route or journey); "Renaissance" is correctly spelt but is not the intended answer here.

mock33.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Conjure
mock33.explanation: Conjure (जादू से बुलाना/विनती करना) means to cause a spirit to appear by magic, or to implore someone. Appeal (गंभीर आग्रह/विनती करना) means to make a serious, urgent, or heartfelt request — same meaning. Appease means to pacify by acceding to demands. Content means in a state of peaceful happiness.

mock33.word: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange them to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Their brother, John, is more blatantly hostile to Jane, reminding her that she is a poor dependent of his mother. B. Orphaned as an infant, Jane Eyre lives at Gateshead with her aunt, Sarah Reed, as the novel opens. C. Her female cousins, Georgiana and Eliza tolerate, but don't love her. D. Jane is ten years old, an outsider in the Reed family.
mock33.explanation: B introduces Jane and her situation. D gives specific details about her age and outsider status. C describes the relationship with her female cousins. A describes the hostile relationship with John (brother). Correct sequence: B (intro) → D (age/status) → C (female cousins) → A (John's hostility).

mock33.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a spelling error. It is difficult to / retain anonimity while / working in the world's / most well-known organisation.
mock33.explanation: The correct spelling is "anonymity" (गुमनामी), meaning the situation where a person's name is not known. "Anonimity" is the misspelt version.

mock34.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences about Ramzan fasting. Select the correct order. A. It is only after sunset that he satisfies his hunger and quenches his thirst. B. In this way, by practicing self-restraint for one month in a year, he is able to lead a life of self-discipline. C. During the month of Ramzan, the believer abstains from food and drink in the daytime of his own free will. D. Fasting is an exercise in self-discipline and is good for everyone.
mock34.explanation: D introduces the general topic of fasting; C explains the practice during Ramzan; A describes when the fast is broken; B concludes with the resulting self-discipline.

mock34.word: Choose the appropriate option that changes the given sentence into passive voice: "They handed her a cheque."
options: A cheque is handed to them by her.
A cheque was handed to them.
mock34.explanation: The indirect object "her" becomes the subject in passive voice, with the verb changing to "was handed," correctly forming "She was handed a cheque."

mock34.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. "Science has been trying innumerable methods to prove the existence of the universe, but the biggest pleasure of science is its inability to produce a single theory that explains the whole universe."
mock34.explanation: "Pleasure" means a feeling of enjoyment or satisfaction — for example, "The music brought her great pleasure." "Blandness" means a lack of excitement, flavour, or interest; something monotonous and unstimulating — for example, "The dish's blandness failed to excite our taste buds." Since pleasure implies a positive, stimulating feeling, its true opposite is the absence of any such stimulation — blandness — rather than a different negative emotion. "Inadequacy" means a lack of competence or sufficiency; "joy" is in fact a near-synonym of pleasure, not an antonym; "fear" is an unpleasant emotion caused by perceived danger, which is a different emotional category altogether and not a direct antonym of "pleasure."

mock34.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Hard and fast
mock34.explanation: "Hard and fast" (कठोर/अपरिवर्तनीय) means something that is rigid and cannot be modified or evaded — essentially meaning strict rules. Example: My company has a hard and fast policy when it comes to granting leaves.

mock34.word: ..so of course the ______ was endless..."
mock34.explanation: Context में Anders bank गया just before closing time — इसलिए "the queue was endless" (लंबी कतार)। "Crowd" (भीड़), "Noise" (शोर), और "Wait" (प्रतीक्षा) — "queue" जितना specific नहीं हैं।

mock34.word: A. She dashed from one side to the other. B. Karen took a stroll in a lovely garden. C. She discovered a swing constructed of tree branches behind a large bush. D. Karen went onto the swing without hesitation. E. The small girl adored the outdoors.
mock34.explanation: B — Karen का garden में आना। E — her love for outdoors। A — garden में दौड़ना। C — swing की खोज। D — swing पर बैठना।

mock34.word: crowned by the chhattri, a parasol-like ______ symbolizing high rank.
mock34.explanation: The chhattri is a parasol-like "structure" (संरचना) symbolizing high rank. "Conduit" means a channel, "bridge" and "umbrella" do not fit architecturally.

mock34.word: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the parts to form a meaningful sentence. (O) arid deserts (P) from lush forests to (Q) hikers encountered diverse ecosystems (R) trekking through the wilderness
mock34.explanation: (R) introduces the action → (Q) states what was encountered → (P) describes the start of the range → (O) completes the range.
"Called around" means to contact people in a particular area, correctly conveying Meenakshi seeking support from her surrounding neighbours.

mock34.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Deliberate (जानबूझकर)
mock34.explanation: "Deliberate" का अर्थ है intentional या carefully considered। इसका antonym "Spontaneous" (बिना सोचे अचानक होने वाला) है।

mock34.word: Regardless of what may or may not be true of my scholastic rigour, ______ I suspect the school-stress dream is quite common.
mock34.explanation: "Consistently" (निरंतर रूप से) means happening the same way over time, fitting the context of the narrator regularly dissatisfying themselves in their dreams about academic failure. "Conflictingly," "Unpredictably," and "Clumsily" do not capture the sense of a regular, repeated pattern of self-disappointment.

mock34.word: … the earlier wars, the civilian populations were … not involved and the casualties were mostly ______ to the warring armies.
mock34.explanation: "Confined" (सीमित) means limited to a specific scope, fitting the context that earlier wars' casualties were limited mainly to soldiers rather than civilians. "Bound" means extremely likely; "Belonged" means being part of something suitable; "Attained" means achieved — none fit the sense of being restricted to a specific group.

mock34.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the correct order: P. Fasting also leads to a feeling of rejuvenation and extended life expectancy. Q. This might be due to the detoxification effect of fasting. R. A study was performed on earthworms that showed the extension of life thanks to fasting. S. The experiment was performed in the 1930s by isolating one worm... [The isolated worm outlived the other worms by 19 generations...]
mock34.explanation: P (fasting leads to rejuvenation) → Q (possible reason: detoxification) → R (study on earthworms) → S (specific experiment details). General claim → reason → evidence → specific example. Correct order: PQRS

mock34.word: Suzerainty is having political control over a ___ state.
mock34.explanation: "Suzerainty" (अधिराज्यता) means the right of a country to partly control another while allowing it internal autonomy. "Dependent" (आश्रित) fits because the controlled state relies on the controlling power, matching the definition of suzerainty.

mock34.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock34.explanation: The correct spelling is "Accessibility" (सुगम्यता), meaning the quality of being easy to obtain or use.

mock34.word: Improve the underlined part of the sentence. Choose 'No improvement' if grammatically correct. The weather on the weekend are supposed to be sunny.
mock34.explanation: "Weather" (मौसम) is a singular subject. It requires the singular verb "is" not "are." "Were supposed" indicates past tense incorrectly. "Are supposing" uses wrong continuous form. Correct sentence: The weather on the weekend is supposed to be sunny.

mock34.word: Sentences about yoga benefits are given in jumbled order. Select the correct logical sequence. a. It regulates the operation of all body organs and protects the body and mind from imbalance. b. It only requires frequent practice of proper body motions and breathing techniques. c. The physical benefits of yoga can be enjoyed for a lifetime without risk or injury. d. It regulates the relationship between the body, the intellect, and the spirit.
mock34.explanation: c introduces the lifelong benefits; b explains the requirement of practice; d describes the regulatory relationship; a concludes with the holistic protective impact of yoga.

mock34.word: One clear ______ from this year's meeting was that climate change...
mock34.explanation: "Takeaway" (मुख्य निष्कर्ष) — meeting से निकाला गया main point या key message। "Standard" (मानक), "Thesis" (शोध-प्रबंध), और "Diffidence" (संकोच) इस context में fit नहीं होते।

mock34.word: Select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the Idiom/Phrase. To lay someone by the heels
mock34.explanation: "To lay someone by the heels" (कारावास में डालना) means to imprison or confine someone. Example: The notorious criminal was finally laid by the heels after a long pursuit.

mock34.word: The education system is ______ more and more frustrations among both students and teachers.
mock34.explanation: "Breeding" (उत्पन्न करना) means generating or producing, fitting the idea that the system is producing increasing frustration.

mock34.word: If there is no need, select 'No substitution required.' The forecast predicts that there will be little rain in the month of July.
mock34.explanation: "Little" (थोड़ा) is correctly used here since "rain" is an uncountable noun. The modal "will" correctly expresses a prediction.

mock34.word: Select the option that can replace the underlined phrase to make the sentence grammatically correct. "Doctor wants Tony to cut back down junk foods."
mock34.explanation: "Cut back on" (कम करना) is the correct phrasal verb meaning to reduce the amount of something.

mock34.word: Select the option that contains an error: "We took dinner in a Chinese restaurant and were amazed to see the interior of a restaurant."
mock34.explanation: Since the same restaurant was already mentioned, the definite article "the" should be used instead of "a," giving "the interior of the restaurant."

mock34.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Interfere
mock34.explanation: Interfere (बाधा डालना) means to intrude or disrupt the normal functioning of something. Advance (आगे बढ़ना) means to move forward or promote progress — the direct opposite. Intercede (मध्यस्थता करना) means to mediate. Hamper and Jam are both synonyms of interfere.

mock34.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. When Mayank came, / I shall go / with him to / the circus.
mock34.explanation: The past tense "came" conflicts with the future tense "shall go" used elsewhere in the sentence. In conditional/time clauses referring to future possibility, the simple present "comes" should be used instead, giving "When Mayank comes, I shall go with him to the circus."

mock35.word: Choose the word that means the same as the given word. Gallivant
mock35.explanation: Gallivant means to wander about aimlessly seeking pleasure. "Meander" (घूमना, भटकना) means to wander aimlessly, making it the correct synonym. Grave means a burial place; Gall means bold impudence; Brave means courageous — none match.

mock35.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Pick the option that gives their correct order. A. Though everything was on the up and up for Holzman, yet... something wasn't quite right. B. At 19, he enrolled in the Culinary Institute of America before heading to the West Coast to cook for well-esteemed restaurants. C. He got a little fed up with the kitchen and started to feel that he wanted to do something different with it. D. Holzman, a well known chef, started his career at the age of 15 when the prodigy began cooking under Eric Ripert at Le Bernadin.
mock35.explanation: D introduces Holzman and his early career start. B continues with his later career at culinary institutes and West Coast restaurants. A and C are paired — A states something wasn't right and C explains the reason (fed up with kitchen). Correct sequence: D (intro) → B (career progression) → A (something not right) → C (reason for discontent).

mock35.word: Julia asked two of her friends about the trip, but ______ of them knew about it.
mock35.explanation: "Neither" (न तो...न ही) is used for negative statements about exactly two people/things, fitting "two of her friends."

mock35.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Exaggerate
mock35.explanation: "Exaggerate" (अतिशयोक्ति करना) means to describe something as larger or greater than it is. Its antonym is "Understate" (कम करके कहना). "Embellish" (सजाना) means to add decorative details.

mock35.word: Select the wrongly spelt word.
mock35.explanation: The correct spelling is harangue (लंबा और आक्रामक भाषण), meaning a lengthy and aggressive speech. Gauche (अनाड़ी) means socially awkward. Fallacious (भ्रामक) means based on a mistaken belief. Erudite (विद्वान) means having great knowledge.

mock35.word: Many Cubans in circumstances similar to what I had become American citizens at the first opportunity.
mock35.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) similar to mine. "Similar to mine" is the most concise and grammatically natural way to express that their circumstances resembled the speaker's own past situation.

mock35.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word. Our locality has a common space for observing various national days and for organising festivals.
mock35.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) coordinating. In this context, "organising" means managing and arranging the logistics. "Coordinating" (समन्वय करना) is the best synonym.

mock35.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Temporal (सांसारिक, लौकिक)
mock35.explanation: "Temporal" का अर्थ है worldly या secular। इसका antonym "Spiritual" (आध्यात्मिक, अलौकिक) है।

mock35.word: Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it. If there is no error, choose 'No error.' Balloons filled with helium travel hundreds or even thousands of mile.
mock35.explanation: After "thousands of," the plural form "miles" (मील) must be used. In English, plural nouns follow plural quantifiers. Correct sentence: Balloons filled with helium travel hundreds or even thousands of miles.

mock35.word: She went to her work corner in the living room ______ and looked at the notes she had pinned up...
mock35.explanation: यह simple conjunction है जो two consecutive actions को join करता है — "went to her work corner" और "looked at the notes." "And" यहाँ सबसे natural connector है।

mock35.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock35.explanation: The correct spelling is Guzzle (गटागट पीना), meaning to drink greedily or excessively. "Guzzel" is misspelled. Gunner, Gullet, and Gutter are all correctly spelled.

mock35.word: driving high performance are encouraged in a ______ environment.
mock35.explanation: Business में high performance के लिए "competitive environment" (प्रतिस्पर्धात्मक माहौल) सबसे उपयुक्त है।

mock35.word: The door ___ in the strong wind.
mock35.explanation: "Slammed" (ज़ोर से बंद होना) means to shut with great force and a loud noise, exactly describing a door affected by strong wind. "Slapped" means to hit with an open hand; "Hooted" means to make a horn-like sound; "Sizzled" (छनछनाना) describes the sound of frying food — none fit a door shutting.

mock35.word: ______ on the banks of the majestic Sardar Sarovar Dam in Gujarat's Kevadia
mock35.explanation: "Situated" (स्थित) — किसी स्थान पर permanently located होने का अर्थ देता है। Statue की position describe करने के लिए यह सबसे सटीक है। "Appeared" (दिखाई दिया) — coming into view suggest करता है। "Found" (पाया गया) — discovering something hidden को indicate करता है। "Established" (स्थापित किया) — abstract है, physical location के लिए उपयुक्त नहीं।

mock35.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute: 'Having a feeling of guilt'
mock35.explanation: Remorse means a feeling of deep regret or guilt for doing something wrong or hurtful - the correct answer. Refulgence means brightness or radiance - completely unrelated. Restitution means restoring something taken, compensation - unrelated. Repugnance means a strong feeling of disgust or aversion - unrelated (about disgust, not guilt). One-Word List: Remorse = guilt for wrongdoing; Repentance = feeling sorry and deciding to change; Contrition = deep sorrow for sin.

mock35.word: Communication can also help us to make positive changes in our communities and ______ for important causes.
mock35.explanation: Advocate (समर्थन करना) means to publicly support a cause, which fits naturally with "for important causes."
The sentence is grammatically correct. "Has given" is present perfect — "given" is the past participle of "give." The structure Subject+has+V3 is correctly applied here.

mock35.word: This year, we were visited by the director of the Austrian Culture Forum, (1) based on Delhi and together we all wanted (2) to initiate an art festival in South India. (3) No error (4)
mock35.explanation: "Based on Delhi" is incorrect. The correct preposition is "based in Delhi." We say someone is "based in" a place, not "based on."

mock35.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Mutant
mock35.explanation: Mutant (परिवर्तित) describes something showing deviation from a standard form due to mutation. Deviant, meaning deviating from norms, is the best match.

mock35.word: A piece of information received "by word of mouth" has no authenticity. Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom.
mock35.explanation: "By word of mouth" (मौखिक रूप से) refers to information passed verbally rather than in writing, making "in spoken form" the correct meaning.

mock35.word: I can't believe that you're (resolute) enough to not even decide what you want for dinner. Select the antonym of the bracketed word.
mock35.explanation: Resolute (दृढ़) means firmly determined. Indecisive, meaning unable to make decisions, is its direct opposite.

mock35.word: Select the most appropriate synonym for the given word. Recumbent
mock35.explanation: "Recumbent" (लेटा हुआ) means lying down in a position of comfort or rest. "Prostrate" (औंधे मुंह पड़ा हुआ) means lying flat on the ground, often face down — the closest synonym.

mock35.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Permeate (व्याप्त होना)
mock35.explanation: "Permeate" का अर्थ है spread throughout करना। "Diffuse" का अर्थ है spread or scatter widely — दोनों समानार्थी हैं।

mock35.word: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them contains an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. Four of earliest civilisations / of the world / were located / on the banks of or near large rivers.
options: on the banks of or near large rivers.
mock35.explanation: The superlative form "earliest" requires the definite article "the" before it, since superlatives generally refer to one specific group or thing. The corrected phrase should be "Four of the earliest civilisations."

mock35.word: Preserve a right, tradition, or idea in a form that ensures it will be protected and respected
mock35.explanation: "Enshrine" का अर्थ है to preserve something in a form that ensures it is protected। Example: A lot of memories are enshrined in this photo album.

mock36.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Meander (इधर-उधर घूमना)
mock36.explanation: "Meander" का अर्थ है to wander aimlessly। इसका antonym "Direct" (straight path without detours — सीधा मार्ग) है।

mock36.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word: 'After years of working in politics, she had become naive and jaded...' (Duplicate of Question 25 in the source)
mock36.explanation: This question is a duplicate of Question 25 in the source material. See Question 25 for the full explanation: Naive means too ready to trust without question; its antonym is cynical, meaning distrustful of human sincerity.

mock36.word: In the absence of the Principal, the Vice-Principal _________ for him.
mock36.explanation: Officiates (कार्यभार संभालना) means to act as an official in charge of something — the Vice-Principal acts in the official capacity of the Principal.

mock36.word: Select the meaning of the given idiom: THE WHOLE BALL OF WAX
mock36.explanation: "The whole ball of wax" (सब कुछ) — इसका अर्थ है Everything — used when referring to the entirety of something। "Sagacious" (बुद्धिमान), "Meagre" (नगण्य), और "Complete disaster" (पूर्ण विनाश) असंबंधित हैं।

mock36.word: amid the inability of the railways ______ adequate space.
mock36.explanation: "Inability to provide" — infinitive form "to provide" यहाँ सही है।

mock36.word: Recycling is a key component of modern waste reduction and is the third component of the 'Reduce, Reuse and Recycle' waste hierarchy ______ aim is to extract the maximum practical benefits from products.
mock36.explanation: "Whose" (जिसका) is a possessive pronoun showing that the waste hierarchy possesses an aim, correctly linking the clause.

mock36.word: Elders drill and ______ the Indian culture within us as we grow.
mock36.explanation: "Pass on" (आगे बढ़ाना) means to give or transfer something, especially knowledge or tradition, to someone else, fitting the context of passing culture to the next generation. "Put on" means to wear; "Hold on" means to wait; "Grow on" means to become appealing over time — none fit the idea of transferring culture.

mock36.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. To be taken aback
mock36.explanation: "To be taken aback" (चौंक जाना) का अर्थ है to surprise or shock someone। Example: I was at first taken aback, not quite sure what was going on.

mock36.word: ...marked by the exemplary choices one has made ______ the freedom struggle...
mock36.explanation: "Along" (के साथ-साथ) means moving in a constant direction through a course, fitting the metaphor of a journey through the freedom struggle.

mock36.word: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains the grammatical error. The reduction in the cost of education due to an increase in subsidies offer by the government is also seen as a reason to get more educated.
options: The reduction in the cost of education
due to an increase in subsidies offer
is also seen as a reason to get more educated
mock36.explanation: "Offer" should be in its past participle form "offered," since the government has already performed the action of increasing subsidies; the phrase should read "due to an increase in subsidies offered by the government."

mock36.word: A. Poor posture may strain neck muscles. B. However, neck pain may occasionally be an indication of a more serious issue. C. Neck pain is a frequent problem among many people these days. D. If your neck pain is accompanied by numbness or lack of strength in your arms...
mock36.explanation: C — topic introduce। A — cause। B — serious issue की possibility। D — serious symptoms और medical attention।

mock36.word: A. Diwali, referred to as the 'festival of lights'. B. In the spiritual world, the event symbolises 'the triumph of light over darkness'. C. The five-day festival of Diwali is observed during the Hindu month of Kartika. D. In India, Diwali is also known as Deepawali.
mock36.explanation: D — Diwali का दूसरा नाम। B — spiritual significance। C — festival की timing। A — festival की विस्तृत जानकारी।

mock36.word: Select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the Idiom/Phrase. To jump out of one's skin
options: To be extremely startled or surprised
mock36.explanation: "To jump out of one's skin" (अचानक चौंक जाना) describes extreme shock or surprise — as if one jumped so high they left their own skin. It has nothing to do with pain, revealing personality, or arguing.

mock36.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. ON THE BALL
mock36.explanation: The idiom "On the ball" means to be very alert and fully aware of what is happening around you. (Hindi: सतर्क रहना / चौकस रहना) Example: The mother is glad to know that her son is always on the ball to learn new things in class. The idiom implies sharp focus, quick thinking, and readiness to respond — the exact opposite of being lazy or doing a bad job. Origin: The phrase likely comes from sports, where a player who is "on the ball" is paying full attention to the game.

mock36.word: ______, they were caught in the fast current.
mock36.explanation: "Soon" (शीघ्र) is an adverb meaning in or after a short time, correctly linking the event of jumping into the river with the immediately following event of being caught in the current. "If" and "when" are conjunctions that cannot join two separate sentences this way without restructuring, and "after" requires a determiner like "this/that" to function here.

mock36.word: Select the most appropriate option: "A herd or flock of animals being driven in a body"
mock36.explanation: "Drove" (पशु-समूह) — a herd or flock of animals being driven together। "Cluster" (गुच्छा), "Crowd" (भीड़ — people के लिए), और "Throng" (बड़ी भीड़) इस specific animal group को describe नहीं करते।

mock36.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. A blessing in disguise
options: A bad thing that becomes good later
A good thing that seemed bad at first
mock36.explanation: This idiom describes a situation that initially appears negative but turns out beneficial in the end.

mock36.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Lock horns
mock36.explanation: "Lock horns" (किसी से भिड़ना) means to engage in conflict with someone, often used for disputes between two parties.

mock36.word: Select the correct version of the following sentence. "He would be meet us next week."
options: He would not be met us next week.
He will be meet us next week.
He will be meeting us next week.
He would be meets us next week.
mock36.explanation: The sentence requires the future continuous tense for a planned future action: "will + be + verb-ing".

mock36.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Pick the option that gives their correct order. A. It is possible to retrain your brain to have a sound sleep. B. Creating a relaxing bedroom environment is very important too... C. This is possible if you make sure your room is undisturbed, quiet and dark. D. Start by regulating the times you go to bed and get up.
mock36.explanation: (As printed in the source, the answer key given is B) A, D, C, B, though the solution text describes the sequence B → D → C → A; this inconsistency exists in the original document.) Solution: B introduces 'retraining the brain' as the main subject. D follows — 'start by regulating times' (how to begin). C clarifies the 'this' from B (make the room undisturbed). A concludes with another important point (relaxing bedroom environment). Correct sequence: B → D → C → A.

mock36.word: ______ the last three years, Check-up
mock36.explanation: "During" (के दौरान) correctly indicates a period of time within which an action occurred, fitting "the last three years."

mock36.word: Every time we switch … the television we find some product or the other ______ pushed towards us.
mock36.explanation: Since the sentence is in the present continuous passive sense, "being" (the present participle form of "be") fits correctly to describe products being continuously pushed onto viewers. "Be" is the base form, "been" is past participle, and "to be" doesn't fit the grammatical construction needed here.

mock36.word: Select the option that will improve the underlined part: "This book provides an unrestrained account of the natural resources in India."
mock36.explanation: "Unrestrained" (अनियंत्रित) यहाँ context में सटीक नहीं है। "Exhaustive" (संपूर्ण/व्यापक) — thorough and complete, covering all aspects — इस context में सबसे सही है। Correct sentence: "This book provides an exhaustive account of the natural resources in India."

mock36.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute: "An omission of duty"
mock36.explanation: "Delinquency" (कर्तव्य-हनन/अपराध) — criminal behavior or omission of duty। "Deliberation" (विचार-विमर्श), "Delineation" (रेखांकन/वर्णन), और "Surrender" (आत्मसमर्पण) असंबंधित हैं।

mock36.word: Select the most appropriate synonym. HIND
mock36.explanation: "Hind" means situated at the back or rear of something (e.g., hind legs). "Rear" means the back part — both are synonyms.

mock37.word: Select the grammatically correct version of the following sentence. I like my puppies, Mac and Matt, but I like Matt best.
options: I like my puppies, Mac and Matt, but I like Matt better.
I like my puppies, Mac and Matt, but I like Matt best.
I like my puppies, Mac and Matt, but I like Matt much.
I like my puppies, Mac and Matt, but I like Matt most.
mock37.explanation: Since only two puppies (Mac and Matt) are being compared, the comparative form "better" should be used instead of the superlative "best," which is reserved for comparisons among three or more things.

mock37.word: In the following question, out of the given four alternatives, select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the Idiom/Phrase. Over and above
mock37.explanation: "Over and above" means something extra or in addition to what has already been mentioned or expected; it implies going beyond the necessary or anticipated requirement. For example, completing a task ahead of schedule along with extra information shows going "over and above" requirements.

mock37.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Desolate
mock37.explanation: Desolate (उजाड़) means empty, lonely, and without people. "Festive" (उत्सवपूर्ण) means joyful and related to celebration, the opposite of a sad, empty place. Allusive means making indirect references; Frugal means economical; Melancholic (उदास) means deeply sad — none oppose "desolate."

mock37.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word: FRIVOLOUS
mock37.explanation: "Frivolous" (तुच्छ/हल्का-फुल्का) का अर्थ है not having any serious purpose or value। इसका antonym है "Serious" (गंभीर) — demanding careful consideration। "Popular" (लोकप्रिय), "Reputed" (प्रतिष्ठित), और "Nervous" (घबराया हुआ) असंबंधित हैं।

mock37.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error: "Since the little girl answers / a few simple questions, / her parents say that / she is too intelligent."
mock37.explanation: "Too intelligent" का अर्थ है अधिक बुद्धिमान होना जो स्वीकार्य नहीं है — यह intended meaning नहीं है। यहाँ "very intelligent" होना चाहिए। Correct sentence: "Since the little girl answers a few simple questions, her parents say that she is very intelligent."

mock37.word: Unable to check her ______, Alice ran after the rabbit down the rabbit hole.
mock37.explanation: Curiosity (जिज्ञासा) means a desire to know or learn. Alice followed the rabbit because she couldn't control her desire to know more. Pleasure means happiness; Nervousness means anxiety; Wisdom means sound judgement.

mock37.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a spelling error. They need to / wave the participation / fees for people / with disabilities.
mock37.explanation: The correct spelling is "waive" — meaning to cancel or refrain from enforcing something. "Wave" is a different word referring to a gesture or a disturbance in water.

mock37.word: Agra's marble-marvel has such an overwhelming presence ______ other monuments in the town have been relegated … oblivion.
mock37.explanation: "Such...that..." is a correlative conjunction used to give a reason or result, fitting the structure of the sentence describing the Taj Mahal's overwhelming presence causing other monuments to fade into obscurity. "Than," "but," and "for" do not complete this correlative structure correctly.

mock37.word: (P) kindness and empathy (Q) in a world that can be (O) bring light and positivity (R) harsh and challenging
mock37.explanation: Q — context set करना। R — world को describe करना। P — kindness and empathy introduce। O — effect बताना। Correct Sentence: In a world that can be harsh and challenging, kindness and empathy bring light and positivity.

mock37.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Vitiate
mock37.explanation: Vitiate (दोषपूर्ण बनाना) means to spoil or impair the quality of something; to make it faulty. Annihilate (पूर्णतः नष्ट करना) means to completely destroy or obliterate something — closest synonym. Facilitate means to make an action or process easier. Validate means to check or prove the validity. Propagate means to spread and promote an idea.
When comparing two things using "than," comparative degree is needed. The comparative of "cute" is "cuter" (अधिक प्यारा). "More cute" is redundant since "cuter" already exists as a standard form.

mock37.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the option that gives their correct order. A. He would sit at the edge of her mother's bed and stare into her crib. B. Arvind bumbled down the interminable corridor, suddenly reminded of his daughter in the days after she was born. C. As a proportion to the fragment of life his daughter had seen, an hour was a vast sprawling space. D. Some days he would imagine the world through her eyes and he would feel in his heart how long an hour actually was.
mock37.explanation: B introduces the main subject independently (Arvind reminded of his daughter), A continues describing what he would do, D follows by describing how he would empathize with her, and C concludes by elaborating on how vast an hour felt to her in proportion to her life, completing the reflective narrative.

mock37.word: After a nice shower, the elephant walked ______ to a rock, lifted it, and drank from a … under it.
mock37.explanation: "Over" (पार/ऊपर से) indicates movement to or across a place, fitting the description of the elephant walking up to and over a rock. "Above" means at a higher level without movement; "Near" means close to; "Behind" means at the back — none accurately describe approaching and standing over a rock.

mock37.word: I ______ the sun sink below the western isles.
mock37.explanation: "Fancied" का अर्थ है imagined या believed। "I fancied I could glimpse" — मुझे लगा मैं देख सकता हूँ।

mock37.word: Choose the word that is opposite in meaning to the given word. Algid
mock37.explanation: Algid (ठंडा/शीतल) is an adjective meaning cold or chilly. Igneous (अग्निज) is a geological term relating to rock formed from solidification of molten material — implies the involvement of heat, thus opposite of cold. Algae is a simple aquatic plant. Gelid also means extremely cold — similar, not opposite. Clean means free from dirt.

mock37.word: ...the book was right out of her hands, and she ______ at her mother standing over her.
mock37.explanation: "Stared" (टकटकी लगाकर देखा) — surprised/shocked होने पर fixedly देखना। माँ ने अचानक किताब छीन ली, इसलिए Gabriella ने stare किया। "Watched" (देखा), "Noticed" (ध्यान दिया), और "Wondered" (सोचा) इस emotional reaction को capture नहीं करते।

mock37.word: Some parts of one or more sentences have been jumbled up, and labelled A, B, C and D. Select the option that gives the correct sequence in which these parts can be rearranged to form a meaningful and grammatically correct sentence. At Paris, just after dark A. of meditation and a meerschaum, in company B. one gusty evening in the autumn C. of 18, I was enjoying the twofold luxury D. with my friend Auguste Dupin, in his little back library
mock37.explanation: The sentence logically reads: "At Paris, just after dark, one gusty evening in the autumn of 18, I was enjoying the twofold luxury of meditation and a meerschaum, in company with my friend Auguste Dupin, in his little back library." This shows the author enjoying meditation and smoking with a friend in Paris, following the order B (time), C (continues time, introduces "I"), A (describes the luxury), D (names the friend and place).

mock37.word: ...canyons are not as long, but they are ______.
mock37.explanation: "Impressive" (प्रभावशाली) — इन canyons की size और nature को describe करता है। Context में author already mentioned canyons से तुलना कर रहा है। "Exciting" (रोमांचक), "Inspiring" (प्रेरणादायक), और "Intense" (तीव्र) इस descriptive context में कम सटीक हैं।

mock37.word: Fill in the blank: '____ he was late, he came home by nine.' (Duplicate of Question 49 in the source)
mock37.explanation: This question is a duplicate of Question 49 in the source material. See Question 49 for the full explanation: The sentence expresses contrast (he was late, yet he came home by nine), best expressed by 'although.'

mock37.word: All at once it was as if the entire scene was ______ by that voice.
mock37.explanation: "Awakened" (जागृत) conveys the scene being brought to life by the voice.

mock37.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Repercussion
mock37.explanation: Repercussion means an unintended, often unwelcome, consequence of an action. "Reaction" (प्रतिक्रिया) means something done or felt in response to an event, which closely matches. Reminder means something that helps recall; Rearrangement means changing the order of things; Recollection means memory — none fit.

mock37.word: All the files are routed ___ the Section Officer of the department.
mock37.explanation: "Through" (के माध्यम से) means by means of, fitting the sense that the files pass via the Section Officer. "Along" means in a line beside something; "Across" means from one side to another; "by" doesn't fit grammatically with "routed" here.

mock37.word: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error. He wondered what was the reason for her refusal to accompany him.
mock37.explanation: When converting a direct interrogative into indirect speech (embedded question), the subject should come before the verb. The correct form is "the reason was."

"Bent to harm" गलत है। सही phrase है "bent on harming" जिसका अर्थ है to be very determined। Gerund "harming" preposition "on" के बाद आता है। Correct sentence: "He is bent on harming my reputation by false accusations."

mock37.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word: SQUALID
mock37.explanation: "Squalid" (गंदा/जीर्ण-शीर्ण) का अर्थ है extremely dirty and unpleasant। इसका antonym है "Clean" (साफ) — free from dirt or stains। "Contemporary" (समकालीन), "Bare" (नग्न/खाली), और "Advanced" (उन्नत) असंबंधित हैं।

mock38.word: A note of _________ during the leaders' meeting at the UN.
mock38.explanation: "A note of discord" (असहमति की सुर) is a fixed phrase meaning the state of not agreeing or sharing opinions. Example: A note of discord has crept into relations between the two countries.

mock38.word: Identify the segment which contains a grammatical error: "Whole country applauded the courage of our soldiers."
mock38.explanation: "Whole country" से पहले definite article "the" होना चाहिए क्योंकि एक specific और unique entity (पूरा देश) की बात हो रही है। Correct sentence: "The whole country applauded the courage of our soldiers."

mock38.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock38.explanation: "Persuit" is incorrectly spelt; the correct spelling is "Pursuit" (खोज, पीछा करना), meaning the act of following or chasing something. "Actually" means in fact, "Parallel" means side by side at equal distance, and "Opinion" means a belief or judgement — all correctly spelt.

mock38.word: Sentences about advertising evolution are given in jumbled order. Arrange them correctly. A. It was as simple as announcing what you have in your store. B. Over the years, advertising has evolved into a major industry. C. Advertising was initially meant to make people aware of the goods available in the market. D. It is a form of brainwashing consumers.
mock38.explanation: C introduces advertising's original purpose; A elaborates on its initial simplicity; B describes its evolution into an industry; D concludes with its modern manipulative impact.

mock38.word: The newspapers are the ______ of day-to-day happenings in the world.
mock38.explanation: "Storehouse" (भंडार) metaphorically conveys newspapers as a vast repository of information.

mock38.word: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains the grammatical error. Having just taking a heavy lunch, she was not ready to have any fruit.
options: Having just taking a heavy lunch
mock38.explanation: The perfect participle construction requires "taken" (past participle), not "taking," giving the structure "having + past participle" to indicate a completed prior action: "Having just taken a heavy lunch, she was not ready to have any fruit."

mock38.word: Choose the word that means the same as the given word. Quail
mock38.explanation: Quail (डर से सिकुड़ना) as a verb means to show fear or apprehension. Cringe (भय से सिकुड़ना) means to shrink back in fear or behave in a timid way — a direct synonym. Approach means to come near. Assail means to attack. Tyrant means an oppressive ruler.

mock38.word: They missed their train / because they were waiting / on wrong platform. / They were on platform 3 instead of platform 8. Identify the segment with the error.
options: They were on platform 3 instead of platform 8
mock38.explanation: Correct Answer: (C) on wrong platform. "Wrong platform" is a specific noun phrase and requires a definite article. It should be "on the wrong platform."

mock38.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Refute (खंडन करना)
mock38.explanation: "Refute" का अर्थ है to prove wrong। इसका antonym "Endorse" (openly approve — खुलकर समर्थन देना) है।

mock38.word: Select the most suitable idiom to fill in the blank. "Extreme inflation in the country has led to a hike in the price of day-to-day commodity. It has made it difficult to ___."
options: Costs an arm and a leg
mock38.explanation: "Keep body and soul together" (जीवित रहना) means to manage to stay alive with just enough of life's necessities, which fits the context of inflation.

mock38.word: Consistency can never be attained in a sporadic or ______ manner.
mock38.explanation: The blank is paired with 'sporadic' (meaning occasional, irregular) using 'or,' so both words must be synonymous. Casual means relaxed and unconcerned - not a direct synonym of sporadic. Intermittent means occurring at irregular intervals, not continuous - a near-perfect synonym for sporadic. Focus and Discipline are nouns and grammatically cannot fill a blank requiring an adjective modifying 'manner'. Grammar Rule: When two words are joined by 'or,' they must be the same part of speech and carry similar meanings.

mock38.word: ...the papers she had ______ on the desk.
mock38.explanation: "Stacked" (ढेर लगाना/व्यवस्थित करना) — papers को orderly pile में arrange करना। "Slacked" (ढीला करना), "Mounded" (ढेर बनाना — informal), और "Tacked" (थोड़ा जोड़ना) इस context में कम appropriate हैं।

mock38.word: According to experts, some sectors remain ______ during these difficult times, including banking and financial services, FMCG, food, and technology.
mock38.explanation: Resilient (लचीला/सहनशील) means able to withstand or recover quickly from difficult conditions, fitting the context of sectors surviving tough times.

mock38.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Prerogative
mock38.explanation: "Prerogative" (विशेषाधिकार) means a right or privilege exclusive to a particular individual or class. "Privilege" (विशेषाधिकार) is a direct synonym. "Bias" (पूर्वाग्रह) means prejudice; "Predisposition" (पूर्वनिर्धारण) means a tendency.

mock38.word: Sentences of a paragraph about Martin Avdeitch are given in jumbled order. Arrange them correctly. A. He had a tiny room in a basement, the one window of which looked out on to the street. B. There was hardly a pair of boots in the neighbourhood that had not been once or twice through his hands. C. In a certain town there lived a cobbler, Martin Avdeitch by name. D. Through it one could see the feet of those who passed by, but Martin recognised the people by their boots.
mock38.explanation: C introduces Martin; A describes his living situation; D explains how he recognized people through the window; B concludes with his extensive cobbling work in the neighbourhood.

mock38.word: They said, "We were just taking pictures." (Change to Indirect Speech)
options: They said that they had just been taking pictures
They said that they had just took pictures
They said that we had just been taking pictures
mock38.explanation: "We were taking" (past continuous) → Indirect में "had been taking" (past perfect continuous) बन जाता है। "We" → "they" में बदलता है।

mock38.word: To skip about playfully
mock38.explanation: Gambol (खेलते हुए उछलना) means to run or jump about playfully — exact match. Scramble means to move hurriedly or clumsily. Amble means to walk at a slow, relaxed pace. Trundle means to move slowly and heavily, typically in a noisy way.

mock38.word: If substitution is not required select 'No improvement.' He was greatly disappointed to be failed again.
mock38.explanation: "To be failed" is an awkward and incorrect construction; the correct participle phrase is "on failing," meaning at the time of or because of failing, which fits naturally after "disappointed."

mock38.word: Choose the word that means the same as the given word: FORTITUDE
mock38.explanation: "Fortitude" (साहस/धैर्य) का अर्थ है strength of mind to endure difficult situations। इसका synonym है "Grit" (दृढ़ता) — a combination of passion, determination, and resilience। "Vacillation" (अनिश्चितता), "Plot" (षड्यंत्र), और "Grim" (उदास) भिन्न अर्थ रखते हैं।

mock38.word: The inter-city transport of parcels from Calcutta ______ Guwahati, Bhubaneswar and Patna.
mock38.explanation: Present simple tense में "comes" — regular route को describe करता है।

mock38.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word: Epitome
mock38.explanation: Epitome means a perfect example or embodiment of a quality, or a condensed summary (e.g. She is the epitome of elegance). Expansion means the process of becoming larger or more extensive - the opposite of a condensed/summarised form. Note: This antonym relationship is somewhat unusual and indirect; the official answer key marks 'Expansion' as correct and this has been retained from the official key. Exhort means to strongly urge - unrelated. Lucid means clearly expressed - unrelated. Precise means exact and accurate - unrelated. Memory Tip: Epitome (condensed) ↔ Expansion (enlarged); think Summary ↔ Elaboration.

mock38.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: BANTER
mock38.explanation: "Banter" (हंसी-मजाक) का अर्थ है playful and friendly exchange of teasing remarks। इसका synonym है "Repartee" (चटपटी बातचीत) — quick, witty remarks in conversation। "Rebuke" (फटकार), "Reliance" (निर्भरता), और "Commendation" (प्रशंसा) असंबंधित हैं।

mock38.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock38.explanation: The correct spelling is "Separate" (अलग करना), meaning to divide or keep apart.

mock38.word: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. Life is hard, unpredictable, but though manageable.
mock38.explanation: "Still" (फिर भी) shows that something continues to be true despite other circumstances. "Controllable" (नियंत्रणीय) means capable of being directed. The word "though" is a conjunction, not suitable here as an adjective modifier. Correct sentence: Life is hard, unpredictable, but still controllable.

mock38.word: If broadly beneficial immigration (प्रवासन) is to be sustained (बनाए रखा जाए), destination countries must acknowledge and ______ the real risks that it raises.
mock38.explanation: "Address the risks" (जोखिमों का समाधान करना) — यहाँ सबसे उपयुक्त है। Correct Sentence: ...must acknowledge and address the real risks.

mock39.word: This particular fashion was largely brought ______ by the urgent need.
mock39.explanation: "Brought about" एक phrasal verb है जिसका अर्थ है caused या induced। "Brought about by the need" — सही है।

mock39.word: Given below are four sentences. Pick the correct order: A. I pointed them out to my companion. B. The woman's face was familiar but I could not recall who she was. C. At the very next table to ours sat Lady Edgware and Brian Martin. D. As I was doing so, another couple came and joined them.
mock39.explanation: C (Lady Edgware spotted at next table) → A (pointed them out to companion) → D (another couple joined them) → B (narrator couldn't recall the woman). Scene set → action → simultaneous event → reflection. Correct order: CADB

mock39.word: If no substitution is required, select 'No improvement.' He made a desperate but also partial successful effort to change the ghastly topic.
mock39.explanation: "Also" is unnecessary when only one contrasting element is mentioned. "Partial" is an adjective that should modify a noun, not "successful." The adverb "partially" correctly modifies the adjective "successful." Also "a" is needed before the noun phrase. Correct sentence: He made a desperate but a partially successful effort to change the ghastly topic.

mock39.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence. Gandhi's approach, while effective in mobilising large segments of Indian society, was criticised by some for being passive and overly accommodating.
mock39.explanation: Accommodating (अनुकूल) means willing to help or adapt to others' needs. "Disobliging" (अनुचित, असहयोगी) means showing a lack of willingness to help, making it the opposite. Reconciling means resolving conflict; Strenuous (कठिन) means requiring great effort; Gratifying means satisfying — none are direct opposites.

mock39.word: Dr Lewin ______ of an important research finding.
mock39.explanation: "Learned" fits the description of Dr Lewin's research finding, an academic discovery.

mock39.word: Its nucleus ______ the relics of the Buddha.
mock39.explanation: "Kept" (रखा) means the nucleus of the stupa held/contained the relics of the Buddha. The simple past tense is consistent with the historical narrative of the passage.

mock39.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. She will be / write her / examination by this / time tomorrow.
mock39.explanation: The sentence uses future continuous tense, which requires will + be + V-ing. So "write her" must be replaced with "writing her." Correct sentence: She will be writing her examination by this time tomorrow.

mock39.word: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. A group of people leading the way in new developments or ideas
mock39.explanation: Vanguard means a group of people leading the way in new developments, ideas, or movements; the foremost position in a field. (Hindi: अग्रदूत / नेतृत्व करने वाला समूह) Example: A style of jazz that the vanguard quickly recognized as new and exciting. Other options: Sycophant (चापलूस) = a person who acts obsequiously towards someone important to gain advantage; Stooge (कठपुतली) = a subordinate used by another for unpleasant routine work; Zealot (कट्टरपंथी) = a person who is fanatical and uncompromising in pursuit of religious or political ideals.

mock39.word: A. Early on Christmas morning, Pip returns to the graveyard. B. On Christmas Eve, Pip encounters an escaped convict. C. The convict scares Pip into stealing food and a file. D. Pip, who lives with his elder sister, steals a file and some food.
mock39.explanation: B (Pip meets convict) → C (convict scares Pip) → D (Pip steals food/file) → A (Pip returns with stolen items). This follows the logical story sequence.

mock39.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences describing a calamity. Pick the option that gives their correct order. [Note: full question stem was cut off at the top of the source page; the passage concerns Kerala facing a deluge, with intense and consistent rainfall leading to many deaths besides rendering thousands homeless.] A) B, A, D, C B) D, B, C, A C) A, B, C, D D) D, B, A, C
mock39.explanation: B introduces the main subject — Kerala facing a deluge again. A provides context about the recent week's rainfall. D elaborates on the intensity and human impact. C details the specific worst-affected districts. The correct sequence: B (introduction of calamity) → A (cause) → D (impact) → C (specific areas).

mock39.word: She reached to (1)/station barely (2)/on time. (3)/No error (4)
mock39.explanation: "reached to" गलत है। "reach" एक transitive verb (सकर्मक क्रिया) है जिसके बाद सीधे object आता है, "to" नहीं। Correct Sentence: She reached station barely on time.

"Pull over" का अर्थ है रास्ते के किनारे रुकना — यह यहाँ गलत है। "Pull through" (बीमारी से उबरना) — to survive a dangerous or difficult situation। Correct sentence: "His disease is no doubt very serious but he will pull through."

mock39.word: Sentences of a paragraph given below in jumbled order: A. A variety of diseases can be linked to unsanitary conditions and careless garbage disposal. B. People understand the value of cleanliness but often forget they are responsible for environmental cleanliness... simply getting rid of waste from their homes will not help. C. A clean and green environment is a fundamental tenet of sustainable development. D. It could be due to a lack of information, pure laziness, or recklessness. Sometimes all it takes is a gentle nudge.
mock39.explanation: C establishes the core importance of a clean environment. A shows the consequence of unsanitary (अस्वच्छ) conditions. B addresses public responsibility, and D highlights the reasons behind this irresponsibility.

mock39.word: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them contains an error. "Mr. Unkerton glanced / to the inspector, / who merely snorted / and looked at the ceiling."
mock39.explanation: The verb "glanced" takes the preposition "at," not "to." The correct phrase is "glanced at the inspector."

mock39.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. I don't buy it
options: Only good decisions can be agreed on
I do not like to buy
mock39.explanation: This idiom expresses skepticism or disbelief about a statement, not a literal act of purchasing.

mock39.word: Select the correct passive form: "My father had to attend the meeting on Saturday."
options: The meeting has to be attended by my father on Saturday.
The meeting had been to be attended by my father on Saturday.
The meeting had been attended by my father on Saturday.
The meeting had to be attended by my father on Saturday.
mock39.explanation: When an infinitive structure "had to + V1" is used, the passive form becomes "had to be + V3," correctly giving "had to be attended."

mock39.word: While I was sitting there ______ over my failure...
mock39.explanation: "Sulking" (मुँह फुलाना/खिन्न होना) — failure के कारण silent और bad-tempered रहना। "Grinning" (मुस्कुराना), "Clapping" (ताली बजाना), और "Smirking" (व्यंग्य से मुस्कुराना) positive या inappropriate emotions हैं।

mock39.word: Portia claims that even if she … to live as long as Sibylla, she would die as ______ as Diana because she can only be claimed … the manner specified by her father's will.
mock39.explanation: "Pure" (शुद्ध) means free from anything inferior or contaminating, fitting the comparison to Diana, the goddess associated with chastity and purity. "Clear" means easily understood; "Real" means actually existing; "Plain" means simple — none capture the symbolic purity comparison with Diana.

mock39.word: ______ most individuals in countries where vaccination is widespread have never personally experienced the vaccine-preventable disease.
mock39.explanation: "For instance" (उदाहरण के लिए) — एक example introduce करता है जो previous statement को support करता है। "Therefore" (इसलिए — cause-effect), "In addition" (इसके अलावा), और "Likewise" (इसी तरह) इस context में fit नहीं होते।

mock39.word: Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains a grammatical error. She is quiet a generous woman / to have donated / such a large amount / for the flood affected people.
options: She is quiet a generous woman
mock39.explanation: The error is the use of "quiet" instead of "quite." These two words are commonly confused but have completely different meanings. QUIET (adjective/adverb) = making little or no noise; silent. (Hindi: शांत / चुप) QUITE (degree adverb) = to a significant extent; completely; very much. (Hindi: काफी / बिल्कुल) The sentence needs the degree adverb "quite" to intensify the adjective "generous." Standard structure: Subject + be + quite + a/an + adjective + noun. Correct sentence: "She is quite a generous woman to have donated such a large amount for the flood affected people." The remaining three parts — "to have donated" (correct perfect infinitive showing past action), "such a large amount" (correct determiner phrase), and "for the flood affected people" (correct prepositional phrase) — are all grammatically accurate.

mock39.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom: TWIDDLING ONE'S THUMBS
mock39.explanation: "Twiddling one's thumbs" (बेकार बैठे रहना) — इसका अर्थ है to be idle or bored because one has nothing to do। Example: "I have been sitting here for goodness knows how long twiddling my thumbs." "Sad" (दुखी), "Hungry" (भूखा), और "Tired" (थका हुआ) गलत हैं।

mock39.word: If there is no need to improve it, select 'No improvement required.' Mrs. Dhar teach in this school for the last ten years.
mock39.explanation: The phrase "for the last ten years" is the key trigger. It signals an action that BEGAN in the past and CONTINUES up to the present moment — this requires Present Perfect Continuous Tense. Structure: Subject + has/have + been + V-ing + for/since + time period. Correct sentence: "Mrs. Dhar has been teaching in this school for the last ten years." Why other options are wrong: "was teaching" = Past Continuous — implies the teaching is already over and completed (wrong — she is still teaching); "had taught" = Past Perfect — used when one past action was completed BEFORE another past event (wrong — no second past event mentioned); "teach" = base form (grammatically wrong entirely). Memory rule: FOR = duration of a time period; SINCE = a specific point in time when the action began.

mock39.word: ...his sister knocked at the door ______.
mock39.explanation: "Relentlessly" (निरंतर रूप से) means without stopping, matching the sister's continuous, anxious knocking.

mock39.word: If no substitution is required, select 'No substitution.': 'Wasim agreed to share his notes with Arif on conditions that he are return them in a day.'
options: on the condition that he would return
on his condition that he is returning
on condition that he will be return
mock39.explanation: The sentence contains two errors: the helping verb 'are' doesn't agree with the subject 'he' and doesn't fit the conditional context; and the conditional mood requires a modal auxiliary verb. Grammar Rule - Conditional Sentences: In a hypothetical condition in reported context, we use modal auxiliaries like would, could, might. Since 'agreed' is past tense, the verb in the conditional clause must also shift to past: 'would return.' 'On the condition that' is a fixed phrase requiring the article 'the.' Rule: 'Agreed + that/on condition that + would + base verb' - when the reporting verb is past tense, the conditional verb shifts accordingly. Correct Sentence: Wasim agreed to share his notes with Arif on the condition that he would return them in a day.

mock39.word: Find the part of the given sentence that has an error: "My cousin's watch is more elegant than your old watch."
mock39.explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct; the comparison between the two watches is clearly and accurately expressed.

mock40.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: PROFESS
mock40.explanation: "Profess" (घोषित करना) का अर्थ है to claim something openly। इसका synonym है "Declare" (घोषणा करना) — to announce clearly and publicly। "Convince" (मनाना), "Assume" (मान लेना), और "Predict" (भविष्यवाणी करना) अलग-अलग अर्थ रखते हैं।

mock40.word: The invention of fertilizers and insecticides has increased agricultural ______ which is required to feed the swelling population.
mock40.explanation: "Agricultural output" (कृषि उत्पादन) — the action or process of producing something। Farm production describe करने का standard term। "Goods" (वस्तुएँ), "Cargo" (माल/सामान), और "Product" (उत्पाद) less specific हैं।

mock40.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Out of the given options pick the one that gives their correct order. A — He meets me in the same frank friendly way as always without mentioning it. B — He has owed it to me for twelve months now. C — I can realise whenever I meet him that he has forgotten that he owes me a dollar. D — My friend Todd owes me a dollar.
mock40.explanation: The rule for sentence rearrangement is: a sentence that begins with a PRONOUN (He, She, It, They, etc.) CANNOT come first unless the noun it refers to has already been introduced. Step 1 — D must come first: It is the only sentence that introduces the main noun "Todd" and the basic situation (Todd owes a dollar). Sentences A, B, C all start with pronouns (He/I) that refer to Todd and the narrator — so none of them can come first. Step 2 — B follows D: It gives the duration — "He has owed it to me for twelve months now." This adds the time context to the established situation. Step 3 — C follows B: The narrator reflects on the realisation — "I can realise whenever I meet him that he has forgotten he owes me a dollar." Step 4 — A concludes: A describes the observed behaviour — "He meets me in the same frank friendly way as always without mentioning it." Correct order: D → B → C → A = DBCA.

mock40.word: Select the most appropriate option for the given group of words: "A group of three novels or plays, each complete in itself"
mock40.explanation: "Trilogy" (त्रयी) — a group of three related novels, plays, or films। "Triplet" (संगीत में तीन notes), "Trivet" (तिपाई), और "Triumvir" (प्राचीन Rome का तीन-अधिकारी समूह) असंबंधित हैं।

mock40.word: Grandmere started ______ me from the phone.
mock40.explanation: "Beckoning" (इशारे से बुलाना) — Grandmere phone से hand gesture से बुला रही थी। "Upsetting" (परेशान करना), "Dismissing" (नज़रअंदाज़ करना), और "Disconcerting" (अस्थिर करना) इस physical action को describe नहीं करते।

mock40.word: By the way a lorry / crashed into the car but / the boys were lucky and / escaped with only minor injuries. Identify the segment with the error.
mock40.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) By the way a lorry. The introductory phrase "By the way" is awkward and contextually irrelevant (अप्रासंगिक) here. The sentence should begin directly with "A lorry crashed..."

mock40.word: Out of the given four alternatives, select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the Idiom/Phrase. To take the veil
mock40.explanation: "To take the veil" means to make a commitment to live a life of religious service within a convent or monastery — to become a nun.

mock40.word: Remember to record ______ does what by when.
mock40.explanation: "Who" (जो/कौन) is used as a relative pronoun introducing a clause providing information about a person, fitting the context of documenting which person does which task. "That" introduces a restrictive clause for things; "Whom" is used as an object pronoun; "Which" refers to things or animals — none fit referring to "who does what."
"Of the Mauryan Emperors" correctly establishes comparison within a specific group using proper grammatical structure.

mock40.word: The percentage of employees who called in sick and the number of employees who left their jobs within 2 years (were reflective of the level of job satisfaction).
options: are reflective of the level of job satisfaction
is reflective of the level of job satisfaction
has reflected the level of job satisfaction
were reflective on the level of job satisfaction
mock40.explanation: दो subjects "and" से जुड़े हैं — compound subject के साथ plural verb "are" आता है। Present tense सही है क्योंकि outcomes अभी भी relevant हैं। Correct Sentence: ...are reflective of the level of job satisfaction.

mock40.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute. His parents didn't allow him to enrol in the arts college because he is a dabbler (उथला ज्ञान रखने वाला) in arts, science or literature.
mock40.explanation: "Dilettante" का अर्थ है a person who takes part in an activity casually without real commitment। Example: As a dilettante, she dabbles in various artistic hobbies.

mock40.word: I should have stopped them then and there. But it never even ___ to me that I should.
mock40.explanation: "Occurred to" (मन में आना) means a thought came into someone's mind. This exactly fits the context — the idea never entered the speaker's mind. "Suggested" and "reminded" require an agent. "Happened" doesn't collocate with "to me" in this sense.

mock40.word: Select the option that correctly expresses the given sentence in passive voice: "The teachers of our school have already taken this training."
options: This training has already been taken by the teachers of our school.
This training has been taken by the teachers of our school.
This training has already been taken by the our school teachers.
This training was already taken by the teachers of our school.
mock40.explanation: Using present perfect passive structure "has + been + V3," the object "this training" becomes the subject, with "by the teachers of our school" added, retaining "already" for clarity.

mock40.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the option that gives their correct order. A. However, new research shows that taking the dog for a walk can have its downsides for seniors. B. Dogs are great companions and provide a healthy excuse to go for a walk and get a bit of exercise. C. It once seemed common sense to believe that having and walking a dog was good for older people. D. A report published in an American medical journal says injuries among seniors related to dog-walking are becoming increasingly prevalent.
mock40.explanation: Sentence C must come first because it is the introductory, standalone statement that presents the general topic ("It once seemed common sense..."). Sentence B comes second because it explains the reasoning behind that common belief — that dogs are good companions for exercise. Sentence A comes third, since it begins with the contrast marker "However," signalling that what follows will challenge the previous claims. Sentence D comes last because it supplies the supporting evidence (the medical journal report) for the contradiction introduced in sentence A. The key strategy for these questions is to look for connector words like "however," "therefore," or "this," which point backward or forward to other sentences in the set, and to identify the one sentence that can stand independently as an opener.

mock40.word: They ______ these birds so well that they do not need to hunt every day.
mock40.explanation: Context में "Nature" subject है जो raptors को इतनी अच्छी तरह से design करती है (option C: nature)। Complete passage: "Nature serves these birds so well that they do not need to hunt every day."

mock40.word: Identify the ANTONYM-like contextual word, idiom replacement. He is too afraid of heights after the bungee jumping experience.
options: Light at the end of tunnel
mock40.explanation: Correct Answer: (C) Once bitten twice shy. "Once bitten twice shy" (एक बार धोखा खाने पर सतर्क होना) means a person is extremely cautious about doing something again because it caused them harm or fear in the past.

mock40.word: Choose the incorrectly spelt word.
mock40.explanation: The correct spelling is Clown (जोकर), meaning a comic entertainer. The extra 'u' in "Clouwn" makes it incorrect. Treasure (खजाना), Poison (जहर), and Mason (राजमिस्त्री) are all correctly spelled.

"To have cleaning up" is incorrect — "have" here wrongly combines with a gerund. The correct structure after "told me" is infinitive: "told me to clean up." "Clean up" (साफ करना) is the right phrasal verb.

mock40.word: Parts of a sentence about the origin of the Moon are given in jumbled order. Arrange them correctly. A. beginning in the 19th century, B. was long a mystery, and C. the origin of the Moon D. three competing theories emerged to solve it
mock40.explanation: C introduces the topic; B states it was mysterious; A provides the time context; D concludes with the emergence of competing theories.

mock40.word: ...the rain makes a more ______ colour.
mock40.explanation: "Soft" describes a faded, muted colour caused by sun and rain, matching the description of worn clothing.

mock40.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Contemptible
mock40.explanation: Contemptible (घृणित) means deserving contempt or despicable. Abhorrent, meaning inspiring disgust, matches this meaning closely.

mock40.word: ...isn't it ______ that science appears to be beyond the common mind's comprehension?
mock40.explanation: "Ironic" (विडम्बनापूर्ण) — Science जिस पर हमारी पूरी ज़िन्दगी टिकी है, वही आम लोगों को समझ नहीं आती — यह irony है। "Logical" (तार्किक), "Obvious" (स्पष्ट), और "Gracious" (शिष्ट) context में गलत हैं।

mock40.word: Choose the correct word for "Bad or immoral behaviour or habits."
mock40.explanation: Vice (दुर्गुण/बुरी आदत) means bad or immoral behaviour or habits. Spar means to argue or box. Diatribe means a forceful bitter verbal attack. Wacky means funny or amusing.

mock40.word: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains the grammatical error. The police wrongly charged an innocent man of murder.
mock40.explanation: The correct preposition after "charged" (when referring to an accusation) is "with," not "of." The corrected sentence reads "The police wrongly charged an innocent man with murder."

mock40.word: 'A stitch in time saves nine!' This wise saying suggests that an (1) action taken on time to rectify an error rules … the possibility of accumulation of such errors and future ______.
mock40.explanation: "Damages" (नुकसान) refers to physical or other harm caused, fitting the context of the saying about preventing future harm by acting promptly. "Qualities," "Advantages," and "Facilities" do not fit the negative consequence implied by failing to act on time.

mock41.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. TO MEET ONE'S WATERLOO
mock41.explanation: "To meet one's Waterloo" means to encounter one's ultimate obstacle and be decisively defeated by it — a defeat from which there is no recovery. (Hindi: अपनी अंतिम हार का सामना करना) Origin: The phrase comes from Napoleon Bonaparte's catastrophic and final defeat at the Battle of Waterloo in 1815, ending his military career permanently. Example: The president met his Waterloo at the election. The key distinction from other options: it is not just "any defeat" or "dying in battle" — it specifically means the FINAL, career-ending defeat.

mock41.word: allowing certain colleges to introduce their ______ courses.
mock41.explanation: "Autonomous" (स्वायत्त) means self-governing, fitting colleges granted independence to design their own courses.

mock41.word: The question below consists of a set of labelled sentences. Out of the four options given, select the most logical order of the sentences to form a coherent paragraph. Even our own languages A — it that the native speakers hardly B — have contributed words to C — consider foreign anymore
mock41.explanation: The base phrase is "Even our own languages" (given, not to be rearranged). We need to connect the three fragments A, B, C to it logically. Step 1 — Eliminate wrong starting fragments: A cannot be first because it begins with "it" (a pronoun) — there is no antecedent yet; C cannot be first because it begins with "consider" (a verb) without a subject. Step 2 — B must come first: "have contributed words to" is the main verb phrase that follows "Even our own languages" — making it: "Even our own languages have contributed words to..." Step 3 — A follows B: "it that the native speakers hardly" = the pronoun "it" now refers to the words contributed. Step 4 — C completes the sentence: "consider foreign anymore." Complete sentence: "Even our own languages have contributed words to it that the native speakers hardly consider foreign anymore." This means: even words from our own native languages have entered common usage to the extent that native speakers no longer think of them as foreign.

mock41.word: If my memory ______ me right, Abhimanyu was not killed by one person but by seven warriors.
mock41.explanation: "If my memory serves me right" एक fixed idiom है जिसका अर्थ है "if I remember correctly — अगर मुझे सही याद है।"

mock41.word: Parts of the given sentence have been given as options. The food was not / as delicious as / (expected).
mock41.explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct. The comparative structure "not as delicious as" is correctly used.

mock41.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute. The dark shape and outline of something visible against a bright background.
mock41.explanation: Silhouette (छाया-चित्र) means a dark shape seen against a light background, exactly matching the given definition; mirage refers to an optical illusion, not a shape outline.

mock41.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Penitent
mock41.explanation: Penitent (पश्चातापी) means feeling or showing sorrow and regret for having done wrong; repentant. Unrepentant (अपश्चातापी) means showing no regret for one's wrongdoings — exact opposite. Sorry means feeling sad. Penniless means having no money. Pertinent means relevant or applicable to a particular matter.

mock41.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Get something off one's chest
options: To defeat someone in a physical fight
To stop oneself from confiding in a friend
To escape from a dangerous situation
Talk about something that is worrying you
mock41.explanation: "Get something off one's chest" (मन की बात कह देना) का अर्थ है to tell someone about something that has been making one upset। Example: I was glad to get it off my chest.

mock41.word: Find out which part of the sentence has an error. Every effect we see, hear or speak in our outside (1) / or physical world has a specific cause that (2) / have its origin in the inner or mental world. (3) / No error (4)
mock41.explanation: The error is "have" — it should be "has." The relative pronoun "that" in Part 2 refers back to "cause" (singular noun). Therefore, the verb that follows must also be singular: "has." Rule: Subject-verb agreement — always trace the relative pronoun back to its antecedent (the noun it refers to) to decide the correct verb form. Here: "cause" (singular) → "that" → "has" (singular verb). Correct Part 3: "has its origin in the inner or mental world." Parts 1 and 2 are grammatically correct.

mock41.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Renegade
mock41.explanation: Renegade (विश्वासघाती/देशद्रोही) means a traitor or a person who betrays another. Follower (अनुयायी/समर्थक) means an admirer or supporter — opposite of traitor. Leader means a head of a group. Fugitive means a person who has escaped. Criminal means one who has committed a crime.

mock41.word: People of the older generation ___ over the good old school days.
