mock41.explanation: "Reminisces over" (पुरानी यादों में खोना) means to indulge in enjoyable recollections of past events. "Remember" and "remind" cannot be combined with "over" to carry this meaning. "Retribute" means to pay back and is contextually wrong.

mock41.word: Please complete the short questionnaire _________ and return it to us.
mock41.explanation: Attached (संलग्न/जुड़ा हुआ) means joined, fastened, or connected to something — a questionnaire attached to a letter or document.

mock41.word: tea output in the next year may reach to 800 tonnes as ______ 780 tonnes last year.
mock41.explanation: "Against" comparison के लिए उपयुक्त preposition है। "800 tonnes as against 780 tonnes" = पिछले साल की तुलना में।

mock41.word: The rope gave way while the workmen were hauling up the iron pillar. Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined phrase.
mock41.explanation: "Gave way" (टूट जाना) means suddenly broke, matching that the rope snapped under pressure while the pillar was being hauled.

mock41.word: Select the word that best completes the given sentence (collocation). Marz would describe it in _____ detail.
mock41.explanation: "In great detail" (बड़े विस्तार से) एक common collocation है। "Great detail" का अर्थ है extensive या thorough detail। Correct Sentence: Marz would describe it in great detail.

mock41.word: ...your point of view undergoes a ______.
mock41.explanation: "Transformation" (परिवर्तन) — point of view में thorough or dramatic change। Studying से perspective में fundamental change आता है। "Backwardness" (पिछड़ापन), "Enlargement" (विस्तार), और "Succession" (क्रम) इस context में गलत हैं।

mock41.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Actions speak louder than words
options: Speaking loudly is the best way to let others know what you want
Whatever you speak is less important than what you write
Intentions can be judged by people's actions
mock41.explanation: This idiom emphasizes that what a person does reveals their true intentions better than what they say.

mock41.word: Select the most appropriate one-word substitution: "The action of examining with care or reading carefully"
mock41.explanation: "Perusal" (ध्यानपूर्वक पठन) — the examination or careful reading of something। "Persuade" (मनाना), "Perverse" (हठी/विकृत), और "Persiflage" (हल्का व्यंग्य) असंबंधित हैं।

mock41.word: Select the option with the correct spelling that can replace the underlined word in the given sentence. Kasturi acts as a liason between patients and staff.
mock41.explanation: Liaison (संपर्क सूत्र) means a person or communication that serves as a connection between individuals, groups, or organizations. All other options are common misspellings. Correct sentence: Kasturi acts as a liaison between patients and staff.

mock41.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Foment (भड़काना)
mock41.explanation: "Foment" का अर्थ है to instigate या stir up। इसका antonym "Regulate" (control or maintain properly — नियंत्रित करना) है।

mock41.word: Select the option that expresses the opposite meaning of the underlined word. The explosive used is of my own formulation, and I can vouch for its efficiency.
mock41.explanation: Vouch (प्रमाणित करना) means to confirm something is true based on one's own experience. "Invalidate" (अमान्य करना) means to make an argument unsound or disprove it, the direct opposite. Maintain means to preserve; Certify means to officially confirm; Witness means to observe an event — none oppose the act of confirming.

mock41.word: The aborigines (आदिवासी) were often ______ as sub-humans with low status.
mock41.explanation: "Perceived" का अर्थ है to notice or realize। यहाँ यह subjective viewpoint दर्शाता है — लोगों ने उन्हें ऐसा समझा।

mock41.word: work as the purifying ______ in the system.
mock41.explanation: "Station" (स्टेशन / स्थान) denotes a place where a specific function (purification) occurs, fitting the metaphor.

mock41.word: Parts of a sentence about watching a movie are given in jumbled order. Arrange them correctly. O. want P. movie tonight Q. do you R. to watch S. the
mock41.explanation: The correct sentence reads "Do you want to watch the movie tonight?" following the logical question structure.

mock41.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. FORLORN
mock41.explanation: Forlorn means feeling sad and abandoned. "Elated" (उल्लसित) means ecstatically happy, the clear opposite. Desperate means having a great need; Solitary means alone; Bereft (वंचित) means deprived of something — none oppose sadness directly.

mock42.word: Select the option that will improve the underlined part. Shubham and I were watching football match in our bedroom.
mock42.explanation: "Shubham and I" plural subject है, इसलिए "were watching" सही है। Subject-verb agreement के अनुसार कोई सुधार नहीं चाहिए।

mock42.word: People have dug ______ monuments and collected artifacts for thousands of years.
mock42.explanation: "Dug up" (खोदकर बाहर निकालना) is a fixed phrasal verb meaning to take something out of the ground by digging, exactly fitting the context of unearthing monuments and artifacts. "Ahead," "Over," and "In" do not form this standard phrasal verb.

mock42.word: Select the one which best expresses the meaning of the given word. Implicit (अप्रत्यक्ष)
mock42.explanation: ऊपर Q.45 में explain किया जा चुका है।

mock42.word: Parts of the given sentence have been given as options. You can take a horse to the water but you cannot make it to drink.
mock42.explanation: The phrase "make it to drink" is incorrect. With the causative verb "make" followed by an object and an infinitive, we do not use "to": "make it drink."

mock42.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of highlighted word: SUBLIME
mock42.explanation: "Sublime" (उत्कृष्ट/श्रेष्ठ) का अर्थ है of extremely high quality। इसका antonym है "Inferior" (निम्न/घटिया) — lower in quality or importance। "Superior" (श्रेष्ठ) synonym है, "Interior" (आंतरिक) और "Exterior" (बाहरी) असंबंधित हैं।

mock42.word: The team played / extremely / good / during the match. Identify the segment with the error.
mock42.explanation: Correct Answer: (C) good. "Good" is an adjective. To modify the verb "played," an adverb is required. The correct adverb form is "well."

mock42.word: It's dinner time for the 25-year-old tusker and he's ______ balls made of steamed ragi flour
mock42.explanation: The sentence uses passive voice describing a general routine, so "fed" (the past participle, used passively) is correct: "he's fed balls of ragi flour..." "Feed," "feeds," and "feeding" don't fit the passive construction needed here.

mock42.word: (Climatic disasters passage) By taking a _________ approach to disaster management, we can reduce the impacts of climatic disasters and build more resilient communities.
mock42.explanation: Proactive (पहले से सक्रिय) means taking action before a situation becomes a problem — a forward-thinking, preventive behaviour, perfect for disaster management context.

mock42.word: Is there ______ buttermilk in the cooler?
mock42.explanation: "Any" is used with countable/uncountable nouns in questions and negative sentences to ask about an unspecified amount.

mock42.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech. I said to Hila, "Don't litter."
options: I advised Hia to not litter.
I implored Hia not to litter.
I ordered Hia to not litter.
I offered Hia to not litter.
mock42.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) I implored Hia not to litter. "Implored" (विनती की) captures the earnest tone of the request better than merely ordering or advising. Also, the spelling variations of "Hila/Hia" correspond to the options provided.

mock42.word: Select the correct passive form of the given sentence. Grow more trees.
mock42.explanation: The sentence is imperative ("Grow" is a command). For imperative sentences in passive voice, the structure is: Let + Object + be + V3.

mock42.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock42.explanation: "Prepaired" गलत spelling है। सही spelling "Prepared" (तैयार) है।

mock42.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined group of words. A poem that tells a story with a regular rhythm and rhyme scheme.
mock42.explanation: Ballad (लोकगीत/गाथागीत) is a type of poem or song that narrates a story in short stanzas with a regular rhythm and rhyme scheme — exact match. Haiku is a very short Japanese poem. Epic is a long narrative poem about heroes. Sonnet is a poem of fourteen lines expressing a single complete thought.

mock42.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word. Ritika was deprived of her father's property as she married a knave.
mock42.explanation: Knave (बेईमान व्यक्ति) means a dishonest or unscrupulous man. Rogue (बदमाश/धूर्त व्यक्ति) also means a dishonest or unprincipled person — synonyms. Gallant means brave or showy. Novice means a person new and inexperienced in a job. Chauffeur means someone whose job is to drive a car for a rich person.

mock42.word: Vivek should be excited / on the promotion / he's got.
mock42.explanation: "on" की जगह "about" होना चाहिए। "about" किसी विषय के लिए प्रयुक्त होता है। Correct Sentence: Vivek should be excited about the promotion he's got.

mock42.word: Similarly, mathematics, the ______ of science, gives meaning...
mock42.explanation: "Bedrock" (आधारशिला) — fundamental principles on which something is based। Mathematics science की foundation है। "Glory" (गौरव), "Secret" (रहस्य), और "Crest" (शिखर/कलगी) इस context में fit नहीं होते।

mock42.word: Choose the option that is the active form of the sentence. All the children who were selected for the dance were asked to bring their costumes the next day.
options: The teacher asked all the children who are going to be selected for the dance to bring their costumes the next day.
The teacher asked all the children select for the dance to bring their costumes the next day.
The children asked the teacher to bring their costumes the next day.
The teacher asked all the children who were selected for the dance to bring their costumes the next day.
mock42.explanation: Since the passive sentence does not name an agent, "the teacher" is supplied as the logical subject in the active voice. The tense (simple past) must remain consistent in both voices, making option D the correctly constructed active sentence.

mock42.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word: 'Although Edward had noble blood, his ignoble qualities made him incapable of taking over the kingdom.'
mock42.explanation: Note: This question appears to contain a source error. Ignoble means dishonourable, not noble; its true antonym would be noble, honourable, or worthy - 'Unworthy' is actually a SYNONYM of ignoble, not an antonym. Among the given options none is a perfect antonym, but the official key marks 'Unworthy' as the answer. Treat this question carefully as it may be flawed in the source material. For learning: Antonyms of Ignoble = noble, honourable, worthy, dignified, virtuous. Salient means most noticeable/important - unrelated. Ostentatious means designed to impress - unrelated.

mock42.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word:
mock42.explanation: 'Seperate' is incorrectly spelt. The correct spelling is SEPARATE, meaning not joined or touching, to cause to move or be apart. Inapplicable, forbidding, and smallish are all correctly spelt. Most Common Spelling Mistake in English: SEPARATE is one of the most misspelled words. Memory trick: there is A RAT in sepA-R-A-Te.

mock42.word: Identify the segment with an error: "In the wildlife reserve / Manisha saw an old elephant / while she was / on safari tour."
mock42.explanation: The indefinite article "a" is missing; the correct phrase should be "on a safari tour."

mock42.word: India had only 1.2 million cell phone users in 1999, which grew to an ______ figure of 1161.7 million in 2019.
mock42.explanation: 1.2 million से 1161.7 million — यह वृद्धि चौंका देने वाली है। "Astounding" (अद्भुत, हैरान कर देने वाला) सबसे उपयुक्त है।

mock42.word: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. "(a) The more it rains, / (b) worse / (c) the roads become / (d) No error"
mock42.explanation: In comparative correlative constructions ("the more..."), the corresponding part must also include "the." It should be "the worse."

mock42.word: Find the correctly spelt word.
mock42.explanation: "Fluorescent" (प्रतिदीप्त) is the correct spelling. It means a surface that glows when light is directed onto it. All other options are misspelt.

mock42.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Make off with
mock42.explanation: "Make off with" (चुराकर भाग जाना) means to carry something away illicitly; it essentially means to steal. For example, "Burglars made off with all their wedding presents."

"In a pickle" (मुसीबत में) — metaphorical expression for a difficult or unfavorable situation। "End up in the same pickle" — बार-बार उसी मुसीबत में पड़ना। "Account" (खाता), "Meal" (भोजन), और "Sauce" (चटनी) literal meaning में हैं।

mock43.word: It is better to be prompt (तत्पर) and organised than to be ______.
mock43.explanation: "Reckless" का अर्थ है not thinking about possible bad results। यहाँ prompt और organised का विपरीत concept है। Correct Sentence: ...than to be reckless.

mock43.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Go easy on something
options: Take as much as one wants
Use only a small amount of
mock43.explanation: "Go easy on something" (किसी चीज़ का कम उपयोग करना) means to use only a limited amount of something, often advised for health reasons. For example, "The doctor told me to go easy on spicy food."

mock43.word: During the past three months, tea prices have shown an ______.
mock43.explanation: Context में prices stabilised at high levels — "uptrend" (बढ़ती हुई प्रवृत्ति) सबसे उपयुक्त है।

mock43.word: The following sentence has been divided into parts. Eva's business module / allowed people's money / to grow steadily. Select the part with the error, or 'No error'.
mock43.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) No error. The sentence is grammatically sound. The possessive apostrophe (अधिकारवाचक चिह्न) in "people's money" is correctly used.

mock43.word: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. "A large, deep pot used both in the oven and as a serving vessel."
mock43.explanation: A "casserole" is a large, deep pan or bowl used for cooking a variety of dishes in the oven and also for serving them at the table; it can also refer to the category of food cooked in such a vessel. This matches the definition exactly, since it captures both the cooking and serving functions described. The distractors describe other kitchen vessels with different specific uses: a "cauldron" is a large metal pot, typically with a handle, traditionally used for cooking over an open fire; a "skillet" is a metal frying pan with a long handle and no lid, used for cooking on a stovetop; a "sauce-pan" is a small metal pot with a lid and a long handle, typically used for cooking over direct heat — none of which are specifically described as serving vessels used in the oven.

mock43.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the correct order: A. They silently ate and having finished they sat down to sing sentimental songs. B. On the way they bought a large loaf of bread, some cheese and a goatskin full of beer. C. When Little John was halfway through his song, Robin stopped him. D. They travelled for a long time towards Sherwood.
mock43.explanation: D (they travelled) → B (bought food on the way) → A (ate and then sang) → C (Robin stopped Little John's song). Logical sequence: travel → buy food → eat and sing → singing interrupted. Correct order: DBAC

mock43.word: Rearrange the parts of the sentence in correct order: P. some 81 percent of the reefs in the area had become severely bleached Q. warm seawater were particularly brutal in the northern part of R. Australia's famous Great Barrier Reef, where surveys reported that S. from late 2015 to the middle of 2016, the effects of overly
mock43.explanation: S (time frame: late 2015 to 2016) → Q (effects of warm seawater in northern part of) → R (Australia's Great Barrier Reef, where surveys reported) → P (81% reefs severely bleached). Logical flow: time → location → specific place → result. Correct order: SQRP

mock43.word: She sat amid a / rocking chair, listening to / some music and / reminiscing about the past. Identify the segment with the error.
mock43.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) She sat amid a. "Amid" means surrounded by (के बीच). When sitting on a piece of furniture like a chair, the correct preposition is "on" or "in" (e.g., "She sat on a rocking chair").

mock43.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Out of the given options pick the one that gives their correct order. A — On the morning of April 12, Gopal rose at 5.30 am. B — Then he drove to the Joggers Park which was four km away. C — There he met his group of walking enthusiasts. D — He put on his track suit and walking shoes.
mock43.explanation: This paragraph follows a simple chronological morning routine sequence. Step 1 — A must come first: A is the only sentence that introduces the proper noun "Gopal" AND gives the specific time (5:30 am). All other sentences begin with "He" — a pronoun that requires "Gopal" to have been introduced first. Step 2 — D follows A: After waking up, the next logical action is to prepare — "He put on his track suit and walking shoes." One does not drive before getting dressed. Step 3 — B follows D: After dressing, "Then he drove to the Joggers Park which was four km away." Step 4 — C is last: "There he met his group of walking enthusiasts." — "There" refers back to the Joggers Park mentioned in B. Correct order: A → D → B → C = ADBC.

mock43.word: protracted (लंबे समय तक चलने वाले) droughts are putting people at risk of ______.
mock43.explanation: Drought के बाद "famine" (अकाल, भुखमरी) का risk होता है — context में सबसे उपयुक्त।

mock43.word: It ______ with the storming of the Bastille.
mock43.explanation: "Commenced" (आरंभ हुआ) means began, correctly describing the Revolution starting with the storming of the Bastille.

mock43.word: Select the sentence that best expresses the passive form of the sentence given below. I remember my sister taking me to a beautiful monastery in Ladakh.
options: I remember I being taken to a beautiful monastery in Ladakh by my sister.
I remember being taken to a beautiful monastery in Ladakh by my sister.
I remember that I was taken to a beautiful monastery in Ladakh by my sister.
I remember having been taken to a beautiful monastery in Ladakh by my sister.
mock43.explanation: The original sentence contains a simple gerund ("taking"), whose passive equivalent follows the structure "being + past participle" (being taken). The object "me" becomes the subject "I" (implicit subject of "remember"), while "my sister" becomes the agent introduced by "by."

mock43.word: It is hard to conceive of a more ___ route than the one that this determined traveler chose to follow.
mock43.explanation: "Daunting" (भयभीत करने वाला) means intimidating or difficult to deal with in prospect, fitting a difficult route requiring determination. "Provoking" means causing a reaction; "Stimulating" means arousing interest; "Dashing" means attractive and stylish — none fit a challenging route.

mock43.word: Identify the segment containing the error: "Much of the art he / discusses is funny, playing / and mischievous."
mock43.explanation: "Playing" should be replaced with the adjective "playful" to maintain parallel structure with "funny" and "mischievous."

mock43.word: playing outdoor games with friends, which ______ from being immensely enjoyable, also develops teamwork.
mock43.explanation: "Apart from" idiomatically means "in addition to," fitting the sense that the games are enjoyable and also build other skills.

"Keep" present tense में है जबकि बाकी sentence past tense में है ("unfurled")। तनाव की एकरूपता के लिए "held" (past tense of hold) होना चाहिए। Correct sentence: "Then I unfurled the Indian tri-colour and held it aloft at the roof of the world."

mock43.word: Select the most appropriate synonym. ERODE
mock43.explanation: "Erode" means to gradually wear away. "Disintegrate" (टुकड़े-टुकड़े होना) means to break up into small parts — both share the meaning of gradual destruction.

mock43.word: Almost ______ economic agency today has a definition of rural India.
mock43.explanation: "Almost every" (लगभग हर) is the correct collocation. It means nearly all — used before a singular noun like "agency." "Almost all" would require a plural noun.

mock43.word: Select the wrongly spelt word.
mock43.explanation: The correct spelling is "idiosyncrasy" (विलक्षणता), meaning a person's particular way of behaving, especially when it is unusual.

mock43.word: She walked among me without greeting me.
mock43.explanation: "Past me" is correct because "Walk past" means to change your position in relation to something by walking — passing it. "Among" implies being in the middle of a group, not a single person.

mock43.word: Select the synonym of the given word. Entice
mock43.explanation: Entice (लुभाना) means to attract or tempt by offering pleasure or advantage. Beguile (आकर्षित करना/बहलाना) means to charm or enchant someone, sometimes in a deceptive way — same meaning. Dissuade means to persuade someone not to take a particular course. Repulse means to drive back by force. Entreat means to ask someone earnestly.

mock43.word: The Young Global Leaders Community is an ______ for a dynamic community...
mock43.explanation: "Accelerator" (त्वरक) — community को drive करने वाला force। "Accelerator for a dynamic community" — यह community के growth को speed करता है। "Impediment" (बाधा — opposite), "Input" (इनपुट), और "Effort" (प्रयास) इस context में गलत हैं।

mock43.word: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase. BE LEFT STRANDED
options: Unable to leave somewhere because of a problem
Having only a single thing to hold on to
To be the last person on whom blame falls
mock43.explanation: "Be left stranded" means to be left in a difficult or helpless situation without assistance or support, often unable to proceed with one's plans or move forward. (Hindi: फँसा हुआ छोड़ दिया जाना / बेसहारा रह जाना) Example: He left me stranded in town with no car and no money for a bus. Word origin: "Stranded" originally described a ship or whale left aground on a strand (beach), unable to move. Figuratively, it means being stuck with no way forward or out.

mock43.word: Different foods in ______ food group have different nutrients.
mock43.explanation: "Each food group" (प्रत्येक food group) — individual groups को refer करने के लिए "each" सही है।

mock43.word: There is no point in allowing the ______ to grow and then take hasty actions to set things right.
mock43.explanation: "Discovered" (खोजा गया) means found unexpectedly, fitting the context of correcting an error as soon as it is found. "Proposed" means suggested for consideration; "Invented" means newly created; "Managed" relates to running or coping with something — none fit the act of noticing an error.

mock44.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences, four of which are jumbled. Pick the option that gives their connect order. Sentence 1. Bill held open the door and I looked around. P. I prayed to be away from this place. Q. There were a dozen or so beds in small, partitioned cubicles along the walls of a large room. R. The wheezing sound of respirators filled the air. S. Most of the beds were occupied by patients who were wired to all kinds of machinery. Sentence 6. Instead, I was wheeled over to a bed with a number printed behind it on the wall – No. 10
mock44.explanation: Q logically follows Sentence 1, describing the scene that immediately follows the opening of the door. S follows by describing the beds being occupied by wired patients, R continues by mentioning the respirators (the "machinery" from S), and P concludes the description with the narrator's emotional reaction.

mock44.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Strenuous
mock44.explanation: Strenuous (कठिन/श्रमसाध्य) means requiring great effort. Formidable, meaning inspiring respect through being intense or demanding, is the closest synonym here.

mock44.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice: "I was elated on winning the chess championship."
options: The chess championship had been elated on my winning.
I had been elated on winning the chess championship.
The championship was won elatedly by me.
mock44.explanation: When a "be" verb form acts as the main verb with an adjective complement (elated), the sentence cannot be converted into passive voice.

mock44.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Astute (चतुर, कुशाग्र)
mock44.explanation: "Astute" का अर्थ है sharp judgment रखना। "Shrewd" भी practical matters में clever होना है — दोनों समानार्थी हैं।

mock44.word: The function began with the ______ of the lamp, followed by the distribution of certificates in a very systematic way.
mock44.explanation: "Lighting" (रोशनी करना) refers to the act of using light to begin a ceremony, exactly fitting the traditional Indian custom of lighting a lamp at the start of a function. "Lightening" means making lighter or brighter; "Sparking" means producing flashes; "Glittering" means shining brightly — none fit the action of beginning a ceremony with a lamp.

mock44.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Flabby (ढीला, कमज़ोर)
mock44.explanation: "Flabby" का अर्थ है soft, loose, and fleshy। इसका antonym "Firm" (solid and hard) है।

mock44.word: Select the option that will improve the underlined part. The eighteenth century saw the middle classes becoming most prosperous.
mock44.explanation: "most prosperous" superlative है जो यहाँ गलत है। Indirect comparison के लिए "more" (comparative) होना चाहिए। Correct Sentence: The eighteenth century saw the middle classes become more prosperous.

mock44.word: The author is not well known. Little bit people have heard of him.
mock44.explanation: The error lies in the wrong use of "little" to represent people. "Few" and "a few" are used with countable nouns in the plural form; "people" is countable.

mock44.word: "His PhD degree seems to be (Penelope's web)."
mock44.explanation: The idiom "Penelope's web" comes from Greek mythology — Penelope, wife of Odysseus, wove and unwove a burial shroud each day to delay her suitors, meaning the task could never actually be completed. The idiom therefore refers to a task that is endless and can never be finished. "An endless task" means a task that seems to have no end or conclusion — for example, "Cleaning the beach of all the plastic waste is an endless task." This matches the idiom precisely. The distractors describe different qualities of a task: "an important task" refers to one of significant value (e.g., "Securing funding for the project is an important task"); "a difficult task" refers to one requiring a lot of effort or skill (e.g., "Completing the marathon was a difficult task"); "a secret task" refers to one kept hidden from others (e.g., "The spy was assigned a secret task"). None of these capture the specific "never-ending" sense central to the idiom.

mock44.word: I'm in ______ of my thoughts, actions and emotions.
mock44.explanation: "In charge of" (प्रभारी/जिम्मेदारी में) means being responsible for or in control of something. It is the most natural fixed phrase for being in command of one's own thoughts.

mock44.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Intriguing
mock44.explanation: Intriguing (रोचक/आकर्षक) means arousing curiosity or interest; fascinating. Gripping (रोमांचक) means very exciting and interesting, holding attention completely — same meaning. Investigating means carrying out a formal inquiry. Simultaneous means occurring at the same time. Monotonous means dull and repetitious.

mock44.word: ...water is life, that it is a precious natural resource and needs to be used ______.
mock44.explanation: "Judiciously" (विवेकपूर्ण तरीके से) — wisely and carefully use करना। Water conservation context में यह सबसे appropriate है। "Effectively" (प्रभावी ढंग से), "Hopefully" (आशा के साथ), और "Carelessly" (लापरवाही से — opposite meaning) गलत हैं।

mock44.word: ...battling a major ______ collapsed following a series of sudden explosions.
mock44.explanation: Blaze (आग की लपट) means a large, strong fire, matching the context of explosions and fire in the factory.

mock44.word: Select the option that contains an error: "Deepak is studying B.tech. at IIT Delhi to become the engineer."
mock44.explanation: For a generic profession, the indefinite article "an" should be used instead of "the," giving "to become an engineer."

mock44.word: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order, except the first part. Arrange the parts in the correct order. Such poststructuralist pronouncements... A. not only in the Western academic world B. on the literature of the Third World C. however, are a common feature of the literary theory practiced D. but also in the Third-World countries
mock44.explanation: C introduces the idea that these pronouncements are a common feature of literary theory practiced. A specifies where — not only in the Western academic world. B focuses this on Third World literature. D extends to Third-World countries themselves. Order: C → A → B → D.

mock44.word: The following sentence has been divided into parts. Saleem has a tendency to switch / off his mobile when things / are not favourable for him. Select the part with the error, or 'No error'.
options: Saleem has a tendency to switch
mock44.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) No error. Each segment of the sentence is grammatically and logically correct.

mock44.word: Rearrange the parts of the sentence in correct order. P. despite pandemic-related travel complications, the Q. governments of India and France facilitated interactions R. NPCIL a binding techno-commercial offer on 22 April 2021 S. between EDF and NPCIL which allowed EDF to submit to
mock44.explanation: P introduces the challenge (pandemic complications). Q explains that governments facilitated interactions. S continues — between EDF and NPCIL, allowing EDF to submit. R concludes with the actual submission of the techno-commercial offer. Order: P → Q → S → R.

mock44.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word "Decipher" from the sentence: "Many sculptures of our history are like the unravelled path as we cannot garble and there is a lot of work that is yet to be interpreted or translated."
mock44.explanation: "Decipher" (अर्थ समझना) means to make out the meaning of something obscure. The antonym is "Garble" (उलझाना) — to confuse or mix up something so its original meaning is hidden.

mock44.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A large bundle bound for storage or transport.
mock44.explanation: Bale (गट्ठर) specifically means a large bundle tightly bound for storage or transport — such as a bale of hay or cotton. Bunch means things fastened together loosely. Barrel is a cylindrical container. Bevy means a large group of similar things or people.

mock44.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Rock the boat
mock44.explanation: "Rock the boat" (स्थिर स्थिति को बिगाड़ना) means to disturb a stable situation or to cause trouble where none is wanted.

mock44.word: Because manure is ______ from animals and plants.
mock44.explanation: "Derived" का अर्थ है obtained or received from। Manure animals और plants से derived होती है।

mock44.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Hang on every word
mock44.explanation: "Hang on every word" (बहुत ध्यान से सुनना) means to listen with complete attention and fascination to what someone is saying.

mock44.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Enrage
mock44.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) Infuriate. "Enrage" (क्रोधित करना) means to make someone extremely angry, which is exactly what "Infuriate" means.

mock44.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Implicit (अप्रत्यक्ष)
mock44.explanation: "Implicit" का अर्थ है suggested but not directly communicated। "Unspoken" भी बिना कहे समझी जाने वाली बात है — दोनों समानार्थी हैं।

mock44.word: Maybe today ______ not be so hot.
mock44.explanation: "Would" expresses a hopeful, hypothetical future possibility, fitting the speaker's wish for cooler weather.

mock45.word: Ravina was very sick today, and we will take her to the hospital as she gets worse. Select the correct substitution.
options: we will take her to the hospital now before she gets worse
we can take her to the hospital now before she get worse
we took her to the hospital now before she gets worse
mock45.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) we will take her to the hospital now before she gets worse. This option logically states the preventative action ("before she gets worse") while maintaining correct subject-verb agreement (she gets).

mock45.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Out of the blue
mock45.explanation: "Out of the blue" (अचानक, बिना चेतावनी के) का अर्थ है something that happens unexpectedly without warning। Example: Out of the blue, my old friend called me.

mock45.word: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences. (A) The Booker is awarded on 17 October... (B) 'If the rocks, caves, lakes... would sound like Alan Garner...' (C) This year Alan Garner became the oldest author ever to be shortlisted... (D) Treacle Walker, a dreamlike fable...
mock45.explanation: (C) introduces the achievement → (A) adds the birthday coincidence → (D) describes the book → (B) gives a concluding poetic thought.

mock45.word: Darkness ______ over the forest increasing the danger of wild animals.
mock45.explanation: Descended means to move or fall downwards; to come down upon something. (Hindi: उतरना / नीचे आना) Darkness "descends" over a landscape — this is standard English usage for the coming of night. Example: Night descended over the valley, bringing silence and cold. Other options: Declined = became smaller, fewer, or less (doesn't describe darkness); Collapsed = suddenly fell down structurally (used for buildings, not darkness); Grounded = well balanced and sensible (adjective, wrong part of speech entirely).

mock45.word: Arrange the sentences to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. In arid regions it may form an almost erect shrub. B. The grape is usually a woody vine, climbing by means of tendrils and often reaching a length of 17 metres or more. C. Small greenish flowers, in clusters, precede the fruit, which varies in colour from almost black to green, red and amber. D. The edible leaves are alternate, palmately lobed and always tooth-edged.
mock45.explanation: B comes first: introduces the subject (the grape) in general terms - the vine's structure - the topic sentence. A comes second: 'it' refers to the grape (from B), describing its form in arid regions - a variation of its typical form. D comes third: describes the leaves, moving from overall plant structure to specific parts. C comes last: describes the flowers and fruit, logically following leaves in the description of the plant's parts. Structure of this paragraph: General → Variation → Parts (leaves) → Parts (flowers/fruit) - a common description pattern in science-related passages.

mock45.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: STYMIE
mock45.explanation: "Stymie" (रोकना/बाधा डालना) का अर्थ है to stop someone from accomplishing a goal। इसका synonym है "Hinder" (बाधा डालना) — to restrict someone's capacity for action। "Amaze" (चकित करना), "Scare" (डराना), और "Tame" (पालतू बनाना) असंबंधित हैं।

mock45.word: is also home ______ rare and endangered animals.
mock45.explanation: The fixed phrase is "home to" (का घर). Example: "The forest is home to many rare animals." The preposition "to" always follows "home" in this context.

mock45.word: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the right order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. At length the olive tree's branches broke with the snow's weight, at once despoiling it of its beauty and killing the tree. B. A shower of snow fell upon them and, finding the Olive full of foliage, it settled upon its branches. C. Finding the fig tree without leaves, the snow fell through the branches to the ground and did not injure it at all. D. The olive tree ridiculed the fig tree because, while she was green all year round, the fig tree changed its leaves with the seasons.
mock45.explanation: D introduces the conflict independently (olive ridiculing the fig tree), B follows by showing snow settling on the leafy olive, A explains how the olive's branches broke under the snow's weight, and C concludes by contrasting this with the leafless fig tree, which remained unharmed.

mock45.word: Marine vertebrates are / valuable for / many reasons.
mock45.explanation: वाक्य पूर्णतः सही है। "Marine vertebrates" plural noun है, इसलिए "are" सही है। Subject-verb agreement के अनुसार कोई गलती नहीं।

mock45.word: Choose the word that is opposite in meaning to the given word: ANNEX
mock45.explanation: "Annex" (जोड़ना/संलग्न करना) का अर्थ है to add or attach। इसका antonym है "Subtract" (घटाना) — to take away or detach। "Profuse" (प्रचुर), "Protract" (लम्बा खींचना), और "Propose" (प्रस्ताव करना) असंबंधित हैं।

mock45.word: As order comes out of ______ chaos so does an invention out of some necessity.
mock45.explanation: "Utter chaos" (पूर्ण अव्यवस्था) emphasises complete disorder, paralleling order arising from chaos.

mock45.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the following idiom. To be light years away
options: To be too distant for human beings to reach
An unlikely event that happened suddenly
An extremely long time from now in the past or future
When something seems like it is about to happen in the near future
mock45.explanation: "To be light years away" (बहुत दूर होना) means to refer to something extremely far away or distant, usually beyond reach. For example, "Exploring galaxies beyond our Milky Way is light years away in terms of current technology."

mock45.word: The following sentence has been divided into four segments. Identify the segment that contains an error in the usage of indefinite articles: "We took / an ice / and applied it on / the wound."
mock45.explanation: "Ice" एक uncountable noun है जिसके पहले indefinite article "an" नहीं लगती। सही रूप "some ice" या simply "ice" होगा। Correct sentence: "We took some ice and applied it on the wound."

mock45.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the highlighted word. This is credible information provided by the informer.
mock45.explanation: Credible (विश्वसनीय) means able to be believed; convincing. Dependable (भरोसेमंद) means trustworthy and reliable — same meaning. Detachable means able to be removed. Commendable means deserving praise. Digestible means able to be digested.

mock45.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A person who has excessive admiration of themselves
mock45.explanation: Narcissist (आत्ममुग्ध व्यक्ति) refers to a person who has an excessive interest in or admiration of themselves and their physical appearance — exact match. Egotist is excessively conceited. Egocentric means thinking only of oneself. Fanatic means a person filled with excessive single-minded zeal.

mock45.word: It is not surprising that the surviving Europeans ______ an image to represent death.
mock45.explanation: "Perished" (नष्ट हो जाना/मरना) means to die or come to an end, fitting the context of population dying due to the Black Death pandemic. "Shriveled" means to shrink and wrinkle; "Vanished" means to disappear suddenly; "Moldered" means to decay over time — none specifically describe mass death from a pandemic.

mock45.word: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence. The gardener said to the children, "Please do not pluck any flowers."
options: The gardener requested the children not to pluck any flowers.
The gardener said that the children should not pluck any flowers.
The gardener ordered the children to not pluck any flowers.
The gardener requested to the children please do not pluck any flowers.
mock45.explanation: "Please" indicates a request, so "requested" replaces the reporting verb. "Do not" becomes "not to" in indirect speech. "Ordered" is too harsh for a polite request. Option D incorrectly retains "please" and "to the children."

mock45.word: She would have attended the event if there was ______.
mock45.explanation: The conditional structure requires a phrase indicating a plausible, concrete condition, making "a real possibility" the logical completion.

mock45.word: The plan details the importance of ecosystems for food production, health and other aspects of ______ survival.
mock45.explanation: "Human survival and sustainable development" — ecosystems और human wellbeing का relationship context में सही है।

mock45.word: UNESCO works to / strengthen an intellectual / and moral solidarity / of humankind.
mock45.explanation: "an" की जगह "the" (definite article) होना चाहिए क्योंकि यह एक specific solidarity को refer करता है। Correct: "strengthen the intellectual".

mock45.word: Identify the segment that contains an error. "One of the great rivers / of China, a Hwang Ho, / originates from the / eastern face of Tibet."
mock45.explanation: "Hwang Ho" is a proper noun (name of a river). Proper nouns do not require an indefinite article ("a"). It should just be "Hwang Ho."

mock45.word: Identify the segment containing the grammatical error: "I don't recognize none of / the politicians who have / been invited to be / the speakers today."
mock45.explanation: "None" should be replaced with "any" since the sentence is already negative; double negatives like "don't...none" are grammatically incorrect.

mock45.word: Select the word that is OPPOSITE in meaning to the underlined word. The project was denounced as a scandalous waste of taxpayer's money.
mock45.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) Defended. "Denounced" (निंदा करना) means publicly declared to be wrong or evil. The opposite is to protect or support it, which is "Defended" (बचाव किया).

mock45.word: Which word in the given sentence is the ANTONYM of susceptible? "The president claimed he was immune from prosecution."
mock45.explanation: "Susceptible" (संवेदनशील/प्रभावित होने वाला) का antonym है "Immune" (प्रतिरक्षित) — protected against something। "President," "Prosecution," और "Claimed" — कोई भी susceptible का antonym नहीं है।

mock45.word: A. Symptoms like nervousness, increased heart rate and feeling weak are some of the starting signs of anxiety (चिंता). B. Whereas anxiety attacks, sweating, trembling and having a feeling of impending danger might suggest advanced anxiety levels. C. Most people don't even realise they have anxiety. D. Anxiety is a sad reality of today's time.
mock45.explanation: D — topic introduce। C — non-recognition। A — initial symptoms। B — advanced symptoms।

mock46.word: A. It superseded India's previous educational strategy, which was created in 1986. B. The Indian union cabinet approved the country's national education strategy on 29 July 2020. C. And it provides a framework for education from the primary grades up to higher education. D. This policy significantly improves India's educational system.
mock46.explanation: B — policy approved। A — पिछली policy supersede की। D — improvement। C — framework provide करती है।

mock46.word: ...killing indiscriminately ______ cities and towns devastated in its wake.
mock46.explanation: "Leaving" (पीछे छोड़ना) — plague ने devastated cities और towns पीछे छोड़े। "Settling" (बसाना), "Reckoning" (हिसाब करना), और "Keeping" (रखना) इस context में गलत हैं।

mock46.word: Sentences from an ice-cream advertisement are given in jumbled order. Arrange them correctly. A. That's why Silky is deliciously different and exceptionally versatile, natural, thick and durable ice cream. B. Beautifully firm, nice, silky rather than thin and watery, it stays in shape. C. Silky thick double ice cream has a unique, fresh taste and texture.
mock46.explanation: C introduces the product; B describes its texture and firmness; A concludes by summarizing why the ice cream is uniquely different.

mock46.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word:
mock46.explanation: 'Persuit' is incorrectly spelt. The correct spelling is PURSUIT, meaning an activity that one spends time doing; the action of pursuing someone or something (e.g. I enjoy outdoor pursuits, like hiking and riding). Bleak, Throttle, and Ordinance are all correctly spelt. Spelling Trick for Pursuit: The root is 'pursue' → pursuit. Don't confuse with 'per' (meaning through) - it's PUR-suit not PER-suit.

mock46.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Every word and every line / in the works of John Keats / express his appreciation / of nature's beauty.
options: in the works of John Keats
mock46.explanation: Correct Answer: (C) express his appreciation. When two singular nouns are connected by "and" and preceded by "every" or "each," they take a singular verb. The correct verb should be "expresses" instead of "express."

mock46.word: In 1901, Edward Henry set up fingerprints records for all the criminals a sentence ______ of more than one month's imprisonment.
mock46.explanation: "Completing" fits the standard collocation describing someone undergoing a prison sentence over a period of time, similar to the phrase "serve a sentence." "Passing" and "Finishing" do not fit this fixed collocation as naturally, while the context calls for a word describing duration of imprisonment.

mock46.word: Countries like China and the United States invest ______ in wind, hydro, solar and biofuels.
mock46.explanation: "Heavily" (भारी मात्रा में) indicates a large degree of investment, matching the context of major spending on renewable energy.

mock46.word: In the following question, four words are given. Select the correctly spelt word.
mock46.explanation: सही spelling "Contortion" है — इसका अर्थ है movements of your body into unusual shapes। Example: I had to admire the contortions of the gymnasts.

mock46.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Stability
mock46.explanation: "Stability" (स्थिरता) means the state of being stable. Its antonym is "Volatility" (अस्थिरता) — meaning the tendency to change rapidly and unpredictably. "Ambiguity" (अस्पष्टता) means inexactness.

mock46.word: Ideas that are gathered from such people provide a global ______ for the nation as a whole.
mock46.explanation: "Global perspective" (वैश्विक दृष्टिकोण) — highly educated लोगों के ideas से nation को broader global perspective मिलता है। "Scope" (दायरा), "Outlook" (नज़रिया), और "Vision" (दृष्टि) भी possible हैं पर "perspective" सबसे standard term है।

mock46.word: The ______ needs of the inhabitants seem to be fruit, iced drinks, shiny boots, and lottery tickets.
mock46.explanation: "Principal" का अर्थ है most important; main। यहाँ "principal needs" = मुख्य ज़रूरतें। Correct Sentence: The principal needs of the inhabitants...

mock46.word: The passage consists of six sentences. The first and sixth sentences (S1 and S6) are given. The middle four sentences (P, Q, R, S) are given in jumbled order. Find out the proper order for the four sentences. S1: Most people who have come close to death say they remember nothing. P. It may permanently change a person's perceptions of what is real and important. Q. The near-death experience is among the most powerful experiences that a person can have. R. That 'something' might be a near-death experience. S. However, a third or more may later report that 'something happened.' S6: However, one extraordinary aspect of near-death experience is that a person's culture, religion, race or education does not affect the pattern of the experience.
mock46.explanation: Q logically follows S1 by elaborating on near-death experience as a powerful phenomenon, P follows by describing how "it" (the experience) can change perceptions, S then contrasts this with "however" introducing the idea that some people report something happened, and R concludes by defining that "something" as the near-death experience, leading into S6.

mock46.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given underline word. We sell magazines, groceries and sundry articles.
mock46.explanation: Sundry (विविध/अनेक) means various items not important enough to be mentioned individually; implies variety. Scanty (अपर्याप्त/कम) means insufficient in quantity — opposite of having variety or abundance. Diverse also means variety, not an antonym. Careless means not giving sufficient attention. Profuse means abundantly plentiful.

mock46.word: He also learns that his parents had not ______ in a car accident.
mock46.explanation: "Perished" का अर्थ है died in a sudden or violent manner — यहाँ parents की tragic death का context है।

mock46.word: The incorrectly spelt word in the passage is 'annax'. The correct spelling is:
mock46.explanation: The correct spelling is A-N-N-E-X (not "annax"). Annex means to take control of another country or region by force; also refers to a building added to a larger one. (Hindi: कब्जा करना / अनुलग्नक) Example: The new barrio coincided with the territory annexed to the town in 1614. The error was replacing the correct 'e' with 'a' to make "annax."

mock46.word: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error. The solution was applied so effective that it resolved the issue immediately.
options: They performed the task surprisingly well despite the challenges
She had scarcely begun her speech when the microphone failed
He spoke softly enough to avoid waking the baby
The solution was applied so effective that it resolved the issue immediately
mock46.explanation: "effective" (adjective) की जगह "effectively" (adverb) होना चाहिए क्योंकि यह verb "was applied" को modify करता है। Correct Sentence: The solution was applied so effectively that it resolved the issue immediately.

mock46.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Apprehension
mock46.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) Fear. "Apprehension" (चिंता / डर) is anxiety or fear that something bad will happen. "Fear" is a direct synonym.

mock46.word: A. A big fancy dress ball was arranged to celebrate their homecoming. B. It must have been a terrible shock for a young girl. C. They had been married a month, just home from their honeymoon. D. Just as the guests were starting to arrive, Charnley locked himself into his bedroom and shot himself.
mock46.explanation: B — shocking event का संकेत। C — background। A — celebration arrange। D — tragic event।

mock46.word: Rajasthan is ______ to the Darrah Wildlife Sanctuary.
mock46.explanation: "Home to" (का घर) means a place where something is located, fitting that the sanctuary is situated within Rajasthan.

mock46.word: Around are half-a-dozen Forest Department ______.
mock46.explanation: "Officials" (अधिकारी) refers to people working in an official capacity for a department, fitting the description of Forest Department personnel gathered for the elephant's feeding. "Ministers" refers to government leaders; "Person" and "staff" are too generic or grammatically mismatched (singular vs needed plural reference) compared to "officials."

mock46.word: Thus we (going) back to what might be termed as old values.
mock46.explanation: "going" (present participle) अकेला नहीं आ सकता। यहाँ simple past tense "went" सही है। Correct Sentence: Thus we went back to what might be termed as old values.

mock46.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Abnegation
mock46.explanation: Abnegation (त्याग) means the act of renouncing or rejecting something valued. Renouncement directly mirrors this meaning of giving something up.

mock46.word: Our saints aspired to the ______ of the infinite.
mock46.explanation: "Realisation" (साक्षात्कार) fits the spiritual context of saints striving for enlightenment or understanding of the infinite.

mock46.word: Select the correctly spelt word.
mock46.explanation: Tuition (ट्यूशन/शिक्षण शुल्क) means teaching or instruction, especially of individual pupils or small groups. All other options are incorrect spellings — "tuetion," "tution," and "tuttion" do not exist in standard English.

mock46.word: The suspects had to go through ______ parade so that the real culprit could be caught.
mock46.explanation: "Identification" (पहचान) is a noun meaning the process of identifying someone, correctly filling the object slot in the sentence; "identify" is a verb and cannot be used here.

mock47.word: Select the option with the correct use of future tense: "By next December, I will have been completed six years in this home."
mock47.explanation: The future perfect tense "will have completed" correctly describes an action finished by a specific future point in time.

mock47.word: In her heart, Sheila knew that her paths with Rahul would ______ soon.
mock47.explanation: "Paths cross" is a fixed idiomatic expression meaning to meet someone, fitting the sentence's intended meaning best.

mock47.word: The text (implies) ___ to several themes that need closer examination. Select the synonym of the bracketed word.
mock47.explanation: Implies (संकेत देना) means to suggest something indirectly. Alludes, meaning to indirectly refer to something, matches this meaning closely.

mock47.word: They grow to about two feet in …, and reportedly mate at around 1000 kilometers from the ______.
mock47.explanation: "Anjarle" fits since the passage specifically discusses the festival held at Anjarle beach, and the turtles are described as mating about 1000 kilometres away from this specific named location. "Festival," "sand," and "sea" do not refer to the specific named place required.

mock47.word: ...when subjects made a public ______ to do something.
mock47.explanation: "Commitment" (प्रतिबद्धता) refers to a firm decision or promise, matching Dr Lewin's finding about public declarations.

mock47.word: Fill in the blank with the correct conjunction: 'Though he is old ____ he is strong.'
mock47.explanation: Grammar Rule - Correlative Conjunctions: 'Though...yet' is used to express contrast; when 'though' begins the clause, the main clause is often introduced by 'yet' (e.g. Though she was tired, yet she continued working). 'So' is not paired with 'though.' 'Till' means 'until' - completely wrong conjunction here. 'Although' cannot be used as the second conjunction - it can only begin a clause, not serve as a mid-sentence connector. Important Correlative Conjunction Pairs: Though...yet; Not only...but also; Neither...nor; Either...or; Lest...should; So...that; Whether...or. Correct Sentence: Though he is old yet he is strong.

mock47.word: Choose the word that means the same as the given word. Ecclesial
mock47.explanation: Ecclesial (चर्च-संबंधी) means relating to a church or its functions in a religious or spiritual sense. Churchly is the direct synonym. Profane means disrespectful toward religion — the opposite. Secular means not bound by religious rule. Profuse means exuberantly plentiful.

mock47.word: ...a half-witted bachelor ______ that.
mock47.explanation: "At that" idiomatically completes the sentence, akin to saying "a half-witted bachelor at that" (meaning "in addition" / "ऊपर से").

mock47.word: Municipal corporations have started to take _____ of the problems of waste management.
mock47.explanation: "Cognizance" (संज्ञान) means to formally recognize or acknowledge something. "Take cognizance of" is the correct fixed phrase used for official recognition of an issue.

mock47.word: The more energy we give to a particular thought (1)/, the greater is its power to attract its corresponding circumstance into (2)/ their physical world through the law of attraction. (3)/No error (4)
mock47.explanation: "their" गलत है क्योंकि sentence में singular subject है। "their" की जगह "our" होना चाहिए।

mock47.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the bracketed word to fill in the blank. "My friend is not stubborn with regard to opinions; in fact, she is ___ (intransigent)."
mock47.explanation: "Intransigent" (अड़ियल) means refusing to change one's views. Its antonym is "Subservient" (आज्ञाकारी) — meaning prepared to obey others submissively. The other options mean stubborn.

mock47.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock47.explanation: The correct spelling is "Supersede" (प्रतिस्थापित करना), meaning to take the place of a person or thing previously in authority or use.

mock47.word: Select the option that gives the most appropriate meaning of the following word. Solicit
mock47.explanation: Correct Answer: (C) Request. "Solicit" (याचना करना / माँगना) means to urgently ask for or try to obtain something from someone.

mock47.word: Identify the segment with the grammatical error: "They could be / pass their exams / if they / studied harder."
mock47.explanation: The auxiliary verb "be" is unnecessary here; the correct phrase is simply "They could pass," without the extra "be."

mock47.word: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the parts in the correct order. A. fell before me B. a carcass of an animal C. when suddenly D. my heart skipped a beat
mock47.explanation: D sets the emotional tone (heart skipped a beat). C signals the timing shift (when suddenly). B introduces the visual element (a carcass of an animal). A concludes with the action (fell before me). Order: D → C → B → A.

mock47.word: Select the most appropriate one-word substitution for the given group of words. A deceased holy person's remains or belongings kept for reverence
mock47.explanation: Relic (अवशेष) means an object surviving from an earlier time, especially one of historical or sentimental interest, or the remains of a holy person — exact match. Trophy is a cup awarded as a prize. Memento means an object kept as a reminder of a person or event. Souvenir means a thing kept as a reminder of a place.

mock47.word: Select the wrongly spelt word.
mock47.explanation: The correct spelling is NINETEENTH — not "Ninteenth." Formation: Nine + teen + th = Nineteenth. The 'e' from 'nine' is retained. Ordinal number rule: nine → ninth (drop 'e'), but nineteen → nineteenth (keep the 'e'). Other words are correctly spelt.

mock47.word: Select the most appropriate idiom for the given group of words: 'A time when money might unexpectedly be needed'
mock47.explanation: 'A rainy day' refers to a time in the future when something (especially money) might unexpectedly be needed - the correct answer. 'A raw deal' means being treated unfairly. 'A rash of something' means a large number of unpleasant events of the same type happening within a short period. 'A rap over the knuckles' means a mild reprimand or scolding. Origin: The 'rainy day' idiom draws on the idea of a literal rainy day when you cannot go out or work, hence needing resources saved up - representing financial prudence.

mock47.word: Oh, what a lovely necklace! You need no buy such an expensive gift.
mock47.explanation: "Need no buy" is grammatically incorrect. The sentence expresses that buying was unnecessary (retrospectively). "Needn't have bought" correctly conveys this meaning.

mock47.word: They can be used for ______ purposes ranging from telling people to be cautious
mock47.explanation: "Varied purposes" (विविध उद्देश्यों) — posters अनेक purposes के लिए use होते हैं।

mock47.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech: "Bidding farewell to me, my juniors wished me a bright and successful career ahead."
options: Bidding farewell to me, my juniors inquired,"Wish you a bright and successful career ahead."
Bidding farewell to me, my juniors said to themselves,"Wish you a bright and successful career ahead."
Bidding farewell to me, my juniors said to me,"Wish you a bright and successful career ahead."
Bidding farewell to me, my juniors wish me,"Wish you a bright and successful career ahead."
mock47.explanation: The verb "wished" in indirect speech corresponds to "said to me" in direct speech, correctly placing the spoken words in quotation marks addressed directly to the speaker.

mock47.word: We are inheritors of several grand ______ in the fields of music, fine arts, dance, drama etc.
mock47.explanation: "Treasures" (खजाने) best conveys valuable cultural inheritances in music, art and dance.

mock47.word: One month ago, he had lost his vision in a car crash, his promising career as an F1 racer ended and all his ambitions tied to the sport were ______.
mock47.explanation: "Crushed" (कुचला हुआ / नष्ट हो जाना) conveys that his ambitions were completely shattered by the accident.

mock47.word: If no substitution is required, select 'No improvement.' Knowing the particular things that motivate each person help you add power to their motivation.
mock47.explanation: The subject is the gerund phrase "Knowing the particular things..." which functions as a singular noun. Therefore the singular verb "helps" is required, not "help." Correct sentence: Knowing the particular things that motivate each person helps you add power to their motivation.

mock47.word: they found Shan's body lying on the floor with arms and legs ______.
mock47.explanation: "Outspread" (फैला हुआ) correctly describes limbs stretched out, fitting the description of the body's position.

mock48.word: The given sentence is divided into three segments. I miss you / when you move / to another building. Select the segment with a grammatical error, or 'no error'.
mock48.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) I miss you. Since the action of moving will happen in the future, the action of missing will also take place in the future. The correct tense is "I will miss you."

mock48.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock48.explanation: The correct spelling is "Calamitous" (विनाशकारी), meaning involving calamity; catastrophic or disastrous.

mock48.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom: GET ONE'S DANDER UP
mock48.explanation: "Get one's dander up" (गुस्से में आ जाना) — इसका अर्थ है to become angry, hostile, or irritable। Example: "The boxer finally got his dander up and went after his opponent with a vengeance." "Difficult situation" (मुश्किल स्थिति) और "Glare venomously" (ज़हरीली नज़र से देखना) गलत हैं।

mock48.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. EXALT
mock48.explanation: Exalt (महिमा-मंडित करना) means to praise, glorify, or honor someone. Praise is the direct synonym. Condemn is the antonym. Challenge means to dare someone. Mock means to ridicule.

mock48.word: ______ richly diverse area is also home to rare animals.
mock48.explanation: "This" (यह) is used to refer to a specific singular noun just mentioned (the Valley of Flowers National Park). "These" is plural, "That" is more distant, "Which" is relative.

mock48.word: Simon ___ the contact details of renowned paediatricians in the telephone directory.
mock48.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) looked up. "Looked up" is a phrasal verb meaning to search for information in a reference source like a directory or dictionary.

mock48.word: " — 'Divisive' means:
mock48.explanation: "Divisive" (विभाजनकारी) — causing disagreement or discord, dividing people। इसका denotation है "Alienating" (अलग करना/दूर करना) — causing people to stop supporting or agreeing। "Spurring" (प्रेरित करना), "Dethroning" (सत्ता से हटाना), और "Encouraging" (प्रोत्साहित करना) अलग meanings रखते हैं।

mock48.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Pensive
mock48.explanation: Pensive (विचारमग्न) means engaged in deep or serious thought. Reflective (चिंतनशील) means given to or marked by deep thought — the closest synonym. Tragic means causing great sorrow. Spontaneous means acting without premeditation. Spiteful means showing malice.

mock48.word: The students / and the teachers / worked hardly / to make the event a success.
options: to make the event a success
mock48.explanation: "Hardly" (मुश्किल से) means scarcely/barely — the opposite of intended meaning. The correct adverb is "hard" (कठिन परिश्रम से), which modifies "worked" to mean great effort.

mock48.word: Select the most appropriate synonym. INSTIGATE
mock48.explanation: "Instigate" (उकसाना) means to rouse to strong feeling or action. "Kindle" (जगाना/भड़काना) means to arouse or inspire — making them synonyms.

mock48.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word: NOVEL
mock48.explanation: "Novel" (नया/अभूतपूर्व) का अर्थ है new and original। इसका antonym है "Traditional" (पारंपरिक) — following long-established ways। "Ancient" (प्राचीन) past से जुड़ा है पर exact antonym नहीं। "Customary" (रीति-रिवाजों के अनुसार) और "Formal" (औपचारिक) भी सटीक नहीं हैं।

mock48.word: The owner of the house was ___ at the watchman for letting in a stranger.
mock48.explanation: "Indignant" (क्रोधित) means feeling or showing anger at something perceived as unfair, fitting the owner's reaction to a security lapse. "Indulgent" means lenient; "Indifferent" means unconcerned; "Impatient" means easily irritated by delay — none fit anger at the watchman's mistake.

mock48.word: ...the little dark boy was striding ______ up and down the lawn.
mock48.explanation: "Imperiously" (शासकीय ढंग से) means in a commanding manner, fitting the boy's dominant behaviour.

mock48.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Petite (छोटी कद-काठी वाली)
mock48.explanation: "Petite" का अर्थ है small and attractively dainty build। "Small" भी छोटे आकार को दर्शाता है — दोनों समानार्थी हैं।

mock48.word: During the war, the comedian proved to be a man of music.
mock48.explanation: "Man of straw" (कमज़ोर चरित्र का व्यक्ति) means a man whose character is weak and who lacks definite beliefs — a comedian in wartime would not be reliable.

mock48.word: Anders — a book critic known for the weary, elegant savagery with which he ______ almost everything he reviewed.
mock48.explanation: "Dispatched" (निपटाया) — to deal with or review promptly and efficiently। Anders ने reviews को swiftly और efficiently handle किया। "Transferred" (स्थानांतरित), "Incorporated" (शामिल किया), और "Conditioned" (अनुकूलित किया) — context में गलत हैं।

mock48.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock48.explanation: "Bizzare" is incorrectly spelt; the correct spelling is "Bizarre" (विचित्र), meaning very strange or unusual. "Colleague" means a co-worker, "Conscious" (सजग) means aware, and "Usually" means generally — all correctly spelt.

mock48.word: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence. The lawyer said, "Your case is very complicated."
options: The lawyer said that my case was very complicated.
The lawyer reported that my case is very complicated.
The lawyer said that my case had been very complicated.
The lawyer warned that your case was very complicated.
mock48.explanation: In indirect speech, when the reporting verb is past tense ("said"), the reported clause shifts one tense back: "is" becomes "was." "You" changes to "my" as the listener reports it. Option D changes "said" to "warned," altering the meaning.

mock48.word: Identify the segment which contains the grammatical error. On the way he was bited on his toe by a venomous snake.
mock48.explanation: "Bited" is not a valid English word. The past participle of "bite" is "bitten" — not "bited." Correct sentence: On the way he was bitten on his toe by a venomous snake.

mock48.word: It was getting difficult for him to bear with his partner's behaviour. Select the appropriate substitution, or 'No substitution'.
mock48.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) No substitution. "Bear with" means to tolerate or endure something patiently (धैर्य के साथ बर्दाश्त करना). It is used correctly in this context.

mock48.word: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the parts in the correct order to form a meaningful sentence. P. a man in a maroon colour flannel shirt Q. rounded a corner and walked into the middle of the main street of Yellow Sky R. which had been purchased for purposes of decoration S. and made principally by some Jewish women on the East side of New York
mock48.explanation: "P" introduces the subject (a man in a shirt), "R" follows logically by describing the shirt's purpose, "S" continues the description of who made the shirt, and "Q" concludes by placing the man in the street, giving: "A man in a maroon colour flannel shirt which had been purchased for purposes of decoration and made principally by some Jewish women on the East side of New York rounded a corner and walked into the middle of the main street of Yellow Sky."

mock48.word: The propaganda was made to invade ______ neighbouring countries.
mock48.explanation: Propaganda for invasion typically targets smaller, weaker nations due to easier conquest, making "smaller" the logical choice over largest or strongest.

mock48.word: Select the correct option to substitute the given set of words. A statement open to more than one interpretation
mock48.explanation: Ambiguous (अस्पष्ट/द्विअर्थी) means something that is open to more than one interpretation — exact match. Explicit means stated clearly and in detail. Palpable means able to be touched or felt. Tangible means perceptible by touch; clear and definite.

mock48.word: ______, the arteries have thick and muscular veins.
mock48.explanation: "Therefore" (इसलिए) correctly shows the cause-effect link between high pressure and thick arterial walls.

mock48.word: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In this scene, a close-up shot was taken by the cameraman for display the emotions.
options: in order to display the emotions
mock48.explanation: After "for," a gerund (V+ing) is used to show purpose — but no such option exists here. "In order to + base verb" is a correct structure to express purpose. "For display" is grammatically incorrect. Correct sentence: In this scene, a close-up shot was taken by the cameraman in order to display the emotions.

mock49.word: In the 1870s, William Herschel, a British official in India, began experimenting with fingerprints as a ______.
mock49.explanation: "Hobby" (शौक) fits because the phrase "experimenting with" suggests a more casual, passionate engagement rather than formal "duty" or "job." "Pastime" suggests merely passing time without deeper passion, whereas "hobby" better reflects genuine personal interest in the activity.

mock49.word: A. India has an upbeat vision of a liberal, nonviolent democracy. B. Epics from mythologies like the Mahabharata and Ramayana are likened to classic Greek works. C. Luminaries like Mahatma Gandhi and Rabindranath Tagore generate soft power assets. D. With Ashoka, Buddha, and Gandhi serving as primary leaders, Indians are known for core values.
mock49.explanation: A — vision introduce। C — modern soft power। D — historical figures और values। B — ancient literary heritage।

mock49.word: The same voice that … everything suddenly to life also drew them into a world that ______ into eternity.
mock49.explanation: "Stretched out" (फैला हुआ) conveys extending into a vast or boundless space, matching the idea of reaching into eternity.

mock49.word: We cannot postpone discussion on this issue although it is urgent. Select the part with error.
mock49.explanation: The phrase "although it is urgent" is incorrect. The correct conjunction is "even though" to express a stronger contrast and emphasis.

mock49.word: Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it. If there is no error, choose 'No error.' The skull bones of a woodpecker has a different chemical composition and density.
mock49.explanation: The subject "skull bones" is plural and requires the plural verb "have" not the singular "has." This is a subject-verb agreement error. Correct sentence: The skull bones of a woodpecker have a different chemical composition and density.

mock49.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word: DULL
mock49.explanation: "Dull" (उबाऊ) का अर्थ है boring, lacking excitement। इसका antonym है "Riveting" (रोमांचक/बेहद दिलचस्प) — extremely interesting and exciting। "Charming" (आकर्षक), "Diverting" (मनोरंजक), और "Plodding" (थकाऊ) exact antonym नहीं हैं।

mock49.word: 'But, mum, I'm almost done,' Gabriella ______.
mock49.explanation: "Protested" (विरोध किया/आपत्ति जताई) — Gabriella ने माँ की बात पर objection किया। "Prevented" (रोका), "Prohibited" (प्रतिबंधित किया), और "Prescribed" (निर्धारित किया) — context में गलत हैं।

mock49.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. LIVING IN A FOOL'S PARADISE
options: To live in a world of imagination
To believe wrongly that your situation is good
mock49.explanation: "Living in a fool's paradise" means to believe wrongly and unrealistically that one's situation is good or satisfactory, when in reality it is not. (Hindi: झूठी खुशी में जीना / भ्रम में रहना) Example: She looks much happier but surely she is living in a fool's paradise. Historical note: The phrase first appeared in the 'Paston Letters' (1462) and was later used by Shakespeare in 'Romeo and Juliet' (1592). Common confusion: "world of imagination" is close but incorrect — the key element is the WRONG BELIEF about one's REAL situation, not just general daydreaming.

mock49.word: The blood is pushed out of the heart to arteries ______ high pressure.
mock49.explanation: "Under high pressure" is the correct prepositional phrase describing the pressure condition of blood flow.

mock49.word: retiring from a job means retiring from life unless ______ by some other activity.
mock49.explanation: "Supplemented by some other activity" — retirement को किसी नई activity से पूरक बनाना ज़रूरी है।

mock49.word: As the company's client base grew at an unexpected rate, management was forced to ___ some work until they could hire new staff.
mock49.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) farm out. "Farm out" (बाहर काम सौंपना) means to outsource work or give it to independent contractors, which is necessary when handling unexpected growth.

mock49.word: Until ______ first by the 160 m Star of Nanchang in 2006.
mock49.explanation: "Surpassed" (पीछे छोड़ दिया) correctly indicates that the London Eye was overtaken in height by later structures.

mock49.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. CHASTE
mock49.explanation: Chaste (पवित्र/शुद्ध) means free from any trace of the coarse or indecent; pure and modest. Defiled (अपवित्र/दूषित) means to damage the purity or appearance of something; corrupted — exact opposite. Liberated means free from social conventions. Divine means of or like God. Pure is actually a synonym of chaste, not an antonym.

mock49.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences about gas balloons. Select the correct order. A. And if you want to get them somehow, you have to approach some party organizers. B. Well, let me tell you how you can make your own gas balloons at home. C. As a child, gas balloons always fascinate you. D. However, when you need them, you only find them being sold on the 'unknown' streets.
mock49.explanation: C introduces the fascination with gas balloons; D explains their limited availability; A discusses approaching organizers; B concludes by offering a homemade solution.

mock49.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. One's cup of tea
mock49.explanation: "One's cup of tea" (पसंद की चीज़) का अर्थ है something that one prefers or appreciates। Example: This kind of music is not everybody's cup of tea.

mock49.word: Select the word that best completes the given sentence (collocation). Varun, the chef, _____ up a delicious meal in just 30 minutes.
mock49.explanation: "Whipped up" एक colloquial expression है जिसका अर्थ है to quickly prepare a meal। Example: Varun whipped up a delicious meal in just 30 minutes.

mock49.word: Select the one which is similar in meaning to the given word. BANTER
mock49.explanation: Banter means the playful and friendly exchange of teasing remarks. (Hindi: हँसी-मजाक / नोंकझोंक) The synonym is Fun = enjoyment, amusement, or light-hearted pleasure. Example: I missed my Dad a lot, especially our playful banter. Other options are entirely unrelated: Angry = feeling strong annoyance; Whisper = speak very softly; Strict = demanding rules to be obeyed.

mock49.word: The heavy medications and strong drugs at the sanatorium will surely turn a patient completely lifeless and unresponsive.
mock49.explanation: Cadaverous (मृतक जैसा) describes someone who looks pale, thin, and resembling a corpse — capturing both "lifeless" and "unresponsive" from the original phrase. Tombic relates to a tomb. Zonic pertains to a zone. Wasted means weakened but is too informal and imprecise here.

mock49.word: has sent a strong message to our ______.
mock49.explanation: Border row के context में "adversaries" (प्रतिद्वंद्वी, दुश्मन) सबसे उपयुक्त है।

mock49.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Prim (अत्यंत शालीन, कड़क)
mock49.explanation: "Prim" का अर्थ है stiffly correct और proper। इसका विपरीत "Informal" (अनौपचारिक) है।

mock49.word: Thomas is a man of word who have been paying back the borrowed money in installments.
options: Thomas is a man of word
mock49.explanation: "Who have" is incorrect. "Thomas" is singular, so the relative clause must use "who has been paying back." Singular subjects take singular verbs.

mock49.word: A. Lord Ganesha, the Destroyer of Obstacles (बाधाओं का नाश करने वाले), is worshipped for his wisdom. B. People adore the goddess Lakshmi and Lord Ganesha on Diwali. C. This 'New Year' i.e., Diwali is marked by merchants establishing new account books. D. Goddess Lakshmi is also worshipped on Diwali for wealth and success.
mock49.explanation: B — दोनों की पूजा। A — Ganesha के बारे में। D — Lakshmi के बारे में। C — New Year का significance।

mock49.word: Select the word segment that substitutes the bracketed word segment correctly. I waited with (some exciting) to view my sudden-found sister.
mock49.explanation: After "with," a noun is needed. "Excitement" (उत्साह) is a noun meaning a state of being emotionally aroused. "Exciting" is a verb/adjective and cannot follow "some" as a standalone noun here. Correct sentence: I waited with some excitement to view my sudden-found sister.

mock49.word: A. A common sight now is antiquated cars alongside new vehicles in Yangon. B. A newly-built highway connects Mandalay to Yangon. C. Similarly, glass-fronted buildings stand next to old colonial structures. D. Yangon is fairly typical of a developing city in Asia.
mock49.explanation: B (introduces Yangon) → D (describes its nature as developing) → A (example: old and new vehicles) → C (similarly, buildings). Logical flow with "Similarly" linking A and C.

mock49.word: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error. I remember a friend to me buying several pairs of shoes at a sale.
mock49.explanation: The correct phrase should be "a friend of mine" — using the possessive pronoun "mine" instead of "me," and the preposition "of" instead of "to."

mock50.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock50.explanation: The correct spelling is "Unforeseen" (अप्रत्याशित), meaning not predicted or expected.
