mock58.explanation: "Condone" का अर्थ है to accept or regard something wrong as acceptable। Example: One cannot condone such behavior.

mock58.word: ...this colossal monument has welcomed nearly 16 crore ______.
mock58.explanation: "Visitors" (पर्यटक/दर्शक) — tourists या sightseers को describe करने का सबसे सटीक शब्द है। "Guests" (मेहमान) — social occasions के लिए। "Invitees" (आमंत्रित लोग) — invited events के लिए। "Callers" (कॉल करने वाले) — short visit या phone call के लिए।

mock58.word: ...supermarkets are now beginning to take ______ responsibility...
mock58.explanation: "Some" (कुछ) — a minimum amount of responsibility। Supermarkets ने कुछ हद तक ज़िम्मेदारी लेनी शुरू की है — "some" यहाँ सटीक है। "Every" (हर), "Any" (कोई भी), और "Little" (थोड़ा) इस context में कम appropriate हैं।

mock58.word: Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error. The teacher asked him not to keep disturbing the class, but it had no affect.
mock58.explanation: "affect" (verb) गलत है। यहाँ noun (संज्ञा) "effect" (परिणाम) होना चाहिए। Correct Sentence: ...but it had no effect.

mock58.word: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error. If there is no error, select 'No error.' We talked about writing a will, but it / was one of those thing you never / get around to doing.
options: was one of those thing you never
We talked about writing a will, but it
mock58.explanation: After "one of those," the noun must always be plural. "Thing" must be "things." This is a fixed grammatical rule — "one of those + plural noun." Correct sentence: ...it was one of those things you never get around to doing.

mock58.word: (Mona Lisa passage) The Mona Lisa's fame is the result of many chance circumstances combined with the painting's _________ appeal.
mock58.explanation: Inherent (अंतर्निहित) means something that is a permanent, essential, or characteristic attribute — the painting's appeal comes from within its own nature.

mock58.word: Select the correct active form of the given sentence. The meal will have been made ready by the cook by the time you reach the guest house.
options: The cook will have made the meal ready by the time you reach the guest house.
The cook have made the meal ready by the time you reach the guest house.
The cook would have made the meal ready by the time you reach the guest house.
The cook will make the meal ready by the time you reach the guest house.
mock58.explanation: Passive sentence is in Future Perfect Tense ("will have been made ready"). Active: Subject ("the cook") + Future Perfect ("will have made") + Object.

mock58.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom: HIGH AND DRY
mock58.explanation: "High and dry" (असहाय छोड़ा गया) — इसका अर्थ है Abandoned — left in a difficult situation without any assistance। Example: "After the company went bankrupt, many employees were left high and dry." "Occasionally" (कभी-कभी), "Perplexed" (भ्रमित), और "Failed" (असफल) गलत हैं।

mock58.word: "The business operates ___ much the same way as the other businesses did."
mock58.explanation: "Much" is the appropriate adverb here, used to modify the phrase "the same way" to indicate the degree of similarity.

mock58.word: After the concert was over, / they go to a restaurant, / had dinner together / and talked until midnight.
mock58.explanation: "They go" is incorrect. The sentence is in past tense, so it should be "they went." All other verbs (had, talked) are correctly in past tense.

mock59.word: Select the option that expresses the following sentence in positive degree of comparison. She is liked by everybody as she is so good.
options: She is liked by everybody for being good.
Everybody like her as she is good.
She is the most liked person because she is good.
She is so good that everybody likes her.
mock59.explanation: The positive degree describes the basic form of an adjective without making a comparison. Here, "good" remains in its positive degree, and option D correctly uses "so good" to emphasize her quality without any comparative or superlative structure.

mock59.word: In the sentence identify the segment which contains the grammatical error: "To whom did you give / the packet?"
mock59.explanation: The sentence is correct; "whom" is properly used as the object of the preposition "to," and the structure follows standard question formation.

mock59.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: SLENDER
mock59.explanation: "Slender" (पतला/दुबला) का अर्थ है thin in an attractive way। इसका synonym है "Frail" (कमज़ोर) — weak or not healthy। "Energetic" (ऊर्जावान), "Defame" (बदनाम करना), और "Lively" (जीवंत) भिन्न अर्थ रखते हैं।

mock59.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. A bird's eye view
options: A clear impression of what is happening
To move into sight from a distance
mock59.explanation: "A bird's eye view" (व्यापक दृष्टिकोण) means a broad, comprehensive perspective of a situation. "A clear impression of what is happening" matches this.

mock59.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Grin from ear to ear
options: To smile a lot because of happiness
mock59.explanation: "Grin from ear to ear" means to look extremely happy — to smile very broadly. Example: "We've had a fantastic response!" he said, grinning from ear to ear. Related idiom: "Grin and bear it" means to accept something bad without complaining.

mock59.word: I cannot allow such a letter to be sent ___ my signature.
mock59.explanation: "Under one's signature" (हस्ताक्षर के अधीन) is a fixed phrase meaning attested or confirmed by one's signature, matching the sentence's meaning that the writer doesn't want a letter sent with their confirmation.

mock59.word: Select the sentence that has a grammatical error. (
options: It is said that Joseph was not ready to go to war. (
Karl Marx was a renowned social scientist. (
Indian force are known for their loyalty and integrity. (
It is impossible to wake Christine up in the morning. A) Option A B) Option B C) Option C D) Option D
mock59.explanation: "Indian force" singular है लेकिन verb "are" plural के लिए है। "Indian force" की जगह "Indian forces" होना चाहिए। Correct Sentence: Indian forces are known for their loyalty and integrity.

mock59.word: The goal of personal development is to learn and apply that (1)/which enable us to attain emotional well-being, understanding, and (2)/effectiveness, and to share this knowledge with others. (3)/No error (4)
mock59.explanation: "which enable" गलत है। "which" के बाद singular verb "enables" आना चाहिए। Correct Sentence: ...which enables us to attain emotional well-being...

mock59.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word:
mock59.explanation: 'Simultaneoues' is incorrectly spelt. The correct spelling is SIMULTANEOUS, meaning occurring, operating, or done at the same time. Simulation, Stylistics, and Synthetic are all correctly spelt. Spelling Tip: Simultaneous = simul + taneous (not 'teoues' or 'taneous'). Mnemonic: Simul-TANEOUS - think of 'simultaneous transmission.'

mock59.word: Readers have been ______ conditioned that they feel embarrassed...
mock59.explanation: "So" (इतना) — यहाँ "very" के समान emphasis देता है। "So conditioned" — बहुत अधिक conditioned होना। "Deeply" (गहराई से), "Rightly" (सही तरह से), और "What is" — context में उपयुक्त नहीं हैं।

mock59.word: driven by the increasing air pollution and ______ impact on health.
mock59.explanation: "Their" (उनका) is the correct possessive pronoun referring to families. "There" is a place adverb, "they're" means "they are" — neither fits here.

mock59.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: DISSIPATE
mock59.explanation: Dissipate means to cause something to spread out and disappear, or to use up resources wastefully (e.g. He dissipated his fortune on gambling). Spend refers to using up or paying out money, time, or resources - both share the meaning of using up or consuming resources, especially in the sense of wasting energy, money, or time. Glorious, Malicious, and Haste are all unrelated in meaning. Tip: Dissipate often appears with abstract nouns (dissipate energy, dissipate anger, dissipate wealth); its synonym in the 'waste/use up' sense is spend or squander.

mock59.word: The laboratory material is to ______ in ourselves.
mock59.explanation: The correct grammatical structure here is a Passive Infinitive: "to be found." The full construction is "The laboratory material is to be found in ourselves." Rule: Passive Infinitive = to be + past participle (V3). "Found" is the past participle (V3) of the verb "find." This means: the material of psychological study can be discovered and located within our own personal experience and introspection. Other options fail grammatically: "finding" is a gerund/present participle (not a passive infinitive); "finds" is 3rd person singular active present tense; "find" is the base form (active, not passive).

mock59.word: Select the one which best expresses the meaning of the given word. Behoove (कर्तव्य होना)
mock59.explanation: "Behoove" का अर्थ है it is a duty or responsibility for someone to do something। Example: It behooves the House to assure itself.

mock59.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Tidy (साफ-सुथरा)
mock59.explanation: "Tidy" का अर्थ है neat and well-organized। "Orderly" भी neat और arranged होने को दर्शाता है — दोनों समानार्थी हैं।

mock59.word: Caught in the fast current, ______ they struggled to stay afloat.
mock59.explanation: "Stayed back" (वहीं रुक गए) is a fixed phrasal verb meaning to remain behind, fitting the context of the youngsters choosing to remain at the spot rather than leave when officials turned others away. "Away," "ahead," and "aside" do not form this standard phrasal verb with "stay."

mock59.word: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence. I said to him,"Please stay here tonight."
options: I requested him to please stay there tonight.
I asked him if he would stay there that night.
I asked him to please stay here that night.
I requested him to stay there that night.
mock59.explanation: In indirect speech, "please" is omitted when converting a polite request. "Said to" changes to "requested," "here" changes to "there," and "tonight" changes to "that night."

mock59.word: Courts help in the resolution of ______ issues.
mock59.explanation: Contentious means causing, involving, or likely to cause disagreement and argument. (Hindi: विवादास्पद / बहसतलब) Courts exist specifically to resolve matters that are disputed and argued — therefore "contentious issues" fits perfectly. Example: The director had a contentious relationship with the eccentric actor. Trap words that sound similar: Conscientious (कर्तव्यनिष्ठ) = putting a lot of effort into one's work; hardworking — not related to disputes; Presumptuous (धृष्ट) = failing to observe the limits of what is appropriate; over-bold; Contemptuous (तिरस्कारपूर्ण) = expressing a lack of respect or strong disapproval.

mock59.word: She swallowed ______ her breakfast in a hurry.
mock59.explanation: "Swallow down" (जल्दी से खाना/पीना) — to eat or drink something quickly। "Swallowed down her breakfast" — जल्दबाजी में नाश्ता निगल गई। "Up," "Into," और "Over" इस phrasal verb में नहीं आते।

mock59.word: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence: 'She said, "If only I could relive my past!"'
options: She said that she could relive her past.
She wished that if she could relive her past.
She wished that she could relive her past.
She wished if she could relive my past.
mock59.explanation: "If only" wish को indicate करता है। Direct → Indirect: (1) "said" → "wished"। (2) "If only" हटाओ, "that" लगाओ। (3) "I" → "she," "my" → "her"। (4) "could" past form रहेगा। Correct: "She wished that she could relive her past."

mock59.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Contentment
mock59.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) Sadness. "Contentment" (संतोष) means a state of happiness and satisfaction. Among the options, "Sadness" (दुख) is its closest opposite.

mock59.word: one can be a hero for one day if only one cares ______.
mock59.explanation: "Cares enough" (पर्याप्त परवाह) — hero बनने के लिए sufficient care का होना ज़रूरी है।

mock59.word: including ______ weather patterns and rising sea levels.
mock59.explanation: "Erratic" (अनियमित) means irregular, fitting unpredictable weather patterns caused by climate change.

mock59.word: The abstract noun form of 'act' is 'action.' Similarly, the abstract noun form of 'wide' will be:
mock59.explanation: "Width" (चौड़ाई) is the correct abstract noun derived from the adjective "wide," representing the measure or extent of something from side to side.

mock59.word: The inter-city ______ of parcels by road.
mock59.explanation: "Movement of parcels" — noun form "movement" यहाँ सही है।

mock60.word: He insisted ______ seeing the documents.
mock60.explanation: "Insisted on" is the correct phrasal combination meaning to demand something firmly.

mock60.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined segment. "My father always tells amusing and true stories from his childhood at bedtime."
mock60.explanation: "Anecdotes" are short, amusing or interesting stories about real incidents or people — this precisely matches "amusing and true stories" in the original sentence. The corrected sentence reads: "My father always tells amusing and true anecdotes from his childhood at bedtime." The distractors all refer to different literary forms: "sonnets" are a fixed form of poetry (14 lines, specific rhyme scheme), entirely unrelated to storytelling content here; "fables" are fictional stories, often involving animals, that teach a moral lesson — but the sentence specifies the stories are "true," ruling this out; "allegories" are narratives in which characters and events symbolically represent a deeper meaning, which is also not indicated in the sentence.

mock60.word: Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it. If there is no error, choose 'No error.' People that are lonely have higher levels of the hormone cortisol.
mock60.explanation: When referring to people, "who" must be used instead of "that." "That" is used for things, not people. Correct sentence: People who are lonely have higher levels of the hormone cortisol.

mock60.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Forthright (सीधा)
mock60.explanation: ऊपर Q.60 में explain किया जा चुका है।

"Come along the way" गलत है। सही idiomatic expression है "come a long way" जिसका अर्थ है to have made significant progress। Correct sentence: "Sherpas have prospered and come a long way from being just porters to becoming mountaineers."

mock60.word: Identify the segment which contains the grammatical error: 'If the report did not have the information needed, it could not be used by them.'
mock60.explanation: The error lies in the use of the conjunction 'If' at the beginning of the sentence. Grammar Rule: 'If' introduces a conditional (hypothetical) clause, but the relationship here is not conditional, it is causal - the report couldn't be used because it didn't have the information. The correct conjunction is 'Since,' used as a subordinating conjunction to introduce a reason or cause. 'If' → hypothetical/conditional; 'Since' → because / given that (introduces reason). Correct Sentence: Since the report did not have the information needed, it could not be used by them.

mock60.word: Given below are four sentences. Pick the option that gives the correct order. A. The invention of the airplane was no exception. B. However, the idea of flying an aircraft was quite repulsive to some people. C. Many great inventions are initially greeted with ridicule and disbelief. D. Most people who heard about the first powered flight on December 17, 1903 were excited and impressed.
mock60.explanation: C introduces the general idea. A gives the example (airplane). D describes initial excitement. B provides the contrast (some found it repulsive). Correct sequence: C → A → D → B.

"Beaming from ear to ear" (खुशी से मुस्कुराना) — having a broad, enthusiastic smile। "Smiling broadly" इसका perfect substitution है। "Criticising bitterly" (कड़ी आलोचना), "Working" (काम करना), और "Thinking deeply" (गहरा सोचना) गलत हैं।
"Dream of" के बाद gerund आता है। "To raise" (infinitive) गलत है। Preposition "of" के बाद "raising" (gerund) होना चाहिए। Correct sentence: "I wouldn't dream of raising the price."

mock60.word: Identify the segment containing the grammatical error: "We noticed that most of the shoppers / in the mall looking for / packets of ready to cook food."
options: packets of ready to cook food
mock60.explanation: The clause needs a verb for its subject "shoppers"; the correct phrase should be "in the mall were looking for."

mock60.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the capitalised word: Affluent people tend to look upon illiteracy with comfortable DETACHMENT.
mock60.explanation: Detachment (तटस्थता/अनासक्ति) means the fact or feeling of not being personally involved in something. Subjectivity (व्यक्तिपरकता) means the quality of being based on personal feelings — opposite of detachment. Justice means fair treatment. Humour refers to funny qualities. Neutrality is similar to detachment, not opposite.

mock60.word: Choose the word that is opposite in meaning to the given word. Recondite
mock60.explanation: Recondite (गूढ़/दुर्बोध) means difficult to understand; obscure and known only to a few people. Clear (स्पष्ट) means easy to perceive, understand, or interpret — exact opposite. Abstruse means also difficult to comprehend — similar to recondite, not opposite. Phlegm refers to thick mucus. Abstract means theoretical rather than physical.

mock60.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech. He said to his friend, "Let us leave for the trek tomorrow."
options: He suggested his friend that let us leave for the trek tomorrow.
He told his friend they should leave for the trek the next day.
He suggested his friend that they would leave for the trek tomorrow.
He suggested to his friend that they should leave for the trek the next day.
mock60.explanation: "Let us" indicates a suggestion, converting to "suggested that they should." "Tomorrow" changes to "the next day" in indirect speech, and "suggested to his friend" is the correct structure.

mock60.word: Select the most appropriate one-word substitution for the given group of words. Dependent on chance or uncertainty
mock60.explanation: Precarious (अनिश्चित/असुरक्षित) means not securely held; dangerously likely to collapse; uncertain; insecure — exact match. Paradox means a statement leading to a self-contradictory conclusion. Presume means to suppose without proof. Presumptuous means failing to observe appropriate limits.

mock60.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Kilter
mock60.explanation: "Kilter" means a proper or usual state or condition — when something is functioning or arranged correctly, it is said to be "in kilter." For example, "After the power outage, everything in the office was out of kilter for a few hours." "Order" refers to a state in which everything is arranged properly or functions correctly, making it the closest synonym. The other options are all near-synonyms of disorder rather than order: "disrepair" means the state of needing repair, not being in good condition; "anarchy" means a state of disorder due to the absence of authority or law; "shambles" means a state of total disorder or chaos. Note how all three distractors are essentially synonyms of each other (disorder), which is a common trap — always anchor your choice to the precise meaning of the headword, not to a vague sense of "related to organisation."

mock60.word: the nation's resolve to defend its sovereignty ______ firm.
mock60.explanation: "Resolve stands firm" (संकल्प दृढ़ है) — simple present tense के साथ "stands" सही है।

mock60.word: A new ______ was organized by Chhattisgarh Tourism Board in the form of National Tribal Dance Festival.
mock60.explanation: "Initiative" (पहल) best describes an organised activity launched by an authority to achieve a cultural goal.

mock60.word: Select the passive form of the given sentence. People claim to have seen the wild and mad elephant in several cities.
options: The wild and mad elephant has been claimed to be seen by the people in several cities.
The wild and mad elephant was claimed to be seen by people in several cities.
The wild and mad elephant is claimed to be seen in several cities.
The wild and mad elephant is claimed to have been seen in several cities.
mock60.explanation: The active sentence is in the present tense with the structure "Subject + claim + to have seen + Object." Converting to passive requires the form "Object + is + claimed + to have been seen + (Subject)," matching option D's correct tense and structure.

mock60.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock60.explanation: The correct spelling is definitely (निश्चित रूप से), meaning without doubt. "Definately" is a very common misspelling. Existence, Mischievous, and Violence are all correctly spelled.

mock60.word: They are made to believe, by ______ publicity, that the new products will enhance their happiness.
mock60.explanation: Ubiquitous (सर्वव्यापी) means seeming to be everywhere; publicity for gadgets is described as appearing everywhere.

mock60.word: Select the correct indirect form: 'The Prime Minister has said, "The government will extend help to the unorganised sector."'
options: The Prime Minister said that the government has extended help to the unorganised sector.
The Prime Minister said that the government would extend help to the unorganised sector.
The Prime Minister has said that the government will extend help to the unorganised sector.
The Prime Minister has said that the government extended help to the unorganised sector.
mock60.explanation: Reporting verb "has said" Present Perfect में है, इसलिए reported speech की tense change नहीं होगी। "Will" remain करेगा। Correct: "The Prime Minister has said that the government will extend help to the unorganised sector."

mock60.word: (Knowledge landscape passage) The need for a skilled workforce will _________ increasingly in greater demand.
mock60.explanation: The passage talks about a future prospect. The structure "will be" (simple future tense) fits as it indicates something very likely to happen in the future.

mock60.word: Select the antonym of the given word. DENSE
mock60.explanation: "Dense" means having the constituent parts crowded closely together. Its true antonym is "sparse," meaning thinly dispersed or scattered. The remaining three options — "opaque" (not able to be seen through), "condensed" (made denser or more concise), and "thick" (densely filled or covered) — are all synonyms or closely related concepts of "dense," not its opposite. This question highlights a common trap in antonym questions: examiners often surround the correct antonym with multiple synonyms of the original word to test whether the candidate truly understands the headword's meaning rather than simply pattern-matching related vocabulary.

mock60.word: Out of the given four alternatives, select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the Idiom/Phrase. Cast someone adrift
options: To leave someone without any support or purpose
To blame someone for one's mistakes
mock60.explanation: "Cast someone adrift" (किसी को सहारे के बिना छोड़ देना) means to abandon or isolate someone, leaving them without support, resources, or a clear sense of direction — from a nautical term.

mock60.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Cumbersome
mock60.explanation: Cumbersome (भारी-भरकम) means difficult to handle or manage. Clumsy (अनाड़ी/बोझिल) means difficult to handle or use; unwieldy — same meaning. Fatigue means extreme tiredness. Easy means presenting few difficulties. Light refers to the natural agent that stimulates sight.

mock61.word: I love the dogs /; but I don't like / the dog my friend Tinzie / owns since it bites people. Identify the segment with the error.
mock61.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) I love the dogs. The speaker is expressing a general affection for dogs. Plural nouns in a general sense do not take the definite article "the." It should simply be "I love dogs."

mock61.word: In the first two months after the monument's inauguration in 2018, the world's ______ statue witnessed...
mock61.explanation: "Tallest" — superlative degree, जो दुनिया की सभी मूर्तियों में सबसे ऊँची होने को indicate करता है। "Tall" (positive degree) और "Taller" (comparative degree) superlative नहीं हैं। "Most tallest" — grammatically incorrect है क्योंकि यह redundantly superlative repeat करता है।

mock61.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Overture
mock61.explanation: Overture (प्रस्ताव/प्रारंभिक संकेत) means an initiative toward agreement or action; a proposal. Preamble (प्रस्तावना) means an introductory statement — same meaning. Slander means a false and defamatory oral statement. Conclusion means the last part. Plunk means to pluck or hit to produce a sound.

mock61.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Method to one's madness
options: Seeming disorderly yet having a structure
mock61.explanation: This idiom means having good reasons for one's actions even though they may seem foolish or strange at first glance.

mock61.word: Select the correct passive form of the given sentence: 'Are you sure these documents belong together?'
options: Is it made sure by you that these documents belong together?
Are you being sure these documents belong together?
Are you sure these documents belong together?
Is it confirmed that these documents belong together?
mock61.explanation: This tests knowledge of stative verbs, which describe a state rather than an action and cannot be converted into passive voice because passive voice requires an action being done to an object. Common stative verbs include: agree, hate, love, belong, feel, prefer, want, wish, hear, look, weigh, know, believe, seem, appear. Here, 'belong' is a stative verb, so the sentence cannot be transformed into passive voice and remains unchanged. Rule: Sentences with stative verbs as the main verb cannot be converted to passive voice; sentences with intransitive verbs (no object) also cannot be passivised.

mock61.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the correct order: A. It was a wonderful, almost awful sight — in place of the hayfield was a loch with waves on it. B. At seven the next morning, I awoke and rushed to the window. C. As I went to sleep I reflected that the rain must be filling the dam. D. That afternoon rain fell in torrents and continued all evening and night.
mock61.explanation: D (rain started) → C (reflection before sleep) → B (woke next morning) → A (saw the flooded hayfield). Chronological order: afternoon rain → going to sleep → waking up → discovering aftermath. Correct order: DCBA

mock61.word: The driver was taken to the nearest dispensary by the beggars. (Change to Active Voice)
options: The beggars take the driver to the nearest dispensary
The beggars took the driver to dispensary
The beggars took the driver to the nearest dispensary
To the nearest dispensary, the beggars took the driver
mock61.explanation: Passive Voice को Active Voice में बदलते समय object subject बन जाता है और subject object। "was taken" → "took" हो जाता है। Correct Sentence: The beggars took the driver to the nearest dispensary.

mock61.word: Identify the segment with the grammatical error: "Though she was able to / finish the work on time, / she couldn't do that / out in fear."
mock61.explanation: The correct phrase is "out of fear" (डर के कारण), not "out in fear," to mean acting due to fear.

mock61.word: Select the option that expresses the opposite meaning of the underlined word. In front of the big screen, this behaviour is generally considered taboo (वर्जित).
mock61.explanation: "Taboo" का अर्थ है socially prohibited। इसका antonym "Acceptable" (permissible — अनुमत) है।

mock61.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Good grief
options: An expression of surprise or frustration
mock61.explanation: "Good grief" (अरे बाप रे!) is an idiom expressing surprise, dismay, annoyance, or exasperation. It is used in response to unexpected or upsetting events. The term is somewhat ironic since the situations it describes are rarely "good."

mock61.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Fastidious
mock61.explanation: Correct Answer: (C) Careful. "Fastidious" (तुनकमिज़ाज / बारीकियों पर ध्यान देने वाला) describes someone very attentive to detail. "Careful" is the closest synonym.

mock61.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. I was playing / snooker / when dad / had arrived.
mock61.explanation: The error is "had arrived" (Past Perfect Tense) — it should be "arrived" (Simple Past Tense). Rule: When one past action INTERRUPTS another past action that was already in progress, we use: Past Continuous (for the action in progress) + Simple Past (for the interrupting action). Structure: Subject + was/were + V-ing + when + Subject + V2 (Simple Past). "I was playing" = Past Continuous ✔ (action in progress). "when dad arrived" = Simple Past ✔ (the interrupting action). Past Perfect (had arrived) would only be used if dad's arrival was completed BEFORE another specific past event — which is not the case here. Correct sentence: "I was playing snooker when dad arrived."

mock61.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. "She looked tired and sallow after the long day she'd had."
mock61.explanation: "Sallow" (पीला / निस्तेज) means having a yellow or pale complexion, indicating illness. The antonym is "Flushed" (गुलाबी) — indicating good health or excitement. "Livid" (नीला / क्रोधित) means very angry or dark bluish.

mock61.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Maverick
mock61.explanation: A "maverick" is an unconventional or independent-minded person, someone who refuses to follow the conventional or established rules of a group. For example, "He was considered a maverick in the corporate world due to his unconventional business strategies." "Bohemian" describes a person who has informal and unconventional social habits, often associated with artists or writers — for example, "She led a bohemian lifestyle, traveling around Europe with nothing but her art supplies." This is the closest match. Note that the remaining three options — "conformist" (a person who conforms to accepted behaviour), "orthodox" (adhering to traditional beliefs), and "follower" (one who follows another's opinions or teachings) — are all near-opposites of "maverick," since they describe conventional, rule-following behaviour rather than independence.

mock61.word: Select the most appropriate synonym. ADJUSTMENT
mock61.explanation: "Adjustment" (समायोजन) means adapting to a new situation. "Balancing" means making equal — it is the closest synonym among the options.

mock61.word: Choose the word that means the same as the given word. Opaque
mock61.explanation: Opaque (अपारदर्शी/अस्पष्ट) in its metaphorical sense means hard to understand or not clear. Arcane (रहस्यमय/गूढ़) means understood by few; mysterious or secret — matching the metaphorical meaning. Translucent allows partial light. Clear is the opposite.

mock61.word: Select the most appropriate synonym for the given word: Grim
mock61.explanation: Grim means very serious or forbidding in appearance, causing feelings of gloom or despair. Gloomy means dark or depressing/distressing - the correct synonym. Cheerful is the exact antonym of grim. Bluff means an attempt to deceive someone - unrelated. Peevish means easily irritated - a different meaning. Synonyms for Grim: gloomy, dismal, bleak, stern, forbidding, dreary, somber.

mock61.word: A. Mendel's simple 'Laws of heredity' came to be widely known. B. With Charles Darwin's popularisation of the concept of evolution, man became interested. C. Starting with the work on heredity of Gregor Mendel in the 1860s. D. This interest was centred on man's phylogenetic (उद्विकास संबंधी) origins.
mock61.explanation: B — Darwin का evolution concept। D — phylogenetic origins में interest। C — Mendel's work से shift। A — Mendel's laws का प्रचार।

mock61.word: Select the option that will improve the underlined part: "Harish took a broom and started to dust in floor."
mock61.explanation: The preposition "in" should be replaced with the article "the," and "dust" (present tense verb) fits the sentence rather than "dusting."

mock61.word: If no substitution is required, select 'No substitution.' With magnifying glass, we can observe how minute insects like ants behave.
mock61.explanation: The original sentence is missing the indefinite article "a" before "magnifying glass," since the specific identity of the magnifying glass is not established. Adding "a" before the consonant-starting word "magnifying" makes the sentence grammatically correct.

mock61.word: In the wake of the recent cross-border tensions, forces have been ______ at strategic locations for immediate action, if required.
mock61.explanation: "Deploy" means to move troops or equipment into position for military action — for example, "They plan to deploy more American soldiers over the next six months." This is the only word that fits the military/strategic context of the sentence. "Deported" means to expel a foreigner from a country, usually due to illegal status or a crime. "Departed" means left, especially to start a journey. "Deposited" means to put or set something down in a specific place. None of these alternatives carry the military positioning sense required here.

mock61.word: Given below are four sentences. Pick the option that gives the correct order. A. She was a busy person and had no time to visit her mother. B. It was Mothers' Day. C. She thought her mother would be pleased to receive the flowers. D. A woman stopped at a flower shop to order some flowers to be sent to her mother.
mock61.explanation: B sets the context — Mother's Day. D establishes the main action — woman orders flowers. A provides reason — she was too busy to visit. C concludes with her motivation — flowers would please the mother. Correct sequence: B → D → A → C.

mock61.word: Shakuntala Devi was born on 4th November 1929. In her childhood she _____ to school.
options: had never been (कभी नहीं गई थीं)
mock61.explanation: Past perfect tense (had never been) — यह एक action को दूसरे past action से पहले दर्शाता है।

mock61.word: It is true that the more you study, the more you ______ your horizon...
mock61.explanation: "Expand your horizon" (अपना दायरा बढ़ाना) — studying more से mental limits बढ़ते हैं। "Broaden" भी correct है पर complete passage में "expand" का उपयोग किया गया है। "Narrow" और "Limit" opposite meaning रखते हैं।

mock61.word: Choose the word which best expresses the OPPOSITE meaning of the word. GRAVE
mock61.explanation: Grave (adjective) means urgent and very bad; serious. (Hindi: गंभीर / भयावह) Example: It was the gravest political crisis of his career. The antonym is Inconsequential = lacking worth or importance; trivial; not significant. (Hindi: महत्त्वहीन / तुच्छ) Important trap: Solemn is actually a SYNONYM of grave (dignified and sombre), NOT an antonym. Senile = mentally or physically infirm with old age — unrelated. Noble = impressive in appearance — also not an antonym.

mock62.word: Select the option that expresses the opposite meaning. Almost immediately, they heard the high shrill (तीखी, कर्कश) piercing cry of tires on the side road.
mock62.explanation: "Screech" एक sharp, high-pitched sound है जो tires की आवाज़ को perfectly describe करता है।

mock62.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. OPAQUE
mock62.explanation: Opaque means not allowing light to pass through; difficult to see through or understand. "Pellucid" (पारदर्शी) means transparent and easily understood, the exact opposite. Paragon means a model of excellence; Glorify means to praise; Dark describes absence of light but does not capture transparency, which is what makes "opaque" meaningful.

mock62.word: English is more ______ than any single Indian language!
mock62.explanation: The blank requires a verb meaning 'to spread through and be present in every part of.' Pervade means to spread through and be perceived in every part of - correct: English has pervaded the entire world. Persuade means to convince someone - doesn't fit since you persuade a person, not the entire world. Invade means to enter forcefully as an enemy - too aggressive for this context. Saturate means to soak thoroughly - can be used metaphorically, but pervade is more precise and natural here. Vocabulary Tip: Pervade, permeate, and penetrate are related words meaning 'to spread through' - learn them as a cluster.

mock62.word: Necessity is an ______ force that compels a man to do things.
mock62.explanation: "Irresistible" (अनूठा / रोके न जाने वाला) describes a force impossible to resist, fitting necessity's compelling nature.

mock62.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word: TAWDRY
mock62.explanation: "Tawdry" (भड़कीला लेकिन घटिया) का अर्थ है showy but cheap and of poor quality। इसका antonym है "Elegant" (सुरुचिपूर्ण) — pleasingly graceful and stylish। "Gaudy" (भड़कीला), "Inferior" (घटिया), और "Showy" (दिखावटी) असंबंधित या similar हैं।

mock62.word: ...there was much about this young man and what happened to him that was unusual, ______ even he never knew the whole of it.
mock62.explanation: "Although" (यद्यपि) — surprise या contrast introduce करता है। "Although even he never knew the whole" — despite the unusual events, he didn't know everything। "Even though" similar है लेकिन "although" more literary है। "In spite" और "Despite" के बाद noun phrase आती है, not clause।

mock62.word: Brick … tempted him with … of water and he loved the palm pith that was used as ______ for baking bricks.
mock62.explanation: "Fuel" fits naturally since the palm pith is described as being burned during the process of baking bricks, requiring material that produces heat. "Fodder" refers to animal feed; "Logs" refers to pieces of wood; "Load" refers to a quantity carried — none specifically fit material burned for heat production.

mock62.word: A small organized dissenting group within a larger one.
mock62.explanation: Faction (गुट/दल) means a small organized dissenting group within a larger one, especially in politics — exact match. Band means a small group of musicians. Set means a group of things that belong together. Cluster means a group of similar things positioned closely together.

mock62.word: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech. I said that when it stopped drizzling we would have to start digging again.
options: I said, "When it stopped to drizzle we must start digging again."
I said, "When it stops drizzling we must start digging again."
I said, "When drizzle will stop then we will start digging again."
I said, "When it stops to drizzle we will start digging again."
mock62.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) I said, "When it stops drizzling we must start digging again." Converting back to direct speech shifts the past tense "stopped" back to the present tense "stops."

mock62.word: Select the one which best expresses the meaning of the given word. GRUDGING
mock62.explanation: Grudging means reluctant; unwilling; disinclined; begrudging. (Hindi: अनिच्छुक / ईर्ष्यालु) The closest synonym is Envious = jealous; feeling resentful longing aroused by someone else's possessions. Example: She won the grudging respect of her boss. Example: I'm very envious of your new coat — it's beautiful. Other options: Favouring = supporting; Generous = kind and giving (ANTONYM); Contented = satisfied.

mock62.word: Mr. Arvind was accused of beating up before the student in the class.
options: accused of beating up of the
accused of beating up in the
mock62.explanation: The original sentence is missing the indefinite article "a" before "student," which is needed since "student" is a singular countable noun being introduced for the first time. The corrected phrase makes clear that Arvind was accused of beating up a student in front of the class.

mock62.word: Carnatic music is usually performed by a small troupe of actors, consisting of a principal performer, a melodic accompaniment, and a rhythm accompaniment.
mock62.explanation: "troupe" (मंडली) को "ensemble of musicians" से replace करना सबसे उचित है क्योंकि Carnatic music में instruments और vocals दोनों होते हैं।

mock62.word: Identify the most appropriate synonym of the following word: IMPLICATE
mock62.explanation: "Implicate" (फँसाना) का अर्थ है to show someone to be involved in a crime। इसका synonym है "Accuse" (आरोप लगाना) — to charge someone with wrongdoing। "Complain" (शिकायत करना), "Interrogate" (पूछताछ करना), और "Confuse" (भ्रमित करना) असंबंधित हैं।

mock62.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word. The poet managed to paint a vivid (सजीव, स्पष्ट) picture of the landscape by the river through words.
mock62.explanation: "Vivid" का अर्थ है powerful mental images उत्पन्न करना। "Evocative" का अर्थ है strong memories या feelings जगाना — दोनों समानार्थी हैं।

mock62.word: Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error: "There is a spectrum / of seas around that / land which attracts / tourists of all aging."
mock62.explanation: "Aging" is incorrect here; the correct phrase should be "tourists of all ages," referring to people of all age groups.

mock62.word: Select the one which is opposite in meaning to the given word. FORGO
mock62.explanation: Forgo means to sacrifice; to give up; to let go of something willingly. (Hindi: त्यागना / छोड़ देना) Synonyms of Forgo: Renounce, Surrender, Relinquish. The antonym is Conquer = to overcome and take control; to keep, retain, and dominate. (Hindi: जीतना / विजय पाना) Example: She conquered her fear of heights. Other options: Pass, Forfeit (similar in meaning to forgo — not antonym), Neglect (unrelated).

mock62.word: Choose the word that means the same as the given word: CAGINESS
mock62.explanation: "Caginess" (सतर्कता/छुपाने की प्रवृत्ति) का अर्थ है being cautious, wary, or secretive। इसका synonym है "Guilefulness" (चालाकी) — the quality of being deceitful or crafty। "Desiccate" (सुखाना), "Dopiness" (सुस्ती), और "Ingenuous" (भोला) भिन्न अर्थ रखते हैं।

mock62.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Inquisitive
mock62.explanation: "Inquisitive" (जिज्ञासु) means having a strong desire to learn or know things. The antonym is "Uninterested" (उदासीन) — having no interest. "Indolent" (आलसी) means lazy.

mock62.word: It is said that civilized man has ______ about twenty wild animals.
mock62.explanation: "Tamed" (पालतू बनाया) — to bring wild animals under control। "Civilized man has tamed twenty wild animals" — domestication। "Stunned" (चौंका दिया), "Amazed" (हैरान किया), और "Staggered" (डगमगाया) इस context में गलत हैं।

mock62.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Chew the scenery
mock62.explanation: "Chew the scenery" (अति नाटकीय व्यवहार करना) का अर्थ है to act in an exaggerated manner। Example: Robbins really gets to chew the scenery in the second half.

mock62.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word. She wore a whimsical dress adorned with colourful patterns.
mock62.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) Weird. "Whimsical" (विचित्र / मनमौजी) means playfully unusual or quaint. "Weird" serves as the best synonym among the choices.

mock62.word: Any resemblance of a character in this book to a living person is _____ incidental.
mock62.explanation: "Purely incidental" (केवल संयोग से) is a standard fixed expression used in books/films to indicate that any resemblance is completely unintentional.

mock62.word: Identify the part that contains a grammatical error. If there is no error, select 'No error.' Ashwatthama, Drona's / only son, has had developed / a fondness for lavish living.
mock62.explanation: "Has had developed" incorrectly combines present perfect and past perfect tenses. Either "has developed" or "had developed" should be used — not both together. Correct sentence: Ashwatthama, Drona's only son, has/had developed a fondness for lavish living.

mock62.word: In the sentence identify the segment which contains the grammatical error. Cyclone Idai is regarded as one of the worst tropical cyclone on record to affect Africa and the Southern Hemisphere as a whole.
options: one of the worst tropical cyclone
Africa and the Southern Hemisphere as a whole
mock62.explanation: The phrase "one of" is always followed by a plural noun, even though the verb agrees with "one" in the singular. The corrected phrase is "one of the worst tropical cyclones," not "cyclone."

mock62.word: Select the most appropriate idiomatic expression. (regarding "wasting time arguing just to oppose what is confirmed")
options: The devil is in the details
mock62.explanation: Correct Answer: (C) Play devil's advocate. To "play devil's advocate" (विरोधी पक्ष रखना) means to express a contentious opinion in order to provoke debate or test the strength of the opposing arguments.

mock63.word: Teenagers have the ______ to learn new skills, make lifelong friends...
mock63.explanation: Opportunity (अवसर) means a favourable circumstance to do something, matching the context of teenagers being able to learn and grow.

mock63.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute: 'A person who criticises traditional beliefs and customs'
mock63.explanation: Iconoclast means a person who attacks or criticises cherished beliefs, customs, or institutions - the correct answer. Heretic means a person holding controversial religious opinions that contradict accepted doctrines - more specific to religion. Absconder means a person who has escaped from custody - unrelated. Apostle means someone sent on a mission or an enthusiastic supporter of a cause - unrelated. One-Word List (SSC Favourites): Iconoclast = attacks traditions; Heretic = defies religious doctrine; Iconophile = lover of images/art; Bibliophile = lover of books.

mock63.word: Select the correct active form: "Were you summoned for interrogation by the police?"
options: Has the police summoned you for interrogation?
Did the police summon you for interrogation?
Does the police summon you for interrogation?
Will the police summon you for interrogation?
mock63.explanation: Since the passive sentence is in simple past tense, the active form uses "Did + subject + V1," giving "Did the police summon you for interrogation?"

mock63.word: A ______ souvenir will be released on the occasion of the World Meet.
mock63.explanation: "Voluminous" (विशाल/बड़ा) — having great volume or bulk। Context में World Meet जैसे बड़े occasion के लिए large souvenir release होगा। "Generous" (उदार), "Bountiful" (प्रचुर), और "Small" (छोटा) इस context में गलत हैं।

mock63.word: I had a stroke at an early age, but what I thought was a great _____ at that time changed my life for the better.
mock63.explanation: "Injustice" (अन्याय) means an unjust act. The sentence implies the writer felt what happened was wrong, but it changed his life for the better. Only "injustice" fits this meaning.

mock63.word: Select the word which is opposite in meaning to the word in capitals. FUSION
mock63.explanation: Fusion means the joining or combining of two or more things into one. "Separation" is the exact opposite process, describing the splitting apart of things. Combination is a synonym of fusion; Disintegration (विघटन) means falling to pieces gradually, which is different from a deliberate separation; Distribution means sharing out.

mock63.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Demure (संकोची, शर्मीली)
mock63.explanation: "Demure" का अर्थ है reserved, modest, and shy। इसका antonym "Strong" (शक्तिशाली, निडर) है।

mock63.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. I ate / the egg / yesterday for / my dinner.
mock63.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) the egg. "The egg" should be replaced with "an egg." The word "egg" starts with a vowel sound (स्वर ध्वनि), so it requires the indefinite article "an." "The" is used for a specific item, whereas here a general egg is being referred to.

mock63.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Curiosity
mock63.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) Indifference. "Curiosity" (जिज्ञासा) is a strong desire to know something. The opposite is a lack of interest, which is "Indifference" (उदासीनता).

mock63.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: RESPLENDENT
mock63.explanation: "Resplendent" (भव्य/चमकदार) का अर्थ है attractive and impressive through rich colours। इसका synonym है "Magnificent" (शानदार) — extremely beautiful and elaborate। "Astonished" (चकित), "Enthusiastic" (उत्साही), और "Exuberant" (जोशीला) भिन्न अर्थ रखते हैं।

mock63.word: Change the following active sentence to passive voice: 'Time and experiences have taught us valuable lessons.'
options: Us have been taught valuable lessons by time and experiences.
The valuable lessons have taught us time and experiences.
Valuable lessons have been taught to us by time and experiences.
We have been taught valuable lessons by time and experiences.
mock63.explanation: This sentence is in the Present Perfect tense (Active Voice). In the passive transformation, the object 'us' becomes the subject and changes to 'we' (subject pronoun); 'have taught' becomes 'have been taught'; the original subject moves to the end with 'by.' Option A is wrong because 'us' cannot be a subject (it's an object pronoun). Option B misinterprets the verb. Option C changes 'us' to 'to us' - not the most natural passive construction. Passive Voice Formula (Present Perfect): Object + has/have + been + Past Participle + by + Subject. Correct Passive: We have been taught valuable lessons by time and experiences.

mock63.word: ...who played his part ______ one
mock63.explanation: "In" (में) indicates inclusion or participation within limits; the sentence discusses Ratan Tata's involvement in the freedom struggle.

mock63.word: Directions: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them contains an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. At the last meeting / which took place in November, / half the members submitted / their resignations on protest.
mock63.explanation: The preposition "on" is incorrect here; the correct preposition is "in," giving the fixed phrase "in protest," meaning as an act of objection. The corrected sentence reads "...their resignations in protest."

mock63.word: The boys were tired, but not too tired to examine the little hut ______.
mock63.explanation: "Thoroughly" का अर्थ है in a careful and complete way। Context में hut की पूरी जांच की बात है। Correct Sentence: ...to examine the little hut thoroughly.

mock63.word: Choose the word that is opposite in meaning to the given word: RESTRICTED
mock63.explanation: "Restricted" (सीमित) का अर्थ है limited or confined। इसका antonym है "Copious" (प्रचुर/भरपूर) — abundant, plentiful। "Circumscribed" (परिसीमित), "Capped" (अधिकतम सीमित), और "Finite" (सीमित) similar meaning रखते हैं।

options: had somewhat of a result on
mock63.explanation: "Fell flat" (असफल होना) means failed to generate the desired effect, matching "had no effect on."

mock63.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Repercussion (प्रतिक्रिया, परिणाम)
mock63.explanation: "Repercussion" का अर्थ है unintended consequence। "Consequence" भी किसी action के result को दर्शाता है — दोनों समानार्थी हैं।

mock63.word: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase. DRIVE SOMEONE UP THE WALL
options: To overcome an obstacle by going over it
To close someone's path of escape
Make someone very irritated or angry
mock63.explanation: "Drive someone up the wall" is an informal idiom meaning to irritate, infuriate, or frustrate someone to an extreme degree. (Hindi: किसी को बहुत परेशान करना / चिड़चिड़ाहट पैदा करना) Example: His constant humming drives me up the wall. The imagery is of someone being so frustrated they could metaphorically "crawl up a wall." It has nothing to do with physical obstacles, helping someone, or blocking escape — those options are literal, incorrect interpretations.

mock63.word: recommending motivational ______ they might undertake that are ______ to Larry's situation and the program's ______ about his attitudes and behaviours.
mock63.explanation: "Interventions" (हस्तक्षेप/उपाय) — motivational steps। "Targeted" (लक्षित) — Larry की specific situation के लिए। "Inferences" (निष्कर्ष) — program के attitudes और behaviors के बारे में conclusions। तीनों मिलकर एक coherent educational context बनाते हैं।

mock63.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Wanton
mock63.explanation: "Wanton" (निर्दयी / अश्लील) means deliberate and unprovoked; often refers to excessive cruelty or indecency. "Obscene" (अश्लील) means offensive by accepted standards of morality — closely synonymous here. "Abrogation" (उन्मूलन) means repeal.

mock63.word: Fashion is not something that exists in dresses only.
mock63.explanation: The original sentence is grammatically correct. "That exists in" correctly uses a relative pronoun "that" with the singular verb "exists" to modify "something." No change is needed.

mock63.word: Our forefathers loved trees and forests so much that they _________ deified and worshipped them as abodes of Vrikshadevata and Vanadevata.
mock63.explanation: Even (यहाँ तक कि) is used to emphasize something surprising — it emphasizes the surprising fact that people actually deified trees.

mock63.word: Select the most appropriate homophone to fill in the blank: "The shareholders are planning to ___ control of the company from the current directors."
mock63.explanation: "Wrest" (छीन लेना) means to forcibly take control away from someone, fitting the business context of seizing power.

mock63.word: This statue of Lord Buddha is carved ______ jade.
mock63.explanation: "Carved out of" indicates the material from which something is made, here referring to jade as the source material.

mock63.word: There was utter ___ after the explosion.
mock63.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) mayhem. "Mayhem" (तबाही / अव्यवस्था) refers to violent or extreme disorder and chaos, fitting the aftermath of an explosion perfectly.

mock64.word: A song or music in praise of God
mock64.explanation: "Hymn" (भजन) is a religious song or poem of praise to God. "Elegy" is a poem lamenting the dead. "Epic" is a long narrative poem. "Ode" is a lyric poem.

mock64.word: A set of rooms especially in a hotel for the use of one person or his family
mock64.explanation: Suite (होटल का सुइट) means a set of rooms, especially in a hotel. Example: the honeymoon/penthouse suite.

mock64.word: ...in response to ______ consumer backlash against the huge amounts of plastic waste...
mock64.explanation: "Growing" (बढ़ती हुई) — consumer backlash बढ़ रहा है। "Believing" (विश्वास करना), "Enlarging" (बड़ा करना), और "Developing" (विकसित करना) इस context में गलत हैं।

mock64.word: ...ideas to help in the community and societal ______.
mock64.explanation: "Societal development" (सामाजिक विकास) — education society के development में contribute करती है। "Disruption" (व्यवधान), "Downfall" (पतन), और "Decline" (गिरावट) — सभी negative meaning रखते हैं।

mock64.word: When she sought refuge from a bad dream, nothing could ______ her back to sleep better than his calm breathing.
mock64.explanation: "Beside" (के पास/बगल में) means next to, fitting the context that Meggie was sitting near Mo to hear his calm breathing while falling asleep. "Around," "behind," and "under" do not logically describe a position from which one could closely hear someone's breathing.

mock64.word: Bottled water demonstrates how much humans ______ nature...
mock64.explanation: Commodify (वस्तु बनाना) means to turn into a sellable product, matching the theme of nature being commercialised through bottled water.

mock64.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Infinite
mock64.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) Evanescent. "Infinite" (अनंत) means limitless or endless. "Evanescent" (अल्पकालिक), meaning quickly fading or temporary, serves as its opposite here.

mock64.word: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Reluctant (अनिच्छुक)
mock64.explanation: "Reluctant" का अर्थ है unwilling and hesitant। इसका antonym "Enthusiastic" (eager and excited — उत्साहित) है।

mock64.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word: VAIN
mock64.explanation: "Vain" (घमंडी) का अर्थ है having excessive pride in one's appearance or achievements। इसका antonym है "Modest" (विनम्र) — having a limited, not overly high opinion of oneself। "Loyal" (वफादार), "Unfair" (अनुचित), और "Energetic" (ऊर्जावान) असंबंधित हैं।

mock64.word: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A short section (as of a book) that leads to or explains the main part.
mock64.explanation: Foreword (भूमिका/प्रस्तावना) is a short introductory section of a book that leads to or explains the main content. Conclusion is the end. Postscript is a remark added after the letter's signature. Paraphrase means rewording something for clarity.

mock64.word: Select the most appropriate idiom for the underlined segment in the following sentence. Everything in the mansion was in great confusion after the robbery.
mock64.explanation: "Pell-mell" (अव्यवस्थित ढंग से) means in a confused, rushed, or disorderly manner, perfectly matching a state of disarray after a robbery. "Pick holes" means to criticize; "Turn over a new leaf" means to start fresh; "Damp squib" means something less impressive than expected — none describe disorder.

mock64.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Vice
mock64.explanation: Vice (दुराचार) means criminal activities or immoral behavior. Immorality (अनैतिकता) means behavior that is morally wrong — same meaning. Wrong means not correct. Looseness means not firmly attached. Villainy means wicked or criminal behavior.

mock64.word: Mahesh's attitude annoyed me and I blew into.
options: annoyed me and I blew down of
annoyed me and I blew up
mock64.explanation: "Blew up" (भड़क उठना) is the idiomatic phrasal verb meaning to react with sudden anger or frustration, fitting the context of a strong emotional reaction. "Blew into" is not a standard idiomatic expression and fails to convey the intensity required.

mock64.word: In the sentence identify the segment which contains the grammatical error. "The Doon Valley with all its lights look beautiful at night from the top of the mountain."
mock64.explanation: This tests subject-verb agreement. The grammatical subject of the sentence is "The Doon Valley" (singular), not "lights." The phrase "with all its lights" is a prepositional phrase modifying the subject — it does not change the subject's number. Since the subject is singular, the verb must also be singular: "looks beautiful," not "look beautiful." The corrected sentence reads: "The Doon Valley with all its lights looks beautiful at night from the top of the mountain." A useful rule of thumb: when a prepositional phrase comes between the subject and the verb, always check agreement against the head noun of the subject, not against any noun inside the intervening phrase.

mock64.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. FURTIVE
mock64.explanation: Furtive (गुप्त/चोरी-छिपे) means attempting to avoid notice, typically due to guilt. Secretive matches this meaning of concealing intentions.

mock64.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom in the given sentence. The people were scared because the burglar was at large.
mock64.explanation: "At large" (अभी तक पकड़ा नहीं गया) means, especially of a criminal, still at liberty or not yet captured, which fits the people's fear of an uncaught burglar.

options: else but your pride which make
else but your pride which makes
mock64.explanation: "Nothing else but" is the correct idiomatic phrase, and since "pride" is singular, the verb "makes" should be used.

mock64.word: The roads at 15000 feet are not easily navigable and the air is __________ and freezing.
mock64.explanation: Intensified means made stronger in force or degree; it does not collocate naturally with 'air'. Elevated means situated at a higher position - one might say 'elevated ground' but it does not describe the quality of air. Exalted means raised to a higher rank or moral position; not used to describe air scientifically. Rarefied means air that is at lower pressure than usual - thin, with reduced oxygen - the scientifically correct and contextually perfect word for air at high altitude. Grammar Rule: When a blank requires a specific, technical, or domain-specific word, eliminate options that are too vague, general, or used in different contexts.

mock64.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word: 'The cynical investors believed in every word the analysts said.' (Duplicate of Question 19 in the source)
mock64.explanation: This question is a duplicate of Question 19 in the source material. See Question 19 for the full explanation: Cynical means distrustful of human sincerity; its antonym is credulous, meaning easily fooled/too ready to believe.

mock64.word: After ______ visit, the secret shoppers prepare a report...
mock64.explanation: "Their" (उनका) is the correct possessive pronoun referring back to the plural "secret shoppers," showing ownership of the visit.

mock64.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence. We were very much enthusiastic about our tour to Agra.
mock64.explanation: Enthusiastic (उत्साही) means showing eager interest. "Casual" (उदासीन) means done without much concern or seriousness, the clear opposite. Obsessed means preoccupied; Anxious (चिंतित) means worried; Inclined means favourably disposed — none oppose enthusiasm directly.

mock64.word: Fill in the blank: 'The old woman walked carefully with her heavy load ____ she should fall.'
mock64.explanation: The sentence expresses cautious action to prevent something from happening - this purpose of prevention is expressed by 'lest.' Grammar Rule: 'Lest...should' is a correlative conjunction used to express preventing something undesirable from happening ('lest she should fall' = so that she wouldn't fall / for fear that she might fall). 'Although' introduces contrast - doesn't fit. 'But' introduces contradiction - doesn't fit. 'So that' also introduces purpose, but is used for positive purposes, while 'lest' is used for negative prevention. Correlative Conjunctions with 'lest': lest...should - always paired together. Correct Sentence: The old woman walked carefully with her heavy load lest she should fall.

mock64.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock64.explanation: "Blizard" गलत spelling है। Correct spelling है "Blizzard" (भीषण बर्फीला तूफान) — double 'z' होता है। "Boisterous" (शोरगुल वाला), "Blonde" (सुनहरे बालों वाला), और "Brusque" (रुखा) correctly spelt हैं।

mock64.word: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment: "How did you come about his purse?"
mock64.explanation: "Come by" (प्राप्त करना) means to acquire something, which fits asking how someone obtained the purse, rather than "come about."

mock64.word: Five years later, I was eating this same dish at the same restaurant.
mock64.explanation: "Five years later" भविष्य के एक specific point को indicate करता है। Present continuous "am eating" उस future moment की action describe करता है। Correct Sentence: Five years later, I am eating this same dish at the same restaurant.

mock65.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock65.explanation: The correct spelling is Descendant (वंशज), meaning a person descended from an ancestor. "Desedent" is completely misspelled. Deceased means dead. Decimate means to destroy a large part of something.

mock65.word: Regardless ______ of what may or may not be true of my scholastic rigour, I suspect the school-stress dream is quite common.
mock65.explanation: "Remote" (दूरस्थ, सीमित) means slight or distant, fitting the context of someone having only limited or indirect knowledge of the narrator's academic career. "Distant" is similar but less precise here; "Bleak" means lacking hope, which doesn't fit; "Instigate" means to provoke, which is unrelated to "knowledge."

mock65.word: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the right order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. The first time that I was seated behind a curtain in a dining car, I felt as if the curtain had been dropped on my selfhood... B. One Sunday, we went to church in Simsbury, and we were the only Negroes there... C. Just before going to college I went to Simsbury, Connecticut, and worked for a whole summer on a tobacco farm... D. After that summer in Connecticut, it was a bitter feeling going back to segregation...
mock65.explanation: C introduces the summer in Connecticut — independent opening. B describes what happened on Sundays during that summer. D talks about returning to segregation after the summer. A concludes with the emotional impact of the segregation (dining car curtain experience). Correct sequence: C → B → D → A.

mock65.word: ...ranging ______ the hills of Pavagadh, and extending into the city of Champaner.
mock65.explanation: "From" (से) indicates distance from a reference point, correctly describing the bastions ranging from the hills.

mock65.word: Scholars point out the one child ______ in China in the late 1970s...
mock65.explanation: "Statute" (क़ानून/विधान) — China का one-child policy एक official law था। "Standard" (मानक), "Average" (औसत), और "Ordeal" (कठिन परीक्षा) — कोई भी official policy को describe नहीं करता।

mock65.word: parts of the Amazon rainforest that ______ converted to agricultural land.
mock65.explanation: Passive voice में past tense के लिए "were converted" (बदले गए) सही है।

mock65.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error: "It is difficult to live in / the societal expectations / when one has / fewer resources."
options: it is difficult to live in
mock65.explanation: "Live in" गलत है। सही phrase "live up to" है जिसका अर्थ है meeting expectations or standards। Correct sentence: "It is difficult to live up to the societal expectations when one has fewer resources."

mock65.word: A very steep or overhanging rock face or cliff on a mountain
mock65.explanation: "Precipice" का अर्थ है a very steep side of a high mountain or cliff। Example: On the latter side there was a great precipice.

mock65.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Languishing
mock65.explanation: "Languishing" (कमजोर पड़ना) means to become weak or feeble. Its antonym is "Flourishing" (फलता-फूलता) — meaning growing or developing in a healthy way. "Diminutive" (लघु) means extremely small.

mock65.word: In the sentence identify the segment which contains the grammatical error. She got two quick promotions in order that she has good communication skills.
options: in order that she has good communication skills
mock65.explanation: "In order that" is used to express purpose, but here the sentence intends to give a reason, so "because" should replace it: "She got two quick promotions because she has good communication skills."

mock65.word: The number of ______ to landline phones had gone up from 9.8 million in 1995 to 21.7 million in 2019.
mock65.explanation: Landline phones के "subscribers" (ग्राहक) की संख्या बढ़ी। यह context में सही है।

mock65.word: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. Beyond or above the range of normal or physical human experience
mock65.explanation: Transcendent means surpassing the ordinary; exceptional; existing above and beyond the limits of normal or physical human experience. (Hindi: अलौकिक / सामान्य से परे) Example: Her transcendent beauty left everyone speechless. Root: Latin "transcendere" = to climb beyond or surpass. Other options: Mundane (सांसारिक) = dull, everyday, monotonous — literally the OPPOSITE meaning; Ribald (अश्लील) = referring to sexual matters in an amusingly rude or irreverent way; Coarse (असभ्य/मोटा) = rough or rude in manner or texture.

mock65.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Virtue
mock65.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) Vice. "Virtue" (सद्गुण) means behavior showing high moral standards. Its direct antonym is "Vice" (दुर्गुण), representing immoral behavior.

mock65.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Terse
mock65.explanation: Correct Answer: (A) Polite. "Terse" (रूखा / संक्षिप्त) means sparing in the use of words, often appearing abrupt or rude. The opposite is "Polite" (विनम्र).

mock65.word: In many places, restoring mangrove forests can be an extreme effective strategy.
mock65.explanation: "extreme" (adjective) की जगह "extremely" (adverb) होना चाहिए क्योंकि यह "effective" (adjective) को modify करता है। Correct Sentence: ...can be an extremely effective strategy.

mock65.word: People did not seem to ______ or cough...
mock65.explanation: "Fidget" (बेचैनी से हिलना-डुलना) — nervousness या impatience के कारण छोटी-छोटी हरकतें करना। Reading session में लोग fidget या cough नहीं कर रहे थे। "Sleep" (सोना), "Sing" (गाना), और "Distract" (ध्यान भटकाना) context में गलत हैं।

mock65.word: Select the wrongly spelt word.
mock65.explanation: The correct spelling is "existence." Existance is incorrectly spelt. Existence means the fact of something or someone existing.

mock65.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom: CHARLEY HORSE
mock65.explanation: "Charley horse" (मांसपेशी में खिंचाव) — इसका अर्थ है a muscle spasm or cramp। Example: "I got a bad Charley Horse after swimming for an hour." "Lucky" (भाग्यशाली), "Cunning" (चालाक), और "Foolish" (मूर्ख) गलत हैं।

mock65.word: They seemed very anxious … her comfort and gave the girl ______ instructions as to where to keep … things, when not to lean out and how to … speaking to strangers.
mock65.explanation: The narrator is observing a couple and inferring their relationship to the girl. Presumably means what is asserted is very likely though not known for certain - a strong contender. Evidently means in a way that is clearly seen or understood, obviously; since the narrator is observing visible behaviour (anxiety, farewell gestures), this fits naturally. Absolutely and likely don't fit the grammatical/contextual structure. Grammar Note: Evidently and presumably are both adverbs of inference; evidently is used when evidence is visibly present, which fits here since the couple's anxious behaviour is visible to the narrator.

mock65.word: Today, success is being ______ with making money...
mock65.explanation: "Confused" (भ्रमित/गड्डमड्ड) — success को money से equate करना एक misunderstanding है। Context में "confusing success with making money" की बात हो रही है। "Decreased" (घटाया), "Amazed" (चकित), और "Challenged" (चुनौती दी) इस context में गलत हैं।

mock65.word: ...their means of ______ being their powerful hind legs.
mock65.explanation: "Locomotion" (संचलन) means the means of movement, correctly describing the function of the hind legs.

options: shall take up any designated obligations
should pick up all tasks and works
mock65.explanation: "Will execute assigned duties" most concisely and correctly conveys performing tasks that have been allocated.

mock65.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM. SUCCINCT
mock65.explanation: "Succinct" (संक्षिप्त) means brief and to the point. Its antonym is "lengthy" (लंबा) meaning overextended. All other options (curt, pithy, terse) are synonyms of succinct.

mock65.word: Choose the word that means the same as the given word: JAPE
mock65.explanation: "Jape" (मज़ाक/चुटकुला) का अर्थ है a joke or jest, often sarcastic। इसका synonym है "Quip" (चतुराई भरी टिप्पणी) — a clever or witty remark। "Quick" (तेज़), "Quarrel" (झगड़ा), और "Quit" (छोड़ना) असंबंधित हैं।

mock65.word: ...in consumer-oriented application such as financial ______...
mock65.explanation: "Portfolios" (वित्तीय दस्तावेज़/प्रबंधन) — financial portfolios — investment और financial management documents। "Literacy" (साक्षरता), "Marketplace" (बाज़ार), और "Burden" (बोझ) इस context में गलत हैं।

mock66.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM. RISIBLE
mock66.explanation: "Risible" (हास्यास्पद) means causing laughter or deserving to be laughed at. Its antonym is "serious" (गंभीर) — solemn and not intended to be funny.

mock66.word: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence: He says to Gayatri, 'You are writing an apology to the warden.'
options: He says to Gayatri that she was writing an apology to the warden.
He tells Gayatri that she is writing an apology to the warden.
He says to Gayatri that she was writing an apology to the warden.
He tells Gayatri that she was writing an apology to the warden.
mock66.explanation: This tests Direct to Indirect (Reported) Speech rules. Reporting Verb Tense: 'says' is present tense, so when the reporting verb is present tense, the tense of the reported speech does NOT change. Verb Change: 'says to' changes to 'tells' in indirect speech when followed by an indirect object like Gayatri. Pronoun Change: 'You' changes to 'she' (third person) based on the object of the reporting clause. Quotation marks are removed and 'that' is added as a connector. Since the reporting verb is present, 'is writing' stays as 'is writing.' Golden Rule: Reporting verb in Present/Future → tense of reported speech does NOT change; Reporting verb in Past → tense shifts back (backshift). Correct Sentence: He tells Gayatri that she is writing an apology to the warden.

mock66.word: ...astounds ______ from the older generation.
mock66.explanation: "Astounds those from the older generation" correctly uses "those" to refer to people of the older generation.

mock66.word: When he beheld the tears trickling down by the face of Gisippus, he then comforted him, and thanked him for his ______ kindness, refused the benefit that he offered, saying that it were better that a hundred such unkind wretches as he was should perish, than so noble a man as was Gisippus should sustain reproach or damage.
mock66.explanation: Since Gisippus is described as exceptionally noble, "incomparable" (अतुलनीय / बेजोड़) kindness, meaning matchless, best fits the context.

दो errors हैं: (1) "Being" की जगह past participle "been" होना चाहिए (present perfect tense)। (2) Preposition "behind" की जगह "in" होना चाहिए — "in prison" सही idiom है। Correct sentence: "He has been put in prison for life."

mock66.word: ..many believed that this was a divine ______ for their sins."
mock66.explanation: "Divine punishment" (ईश्वरीय दंड) — plague को लोगों ने पापों की सज़ा माना। "Blessing" (आशीर्वाद — opposite), "Reward" (पुरस्कार), और "Retribution" (प्रतिशोध — less specific) इस religious context में कम सटीक हैं।

mock66.word: Abraham narrated Reshma his last escapade in a dramatic way.
mock66.explanation: Escapade implies a thrilling or exciting experience. "Adventure" (रोमांचक अनुभव) carries the same sense of an unusual and exciting event, making it the correct replacement. Exertion means physical effort; Ordeal (कष्टदायक अनुभव) means a painful experience; Escape simply means getting away — none capture the excitement of "escapade."

mock66.word: Fall back or sink again into a worse state after an improvement
mock66.explanation: "Relapse" (पुनः बिगड़ना) means to become worse again after a period of improvement. "Rekindle" means to revive feelings, "Reiterate" means to repeat.

mock66.word: If no substitution is required, select 'No improvement.' Applications are to be sent to the Principal before 30th May.
mock66.explanation: The construction "be + to + verb" (are to be sent) is correctly used here to express a formal instruction or something that must be done, so the sentence is already correct and needs no improvement.

mock66.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Out for the count
mock66.explanation: "Out for the count" (गहरी नींद में होना या बेहोश होना) का अर्थ है unconscious or soundly asleep। Example: It looks like Ben's out for the count.

mock66.word: Select the sentence that contains a spelling error.
options: Researchers recommend that pesticides should not be used indiscriminately
Journalism is often the work of painstaking rigour and perseverence (गलत) that detail the socio-political experiences
The venerated singer was also secretive, moody and very intense
Elephants are in trouble mainly because people desire ivory trinkets
mock66.explanation: "perseverence" गलत spelling है। सही spelling "perseverance" (दृढ़ता) है।

mock66.word: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error. Sam took a little time to thought before he replied.
mock66.explanation: After "to" (infinitive), we must use the base form of the verb, not the past form. "Thought" should be "to think."

mock66.word: Choose the word that can substitute the given group of words. Study of heavenly bodies.
mock66.explanation: Astronomy (खगोल विज्ञान) is the scientific study of celestial objects such as stars, planets, and galaxies. Numerology is the study of mystical relationships between numbers and events. Astrology uses celestial positions to predict human affairs. Stargazing is an informal hobby, not a formal study.

mock66.word: I trust him completely. His conduct is _________ SUSPICION.
mock66.explanation: "Above suspicion" (संदेह से परे) is a commonly used idiomatic expression meaning someone's conduct is so trustworthy that they are not suspected of doing anything wrong.

mock66.word: Did you know whether or not we can exchange the dress if my sister doesn't like it?
options: if my sister doesn't like it
mock66.explanation: "Did you know" is incorrect. Since this is a general/present statement, it should be "Do you know." The simple present tense is required here.

mock66.word: I am quite satisfied that I have not been ___ in doing whatever was needful for building up their character.
mock66.explanation: "Negligent" (लापरवाह) means failing to take proper care, fitting the sentence's sense that the speaker was satisfied at not having failed in their duties. "Affectionate," "Caring," and "Devoted" describe positive qualities and don't fit the structure "satisfied that I have not been ___."

mock66.word: There is no point in allowing the ______ to grow before taking hasty action to set things right.
mock66.explanation: "Remedy" (उपचार) means a solution or treatment for a problem, fitting the context of correcting an error before it grows unmanageable. "Malady" means a disease; "Melody" means a tune; "Parody" means an exaggerated imitation — none fit the idea of a corrective solution.

mock66.word: The eagle's gaze was non-intimidating from afar, but the experienced climbers were undaunted by the challenge. Select the antonym of the underlined word.
mock66.explanation: Non-intimidating (भयरहित) means not causing fear. Formidable, meaning inspiring fear or awe, is the direct opposite.
