mock66.word: Sentences of a paragraph are given below. S1 and S6 are fixed; arrange A, B, C, D in order. S1. Time has made the face A. and it remains so B. that is being put on it C. in spite of the lipstick D. of my country stark, chastened and sad S6. by the hand of the spiritual half-castes.
mock66.explanation: Correct Answer: (C) DACB. D logically continues S1 ("the face of my country"). A elaborates on that state. C introduces the metaphor (रूपक) of lipstick, and B completes the thought before leading into S6.

mock66.word: (Knowledge landscape passage — CPO) There will be a sizeable shift in how we _________ the world's energy, water, food, and sanitation needs.
mock66.explanation: Meet (पूरा करना) means to fulfill or satisfy a need, requirement, or condition — "meeting the world's energy, water, food needs."

mock66.word: Four words are given out of which one word is correctly spelt. Select the correctly spelt word.
mock66.explanation: The correct spelling is P-E-R-C-O-L-A-T-I-O-N. Percolation means the process by which a liquid filters through a porous substance. (Hindi: रिसाव / छनना) Example: The percolation of rainwater through the soil. The prefix is "per-" (through), not "par-". All other options have incorrect vowels in the prefix.

mock66.word: With tear eyes, she said goodbye to her dear friend.
mock66.explanation: "Teary" is the adjective form of "tear," meaning "full of tears; tearful." An adjective is needed to describe "eyes."

mock66.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. To have sticky fingers
options: To have a tendency to steal
mock66.explanation: "To have sticky fingers" (चोरी करने की आदत होना) idiomatically means to have a habit of stealing — as if things stick to one's fingers.

mock66.word: Given below are four sentences. Pick the option that gives the correct order: A. The Egyptians believed that the body and soul were important to human existence, in life and in death. B. This ensured the soul's return to the body so that the deceased would live happily ever after. C. Their funerary practices...were designed to assist the deceased find their way in the after world. D. The tombs were thus, filled with food, tools, domestic wares, treasures...
mock66.explanation: A (belief about body/soul) → C (funerary practices) → D (tombs filled with necessities) → B (soul's return ensured). A establishes belief, C explains practices, D elaborates on tombs, B gives the purpose/result. Correct order: ACDB

mock66.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom: BE AT A LOOSE END
mock66.explanation: "Be at a loose end" (खाली बैठे होना) — इसका अर्थ है to be idle, having nothing to do। Example: "If you find yourself at a loose end, you could always read the novel." "Turning point" (महत्वपूर्ण मोड़), "Defining moment" (निर्णायक क्षण), और "In much trouble" (बड़ी मुसीबत में) गलत हैं।

mock67.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom: 'Neither fish nor fowl'
options: Something which can neither be supplied easily nor be made available readily
Something which can neither be eaten nor be preserved
Something which can neither be categorised easily nor be accepted befittingly
Something which is very hard to prepare
mock67.explanation: 'Neither fish nor fowl' means something that does not fit into a recognised category, something that doesn't rightly belong or fit well in a given group or situation (e.g. Graduate teaching assistants are neither fish nor fowl - neither completely students nor teachers). Mistake Point: Don't be misled by the literal words 'fish' and 'fowl' - this idiom has nothing to do with food; it is about categorisation and belonging. Origin: According to the Oxford Dictionary of English Idioms, this idiom derives from dietary laws of the church relating to fasting, referring to something not fit for any class of people - neither for those who ate fish (clergy) nor those who ate fowl (common people).

mock67.word: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Rahul sings / very sweet / when he is / in a good mood.
mock67.explanation: "Sweet" (मीठा) is an adjective that modifies nouns. Here, it is modifying the verb "sings," so an adverb is needed. "Sweetly" (मधुरता से) is the correct adverb form. Correct sentence: Rahul sings very sweetly when he is in a good mood.

mock67.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock67.explanation: The correct spelling is Privilege (विशेषाधिकार), meaning a special right or advantage granted to a particular person or group. "Priviledge" incorrectly places 'd' before 'g'. Occurrence, Accommodation, and Erroneous are all correctly spelled.

mock67.word: Radha climbed the / ladder cautious, / but she fell off / despite being careful. Identify the segment with the error.
mock67.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) ladder cautious. "Cautious" is an adjective. It needs to be replaced by the adverb "cautiously" (सावधानीपूर्वक) to properly modify the verb "climbed."

mock67.word: It is important to let others know ______ you have understood the instructions.
mock67.explanation: "That" correctly introduces the subordinate clause explaining the content others should know.

mock67.word: announcing his presence by letting ______ ear-piercing shrieks every 45 seconds.
mock67.explanation: "Letting out shrieks" (चीखना) is the correct phrase — meaning to produce or release sounds. "Letting loose" also works but "letting out" is more standard for sounds.

mock67.word: Arrange the sentences to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. On the answer sheet, darken the circle of the correct answer against each question. B. You must select the best answer for each question. C. Every question is followed by four answer choices marked 1, 2, 3 and 4. D. If you darken more than one circle, the answer will be counted as wrong.
mock67.explanation: C comes first: introduces the structure of the question paper (four answer choices) - the starting point of instructions. B comes second: instructs the candidate to select the best answer - the first action to take. A comes third: instructs how to record the answer (darken the circle) - the second action. D comes last: provides a warning about a specific mistake (darkening more than one circle) - naturally the concluding instruction. Logical Sequence: Setup (C) → Action 1 (B) → Action 2 (A) → Warning/Consequence (D).

mock67.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Pick the option that gives their correct order. A. New ideas on marketing were brainstormed and all the members agreed to try them forthwith. B. Upon their return from an off-site camp Ravi and Sunil decided to hold a meeting. C. As soon as everyone assembled, the meeting which lasted for four hours, began. D. Its purpose was to replace some of the old ways of marketing used by the company with new ones.
mock67.explanation: B introduces the meeting (subject introduction). C describes when the meeting began (immediate follow-up). D explains the purpose of the meeting. A states the outcome — new ideas and consensus.

mock67.word: in 2000, Total ______ with its main French competitor, Elf Aquitaine.
mock67.explanation: "Merged with" (विलय होना) — two companies के एक होने के लिए "merged" सही past tense है।

mock67.word: His ______ career as an F1 racer ended.
mock67.explanation: "Promising" (संभावनाशील) means showing signs of future success, fitting his once-bright F1 career.

mock67.word: Select the correct passive form: "Somebody has stolen the purse which I had bought recently."
options: I had bought a purse recently and it has been stolen.
My purse which had been bought recently by her has been stolen.
The purse which had been bought recently by me has been stolen.
The purse recently bought has been stolen by somebody.
mock67.explanation: Using the past perfect passive structure "had been + V3," the subordinate clause becomes "had been bought," and "has been stolen" follows correctly for the present perfect main clause.

mock67.word: Without it, our jobs, our free time and our mobility ______ all be very different.
mock67.explanation: Hypothetical situation के लिए "would" (conditional modal) सही है। "Without oil, everything would be different."

mock67.word: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom: 'Instead of confusing others, let us call a spade a spade in front of the audience.'
mock67.explanation: 'Call a spade a spade' means to speak frankly and directly, without disguising or softening what one means; to say things as they really are - the idiom encourages direct, honest communication without euphemism or evasion. Pretend superiority is unrelated. Remain silent is the opposite of the idiom's meaning. Be in conflict is unrelated. Origin: The phrase dates back to ancient Greek texts and has been used in English since the 16th century to mean plain-speaking.

mock67.word: Mobile phones have also literally ______ phone booths.
mock67.explanation: Phone booths की संख्या 6.2 million से 0.2 million हो गई — mobile phones ने phone booths को literally "destroyed" (नष्ट) कर दिया।

mock67.word: Sometimes, the ______ is so wide that there hardly … to be any possibility of bridging it.
mock67.explanation: "Gulf" (गहरी खाई/अंतर) means an important difference between the ideas or situations of two groups, exactly fitting the context of the wide generational divide between young and old. "Bay" and "Coast" refer to geographical land-sea features; "Crater" refers to a volcanic hole — none fit the metaphorical sense of "difference."

mock67.word: Select the active/passive conversion: "Someone dealt with the problem by developing a reference test."
options: The problem is dealt with by developing a reference test.
The problem was dealt with by developing a reference test.
A reference test was being developed to deal with the problem.
The problem has been dealt with.
mock67.explanation: Since "dealt" is simple past tense, the passive form uses "was + V3," giving "was dealt with," correctly retaining the original meaning.

mock67.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Impudent
mock67.explanation: Impudent (ढीठ/असभ्य) means very rude and lacking respect. Polite (विनम्र) means having good manners and showing respect for others — making it the perfect antonym. Insolent (अशिष्ट) is a synonym. Confident (आत्मविश्वासी) and Triumphant (विजयी) are unrelated.

mock67.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: RESOLUTE
mock67.explanation: "Resolute" (दृढ़निश्चयी) का अर्थ है having or showing great determination। इसका synonym है "Strong" (मज़बूत/दृढ़) — very firm; difficult to fight against। "Bulky" (भारी-भरकम), "Agreeable" (सुखद), और "Victorious" (विजयी) भिन्न अर्थ रखते हैं।

mock67.word: We live in ______ age of wonders and miracles.
mock67.explanation: "An age" is correct as "age" begins with a vowel sound.

"Procure" (प्राप्त करना/खरीदना) — to obtain something, especially with care or effort। Military equipment को formally obtain करने के लिए "procure" standard term है। "Accomplish" (सिद्ध करना), "Advance" (आगे बढ़ना), और "Achieve" (प्राप्त करना) इस specific context में गलत हैं।

mock67.word: Sir, can you reflect on the usefulness or ______ of blocking all the highways?
mock67.explanation: "Futility" (व्यर्थता/निरर्थकता) — pointlessness of an action। "Usefulness or futility" — दोनों contrasting words हैं। "Scorch" (जलाना), "Incompatibility" (असंगति), और "Impotence" (शक्तिहीनता) इस context में गलत हैं।

mock67.word: In less than five years ______ its inauguration, this colossal monument...
mock67.explanation: "Since" (जब से) — inauguration के बाद से अब तक की continuous timeline को indicate करता है। "Before" (पहले) — event से पूर्व का समय बताता है। "When" (जब) — specific occasion introduce करता है। "On" (पर) — specific date/day के लिए है।

mock67.word: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
mock67.explanation: "Aquaintance" में 'c' missing है। Correct spelling है "Acquaintance" (जान-पहचान)। "Treacherous" (विश्वासघाती), "Serendipity" (सुखद संयोग), और "Malicious" (दुर्भावनापूर्ण) सभी correctly spelt हैं।

mock67.word: They seemed very anxious … her comfort and gave the girl ______ instructions as to where to keep … things, when not to lean out and how to … speaking to strangers.
mock67.explanation: The context describes anxious parents giving instructions before a train journey; anxious parents naturally give thorough, complete instructions, not brief ones. Concise means brief and to the point - contradicts the anxious nature of the parents. Elaborate is close but implies complexity for its own sake. Detailed means containing a lot of information and attention to particular facts - the most natural and fitting word. Unspecified means not clearly identified - the opposite of what anxious parents would give. Context Clue: The phrase 'very anxious' earlier in the passage is the clue - anxious parents give detailed, not concise, instructions.

mock67.word: The following sentence has been divided into parts. The committee recommended / that the bill / be passed immediately. Select the part with the error, or 'No error'.
mock67.explanation: Correct Answer: (D) No error. The subjunctive mood (संभाव्य रूप) "be passed" is correctly used after the phrase "recommended that."

mock68.word: Select the correct spelling of the incorrectly spelt word: "Good characteristics are indispensible to succeed in life."
mock68.explanation: The correct spelling is "Indispensable" (अपरिहार्य), meaning absolutely necessary or essential.

mock68.word: Select the option that will improve the (bracketed) part of the sentence. In case of no improvement select 'No improvement.' Check all radiators for (meagre leaks, special) round pipework connections.
mock68.explanation: "Meagre leaks" (अपर्याप्त/बहुत कम) is an unusual combination. "Small leaks" (छोटे रिसाव) is precise and natural. "Especially" (विशेष रूप से) correctly emphasizes attention to pipework connections. "Specially" is less appropriate here. Correct sentence: Check all radiators for small leaks, especially round pipework connections.

mock68.word: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Repercussion (प्रतिक्रिया)
mock68.explanation: ऊपर Q.50 में explain किया जा चुका है।

mock68.word: Cloud computing and storage solutions provide users and enterprises ______ various capabilities...
mock68.explanation: "Provide someone with something" — यह fixed structure है। "Provide users with various capabilities" सही है। "For," "To," और "By" इस structure में नहीं आते।

mock68.word: Parts of a sentence about a world tour are given in jumbled order. Arrange them correctly. A. to become severely seasick B. a world tour, but he was C. a nervous flyer and tended D. he wanted to go on
mock68.explanation: D introduces the subject's desire; B states the object of desire; C describes his fear of flying; A completes the sentence with his tendency toward seasickness.

mock68.word: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Out of the given options pick the one that gives their correct order. A. This cafe, which has just opened up in Tokyo, is not for the faint of heart. B. Here you sip your drink in the company of 35 snakes. C. None of them are venomous, meaning customers can get up close and personal with the reptiles. D. The cafe's owner says he's interested in conservation, and hopes customers will realise the animals are worth saving, even though they often have a bad reputation.
mock68.explanation: A independently introduces the topic of the snake cafe in Tokyo, C follows by describing what happens there, B continues with the detail that the snakes are non-venomous, and D concludes with the owner's perspective on conservation, forming a coherent narrative.
The correct spelling is Wipe (पोंछना), meaning to clean or dry by rubbing. The extra 'h' in "Whipe" makes it incorrect. Wish, Witch, and Wipe are all correctly spelled.

mock68.word: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Penchant
mock68.explanation: Penchant (रुचि/झुकाव) means a strong liking or habit for something. Hatred (घृणा) is the direct opposite — a strong dislike. Fear (डर), Joy (खुशी), and Anxiety (चिंता) are emotions unrelated to being antonyms of penchant.
